text: Overview of Alloy GH4586: GH4586 is a Ni-Cr-Co based precipitation-hardening wrought superalloy with a service temperature range of -196°C to 800°C, and it can be used for short periods at 850°C. The alloy forms γ' precipitation strengthening phases through the addition of aluminum and titanium, and is solid solution strengthened by cobalt, chromium, tungsten, and molybdenum. Under suitable hot working and heat treatment conditions, fine granular carbides precipitate along grain boundaries, providing supplementary strengthening. The alloy exhibits high high-temperature strength, good oxidation resistance, corrosion resistance, and ablation resistance, along with favorable hot workability and weldability. The main products include bar stock, round disc blanks, and die forgings.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy GH4586: This alloy is a high-performance superalloy turbine disc material independently developed in China with proprietary intellectual property rights. It has been used to manufacture heat-resistant load-bearing components for engines in aviation and aerospace fields, including turbine rotors for liquid oxygen/kerosene rocket engines and die forgings for turbine rotors in various types of aerospace engines, demonstrating satisfactory performance in service.
Material Designation of Alloy GH4586: GH4586 (GH586).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH4586: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Q/GYB 05010 Hot-rolled and Forged Bars of GH4586 Alloy Q/GYB 05021 Forged Round Disc Blanks of GH4586 Alloy Q/GYB 05024 Die Forgings of GH4586 Alloy
Melting Process for Alloy GH4586: Vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting double-melt process.

Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy GH4586: Extracted from Q/GYB 05010, Q/GYB 05021, and Q/GYB 05024. The standard heat treatment regime for hot-rolled and forged bars, forged round disc blanks, and die forgings is: 1080°C ± 20°C × 4h / AC + 760°C ± 10°C × 4h / AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy GH4586: Extracted from Q/GYB 05010, Q/GYB 05021, and Q/GYB 05024. Hot-rolled and forged bars from d10mm to 300mm; forged round disc blanks from d150mm to 600mm; die forgings from d100mm to 630mm. Bars are generally supplied in the non-heat-treated condition; forged round disc blanks and die forgings are generally supplied in the non-heat-treated condition, but can also be supplied after heat treatment and machining.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy GH4586: 1317°C ~ 1342°C[1]
Density of Alloy GH4586: ρ = 8.39 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy GH4586: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Weldability of Alloy GH4586: Using Ni-Cr-Si-Cu-Mo-Co brazing filler metal to braze GH4586 with 1Cr18Ni9Ti or GH4141 alloys results in satisfactory tensile strength of the joints at room temperature and elevated temperatures.
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy GH4586: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product form.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy GH4586: The alloy has good machinability.
Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy GH4586: The peak precipitation temperature for M₂₃C₆ type carbides is 800°C.

Overview of Alloy FGH4095: FGH4095 is a Ni-based precipitation-hardened superalloy produced via powder metallurgy process. The volume fraction of the γ' phase accounts for approximately 47% to 50% of the alloy, suitable for long-term use below 650°C. Compared to conventional cast and wrought superalloys, it offers uniform microstructure, fine grains, high yield strength, and good fatigue resistance. It is currently one of the highest strength level alloys for turbine discs operating at 650°C, and is also used for high-performance aero-engine drum shafts, ring components, and other rotating hot-section parts.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy FGH4095: The alloy has been used to manufacture turbine discs, compressor discs, diffuser discs, front and rear shields for turbine blades, and elastic rings for aero-engines, with good batch production and service performance. Similar grades are widely used in foreign aviation and aerospace fields, primarily for manufacturing rotating components of high-performance aero-engines, such as discs, rings, and drum shafts. The alloy exhibits stable microstructure and no precipitation of harmful phases after long-term aging and stress-aging at service temperatures.
Material Designation of Alloy FGH4095: FGH4095 (FGH95).
Similar Grades to Alloy FGH4095: René 95 (USA).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy FGH4095: GB/T14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials HB7727 Specification for FGH95 Powder Metallurgy Superalloy Disc Components for Turboshaft Engines Q/GYB 05085 FGH95 Alloy Hot Isostatically Pressed Components Q/GYB 05082 Heat Treatment of FGH95 Alloy Components

Melting and Primary Manufacturing Process for Alloy FGH4095: Master alloy is produced by vacuum induction melting. Alloy powder is obtained via argon atomization (AA) or plasma rotating electrode process (PREP). Component blanks are made by processes such as direct hot isostatic pressing (HIP) of powder (AS-HIP), HIP + isothermal forging, or HIP + hot die forging. PREP powder particle size is 50μm ~ 150μm; AA powder particle size is 20μm ~ 100μm. Extracted from Q/GYB 05085: (1170 ~ 1190)°C ± 10°C, pressure not less than 120 MPa, holding time not less than 2h.

Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy FGH4095: Extracted from HB7727 and Q/GYB05082. The alloy has two standard heat treatment regimes: Regime I: (1120 ~ 1160)°C ± 10°C × (2 ~ 4)h / salt quench or oil quench + 870°C ± 10°C × 1h / AC + 650°C ± 10°C × 24h / AC, suitable for disc-shaped components. Regime II: (1140 ~ 1160)°C ± 10°C × (1 ~ 2)h / salt quench + 870°C ± 10°C × 1.5h / AC + 650°C ± 10°C × 24h / AC, suitable for ring-shaped components.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy FGH4095: Extracted from HB7727 and Q/GYB05085. Disc-shaped components: d170mm ~ 180mm, thickness 35mm ~ 65mm. Ring-shaped components: outer diameter 636mm, inner diameter 525mm, thickness 21mm ~ 30mm. Rings and discs are supplied in the hot isostatically pressed, or HIP + isothermal forged, or HIP + hot die forged condition, and after heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy FGH4095: 1267°C ~ 1344°C (DSC method)[5].
Density of Alloy FGH4095: ρ = (8.27 ~ 8.29) g/cm³[1,2].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy FGH4095: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy FGH4095: Same as the alloy's heat treatment regime.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy FGH4095: No special requirements.
Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy FGH4095: γ' phase precipitation peak temperature is 850°C ~ 900°C; complete solution temperature is 1160°C[5].

Overview of Alloy GH4049: GH4049 is a Ni-Co-Cr based precipitation-hardening wrought superalloy with a service temperature below 950°C. The alloy possesses good oxidation resistance below 1000°C and high high-temperature strength below 950°C. It is suitable for manufacturing gas turbine working blades operating between 850°C and 950°C. Main products include hot-rolled and forged bars, and die-forged turbine blades.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy GH4049: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine turbine blades and other high-temperature load-bearing components. After long-term production and service validation, it has become one of the most widely used blade materials. Compared to similar Ni-based alloys, GH4049 has poorer hot workability, but its workability can be improved, and its impact toughness at 1000°C ~ 1170°C can be increased by 1-3 times after electro-slag remelting or vacuum arc remelting.[6]
Material Designation of Alloy GH4049: GH4049 (GH49, 212).
Similar Grades to Alloy GH4049: ЭИ929, ЭИ929-BД, XH55BMTΦКЮΡ (Russia).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH4049: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GB/T 14993 Hot-Rolled Bars of Superalloys for Rotating Components GJB 1953A Specification for Hot-Rolled Bars of Superalloys for Aero-Engine Rotating Parts HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys
Melting Process for Alloy GH4049: Vacuum induction melting + electro-slag remelting, or vacuum induction melting + vacuum arc remelting.

Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy GH4049: Extracted from HB/Z 140. The standard heat treatment regime for hot-rolled bars for rotating components is: 1200°C ± 10°C × 2h / AC + 1050°C ± 10°C × 4h / AC + 850°C ± 10°C × 8h / AC, HB 363 ~ 302.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy GH4049: Extracted from GB/T 14993 and GJB 1953A. Hot-rolled bars: d8mm ~ 55mm. Bars are supplied in the non-heat-treated condition, fully ground or turned. Die-forged blades are supplied after component heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy GH4049: 1320°C ~ 1390°C.[5]
Electrical Resistivity of Alloy GH4049: 20°C, ρ = 1.36 × 10⁻⁶ Ω·m.[3]
Density of Alloy GH4049: ρ = 8.44 g/cm³.[1]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy GH4049: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming Process and Properties of Alloy GH4049: Deformation temperature range: 1180°C ~ 1080°C. Ingots are directly rolled into bars with a heating temperature of 1160°C ± 10°C and a finish rolling temperature not lower than 1020°C. Blade die forging heating temperature is 1170°C ± 10°C, and the stop forging temperature is not less than 1050°C. For near-net-shape blade die forging, heating or heat treatment should be conducted in a protective atmosphere. When heating or heat treatment is performed in an oxidizing atmosphere, the machining allowance per side must not be less than 1.5mm.
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy GH4049: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product form.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy GH4049: No special requirements.

Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy GH4049: γ' phase precipitation peak is around 850°C ~ 900°C, complete dissolution temperature is about 1100°C ~ 1130°C. M₆C maximum precipitation temperature is 950°C ~ 1050°C.

Overview of Alloy K403: K403 is a Ni-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-cast superalloy strengthened by multiple metallic elements, with a service temperature below 1000°C. The alloy has relatively high high-temperature strength, with a stress rupture strength of 150MPa at 1000°C for 100h and 94MPa for 1000h. It has good castability and can be precision cast into complex shapes, suitable for manufacturing gas turbine guide vanes operating below 1000°C and turbine rotor blades operating below 900°C, as well as other components.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy K403: The alloy has been used to manufacture various aero-engine turbine rotor blades, guide vanes, and other high-temperature components. The alloy contains only about 5 wt.% Co, making it relatively inexpensive, but its intermediate temperature ductility is low. When the contents of chromium, aluminum, titanium, and molybdenum are near the upper limit, there is a tendency for σ phase precipitation during long-term aging at 850°C. The alloy's hot corrosion resistance is poor; protective coatings are required for long-term service at high temperatures.
Material Designation of Alloy K403: K403 (K3).
Similar Grades to Alloy K403: жC6K (Russia).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy K403: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 1: Master Alloys for Ordinary Precision Castings of Superalloys HB7728 Specification for Precision Cast Turbine Blades of Aviation Superalloys HB7763 Specification for Equiaxed-Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero-Engines HB/Z140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys

Melting Process for Alloy K403: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, and then remelted in a vacuum induction furnace for investment casting of components and test bars. Pouring temperature: 1400°C ~ 1420°C; mold temperature: 780°C ~ 950°C.
Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy K403: Extracted from HB/Z140. The alloy can be used in the as-cast condition or after heat treatment. The standard heat treatment regime is: 1210°C ± 10°C × 4h / AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy K403: Extracted from GJB5512.1 and HB7763. Master alloy is cast into round bars with diameters ranging from 70mm to 90mm. Master alloy and rotor blades are supplied in the as-cast condition; guide vanes are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy K403: 1260°C ~ 1338°C[2]
Density of Alloy K403: ρ = 8.10 g/cm³[2]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K403: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Casting Process and Properties of Alloy K403: The alloy has good castability and can be investment cast into complex shapes such as thin-walled hollow blades and integral turbines. Casting shrinkage is about 2%.
Weldability of Alloy K403: Welding can be performed using vacuum brazing or pulsed argon arc welding.

Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K403: Non-machined net-shape precision castings should be heat treated under argon protection or vacuum. The parameters are the same as the standard heat treatment.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K403: Solid state aluminizing or slurry aluminizing-silicon coating can be applied.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy K403: Turning, drilling, milling, planing, and grinding should be performed at relatively low speeds.

Overview of Alloy GH2901: GH2901 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based precipitation-hardening wrought superalloy. The alloy is strengthened by the metastable γ' [Ni₃(Ti,Al)] phase through aging. Trace aluminum inhibits the transformation of γ' to the η-Ni₃Ti phase. It exhibits high yield strength and creep rupture strength below 650°C, good oxidation resistance below 760°C, and stable microstructure during long-term service. Main products include turbine discs, compressor discs, shafts, static structural components, turbine outer rings, and fasteners.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy GH2901: The alloy has been used to manufacture rotating components and fasteners for aero-engines and land-based gas turbines, providing long service life for components. The alloy shows no notch sensitivity after standard heat treatment or after long-term aging for various durations within 550°C ~ 750°C. However, if forging parameters or operations are inappropriate, harmful long or plate-like Ti₂SC phases, large granular borides, and Ti(CN) phases can precipitate at grain boundaries after standard heat treatment, leading to notch sensitivity during creep rupture tests. This notch sensitivity cannot be eliminated by heat treatment but only by re-hot working to obtain a suitable microstructure.[10] The thermal expansion coefficient of the alloy is close to that of ferritic heat-resistant steels, allowing joining of the two materials without special provisions for thermal expansion.
Material Designation of Alloy GH2901: GH2901 (GH109).
Similar Grades to Alloy GH2901: Incoloy 901 (USA), Nimonic 901 (UK), Z8NCDT42 (France), 2.4662 (Germany).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH2901: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GJB 5280 Specification for Disc Forgings of Superalloys for Aero-Engines GJB 5301 Specification for Ring Components of Aviation Superalloys HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys WS9-7029 Billet GH901 Alloy Bars for Shafts WS9-7029 Bar Stock GH901 Alloy Bars for Rotating Parts WS9-7031 GH901 Alloy Hot-rolled and Forged Bars Liao Xin 6-0055 Technical Condition for GH901 Alloy Bars for Steam Turbine Valve Stems QJ/DT 0160013 Technical Condition for GH901 Alloy Bars for Gas Turbines

Melting Process for Alloy GH2901: Non-vacuum induction melting + vacuum arc remelting + vacuum arc remelting, or vacuum induction melting + electro-slag remelting, or vacuum induction melting + vacuum arc remelting.
Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy GH2901: Extracted from HB/Z 140, Liao Xin 6-0055, and QJ/DT 0160013

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy GH2901: Hot-rolled bars: d20mm ~ 100mm; Forged bars: d50mm ~ 300mm; Various disc forgings and ring forgings. Bars are supplied in the non-heat-treated, ground or turned condition; Disc blanks and ring blanks may be supplied heat-treated or non-heat-treated.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy GH2901: Melting point 1360°C[10].
Density of Alloy GH2901: ρ = 8.21 g/cm³[10].
Corrosion Resistance of Alloy GH2901: The alloy is susceptible to pitting corrosion in hydrochloric acid solutions.
Weldability of Alloy GH2901: The alloy can be argon arc welded using GH2901 alloy filler wire.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy GH2901: Oxide scale can be removed by pickling in HNO₃-HF aqueous solution or by an alkaline descaling-acid pickling process.

Machinability and Grindability of Alloy GH2901: Use carbide tools with positive rake angles and keep the cutting edge sharp. Sulfur-based oil lubricant/coolant is recommended, but must be thoroughly removed before high-temperature heating.[10] Grinding direction should be controlled to avoid inducing residual tensile stress on the component surface. If unavoidable, shot peening should be applied. Rubber-bonded grinding wheels are recommended for component surface grinding.
Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy GH2901: γ' phase begins to dissolve around 950°C; η phase begins to dissolve around 975°C.

Overview of Alloy GH4742: GH4742 is a Ni-Cr-Co based precipitation-hardening wrought superalloy with a service temperature below 800°C. The alloy exhibits high creep rupture strength and creep strength in the range of 550°C ~ 800°C, good overall properties, as well as good microstructural stability and corrosion resistance. It is suitable for high-temperature load-bearing components such as turbine discs, compressor discs, and shafts operating under high stress. Main products include hot-rolled and forged bars, disc blanks, and forgings.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy GH4742: The alloy has been widely used in manufacturing high-temperature load-bearing components for aviation, aerospace engines, and marine gas turbines, such as high-pressure compressor discs, turbine discs, seal shroud discs, load-bearing rings, and fasteners. The alloy has good oxidation resistance below 800°C and satisfactory corrosion resistance under all-weather conditions.
Material Designation of Alloy GH4742: GH4742 (GH742).
Similar Grades to Alloy GH4742: эΠ742, XH62БMKЮ (Russia).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH4742: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Liao Xin 6-0026 Technical Condition for GH742 Alloy Turbine Disc Forgings for Aerospace Applications Q/GYB 05012 Standard for GH4742 Alloy Hot-rolled and Forged Bars Q/GYB 05022 Standard for GH4742 Alloy Forged Round Disc Blanks Q/GYB 05026 Standard for GH4742 Alloy Die Forgings

Melting Process for Alloy GH4742: Vacuum induction melting + vacuum arc remelting, or vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting, or plasma arc melting + vacuum arc remelting, or vacuum induction melting + electron beam remelting.

Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy GH4742: Extracted from Liao Xin 6-0026, Q/GYB 05012, Q/GYB 05022, and Q/GYB 05026. There are two standard heat treatment regimes: Regime I: (1090 ~ 1120)°C × 8h / AC + 850°C ± 10°C × 6h / AC + 780°C ± 10°C × (10 ~ 16)h / AC, suitable for components used at relatively high temperatures. Regime II: 1080°C ± 10°C × 8h / AC + 780°C ± 10°C × 16h / AC, suitable for components used at relatively low temperatures requiring higher strength.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy GH4742: Extracted from Liao Xin 6-0026, Q/GYB 05012, Q/GYB 05022, and Q/GYB 05026. Bars: d ≤ 300mm; Various forged round disc blanks and die forgings. Bars and disc forgings are generally supplied in the non-heat-treated condition.
Density of Alloy GH4742: ρ = 8.23 g/cm³.
Magnetic Properties of Alloy GH4742: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Oxidation Resistance of Alloy GH4742: The alloy has good oxidation resistance below 800°C.
Corrosion Resistance of Alloy GH4742: The alloy has good corrosion resistance under all-weather conditions.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy GH4742: No special requirements.
Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy GH4742: γ' phase begins to precipitate at 855°C ± 10°C; dissolution temperature is 1040°C ~ 1125°C.

Overview of Alloy GH2038: GH2038 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based precipitation-hardening wrought superalloy with a service temperature below 700°C. The alloy is strengthened by precipitation phases formed from elements like aluminum and titanium, with boron added to purify and strengthen grain boundaries. It possesses relatively high thermal strength below 700°C, as well as good machinability and weldability. Main products include hot-rolled and forged bars, plates, and disc blanks.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy GH2038: The alloy is primarily used for hot-section components operating below 700°C, such as turbine discs, combustion chamber shells, turbine casings, supports, shafts, and blades. Plate-like Laves phase precipitates at grain boundaries after heat treatment.[1]
Material Designation of Alloy GH2038: GH2038 (GH38A).
Similar Grades to Alloy GH2038: ЭН696А (Russia).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH2038: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials YB/T 5245 Hot-rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for General Load-Bearing Parts HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys
Melting Process for Alloy GH2038: Non-vacuum induction melting + electro-slag remelting.

Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy GH2038: Extracted from HB/Z 140. The standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Hot-rolled and forged bars: 1180°C ± 10°C × 2h / AC or WQ + 760°C ± 10°C × (16 ~ 25)h / AC, HBS 302 ~ 241. b) Plates: (1020 ~ 1060)°C / AC + 720°C ± 10°C × 8h / AC, solution holding time calculated as (0.8 ~ 2.0) min/mm.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy GH2038: Bars: d > 8mm; Plates: thickness 4mm ~ 14mm. Bars are supplied in the as-worked condition, or solution treated (annealed), or solution treated (annealed) + pickled. Plates are supplied solution treated + alkali-acid pickled + leveled + straightened + edge trimmed.
Density of Alloy GH2038: ρ = 7.94 g/cm³[1].

Forming Process and Properties of Alloy GH2038: Forging of ingots: charging temperature not above 700°C, heating temperature 1090°C ± 10°C, stop forging temperature > 850°C. Hot rolling: heating temperature 1090°C ± 10°C, stop rolling temperature > 700°C.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy GH2038: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH2150: GH2150 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based precipitation-hardening wrought superalloy with a service temperature below 750°C. The alloy is solid solution strengthened by chromium, tungsten, and molybdenum, and precipitation strengthened by titanium, aluminum, and niobium forming aging phases. Trace boron, zirconium, and cerium purify and strengthen grain boundaries. The alloy exhibits high strength, good ductility, low thermal expansion coefficient, and stable microstructure during long-term service. It has good hot workability, as well as satisfactory weldability, cold formability, and machinability. It is suitable for manufacturing welded load-bearing sheet structures for jet engines operating below 700°C, and gas turbine rotors and compressor blades for long-term service below 600°C. Main products include hot-rolled plates, cold-rolled sheets, bars, welding wires, rings, and forgings.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy GH2150: The alloy has been used to manufacture high-temperature components such as aero-engine combustion chamber outer casings and mounting flanges, with good batch production and service records. Similar alloys abroad have been used for jet engine combustion chamber outer casings and turbine blades operating below 600°C, and have passed long-term service validation. If used above 800°C, precipitation of μ phase and coarsening of γ' phase will lead to degradation of mechanical properties.
Material Designation of Alloy GH2150: GH2150 (GH150).
Similar Grades to Alloy GH2150: ЭП718, ВЖ105, ХН45МВТЮБP (Russia).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH2150: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GJB 1952A Specification for Cold-Rolled Sheets of Aviation Superalloys GJB 3020A Specification for Ring Blanks of Aviation Superalloys GJB 3165A Specification for Hot-rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for Aviation Load-Bearing Parts GJB 5301 Specification for Ring Components of Aviation Superalloys HB 5499 Cold-Drawn Welding Wire of HGH150 and HGH533 Alloys HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys
Melting Process for Alloy GH2150: Vacuum induction melting + electro-slag remelting.

Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy GH2150: Extracted from HB/Z 140 and literature [9]. Standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Bars, disc blanks, ring components: (1040 ~ 1060)°C / AC + 750°C ± 10°C × (16 ~ 24)h / AC, HBW 375 ~ 277. b) Cold-rolled sheets: (1040 ~ 1080)°C / AC + 750°C ± 10°C × 16h / AC. c) Cold-drawn welding wire: 1050°C ± 10°C / AC. Similar foreign alloys are recommended to be solution treated at (1000 ~ 1080)°C, followed by two-step aging at 780°C and 650°C.
Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy GH2150: Extracted from GJB 1952A, GJB 3020A, GJB 3165A, GJB 5301, and HB 5499.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy GH2150: 1320°C ~ 1365°C[2].
Density of Alloy GH2150: ρ = 8.26 g/cm³[9].

Corrosion Resistance of Alloy GH2150: Hot corrosion test results (kerosene flow 200 mL/h, air-fuel ratio 45:1, sea salt content 20 ppm, temperature cycle θ°C ⇌ 20°C for 100h): weight loss at 800°C = 7.01 g/m², at 900°C = 17.69 g/m²[9]. The alloy exhibits satisfactory hot corrosion resistance under all-weather conditions[2].

Forming Process and Properties of Alloy GH2150: Ingot forging: heating temperature 1120°C ± 20°C, finish forging temperature above 900°C. Slab hot rolling: heating temperature 1100°C ~ 1150°C. Plate hot rolling: rough rolling heating 1130°C ± 20°C, finish rolling heating 1100°C ± 20°C. Hot-rolled sheet intermediate quenching: 1050°C ~ 1100°C. Cold rolling total reduction ~40%. Cold-rolled finish sheet quenching: 1040°C ~ 1080°C.

Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy GH2150: Intermediate heat treatment for sheet stamping components: 1000°C ± 10°C. Final heat treatment for components: (1040 ~ 1060)°C / AC + 750°C ± 10°C × (16 ~ 24)h / AC. For similar foreign alloys, welded structures and assemblies are recommended to be solution treated at 1070°C ± 10°C, followed by two-step aging: 780°C ± 10°C × 5h and 650°C ± 10°C × 16h. For complex assemblies, aging at 780°C ± 10°C × 5h, furnace cooling to 400°C ~ 600°C, then air cooling is allowed. For bar and forging components, solution treatment at 1080°C ± 10°C × (2 ~ 3)h, oil quenching, then two-step aging is recommended. For components operating at 700°C, solution treatment at 1120°C + 10°C × (2 ~ 3)h, oil quenching, followed by two-step aging is allowed.

Surface Treatment Process for Alloy GH2150: Oxide scale after component heat treatment can be removed by sand blasting or pickling. For pickling, composite alkaline-acid pickling or a single solution of sulfuric acid-nitric acid-hydrofluoric acid-water can be used. Surfaces for argon arc welding can be cleaned by mechanical polishing or chemical methods. Surfaces for resistance welding should be cleaned chemically.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy GH2150: The alloy can be machined satisfactorily.

Overview of Alloy GH4163: GH4163 is a Ni-Cr-Co based precipitation-hardening wrought superalloy with a service temperature up to 850°C. It has relatively good oxidation resistance below 800°C, high yield strength and creep strength, low susceptibility to strain-age cracking, and good thermal fatigue resistance. The alloy has satisfactory formability and weldability. It is suitable for manufacturing welded sheet structures and load-bearing parts for aero-engine main combustors and afterburners. Main products include hot-rolled and cold-rolled sheets, hot-rolled and forged bars, cold-drawn (rolled) seamless tubes, and ring components.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy GH4163: The alloy has been used to manufacture components such as aero-engine flame tubes, afterburner casings, mounting flanges, mounting bases, and tubular parts, with good batch production and service records. Components must be welded in the solution-treated condition, using methods such as spot welding, seam welding, and argon arc welding. η phase precipitates during long-term high-temperature aging or stress aging.
Material Designation of Alloy GH4163: GH4163 (GH163).
Similar Grades to Alloy GH4163: Nimonic 263, C263 (UK), NCK20D (France).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH4163: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GB/T 15062 General Purpose Superalloy Tubes GJB 1952A Specification for Cold-Rolled Sheets of Aviation Superalloys GJB 2297A Specification for Cold-Drawn (Rolled) Seamless Tubes of Aviation Superalloys GJB 3165A Specification for Hot-rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for Aviation Load-Bearing Parts GJB 3317A Specification for Hot-Rolled Plates of Aviation Superalloys GJB 5301 Specification for Ring Components of Aviation Superalloys HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys

Melting Process for Alloy GH4163: Non-vacuum induction melting + electro-slag remelting, or non-VIM + vacuum consumable remelting, or non-VIM + VAR, or VIM + ESR, or VIM + VAR, or VIM + vacuum arc remelting.

Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy GH4163: Extracted from HB/Z 140. Standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: Solution treatment: 1150°C ± 10°C; Aging treatment: 800°C ± 10°C × 8h / AC. Details: a) Forgings, ring components: solution hold (1.5 ~ 2.5)h, cool. b) Tubes: solution hold ≤15min, air cool or appropriate cooling. c) Sheets, strips: solution hold time: thickness ≤4mm, (5~15)min, AC; 4mm < thickness ≤10mm, ≤30min, AC or appropriate cooling; thickness >10mm, ≤120min, AC. d) Hot-rolled and forged bars: solution hold time: diameter ≤8mm, 30min, water quench (AC or oil quench); diameter >8mm, (90~150)min, water quench (AC or oil quench).

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy GH4163: Extracted from GB/T 15062, GJB 1952A, GJB 2297A, GJB 3165A, GJB 3317A, and GJB 5301. Cold-rolled sheets: thickness 0.5mm ~ 4.0mm; Hot-rolled plates: thickness 4.00mm ~ 14.00mm; Hot-rolled and forged bars: diameter 20mm ~ 300mm; Cold-drawn (rolled) seamless tubes: outer diameter ≤50mm; Various forgings and ring components. Sheets are supplied solution treated + alkali-acid pickled + leveled + straightened + edge trimmed; Bars are generally supplied non-heat-treated, ground or turned; Tubes are supplied in cold-rolled condition, or solution treated + pickled, or bright solution treated; Ring components are generally supplied non-heat-treated, or can be supplied solution treated, or solution treated + aged.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy GH4163: 1320°C ~ 1375°C[4].
Density of Alloy GH4163: ρ = 8.35 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy GH4163: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Elastic Properties of Alloy GH4163: 20°C, Shear modulus G = 86 GPa[4].

Forming Process and Properties of Alloy GH4163: Ingot forging heating temperature: 1120°C ± 10°C, finish forging temp. not below 900°C; Slab & hot-rolled plate rolling heating temp.: 1130°C ± 10°C; Cold-rolled sheet total reduction >20%, intermediate quenching temp.: 1080°C ± 10°C.
Processability of Alloy GH4163: Limit drawing ratio in supplied condition: 2.05; Bending coefficient for sheets (thickness 0.5mm~4.0mm) in supplied condition: 3.0.

Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy GH4163: Solution treatment for semi-finished products (sheet, bar, strip, etc.): 1150°C ± 10°C; Intermediate annealing to relieve cold working stress: 1080°C ± 10°C; Components are welded in solution-treated condition, then aged at 800°C ± 10°C × 8h / AC.

Surface Treatment Process for Alloy GH4163: Oxide scale after solution treatment and intermediate annealing is removed chemically. Oxide scale on assemblies after aging is generally not removed. Components are not allowed to be chemically descaled after aging treatment.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy GH4163: The alloy can be machined satisfactorily.

Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy GH4163: γ' phase begins to precipitate around 600°C, peaks at 800°C, dissolves back above 950°C. M₂₃C₆ carbide begins to precipitate around 650°C, peaks at 850°C, dissolves into matrix above 900°C.

Overview of Alloy K4951: K4951 is a Ni-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-cast superalloy characterized by high melting point and low density, with a service temperature below 1100°C. The alloy has stable microstructure and high cost-performance ratio, offering good high-temperature mechanical properties, thermal fatigue resistance, relatively high oxidation and hot corrosion resistance, and excellent castability. Main products are investment cast structural components such as combustion chamber floating wall tiles and turbine guide vanes.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy K4951: The alloy is suitable for manufacturing components operating in high-temperature environments around 1100°C, such as combustion chamber floating wall tiles, turbine guide vanes, nozzle seal segments, and fairing struts for gas turbine engines. The alloy's high-temperature strength is relatively low, making it unsuitable for rotating hot-section components. When used at 1100°C, surface treatments such as pack aluminizing or low-pressure aluminizing can be applied to form aluminide or AlSi coatings, improving hot corrosion resistance. The alloy exhibits good microstructural stability; no TCP phases precipitate during long-term aging at 900°C ~ 1100°C.
Material Designation of Alloy K4951: K4951 (M951).
Material Technical Standards for Alloy K4951: Q/KJ.J02.30 Specification for M951 Cast Superalloy Ingots

Melting and Casting Process for Alloy K4951: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, remelted in a VIM furnace, and components and test bars are cast by investment casting using the lost-wax process and sand-filled molds.

Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy K4951: Extracted from literature [1]. Two standard heat treatment regimes exist: Regime I: 1100°C × 4h / AC, suitable for precision castings requiring higher toughness (e.g., floating wall tiles). Regime II: 1220°C × 4h / AC + 1100°C × 4h / AC + 870°C × 24h / AC, suitable for precision castings requiring higher strength (e.g., turbine guide vanes).

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy K4951: Extracted from Q/KJ.J02.30. Master alloy is cast into round bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy K4951: 1367°C ~ 1398°C[1].
Density of Alloy K4951: ρ = 8.15 g/cm³ ~ 8.25 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K4951: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K4951: As-cast alloy, room temperature hardness is HRC 34 ~ 35.5[1].
Impact Properties of Alloy K4951: As-cast precision cast test bars, room temperature impact toughness: αKU = (310 ~ 370) kJ/m²[1].
Casting Process and Properties of Alloy K4951: Can be investment cast into thin-wall components with wall thickness as low as 1mm, and also integral turbines. Casting shrinkage is about 2%.
Weldability of Alloy K4951: Can be brazed. Recommended brazing filler: B-NA55NbCoWCrAlSiMoTi(C)-S filler metal (domestic BIIP24).

Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K4951: Components can be used in the as-cast condition, or treated using Regime I or II. The process for aluminizing and stress relief annealing is 1100°C × 4h / AC.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K4951: Pack aluminizing or low-pressure aluminizing is used to form aluminide or AlSi coatings on the alloy surface.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy K4951: Turning, drilling, milling, planing, and grinding can be performed at relatively low speeds.

Overview of Alloy K441: K441 is a Ni-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-cast superalloy, a low-segregation alloy with a service temperature below 1050°C. The alloy contains a relatively high amount of tungsten, resulting in a high density. It possesses excellent thermal fatigue resistance, and good overall properties including creep, stress rupture, and fatigue, with mechanical property levels comparable to K640 alloy. It also has relatively good oxidation and hot corrosion resistance, as well as good weldability. Suitable for manufacturing gas turbine guide vanes operating at 1050°C and other components subjected to high temperatures.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy K441: The alloy has been used to manufacture guide vanes for a certain aero-engine and is in batch production. The alloy's high-temperature oxidation and hot corrosion resistance can be further enhanced by surface coatings such as aluminizing or Al-Cr co-diffusion.
Material Designation of Alloy K441: K441 (M41, K41)

Material Technical Standards for Alloy K441: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 1: Master Alloys for Ordinary Precision Castings of Superalloys HB 7728 Specification for Precision Cast Turbine Blades of Aviation Superalloys HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed-Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero-Engines HB/Z140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys Q/KJ.J02.01 K441 Alloy Ingots
Melting and Casting Process for Alloy K441: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, and components and test bars are cast by vacuum induction remelting and investment casting.
Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy K441: Extracted from HB/Z 140. The standard heat treatment regime is: 1000°C ± 10°C × (2 ~ 2.5)h / FC → 900°C ± 10°C / AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy K441: Extracted from GJB 5512.1, HB 7728, HB 7763, and Q/KJ.J02.01. Master alloy is cast into round bars with diameters ranging from 65mm to 85mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy K441: 1320°C ~ 1410°C[1]
Density of Alloy K441: ρ = 8.8 g/cm³[1]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K441: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Casting Process and Properties of Alloy K441: The alloy has good castability and can be cast into thin-wall components with walls as thin as 1mm and turbine blades. Casting shrinkage is about 2%.
Weldability of Alloy K441: The alloy can be argon arc welded.
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K441: Same as the alloy's standard heat treatment regime.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K441: The alloy can be coated via aluminizing, Al-Cr co-diffusion, etc.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy K441: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH1140: GH1140 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based solid-solution strengthened wrought superalloy, strengthened by chromium, tungsten, molybdenum, etc., with a service temperature below 850°C. The alloy possesses moderate thermal strength, high ductility, good thermal and cold fatigue resistance, microstructural stability, and weldability. Suitable for manufacturing sheet structures like combustion chambers for aero-engines and gas turbines, and other high-temperature components. Main products include sheet, bar, tube, wire, strip, and forgings.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy GH1140: The alloy has been used to manufacture numerous components for aero-engines and gas turbines, such as combustion chamber flame tubes, afterburner diffusers, fairing struts, stabilizers, fuel distribution rings, afterburner variable nozzle casings, pipe fittings, liners, and aircraft tail cone skins, and is in batch production. After long-term service at 550°C ~ 800°C, slight hardening occurs, reducing room temperature ductility; intergranular oxidation occurs after long-term service above 700°C. Effective protection can be provided by enamel coatings (No.65, 66-4, W-2, W69-1), or solid state aluminizing and vacuum Al spraying. Its overall properties are higher than GH3030 and comparable to GH3039, and can serve as a substitute for these two alloys.
Material Designation of Alloy GH1140: GH1140 (GH140, GR-2)

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH1140: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GB/T 15062 General Purpose Superalloy Tubes GJB 1952A Specification for Cold-Rolled Sheets of Aviation Superalloys GJB 2297A Specification for Cold-Drawn (Rolled) Seamless Tubes of Aviation Superalloys GJB 2612 Specification for Cold-Drawn Welding Wire of Superalloys GJB 3020A Specification for Ring Blanks of Aviation Superalloys GJB 5301 Specification for Ring Components of Aviation Superalloys GJB 3165A Specification for Hot-rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for Aviation Load-Bearing Parts GJB 3167 Specification for Cold-Drawn Wire of Superalloys for Cold Heading GJB 3317A Specification for Hot-Rolled Plates of Aviation Superalloys GJB 3318A Specification for Cold-Rolled Strips of Aviation Superalloys HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys
Melting Process for Alloy GH1140: Electric arc furnace, or EAF + ESR, or non-VIM + ESR, or VIM + ESR.
Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy GH1140: Extracted from GJB 1952A, GJB 2297A, GJB 3165A, GJB 3020A, GJB 3167, GJB 3317A, GJB 3318A, and GJB 5301.
Electrical Resistivity of Alloy GH1140: 20°C, ρ = 1.07 × 10⁻⁶ Ω·m[1]
Density of Alloy GH1140: ρ = 8.09 g/cm³[3]

Corrosion Resistance of Alloy GH1140: Domestic aviation kerosene, with or without CS₂ additive, has no corrosive effect on GH1140 alloy, while Ni-based alloys require additives to prevent corrosion. Severe pitting corrosion has sometimes been observed with foreign aviation kerosene.[3,16]
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy GH1140: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH2328: GH2328 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based precipitation-hardening wrought superalloy with a service temperature below 500°C. The alloy contains approximately 30 wt.% Cr, 30 wt.% Ni, and 5 wt.% (Al+Ti). In the heat-treated condition, precipitation strengthening by γ' phase and α-Cr phase ensures high hardness and excellent corrosion resistance, meeting the special needs of parts working under friction in certain corrosive media under all-weather conditions. Main product is hot-rolled bars.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy GH2328: The alloy has been used to manufacture combustion chamber nozzles and high-precision parts of fuel control systems for gas turbines operating at temperatures not exceeding 500°C. The alloy has low hardness in the solution-treated condition, facilitating component forming, and only requires grinding allowance on one side. Processed components can be directly age-hardened. If vacuum aged, hardness is even higher, with minimal component distortion and almost no oxide scale, requiring only slight grinding before use.
Material Designation of Alloy GH2328: GH2328 (GH328).
Similar Grades to Alloy GH2328: ЭП655-ВИ (Russia).
Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH2328: Q/KJ.J01.19 Technical Condition for GH2328 Alloy Hot-Rolled Bars
Melting Process for Alloy GH2328: Vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable double-melt process.

Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy GH2328: Extracted from Q/KJ.J01.19. Two standard heat treatment regimes exist: a) Regime I: 1160°C ± 10°C × 40min ± 5min / WQ + 750°C ± 10°C × (8 ~ 12)h / AC. b) Regime II: 1160°C ± 10°C × 40min ± 5min / WQ + 650°C ± 10°C × (2 ~ 5)h / AC. If hardness is low, supplementary aging is permitted: 650°C × (6 ~ 8)h / AC.
Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy GH2328: Extracted from Q/KJ.J01.19.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy GH2328: 1251°C ~ 1346°C[1].
Density of Alloy GH2328: ρ = 7.557 g/cm³[1].
Compressive Properties of Alloy GH2328: Hot-rolled bar treated per Regime I, room temperature compressive strength σbc = 2030 MPa[1].
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy GH2328: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for the product form.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy GH2328: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH3652: GH3652 is a Ni-Cr based precipitation-hardening wrought superalloy used in the solution-treated condition, with a service temperature below 1100°C. The alloy offers excellent oxidation and corrosion resistance, moderate tensile and creep rupture strength, good formability and machinability, and microstructural stability. Suitable for manufacturing combustion chambers and heat shields for aero-engines operating below 1100°C. In civilian industries, it can be used for high-temperature equipment parts operating below 1200°C. Main products include hot-rolled and forged bars, hot-rolled and cold-rolled sheets, strips, and forgings.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy GH3652: The alloy has been used to manufacture combustion chamber and afterburner components for aero-engines (e.g., nozzle shrouds, boxes, diffusers, heat shields), with good batch production and service records. The alloy has a tendency to crack under thermal cycling conditions above 800°C.
Material Designation of Alloy GH3652: GH3652 (GH652).
Similar Grades to Alloy GH3652: ХН70Ю, ЭН652 (Russia).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH3652: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys QJ/DT 01.73023 Hot-Rolled and Cold-Rolled Sheets of GH652 Alloy for Aviation GY 05-014 Technical Condition for GH652 Alloy Bars Q/GYB 05005 GH3652 Alloy Hot-rolled and Forged Bars
Melting Process for Alloy GH3652: Non-vacuum induction melting, or non-VIM + ESR, or VIM + ESR, or non-VIM + VAR, etc.

Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy GH3652: From HB/Z 140, QJ/DT 01.73023, and literature [4]. The alloy is used in the solution-treated condition. Standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Hot-rolled and forged bars, forgings: (1150 ~ 1200)°C × (40 ~ 60)min / AC. b) Hot-rolled and cold-rolled plates: (1080 ~ 1200)°C / AC. c) Cold-rolled strips: (1100 ~ 1130)°C / AC.
Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy GH3652: Extracted from QJ/DT 01.73023, GY 05-014, and Q/GYB 05005.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy GH3652: 1355°C ~ 1380°C[2].
Electrical Resistivity of Alloy GH3652: 20°C, ρ = 1.36 × 10⁻⁶ Ω·m[1].
Density of Alloy GH3652: ρ = 7.97 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy GH3652: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming Process and Properties of Alloy GH3652: Ingot forging heating temp.: 1140°C ~ 1160°C, start forging >1050°C, finish forging >900°C. After ingot removal from furnace, light, quick hammer blows are used initially; after some deformation and breakdown of cast structure, heavy hammer blows follow. For forging or die forging, deformation per heat is about 50%. For precision die forgings, heating in non-oxidizing medium is recommended.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy GH3652: The alloy has good machinability; turning, drilling, milling, and grinding can be performed.

Overview of Alloy K4648: K4648 is a Ni-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-cast superalloy containing 32% to 35% chromium by weight, with a service temperature below 1000°C. The alloy has good overall mechanical properties and thermal fatigue resistance, excellent oxidation and hot corrosion resistance. Suitable for components in aviation and aerospace engines requiring high temperature resistance, moderate stress, high oxidation and hot corrosion resistance. Main products are precision castings like guide vanes and integral guide assemblies.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy K4648: The alloy has been used to manufacture components such as exhaust nozzles and mounting flanges for aero-engines. Given its high chromium content, the balance between other alloying elements must be strictly controlled. The α-Cr phase is the most voluminous phase in the alloy (~10%) and plays an important role in its service properties.
Material Designation of Alloy K4648: K4648 (K648).
Similar Grades to Alloy K4648: BX4Л (Russia).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy K4648: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Q/GYB 05046 K4648 Casting Master Alloy Q/GYB 05050 K4648 Alloy Castings

Melting and Casting Process for Alloy K4648: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace; components and test bars are cast by vacuum induction remelting and investment casting. Refining temperature: 1500°C ~ 1530°C, pouring temperature: 1440°C ~ 1460°C[1].
Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy K4648: Extracted from Q/GYB 05046 and Q/GYB 05050. The standard heat treatment regime is: 1180°C ± 15°C × 4h / AC + 900°C ± 10°C × 16h / AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy K4648: Extracted from Q/GYB 05046 and Q/GYB 05050. Master alloy is cast into round bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy K4648: 1308°C ~ 1336°C[1].
Density of Alloy K4648: ρ = 8.266 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K4648: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Casting Process and Properties of Alloy K4648: The alloy has good castability. Large thin-wall castings can be produced.
Weldability of Alloy K4648: The alloy has good weldability; castings can be repair welded.

Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K4648: Heat treatment is performed under protective atmosphere (vacuum or argon): castings requiring welding are supplied after solution treatment; non-welded castings are supplied after solution + aging treatment. Cooling rate for both: equivalent to or faster than air cooling.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy K4648: Machinability is better than that of general cast superalloys.

Overview of Alloy K435: K435 is a Ni-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-cast superalloy with a service temperature below 900°C. The alloy is strengthened by approximately 18.5 wt.% (Co+W+Mo), 7.5 wt.% (Al+Ti+Nb), 16 wt.% Cr, and multiple trace elements, providing sufficient solid solution, precipitation, and grain boundary strengthening. This alloy has relatively high high-temperature strength and good hot corrosion resistance. Main product is precision cast blades.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy K435: The alloy has been used to manufacture high-temperature components such as turbine guide vanes for power generation or marine gas turbines operating below 900°C. Its hot corrosion resistance is superior to K438 and IN738 alloys. Under identical hot corrosion test conditions, the average corrosion depth for K435 alloy was only 11.4 μm; the average corrosion rate in a 90% Na₂SO₄ + 10% NaCl mixed salt at 900°C × 200h was only 0.28 mg/(cm²·h).
Material Designation of Alloy K435: K435 (K35)
Similar Grades to Alloy K435: ЧC70-BH (Russia)
Material Technical Standards for Alloy K435: Q/KJ.J02.27 K435 Cast Nickel-Based Superalloy Ingot

Melting and Casting Process for Alloy K435: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, remelted in a VIM furnace, and components and test bars are cast by investment casting using the lost-wax process and sand-filled molds. Pouring temperature: 1400°C ~ 1420°C, mold temperature: 780°C ~ 950°C.
Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy K435: Extracted from Q/KJ.J02.27. The standard heat treatment regime is: 1160°C ± 10°C × 4h / AC + 1050°C ± 10°C × 4h / AC + 850°C ± 10°C × 16h / AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy K435: Extracted from Q/KJ.J02.27. Master alloy is cast into round bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy K435: 1280°C ~ 1307°C[3]
Density of Alloy K435: ρ = 8.239 g/cm³[3]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K435: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K435: After standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness is about HBW 377[3].
Impact Properties of Alloy K435: After standard heat treatment, room temperature impact toughness αku ≈ 163 kJ/m²[3].
Casting Process and Properties of Alloy K435: The alloy has good castability and can be investment cast into complex thin-walled hollow blades. Casting linear shrinkage is about 1.9%.
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K435: Same as the alloy's standard heat treatment regime.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy K435: Suitable processes are required for creep-feed grinding of blade root serrations to prevent grinding cracks.

Overview of Alloy DD499: DD499 is a Ni-based precipitation-hardened single-crystal superalloy, belonging to the first generation of single-crystal superalloys. It has a simple composition, relatively low density, high creep strength and fatigue resistance, good single-crystal castability, a wide solution heat treatment temperature range, good microstructural stability, and coating compatibility. The alloy contains no scarce or expensive elements, resulting in lower cost. Suitable for manufacturing gas turbine working blades with complex internal cavities operating below 1050°C and guide vanes operating below 1100°C.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy DD499: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine gas turbine rotor blades and guide vanes. The similar SRR99 alloy has been successfully used in engines like Spey abroad. No TCP phases precipitate during long-term aging at 900°C ~ 1100°C. For single-crystal blades used at 1050°C, pack aluminizing or low-pressure aluminizing should be applied to form aluminide or aluminum-silicon coatings on the blade surface.
Material Designation of Alloy DD499: DD499.
Similar Grades to Alloy DD499: SRR99 (UK).
Material Technical Standards for Alloy DD499: Q/KJ.J02.29 DD499 Cast Superalloy Ingot

Melting and Casting Process for Alloy DD499: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace; single-crystal components or test bars are produced by remelting in a vacuum directional solidification furnace using helical grain selector or seed crystal methods.
Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy DD499: Extracted from Q/KJ.J02.29. The standard heat treatment regime is: 1300°C × 4h / AC + 1100°C × 4h / AC + 870°C × 16h / AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy DD499: Extracted from Q/KJ.J02.29. Master alloy is cast into round bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; single-crystal blades are supplied after solution treatment or standard heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy DD499: 1339°C ~ 1398°C[1].
Density of Alloy DD499: ρ = 8.512 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy DD499: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Casting Process and Properties of Alloy DD499: Using directional solidification, thin-wall components with wall thickness less than 1mm can be cast. Casting shrinkage is about 2%.
Weldability of Alloy DD499: Can be brazed; B-Ni55NbCoWCrAlSiMoTi(C)-S filler metal is recommended.

Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy DD499: Same as the alloy's standard heat treatment regime. Components can also be used in the as-cast condition. The process for aluminizing and stress relief annealing is: 1100°C × 4h / AC.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy DD499: Pack aluminizing or low-pressure aluminizing is used to form aluminide or AlSi coatings on the alloy surface.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy DD499: Turning, drilling, milling, planing, and grinding can be performed at relatively low speeds.

Overview of Alloy GH4202: GH4202 is a Ni-Cr based precipitation-strengthened wrought superalloy with a service temperature range of -253°C to 800°C. The alloy contains about 4 wt.% (Al+Ti) forming γ' aging strengthening phases, about 9 wt.% (W+Mo) for solid solution strengthening, and small amounts of boron and cerium to strengthen and purify grain boundaries. It has good overall mechanical properties and stable microstructure. It possesses relatively high corrosion and oxidation resistance, and excellent resistance to oxygen-rich erosion. The alloy has excellent weldability and is a typical multi-purpose superalloy material. Main products include bars, forgings, plates, tubes, and wires.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy GH4202: The alloy has been used to manufacture dozens of components for new-generation high-thrust liquid rocket engines, such as turbine rotors, rectifying grids, turbine spherical shells, bellows, and gas pipes, with good service performance. The alloy can also be extended to applications in aviation, nuclear energy, petroleum, and chemical industries for heat-resistant load-bearing parts and welded components requiring high corrosion and oxidation resistance. The alloy must be welded in the solution-treated condition, and post-weld heat treatment is required to relieve residual stress. No harmful phases precipitate after long-term high-temperature aging.
Material Designation of Alloy GH4202: GH4202 (GH202).
Similar Grades to Alloy GH4202: эΠ202 (Russia).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH4202: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Liao Xin 7-0029 Technical Condition for GH202 Alloy Hot-rolled and Forged Bars Liao Xin 7-0030 Technical Condition for GH202 Alloy Cold-Rolled Sheets Liao Xin 7-0031 Technical Condition for GH202 Alloy Hot-Rolled Plates Q/GYB 05032 Bars of GH420 Alloy for Fasteners Q/GYB 05018 GH4202 Alloy Cold-Drawn (Rolled) Seamless Tubes Q/GYB 05020 GH4202 Alloy Forgings
Melting Process for Alloy GH4202: Vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting.

Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy GH4202: Extracted from Liao Xin 7-0029, Liao Xin 7-0030, Liao Xin 7-0031, Q/GYB 05032, Q/GYB 05018, and Q/GYB 05020. Standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Cold-rolled sheets: (1060 ~ 1120)°C / AC + 850°C × 5h / AC, solution time depends on sheet thickness. b) Hot-rolled plates: (1090 ~ 1150)°C / AC + 850°C × 5h / AC, solution time depends on plate thickness. c) Bars, forgings: (1060 ~ 1150)°C × 5h / AC + (800 ~ 865)°C × 5h / AC. d) Seamless tubes: 1100°C × (5 ~ 10)min / rapid cool + 850°C × 5h / AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy GH4202: Extracted from Liao Xin 7-0029, Liao Xin 7-0030, Liao Xin 7-0031, Q/GYB 05032, Q/GYB 05018, and Q/GYB 05020. Cold-rolled sheets: thickness 0.8mm ~ 4.0mm; Hot-rolled plates: thickness 4mm ~ 25mm; Hot-rolled and forged bars: diameter 20mm ~ 300mm; Cold-drawn bars: diameter 5mm ~ 30mm; Seamless tubes: OD 19mm ~ 85mm, wall thickness 1.5mm ~ 3mm; Various forgings, rings, and shells. Hot-rolled and cold-rolled sheets are supplied solution treated + pickled + leveled + edge trimmed; Hot-rolled and forged bars are generally supplied non-heat-treated; Seamless tubes are supplied solution treated + pickled + straightened; Forgings are supplied solution treated, or rough machined. Bar for fasteners: Grade A: hot-rolled + annealed + ground or turned; Grade B: cold-drawn, ground if necessary; Grade C: cold-drawn + solution treated + ground; Grade D: cold-drawn + solution treated + aged + ground or turned.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy GH4202: 1305°C ~ 1360°C[1].
Density of Alloy GH4202: ρ = 8.36 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy GH4202: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Corrosion Resistance of Alloy GH4202: The alloy has excellent resistance to hot corrosion in combustion gas at 1000°C.
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy GH4202: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product form.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy GH4202: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy DZ404: DZ404 is a Ni-based precipitation-hardened directionally solidified columnar grain superalloy, a high-strength oxidation-resistant alloy with a service temperature below 1050°C. It contains no hafnium or other scarce/expensive elements, resulting in low cost and relatively low density. The alloy has good overall properties and castability. Its microstructure contains no (γ+γ') eutectic phase, and has a high incipient melting point. Main products are gas turbine engine turbine working blades, guide vanes, and other high-temperature components.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy DZ404: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine turbine working blades and guide vanes. It is suitable for gas turbine rotor blades operating below 1000°C and guide vanes operating below 1050°C. The alloy has excellent castability and can be directionally solidified into net-shape columnar grain blades and thin-wall hollow components with complex internal cavities as thin as 0.5mm, without cracking.
Material Designation of Alloy DZ404: DZ404 (DZ4).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy DZ404: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials HB 7569 DZ4 Alloy Ingots HB 7728 Specification for Precision Cast Turbine Blades of Aviation Superalloys HB 7762 Specification for Directionally Solidified Columnar Grain and Single Crystal Superalloy Ingots for Aero-Engines HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys Q/3B 475 DZ4 Alloy Directionally Solidified Investment Cast Turbine Blades
Melting Process for Alloy DZ404: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace; directionally solidified castings and test bars are poured in a vacuum directional solidification furnace.
Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy DZ404: Extracted from HB/Z 140. The standard heat treatment regime is: 1220°C ± 10°C × 4h / AC + 870°C ± 10°C × 32h / AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy DZ404: Extracted from HB 7569, HB 7762, and Q/3B 475. Master alloy is cast into round bars with diameters ranging from 70mm to 90mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; blade castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy DZ404: 1310°C ~ 1365°C (Differential thermal analysis)[1].
Density of Alloy DZ404: ρ = (8.10 ~ 8.15) g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy DZ404: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy DZ404: After standard heat treatment, average room temperature hardness: HBS 356[1].
Casting Process and Properties of Alloy DZ404: Excellent castability; thin-wall hollow components can be formed without cracking.
Weldability of Alloy DZ404: Can be vacuum brazed.
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy DZ404: Heat treatment for net-shape blades must be performed under vacuum or protective atmosphere: 1220°C ± 10°C × 4h / AC + 870°C ± 10°C × 32h / AC.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy DZ404: Can be aluminized by slurry or vapor phase processes, or coated by vapor physical deposition or other methods.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy DZ404: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy K4002: K4002 is a Ni-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-cast superalloy with a service temperature below 1000°C. Its intermediate and high-temperature property levels are among the highest for existing equiaxed-cast Ni-based superalloys. The alloy has stable microstructure and relatively good oxidation and hot corrosion resistance. The properties of samples taken from components are close to those of separately cast test bars, with low data scatter. It has good casting processability, but due to relatively high tungsten, tantalum, and hafnium content, its density is higher compared to other equiaxed-cast Ni-based superalloys. Suitable for turbine rotor blades and integral turbine discs operating below 1000°C.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy K4002: The alloy has been used to manufacture high-pressure turbine working blades for aero-engines. The similar MAR-M002 alloy has been used for turbine blades and integral turbines in various UK and US engines.[2] Component surfaces are typically treated by pack aluminizing to further improve hot corrosion resistance. No TCP phases precipitate after long-term high-temperature aging.
Material Designation of Alloy K4002: K4002 (K002).
Similar Grades to Alloy K4002: MAR-M002 (USA).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy K4002: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed-Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero-Engines HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys
Melting and Casting Process for Alloy K4002: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace; components and test specimens are cast by vacuum induction remelting and investment casting.
Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy K4002: Extracted from HB/Z 140. The standard heat treatment regime is: 870°C ± 10°C × 16h / AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy K4002: Extracted from HB7763. Master alloy is cast into round bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy K4002: 1280°C ~ 1380°C[1].
Density of Alloy K4002: ρ = 8.5 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K4002: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K4002: After standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness HBS 370 ~ 360[1].
Casting Process and Properties of Alloy K4002: The alloy has good castability and can be cast into various complex shapes such as hollow turbine rotor blades, guide vanes, and integral turbine discs.
Weldability of Alloy K4002: The alloy can be brazed at 1190°C.
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K4002: Non-aluminized components are heat treated per standard specification; aluminizing can be combined with diffusion treatment.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K4002: Pack aluminizing is used for surface coating.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy K4002: No special requirements.

Typical Microstructure of Alloy K4002: As-cast microstructure consists of γ solid solution, γ' phase, (γ+γ') eutectic, Ta-rich MC (I) and Hf-rich MC (II) carbides, M₃B₂ borides, minor Ni₅Hf phase, and sulfides. After standard heat treatment or long-term high-temperature service, minor M₂₃C₆ and M₆C carbides precipitate; Ni₅Hf dissolves and fine HfC carbides precipitate. No TCP phases precipitate during long-term high-temperature aging.

Overview of Alloy DD408: DD408 is a Ni-based precipitation-hardened single-crystal superalloy with a service temperature below 1000°C. It has relatively high creep strength and fatigue resistance, good single-crystal castability, and good microstructural stability. The alloy can work for extended periods in oxidizing and hot corrosive environments; its oxidation and hot corrosion resistance are superior to K438 alloy. Its temperature capability is about 40°C higher than K438 and about 20°C higher than DZ438G. Suitable for manufacturing turbine blades for coastal aircraft engines and marine gas turbines operating below 1000°C, and other high-temperature components.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy DD408: The alloy has been used to manufacture high-pressure turbine working blades for an advanced aero-engine and has passed component testing. The alloy contains relatively few precious metal elements, has low density, and is relatively inexpensive. Component surfaces are treated with pack aluminizing or vapor phase aluminizing to form protective coatings, further enhancing hot corrosion resistance. No TCP phases precipitate after long-term aging.
Material Designation of Alloy DD408: DD408 (DD8).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy DD408: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GJB 5512.3 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 3: Master Alloys for Single Crystal Castings of Superalloys Q/KJ.J02.13 Provisional Technical Standard for DD8 Single Crystal Superalloy

Melting and Casting Process for Alloy DD408: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace; single-crystal components or test bars are produced by remelting in a vacuum directional solidification furnace using helical grain selector or seed crystal methods.

Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy DD408: Extracted from GJB 5512.3 and Q/KJ.J02.13. The standard heat treatment regime is: 1100°C ± 10°C × 8h / AC + 1240°C ± 10°C × 4h / AC + 1090°C ± 10°C × 2h / AC + 850°C × 24h / AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy DD408: Extracted from GJB5512.3 and Q/KJ.J02.13. Master alloy is cast into cylindrical bars with diameters ranging from 70mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; single-crystal blades are supplied after solution treatment or standard heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy DD408: 1295°C ~ 1360°C[1].
Density of Alloy DD408: ρ = 8.26 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy DD408: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Casting Process and Properties of Alloy DD408: Turbine blades or specimens are pulled in a directional solidification furnace using grain selector method, with minimal micro-porosity and good surface quality.

Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy DD408: Same as the alloy's standard heat treatment regime. Components must not be struck, hammered, or collided before, during, or after heat treatment. Machining and sand blasting are not allowed before or during heat treatment.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy DD408: Surface protective coatings are prepared by pack aluminizing or vapor phase aluminizing. Process for both: aluminizing at 950°C for (1 ~ 3)h.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy DD408: The alloy has good machinability and grindability. Feed rates should be appropriately controlled during machining or grinding of components or blades.

Typical Microstructure of Alloy DD408: As-cast microstructure consists of γ matrix, γ' phase, and (γ+γ') eutectic. After standard heat treatment, primary γ' phase and (γ+γ') eutectic are mostly dissolved. Secondary γ' phase is cuboidal, regularly arranged, coherent with γ, and aligned. The alloy contains no carbides or TCP phases. After creep or stress rupture testing (stress axis parallel to [001]), γ' phase undergoes directional coarsening (rafting), with coarsening direction perpendicular to the stress axis.

Overview of Alloy K417G: K417G is a Ni-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-cast superalloy, a modified version of K417 alloy, with a service temperature below 950°C. Compared to K417, this alloy has 5% lower Co and 0.3% lower Ti content, while maintaining comparable properties, as well as advantages like low density, good ductility, and high intermediate temperature strength. Its microstructural stability is significantly improved. The alloy has good castability and can be cast into complex hollow blades. Main products include gas turbine blades, guide vanes, and other high-temperature components.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy K417G: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine stage I, II turbine blades and stage I, II triple-hollow guide vanes, now in batch production with good service performance. The alloy is relatively inexpensive. No σ phase precipitates after long-term aging at 850°C, making components safer and more reliable during long-term service, and also reducing segregation tendency, facilitating component production.
Material Designation of Alloy K417G: K417G (K17G, M17G)
Similar Grades to Alloy K417G: René 100, IN731 (USA)

Material Technical Standards for Alloy K417G: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 1: Master Alloys for Ordinary Precision Castings of Superalloys HB 7728 Specification for Precision Cast Turbine Blades of Aviation Superalloys HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed-Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero-Engines HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys

Melting and Casting Process for Alloy K417G: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace; components and test bars are cast by vacuum induction remelting and investment casting. Pouring temperature: 1400°C~1420°C, mold temperature: 780°C~950°C.
Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy K417G: Extracted from HB/Z 140. The alloy is used in the as-cast condition.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy K417G: Extracted from GJB 5512.1, HB 7728, and HB7763. Master alloy is cast into round bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Both master alloy and precision castings are supplied in the as-cast condition.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy K417G: 1281°C ~ 1327°C[1,2]
Density of Alloy K417G: ρ = 7.85 g/cm³[1,2]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K417G: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K417G: As-cast alloy, room temperature hardness is HRC 34 ~ 36[1,2].
Casting Process and Properties of Alloy K417G: The alloy has good castability and can be investment cast into complex thin-walled hollow blades. Casting shrinkage is about 2%.
Weldability of Alloy K417G: Local CoCrW build-up welding can be done by argon arc or micro-plasma welding, and local repair welding by argon arc. It can also be joined by brazing.
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K417G: Components are used in the as-cast condition.

Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K417G: Slurry aluminizing (pack cementation): FeAl alloy or pure Al powder as diffusant, cellulose acetate as binder, heat treatment at 950°C for 6h~8h under argon. Low-pressure vapor phase AlSi coating: FeAl alloy powder + Si powder, NH₄Cl activator, 950°C for 2h~5h.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy K417G: No special requirements.
Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy K417G: Complete dissolution temperature of γ' is about 1250°C.

Overview of Alloy K423: K423 is a Ni-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-cast superalloy. The alloy contains solid solution strengthening elements like chromium, cobalt, and molybdenum, γ' forming elements aluminum and titanium, and grain boundary purifying/strengthening elements boron and hafnium. It has good oxidation resistance and thermal fatigue resistance below 1000°C. The alloy has relatively good castability and is suitable for manufacturing hollow and solid gas turbine guide vanes and integral guide assemblies.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy K423: The alloy has been used to manufacture stage II, III guide vanes for aero turbofan engines, with good batch production and service records. The similar C1023 alloy abroad is used for guide vanes in RB211, TF41, Spey MK511 engines. Surface protection is generally not required. For use above 900°C, CrAl co-diffusion treatment can be applied to improve corrosion resistance. The alloy has poor microstructural stability; significant amounts of plate-like σ phase precipitate during long-term service at 750°C~900°C.
Material Designation of Alloy K423: K423
Similar Grades to Alloy K423: C1023 (UK)

Material Technical Standards for Alloy K423: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 1: Master Alloys for Ordinary Precision Castings of Superalloys HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed-Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero-Engines HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys

Melting and Casting Process for Alloy K423: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace; components and test bars are cast by vacuum induction remelting. Investment ceramic molds are used, sand-filled for pouring. Refining temp.: 1550°C~1600°C, pouring temp.: 1420°C~1480°C, mold preheat: 900°C.
Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy K423: Extracted from HB/Z 140. The alloy is used in the as-cast condition.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy K423: Extracted from GJB 5512.1 and HB 7763. Master alloy is cast into round bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Both master alloy and precision castings are supplied in the as-cast condition.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy K423: 1240°C ~ 1325°C[2]
Density of Alloy K423: ρ = 8.00 g/cm³[1]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K423: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K423: As-cast alloy, room temperature hardness is HRC 37 ~ 41[1,2].

Casting Process and Properties of Alloy K423: The alloy has good castability and can be cast into hollow blades with complex internal cavities and wall thickness less than 1mm. Linear shrinkage is 2.2%.
Weldability of Alloy K423: Can be argon arc welded and electron beam welded. No cracks were found in the HAZ during build-up welding tests.
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K423: Under argon protection, heat component to 1190°C, hold 15min, then furnace cool to 1000°C over 45min, air cool.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K423: Generally not required. For use above 900°C, CrAl co-diffusion can improve corrosion resistance.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy K423: Use carbide tools.
Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy K423: σ phase dissolves back above 950°C.

Overview of Alloys GH4133 and GH4133B: GH4133 and GH4133B are Ni-Cr based precipitation-hardening wrought superalloys; GH4133B is a modified version of GH4133 with additions of magnesium and zirconium. Both have a service temperature below 750°C, good overall mechanical properties with high yield strength, good oxidation resistance, stable and fine-grained microstructure, and good hot workability. Particularly, GH4133B improves the notch sensitivity present in GH4133 below 750°C, significantly increasing service life, and substantially improving creep rupture strength and ductility. Both are suitable for manufacturing important load-bearing parts like turbine discs and working blades for aero-engines operating below 750°C. Main products include hot-rolled and forged bars, disc forgings, and ring components.

Application and Characteristics of Alloys GH4133 and GH4133B: GH4133 and GH4133B have been used to manufacture turbine discs and load-bearing rings for various aero-engine models, with good batch production and service records. GH4133 is also used for high-temperature bolts and tie rods in aircraft engines and industrial steam turbines. After long-term aging above 700°C, η phase precipitates at grain boundaries, increasing significantly after 3000h and forming large cellular colonies. Creep ductility is relatively low above 700°C, and the alloys exhibit notch sensitivity.
Material Designation of Alloys GH4133 and GH4133B: GH4133 (GH33A) and GH4133B.
