Material Technical Standards for Alloys GH4133 and GH4133B: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GB/T 14997 Forged Round Disc Blanks of Superalloys GJB 1953A Specification for Hot-Rolled Bars of Superalloys for Aero-Engine Rotating Parts GJB 3165A Specification for Hot-rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for Aviation Load-Bearing Parts GJB 3782A Specification for Forged Round Disc Blanks of Aviation Superalloys GJB 5280 Specification for Disc Forgings of Superalloys for Aero-Engines GJB 5301 Specification for Ring Components of Aviation Superalloys HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys YB 4093 Standard for Longitudinal Macrostructure of GH4133B Alloy Disc Forgings
Melting Process for Alloys GH4133 and GH4133B: GH4133: Electric arc furnace + ESR, or EAF + VAR, or non-VIM + ESR, or VIM + ESR, or VIM + VAR. GH4133B: VIM + ESR, or VIM + VAR.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloys GH4133 and GH4133B: Extracted from GB/T 14997, GJB 1953A, GJB 3165A, GJB 3782A, GJB 5280, and GJB 5301. Hot-rolled and forged bars: d15mm ~ 300mm; Various disc forgings and ring components. Bars are generally supplied non-heat-treated, ground or turned; Disc forgings are supplied in forged condition + descaled, or heat treated + rough machined; Ring components are generally supplied non-heat-treated, or can be supplied solution treated, or solution treated + aged.
Density of Alloys GH4133 and GH4133B: ρ = 8.21 g/cm³[2].
Magnetic Properties of Alloys GH4133 and GH4133B: The alloys are non-magnetic.
Elastic Properties of Alloys GH4133 and GH4133B: GH4133B, room temperature compressive modulus EC = 217 GPa[6].

Corrosion Resistance of Alloys GH4133 and GH4133B: Fully immersed in static seawater at 40°C, annual corrosion rate: 0.007207 mm/year. Seawater composition: 35g/L NaCl, 11g/L MgCl₂·6H₂O, 3.5g/L Na₂SO₄, 2.2g/L CaCl₂, 0.2g/L KBr.
Hardness of Alloys GH4133 and GH4133B: After standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness: HBS 363 ~ 262[1].
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloys GH4133 and GH4133B: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product form.

Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloys GH4133 and GH4133B: γ' phase begins to precipitate at 600°C, peaks at 800°C, begins to dissolve at 900°C, completely dissolves at 1010°C. M₂₃C₆ carbide begins to precipitate at 600°C, peaks at 850°C, begins to dissolve at 950°C, completely dissolves at 1010°C. η phase normally precipitates in the range 900°C ~ 1020°C, peaks at 980°C, completely dissolves at 1040°C. However, with severe η phase segregation, η phase may dissolve completely only at 1100°C.

Overview of Alloy K409: K409 is a Ni-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-cast superalloy characterized by containing 4.2 wt.% tantalum. Service temperature is below 950°C. It has relatively high high-temperature strength and good oxidation and corrosion resistance. Complex-shaped components can be cast using investment casting. Suitable for blades and other high-temperature parts for aero-engines and industrial gas turbines for long-term service below 950°C.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy K409: The alloy has been used to manufacture stage I turbine rotor blades for aero turbo-engines, passing flight tests and life bench tests with good performance. Due to tantalum content, cost is slightly higher than similar alloys; low chromium content reduces electron vacancy number to 2.14, improving structural stability—no σ phase precipitates after nearly ten thousand hours aging at 850°C~950°C.[9]
Material Designation of Alloy K409: K409 (K9)
Similar Grades to Alloy K409: B1900 (USA)
Material Technical Standards for Alloy K409: GB/T14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials HB 5330 K9 Alloy Ingots for Aviation
Melting and Casting Process for Alloy K409: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace; components and test bars are cast by vacuum induction remelting and investment casting.
Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy K409: Extracted from HB5330. The alloy can be used as-cast or after heat treatment. Standard heat treatment regime: 1080°C × 4h / AC + 900°C × 10h / AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy K409: Extracted from HB 5330. Master alloy is cast into round bars with diameters ranging from 65mm to 85mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied as-cast; precision cast blades are supplied as-cast or heat-treated.
Melting Temperature Range for Alloy K409: 1260°C ~ 1289°C[1]
Density of Alloy K409: ρ = 8.18 g/cm³[1]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K409: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K409: Standard heat-treated condition, room temperature hardness: HRC 34 ~ 44[1].

Casting Process and Properties of Alloy K409: Good castability, low porosity tendency. Controlling pouring temperature at 1420°C and mold temperature at 750°C~850°C yields castings with uniform surface grains.[9]
Weldability of Alloy K409: Wear-resistant surfaces on blade tip shrouds can be built up using CoCrW argon arc welding.
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K409: Components are generally used as-cast; standard heat treatment is required if diffusion coatings are applied.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K409: Blade surfaces are aluminized-silicon coated via slurry spraying. Diffusion treatment process: 1080°C × 4h / AC + 900°C × 10h / AC[9].
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy K409: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH4033: GH4033 is a Ni-Cr based precipitation-hardening wrought superalloy strengthened by aluminum and titanium forming precipitation phases, with service temperature below 700°C. It has sufficient high-temperature strength at 700°C~750°C and good oxidation resistance below 900°C. The alloy has good cold and hot workability. Suitable for turbine blades operating below 750°C and turbine discs operating below 700°C. Main products: hot-rolled and forged bars, cold-drawn bars, cold-rolled sheets, cold-drawn wires, forged disc blanks, turbine blades, and disc (ring) components.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy GH4033: This alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine turbine blades, discs, and other high-temperature load-bearing components. The alloy has a strong tendency to form non-uniform and coarse grains during hot rolling and forging, leading to scrap. Additionally, process control must be strict to avoid the drop in tensile ductility at 700°C.
Material Designation of Alloy GH4033: GH4033 (GH33).
Similar Grades to Alloy GH4033: ЭП437Б, ХН77ТЮР (Russia).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH4033: GB/T14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GB/T14993 Hot-Rolled Bars of Superalloys for Rotating Parts GB/T 14994 Cold-Drawn Bars of Superalloys GB/T14997 Forged Round Disc Blanks of Superalloys GB/T14998 Ring Blanks of Superalloys GJB1953A Specification for Hot-Rolled Bars of Superalloys for Aero-Engine Rotating Parts GJB 2611A Specification for Cold-Drawn Bars of Aviation Superalloys GJB 2612A Specification for Cold-Drawn Welding Wire of Superalloys GJB 3020A Specification for Ring Blanks of Aviation Superalloys GJB3165A Specification for Hot-rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for Aviation Load-Bearing Parts GJB 3782A Specification for Forged Round Disc Blanks of Aviation Superalloys GJB 5301 Specification for Ring Components of Aviation Superalloys HB 5198 Bars of Wrought Superalloys for Aviation Blades HB/Z140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys YB/T 5245 Hot-rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for General Load-Bearing Parts QJ/DT01.63000 Technical Condition for GH4033 Alloy Cold-Rolled Sheets
Melting Process for Alloy GH4033: Electric arc furnace, or EAF + ESR, or EAF + VAR, or EAF + vacuum consumable, or non-VIM + ESR, or VIM + ESR, or VIM + VIM, or VIM + VAR.

Heat Treatment Regime for Alloy GH4033: Extracted from HB/Z140, GJB5301, and QJ/DT01.63000. Standard heat treatment regimes for various products: a) Hot-rolled bars for aviation rotating parts: 1080°C ± 10°C × 8h / AC + 700°C ± 10°C × 16h / AC, HB321 ~ 255. b) Cold-drawn bars: 1080°C ± 10°C × 8h / AC + 700°C ± 10°C × 16h / AC. c) Ring blanks and forged disc blanks: 1080°C × 8h / AC + 750°C × 16h / AC, HB321 ~ 255. d) Hot-rolled and forged bars: Regime I: 1080°C ± 10°C × 8h / AC + 750°C ± 10°C × 16h / AC, HB321 ~ 255; Regime II: 1080°C ± 10°C × 8h / AC + 700°C ± 10°C × 16h / AC, HB311 ~ 255. e) Forgings: Regime I: (1070~1090)°C × (8~8.5)h / AC + (690~710)°C × (16~16.5)h / AC, HB311 ~ 255; Regime II: (1070~1090)°C × (8~8.5)h / AC + (740~760)°C × (16~16.5)h / AC, HB321 ~ 255. f) Cold-rolled sheets: as-delivered condition + 750°C ± 10°C × 4h / AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition for Alloy GH4033: Extracted from GB/T14993, GB/T14994, GB/T14997, GB/T14998, GJB1953A, GJB2611A, GJB3020A, GJB3165A, GJB3782A, GJB5301, HB5198, YB/T5245, and QJ/DT01.63000. Hot-rolled and forged bars: d20mm ~ 300mm. Cold-drawn bars: round d8mm~45mm, square a8mm~30mm, hexagonal (inscribed circle) d8mm~36mm. Forged disc blanks: d≤600mm, height 60mm~150mm. Ring blanks: OD 200mm~800mm, ID 50mm~600mm, thickness 60mm~250mm. Welding wire: d0.5mm~4mm; Cold-rolled sheets: thickness 0.2mm~10mm. Hot-rolled bars for rotating parts: supplied in as-rolled condition, fully ground or turned. Cold-drawn bars: supplied solution treated + pickled, or cold-drawn. Hot-rolled and forged bars for general load-bearing parts: generally supplied non-heat-treated, may be ground or turned. Cold-rolled sheets: supplied solution treated + pickled + leveled + edge trimmed. Ring blanks: supplied non-heat-treated, may be turned. Disc blanks: supplied forged + surface ground. Welding wire: supplied in hard, semi-hard, solution treated + pickled, or bright solution treated condition in coils or straight lengths.
Electrical Resistivity of Alloy GH4033: 20°C, ρ = 1.24 × 10⁻⁶ Ω·m.[12]
Density of Alloy GH4033: ρ = 8.20 g/cm³[12]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy GH4033: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy GH4033: Statistical average of room temperature hardness after standard heat treatment for 149 heats: HBS 285.[4]
Weldability of Alloy GH4033: The alloy can be argon arc welded in the solution-treated condition; post-weld treatment is required to relieve stress.
Component Heat Treatment Process for Alloy GH4033: Stress relief annealing for finished components: in argon, 850°C × 2h → cool to 600°C / AC + 700°C × 8h / AC in air.[8]
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy GH4033: Mechanically processed components require electrolytic polishing; final polishing marks should align with blade length direction.
Machinability and Grindability of Alloy GH4033: No special requirements.

Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy GH4033: γ' phase begins to precipitate at 500°C~600°C, peaks at 775°C~800°C, dissolves in matrix at 950°C~975°C. Cr₃C₆ carbide begins to precipitate at 650°C, peaks at 800°C~900°C, dissolves at 1050°C. Cr₇C₃ carbide begins to precipitate at 1020°C, peaks at 1050°C~1080°C, dissolves at 1120°C~1150°C.

Overview of Alloy GH3030: GH3030 is a Ni-Cr based solid-solution strengthened wrought superalloy with a service temperature below 800°C. It has relatively good thermal strength and high ductility, as well as thermal/cold fatigue resistance and oxidation resistance, along with good cold stamping and welding characteristics. After solution treatment, it is single-phase austenitic with stable microstructure during service. Primarily used for combustion chamber components of turbo-engines operating below 800°C, and for high-temperature parts requiring oxidation resistance but carrying low loads below 1100°C. Main products: cold-rolled sheets, bars, rings, wires, tubes.

Application and Characteristics of Alloy GH3030: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine combustion chambers, afterburners, casing flanges, etc. Excellent weldability: argon arc, spot, seam, or brazing welding possible. If solution temperature is too low during production, old grain boundaries formed by residual carbides may remain.
Material Designation of Alloy GH3030: GH3030 (GH30)
Similar Grades to Alloy GH3030: ЭН435, ХН78Т (Russia)

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH3030: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GB/T 14994 Cold-Drawn Bars of Superalloys GB/T 14995 Hot-Rolled Plates of Superalloys GB/T 14996 Cold-Rolled Sheets of Superalloys GB/T 15062 General Purpose Superalloy Tubes GJB 1952A Specification for Cold-Rolled Sheets of Aviation Superalloys GJB 2297A Specification for Cold-Drawn (Rolled) Seamless Tubes of Aviation Superalloys GJB 2611A Specification for Cold-Drawn Bars of Aviation Superalloys GJB 2612 Specification for Cold-Drawn Welding Wire of Superalloys GJB 3020A Specification for Ring Blanks of Aviation Superalloys GJB 3165A Specification for Hot-rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for Aviation Load-Bearing Parts GJB 3167A Specification for Cold-Drawn Wire of Superalloys for Cold Heading GJB 3317A Specification for Hot-Rolled Plates of Aviation Superalloys GJB 3318A Specification for Cold-Rolled Strips of Aviation Superalloys HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Aviation Superalloys YB/T 5245 Hot-rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for General Load-Bearing Parts

Melting process of alloy GH3030: Electric arc furnace, or non-vacuum induction furnace, or electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting, or electric arc furnace + vacuum arc remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + vacuum arc remelting, or vacuum duplex melting process.

Heat treatment system of alloy GH3030: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, the standard heat treatment systems for various product forms are: a) Cold-rolled sheets, (980～1020)℃/AC, where for thickness δ≤3mm, holding time (8～12)min; for thickness δ 3mm～5mm, holding time (12～16)min; b) Wires, (980～1020)℃/WQ or AC, where for diameter d≤3mm, holding time (8～12)min; for diameter d 3mm～5mm, holding time (12～16)min; c) Tubes, (980～1020)℃/WQ; d) Cold-drawn bars, (980～1000)℃/WQ or AC; e) Forgings, (990～1010)℃×(2～3)h/AC; f) Welded parts, (990～1020)℃/AC, where for thickness/diameter δ(d)≤3mm, holding time (8～12)min; for thickness/diameter δ(d) 3mm～5mm, holding time (12～16)min.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy GH3030: Bars with diameters from d8mm to 300mm; hot-rolled sheets with thickness from δ4mm to 14mm; cold-rolled sheets with thickness from δ0.5mm to 4mm; cold-rolled strips with thickness from δ0.1mm to 0.8mm; cold-drawn (rolled) seamless tubes with outer diameter ≤50mm; cold-drawn wires with diameters from d0.2mm to 10.0mm; ring-shaped parts and forgings of various dimensions. Hot-rolled and forged bars are generally supplied without heat treatment, polished or turned; cold-drawn bars are supplied in annealed, or annealed + pickled, or annealed + polished, or cold-drawn condition; hot-rolled sheets are supplied after solution treatment + alkali-acid pickling + leveling + edge trimming; cold-rolled sheets are supplied after solution treatment + alkali-acid pickling + leveling + straightening + edge trimming; cold-rolled strips are supplied in coils after solution treatment + alkali-acid pickling + leveling + edge trimming; cold-drawn tubes are supplied after solution treatment + pickling, or in cold-drawn (rolled) condition; welding wires are supplied in cold-drawn condition, or solution treated + pickled, or semi-hardened condition, in coils; wires for cold heading are supplied in cold-drawn condition, or solution treated + pickled, or solution treated + polished, in coils or straight lengths; ring-shaped parts and forgings are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy GH3030: 1374℃～1420℃[3].
Density of alloy GH3030: ρ=8.4g/cm³[1].
Magnetic properties of alloy GH3030: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and properties of alloy GH3030: Forging temperature for ingot breakdown should not exceed 800℃, heating temperature is 1160℃～1200℃, final forging temperature should not be lower than 900℃, deformation per heat is 50%; hot rolling temperature for sheets is 1040℃～1100℃, cold rolling reduction should be greater than 30%.

Weldability of alloy GH3030: 4.3.1、During argon arc welding, the fluidity and formability of the molten pool are good, and cracking tendency is low; during spot welding and seam welding, there is a wide range of process parameters, core shrinkage is small, and generally no cracks or bond line penetration occur. This alloy can be welded in combination with alloys such as 1Cr18Ni9Ti, GH1140, GH3039, GH3044, GH3128, etc.[14].

Parts heat treatment process for alloy GH3030: Intermediate and final heat treatment processes for parts in aviation factories are: 1000℃×(5～10)min/AC or WQ. If high thermal strength is required for the part, the final solution treatment temperature can be increased to 1150℃[15].

Surface treatment process for alloy GH3030: After heat treatment, the oxide scale on the part surface can be removed by sandblasting or pickling. For pickling, a single-step pickling process using HF-H₂SO₄-HNO₃ aqueous solution, or a composite alkali-acid pickling process using NaOH-NaNO₃ and H₂SO₄-NaCl-H₂O solutions can be employed[15].
Machining and grinding performance of alloy GH3030: No special requirements.

Overview of alloy DD432: DD432 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened single-crystal superalloy for use at temperatures up to 1100℃. The alloy possesses good high-temperature creep and rupture strength, good high-temperature oxidation resistance and marine atmosphere corrosion resistance; it has good directional solidification, welding, and heat treatment process performance, low recrystallization tendency, and good microstructural stability. Suitable for manufacturing hot-section components such as high-pressure and low-pressure turbine blades and guide vanes in gas turbine engines.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy DD432: The alloy has been used to manufacture high-pressure and low-pressure turbine blades and guide vanes operating around 1100℃ in gas turbine engines. It is especially recommended for use in high-thrust-to-weight ratio aero engines or advanced carrier-based aero engines. Slurry aluminizing/siliconizing (AlSi) coating or arc ion plating to deposit NiCrAlY coatings can be applied to further improve the high-temperature oxidation and marine atmosphere corrosion resistance of blades. Only a small amount of μ-phase precipitates during long-term aging at 900℃～1100℃.
Material designation of alloy DD432: DD432 (DD32).
Similar designations of alloy DD432: ЖC32 (Russia).
Material technical standards for alloy DD432: Q/KJ.J02.32 DD32 Cast Superalloy Ingot Specification

Melting and casting process of alloy DD432: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, then remelted in a vacuum directional solidification furnace using helical grain selection or seed crystal methods to produce single-crystal parts or test bars.
Heat treatment system of alloy DD432: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.32, the standard heat treatment system for the alloy is: 1290℃×4h/AC + 1280℃×4h/AC + 1150℃×4h/AC + 870℃×24h/AC.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy DD432: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.32. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; single-crystal blades are supplied after solution treatment or after standard heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy DD432: 1362℃～1397℃[1].
Density of alloy DD432: ρ=8.849g/cm³[1]
Magnetic properties of alloy DD432: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming process and properties of alloy DD432: Directional solidification can be used to cast thin-walled parts with thickness less than 1mm. Casting shrinkage is approximately 2%.
Weldability of alloy DD432: Brazing welding is possible. The recommended brazing filler metal is B-Ni55NbCoWCrAlSiMoTi (C)-S.
Surface treatment process of alloy DD432: Slurry aluminizing/siliconizing (AlSi) coating or arc ion plating to deposit NiCrAlY coatings can be applied.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy DD432: Turning, drilling, milling, planing, and grinding can be performed at lower speeds.

Overview of alloy GH6783: GH6783 is a Co-Ni-Fe base precipitation-hardened ferromagnetic oxidation-resistant low-expansion wrought superalloy for use at temperatures less than 750℃. The alloy is strengthened by precipitation of γ'-Ni₃ (Al, Nb) dispersed phase and blocky β-NiAl phase during aging by adding large amounts of aluminum and niobium. The alloy achieves complete oxidation resistance at 750℃ and possesses excellent room temperature and high-temperature mechanical properties, low thermal expansion coefficient, and low density. Fine grain size can be controlled through precipitation of β-NiAl phase during thermal deformation. The micro-alloyed alloy has excellent hot workability and cold forming processability. Main products include bars, discs, rings, cold-rolled sheets, and strips.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy GH6783: The alloy has been used to manufacture seal rings, load-bearing rings, and casings for clearance control components in advanced aero engines. Due to its combination of high-temperature oxidation resistance, excellent mechanical properties, and low thermal expansion coefficient, the alloy can also be extended to applications such as bolts and fasteners and load-bearing shafts in land-based gas turbines operating at medium to high temperatures, as well as guide tubes in rocket engines. Compared to other low-expansion superalloys GH2907 and GH2909, the excellent oxidation resistance and low density are the main characteristics of GH6783 alloy. The alloy has excellent properties and microstructural stability below 700℃, but its thermal stability is poor above 750℃.
Material designation of alloy GH6783: GH6783 (GH783).
Similar designations of alloy GH6783: Inconel 783 (USA).

Material technical standards for alloy GH6783: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; Q/GYB 528 GH6783 Oxidation-Resistant Low-Expansion Superalloy Bars, Forgings and Rings; Q/GYB 05066 GH6783 Oxidation-Resistant Low-Expansion Superalloy Cold-Rolled Sheets and Strips; Q/6S 2040 Low-Expansion GH783 Alloy Bars and Rings Specification; AMS 5940 Multiple-Melted, High-Temperature, Oxidation-Resistant, Low-Expansion 34Co-3.0Cr-28Ni-3.0Nb-5.5Al-0.008B-25.5Fe Alloy Bars, Forgings, Rings
Melting process of alloy GH6783: Non-vacuum induction melting + consumable remelting process is used.

Heat treatment system of alloy GH6783: Excerpted from AMS 5940, Q/GYB 528, Q/GYB 05066 and Q/6S 2040, the standard heat treatment systems for various product forms are: a) Bars, forgings and rings, 1115℃±5℃/AC (or faster cooling) + 845℃±10℃×(2～4)h/AC + 720℃±10℃×(8±0.5)h/FC (56℃/h) → 620℃±10℃×(8±0.5)h/AC, where the solution holding time is determined by the component cross-section; b) Cold-rolled sheets and strips, 1115℃±5℃×(10～30)min/AC (or air cool) + 845℃±10℃×3h/AC + 720℃±10℃×8h/FC (56℃/h) → 620℃±10℃×8h/AC (or air cool).

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy GH6783: Excerpted from AMS 5940, Q/GYB 528, Q/GYB 05066 and Q/6S 2040. Bars from d8.0mm to 250mm; cold-rolled sheets and strips from δ0.5mm to 4.0mm; disc (ring) blanks from d250mm to 500mm; rings from d350mm to 900mm. Bars, disc (ring) blanks are generally supplied without heat treatment; hot-rolled plates, cold-rolled sheets and strips are supplied after solution treatment + pickling + leveling + edge trimming; rings are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Density of alloy GH6783: ρ=7.81g/cm³.
Magnetic properties of alloy GH6783: The alloy is magnetic.

Parts heat treatment process for alloy GH6783: Final heat treatment for parts: Solution: 1115℃±5℃/AC, holding time determined by component cross-section; First aging: (843±8)℃×(2～4)h/AC; Second aging: (718±8)℃×(8±0.5)h/FC (56℃/h) → (621±8)℃×(8±0.5)h/AC.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy GH6783: No special requirements.

Overview of alloy K4208: K4208 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-crystal cast superalloy characterized by excellent wear resistance. The alloy can be used long-term under high-temperature wear and corrosive environments at 800℃, with short-term use temperature up to 1000℃. The alloy has high strength and hardness, good weldability and castability. Suitable for manufacturing wear-resistant inserts for engine blades, welding repair materials for blades, sleeves, and parts for friction components operating at high temperatures.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy K4208: The alloy has been used to manufacture wear-resistant inserts for aero engine blades with good results; similar designation alloys abroad have been used to manufacture parts for friction components operating long-term at high temperatures (≤800℃) in distillate water and liquid metal (Na, Na-K) media in nuclear reactors, as well as components requiring high wear resistance in aviation, marine, nuclear energy, petrochemical and other fields. The alloy can be used in the as-cast or annealed condition. After solution + aging treatment, properties are better. The alloy has poor plasticity and is difficult to work. Using an ingot mold casting solidification process can improve plasticity and increase strength.
Material designation of alloy K4208: K4208.
Similar designations of alloy K4208: BЖЛ2-BИ (Russia).
Material technical standards for alloy K4208: Q/GYB 05101 K4208 Master Alloy for Casting; Q/GYB 05102 K4208 Alloy Wear-Resistant Insert Castings

Melting and casting process of alloy K4208: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, remelted in a vacuum induction furnace to cast investment precision castings and test bars. Refining temperature 1520℃～1550℃, pouring temperature 1400℃～1450℃.
Heat treatment system of alloy K4208: Excerpted from Q/GYB 05101 and Q/GYB 05102. a) Annealing system: 950℃±10℃×3h/AC; b) Standard heat treatment system: 1200℃±10℃×3h/AC + 950℃±10℃×5h/FC→300℃/AC.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy K4208: Excerpted from Q/GYB 05101 and Q/GYB 05102. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; welded investment castings are supplied after solution treatment; non-welded investment castings are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy K4208: 1265℃～1284℃[1].
Density of alloy K4208: ρ=8.70g/cm³[1].
Magnetic properties of alloy K4208: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Hardness of alloy K4208: As-cast alloy, room temperature hardness HBW 460～430; after annealing, room temperature hardness HBW 500～450; after standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness HBW 550～450[1].
Forming process and properties of alloy K4208: The alloy has good castability and can be cast into thin-walled castings and complex-shaped precision castings.

Weldability of alloy K4208: The alloy has good weldability and can be argon arc welded with other superalloys and materials. Especially suitable as a repair material for welding precious blades and other parts.
Parts heat treatment process for alloy K4208: Same as the standard heat treatment system of the alloy.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy K4208: The alloy has high hardness and poor machinability. Wire-cut EDM is recommended.

Overview of alloy K438G: K438G is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-crystal cast superalloy characterized by low phosphorus content and low solidification segregation [1], for use at temperatures below 900℃. This alloy is a modified version of K438 alloy, possessing not only the excellent hot corrosion resistance of K438 alloy but also increased rupture strength by (20～50)MPa and a use temperature increase of 20℃, approaching the level of K417 alloy. The alloy also has good microstructural stability and castability. Main products are precision-cast blades for gas turbines.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy K438G: The alloy has been used to manufacture industrial gas turbine blades for long-term operation below 900℃, and parts for marine aircraft and marine gas turbines. During use, the alloy can be surface aluminized (Al), chromaluminized (AlCr), or aluminosiliconized (AlSi) to improve corrosion resistance. Technologically, powder pack, low-pressure gaseous diffusion, or slurry methods can be used. Magnetron sputtering or low-pressure plasma spraying can also be used to apply multi-component, multi-layer protective coatings. After aging at 850℃ for thousands of hours, no σ phase was found.
Material designation of alloy K438G: K438G (M38G)
Similar designations of alloy K438G: IN738 (USA)

Material technical standards for alloy K438G: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; SGZM 38G-1 M38G Cast Superalloy Master Alloy Technical Specification

Melting and casting process of alloy K438G: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, remelted in a vacuum induction furnace, and cast into parts and test bars using investment precision casting with sand filling. Pouring temperature 1410℃~1550℃, mold temperature 850℃~980℃.
Heat treatment system of alloy K438G: Excerpted from SGZM 38G-1, the standard heat treatment system for the alloy is: 1120℃±10℃×2h/AC + 850℃±10℃×24h/AC.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy K438G: Master alloy is cast into round bars of d70mm～90mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy K438G: 1220℃~1320℃[2] (Metallographic probe method)
Density of alloy K438G: ρ=8.20g/cm³[2]
Magnetic properties of alloy K438G: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of alloy K438G: In standard heat-treated condition, room temperature hardness is HBS 368~350[2].

Forming process and properties of alloy K438G: Good castability, complex thin-walled hollow blades can be cast using investment casting, and integral turbines can also be cast, casting shrinkage is about 2%.
Parts heat treatment process for alloy K438G: Same as the standard heat treatment system of the alloy. During solution treatment cooling, parts should be spread out for air cooling.

Surface treatment process of alloy K438G: The alloy can be aluminized (Al), chromaluminized (AlCr), or aluminosiliconized (AlSi); technologically, powder pack, low-pressure gaseous diffusion, or slurry methods can be used. Magnetron sputtering or low-pressure plasma spraying can also be used to apply multi-component, multi-layer protective coatings.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy K438G: No special requirements.

Overview of alloy K420: K420 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-crystal cast superalloy for use at temperatures up to 1050℃. The alloy composition is characterized by high tungsten content and low chromium content. The alloy has relatively high high-temperature strength in the range of 900℃~1050℃. The alloy has good castability, suitable for manufacturing gas turbine blades and guide vanes, as well as other high-temperature parts.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy K420: This alloy has been used to manufacture mold materials. The alloy is a complex alloyed type with high tungsten and low chromium, containing no expensive metallic elements. The microstructure is stable after aging at 1050℃×(100～500)h, with no harmful phases precipitated. After overheating at 1150℃ and 1180℃ for 2h, γ' phase coarsens, but no re-dissolution occurs[2].
Material designation of alloy K420: K420 (K20)
Melting and casting process of alloy K420: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, remelted in a vacuum induction furnace to cast investment precision cast parts and test bars.
Heat treatment system of alloy K420: The alloy is used in the as-cast condition.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy K420: Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy and precision castings are supplied in the as-cast condition.
Melting temperature range of alloy K420: 1270℃~1340℃[1]
Density of alloy K420: ρ=8.75g/cm³[3]
Magnetic properties of alloy K420: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of alloy K420: As-cast alloy, room temperature hardness is HBW 402[3].
Forming process and properties of alloy K420: The alloy has good castability, casting shrinkage is approximately 1.94%.
Parts heat treatment process for alloy K420: Parts are used in the as-cast condition.
Surface treatment process of alloy K420: Parts can be solid-state or gas-phase aluminized.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy K420: No special requirements.

Overview of alloy K480: K480 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-crystal cast superalloy for long-term use at 950℃. The alloy has medium strength level, good hot corrosion resistance and long-term microstructural stability, and is a turbo blade material with good comprehensive properties. The alloy has good castability and can also be used to manufacture directionally solidified columnar grain castings. The alloy contains no expensive element tantalum, making it lower cost than comparable alloys with similar performance levels. Suitable for manufacturing aero engine and marine gas turbine turbine blades and guide vanes.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy K480: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero engine turbine blades and hot-section components for marine gas turbines, with good batch production and application. Similar designations are widely used abroad for turbine blades and guide vanes. Applying a plasma-sprayed CoCrAlY coating to the part surface can further improve the hot corrosion resistance of the alloy.
Material designation of alloy K480: K480 (K80).
Similar designations of alloy K480: René80 (USA).

Material technical standards for alloy K480: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 1: Master Alloys for Ordinary Precision Castings of Superalloys; HB 7728 Specification for Precision Cast Blades of Superalloys for Aviation; HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed-Crystal Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero Engines; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation

Melting and casting process of alloy K480: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, remelted in vacuum or non-vacuum induction furnace, and cast into parts and test bars using investment precision casting.
Heat treatment system of alloy K480: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, the standard heat treatment system for the alloy is: 1220℃±10℃×2h/AC + 1090℃±10℃×4h/AC + 1050℃±10℃×4h/AC + 840℃±10℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy K480: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1, HB 7728 and HB 7763. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy K480: 1270℃~1320℃[5].
Density of alloy K480: ρ=8.16g/cm³[5].
Magnetic properties of alloy K480: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of alloy K480: After standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness is HRC 40～41[1].
Forming process and properties of alloy K480: The alloy has good castability, linear casting shrinkage is approximately 2%.
Parts heat treatment process for alloy K480: Same as the standard heat treatment system of the alloy. For net-shape precision castings, it is recommended to perform in inert atmosphere.
Surface treatment process of alloy K480: Plasma-sprayed CoCrAlY coating.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy K480: The alloy has good machinability.
Phase transformation temperature of alloy K480: M₂₃C₆ carbide precipitation peak is at 1000℃, begins to dissolve at 1020℃, and completely dissolves at 1040℃.

Overview of alloy GH2035A: GH2035A is an Fe-Ni-Cr base precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy for use at temperatures below 750℃. This alloy was developed based on GH2035 alloy by adjusting the contents of carbon, boron, aluminum, and titanium, and modifying the heat treatment system. This alloy not only retains the advantages of the original alloy but also has higher strength, better microstructural stability, and good hot workability. Main products include hot-rolled and forged bars, forgings, and ring-shaped parts.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy GH2035A: The alloy has been used to manufacture over ten types of components such as turbine inner and outer rings and support rings for various aero engines, and has been put into batch production with good results. After long-term aging, the alloy precipitates σ phase, but the growth rate is very slow, and after growing to a certain length it stops increasing. During aging at 700℃×10000h and 750℃×4000h, the precipitated σ phase size is below 20um, having a minor effect on alloy properties[3].
Material designation of alloy GH2035A: GH2035A (GH1035A, GH35A).

Material technical standards for alloy GH2035A: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; GJB 3165A Specification for Hot-Rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for Aviation Load-Bearing Components; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation

Melting process of alloy GH2035A: Electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting process is used.
Heat treatment system of alloy GH2035A: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, the standard heat treatment system for hot-rolled and forged bars, forgings is: 1080℃×2h/WQ + 680℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy GH2035A: Bar diameters typically range from 20mm to 300mm, usual lengths 2000mm to 6000mm; forgings and ring-shaped parts of various specifications. Hot-rolled and forged bars, forgings and ring-shaped parts are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Density of alloy GH2035A: ρ=8.17g/cm³[5,7].
Hardness of alloy GH2035A: After standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness is HBS 230[7].
Fatigue properties of alloy GH2035A: At 650℃, N=10^7 cycles, rotating bending fatigue limit σ-1=370MPa[4].
Parts heat treatment process for alloy GH2035A: Part heat treatment system is: 1080℃×2h/WQ + 680℃×16h/AC.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy GH2035A: No special requirements.

Overview of alloy GH4710: GH4710 is a Ni-Cr-Co base precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy, also usable as a casting alloy, for service temperatures up to 980℃. The alloy has high strength below 900℃, high sulfur corrosion resistance, oxidation resistance, and relatively good microstructural stability. Can be used to manufacture turbine rotor blades and discs, and integral gas turbine rotors. Main products include bars, forgings, disc forgings, and precision castings.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy GH4710: The alloy has been used to manufacture integral turbine discs for turboshaft engines, used in helicopters and oilfield generators. After long-term aging, the high-temperature plasticity, room temperature tensile strength, and yield strength of the alloy decrease significantly. The higher the aging temperature, the greater the decrease, but high-temperature strength changes are smaller. After long-term aging at 850℃ and 950℃, the thermal fatigue life of the alloy significantly decreases due to the precipitation of acicular σ phase. After re-heat treatment of material aged at 850℃, its rupture life can recover to 90%, and rupture plasticity can recover to the original level.
Material designation of alloy GH4710: GH4710 (GH710).
Similar designations of alloy GH4710: Udimet710 (USA), NCK18TDA (France).

Material technical standards for alloy GH4710: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation; HB 5423 Disc Forgings of GH710 Alloy for Aviation
Melting process of alloy GH4710: Wrought alloy uses vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting process.

Heat treatment system of alloy GH4710: Excerpted from HB/Z 140 and literature[1], the standard heat treatment systems for various product forms are: a) Disc forgings, 1170℃±10℃×4h/AC + 1080℃±10℃×4h/AC + 845℃±10℃×24h/AC + 760℃±10℃×16h/AC, HV≥370; b) Precision castings, 1150℃±10℃×2h/AC + 1065℃±10℃×4h/AC + 760℃±10℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy GH4710: Excerpted from HB 5423. Various specifications of open-die forged discs and bars. Discs and bars are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy GH4710: Melting point 1245℃±15℃[12].
Density of alloy GH4710: ρ=8.08g/cm³[12].
Magnetic properties of alloy GH4710: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Parts heat treatment process for alloy GH4710: For wrought alloy parts, the first solution heat treatment offers two optional temperatures: 1170℃±10℃ or 1150℃±10℃. The latter three stages are the same as the standard heat treatment system for the product form. Material treated with the former has coarse grains, the latter fine grains, affecting all grain-size-related properties. During part heat treatment heating, slow heating and uniform heating should be used, typically using stepwise heating methods to control the solution treatment temperature strictly to ensure quality.

Surface treatment process of alloy GH4710: The alloy uses argon gas protection during stress relief annealing; surface shot peening and electropolishing can be performed. To ensure safe and reliable use of parts, attention should be paid to surface integrity.
Phase transformation temperature of alloy GH4710: γ' phase begins to dissolve at about 1130℃; MC-type carbides begin to dissolve at about 1170℃; M23C6-type carbides dissolve at about 1195℃.

Overview of alloy K444: K444 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-crystal cast superalloy for use at temperatures below 900℃. The alloy contains w(W+Mo+Co) 18%, w(Al+Ti+Nb+Hf) 8%, w(Cr) 16%, and various trace elements, achieving sufficient solid solution strengthening, precipitation strengthening, and grain boundary strengthening. The boron content in the alloy is very high, belonging to a low-carbon high-boron alloy, which can significantly improve the intermediate temperature plasticity, especially creep plasticity. This alloy has good high-temperature strength and hot corrosion resistance. Main product is precision-cast blades.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy K444: The alloy is suitable for manufacturing high-temperature structural components such as turbine blades operating below 900℃ in modern power generation or marine gas turbines. The alloy has excellent hot corrosion resistance, significantly better than the widely used K438 and IN738 alloys at home and abroad. The average corrosion rate of this alloy in a mixed salt of 90% Na2SO4 + 10% NaCl at 900℃×300h is 0.08 mg/(cm²·h), with an average corrosion depth of 65.5 μm, while K438 alloy specimens would be completely corroded in the same environment.
Material designation of alloy K444: K444 (K44, K488)
Similar designations of alloy K444: ЧC88y-BM (Russia)
Material technical standards for alloy K444: Q/KJ.J02.26 K444 Cast Nickel-Based Superalloy Ingot

Melting and casting process of alloy K444: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, remelted in a vacuum induction furnace, and cast into parts and test bars using investment precision casting with sand filling. Pouring temperature 1430℃±20℃, mold temperature 950℃.
Heat treatment system of alloy K444: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.26, the standard heat treatment system for the alloy is: 1170℃±10℃×4h/AC + 1050℃±10℃×4h/AC + 850℃±10℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy K444: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.26. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy K444: 1262℃~1319℃[1]
Density of alloy K444: ρ=8.232g/cm³[1]
Magnetic properties of alloy K444: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of alloy K444: After standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness is approximately HBS 390[2].
Impact properties of alloy K444: After standard heat treatment, room temperature αku≈145 kJ/m²[2].
Forming process and properties of alloy K444: The alloy has good castability. Complex thin-walled hollow blades can be cast using investment casting. Linear casting shrinkage is approximately 1.9%.
Parts heat treatment process for alloy K444: Same as the standard heat treatment system of the alloy.
Surface treatment process of alloy K444: Thermal barrier coating is applied to blade surfaces.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy K444: Appropriate processes are required for creep-feed grinding of blade fir-tree roots to prevent grinding cracks.

Overview of alloy GH5605: GH5605 is a Co-Cr-Ni base solid-solution strengthened wrought superalloy for use at temperatures below 1000℃. The alloy is solid-solution strengthened by adding ω(Cr) 20% and ω(W) 15%. The alloy has moderate creep and rupture strength below 815℃, excellent oxidation resistance below 1090℃, and good processability and weldability. Main products include hot-rolled and cold-rolled plates, cold-rolled strips, bars, forgings, wires, and precision castings.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy GH5605: The alloy has been used to manufacture high-temperature components such as aero engine guide vanes, turbine outer rings, outer walls, swirlers, and seal segments. The alloy is suitable for working in jet engines, gas turbines, and marine atmospheres; the minimum temperature for oxidation and carburization resistance during intermittent operation is 870℃, and it can withstand temperatures up to 1090℃ during continuous operation in air. The alloy is very sensitive to silicon content; silicon promotes the formation of Co2W type Laves phase during exposure between 760℃ and 925℃, reducing the room temperature plasticity of the alloy; therefore, w(Si) should be controlled <0.4%[1].
Material designation of alloy GH5605: GH5605 (GH605).
Similar designations of alloy GH5605: L605, HS25, WF-11, A1S1670, UNSR30605 (USA), KC20WN (France).

Material technical standards for alloy GH5605: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation; Liaoxin 6-0041 GH605 Alloy Hot-Rolled Plates, Cold-Rolled Sheets and Strips; QJ/DT 01.63025 GH605 Alloy Cold-Rolled Strips (Hard Condition) New Product Technical Specification; QJ/DT 01.63073 GH605 Alloy Cold-Drawn Bar Technical Specification; QJ/DT 01.63079 Hot-Rolled Bars of GH605 Alloy for Aero Engines Technical Specification

Melting process of alloy GH5605: Electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting process is used.

Heat treatment system of alloy GH5605: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, Liaoxin 6-0041, QJ/DT 01.63025, QJ/DT 01.63073, QJ/DT 01.63079, and reference [1], the standard heat treatment systems for various product forms are: a) Hot-rolled bars, 1200℃～1230℃/fast cool, HB≤282; b) Cold-drawn bars, 1177℃～1232℃/air cool or fast cool; c) Hot-rolled plates, cold-rolled sheets, strips, wires, 1175℃～1230℃, cooled at a rate not lower than air cooling; where: for thickness/diameter δ(d)≤3mm, hold 8min～10min; for thickness/diameter δ(d) 3mm～5mm, hold 15min～20min; HB≤290 HV; d) Forged bars, ring-shaped parts, 1175℃～1230℃/fast air cool or water quench; HB≤248; e) Stress relief annealing for welded parts, 1165℃～1185℃/cooled at a rate not lower than air cooling; where: for thickness/diameter δ(d)≤3mm, hold 8min～10min; for thickness/diameter δ(d) 3mm～5mm, hold 15min～20min.
Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy GH5605: Excerpted from Liaoxin 6-0041, QJ/DT 01.63025, QJ/DT 01.63073, QJ/DT 01.63079, and reference [1].
Melting temperature range of alloy GH5605: 1330℃～1410℃[2].
Specific heat capacity of alloy GH5605: 20℃～100℃, c=377 J/(kg·℃)[2].
Density of alloy GH5605: ρ=9.13 g/cm³[2].
Magnetic properties of alloy GH5605: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and properties of alloy GH5605: Hot working temperature range is 1200℃～980℃. Grain boundary carbides can be reduced when forging temperature is sufficiently high, and grain size can be controlled when temperature is sufficiently low. Suitable forging temperature is approximately 1170℃. Care should be taken to prevent corner cracking during forging. Furnace atmosphere should be neutral or weakly oxidizing. Heating hold time is calculated at about 24 min/10 mm, and forging can be cooled at any rate. The alloy can be cold formed using various processes in the solution-treated condition, requiring more powerful forming equipment. Due to the high work hardening rate, multiple intermediate anneals are required during cold forming. During sheet and other product forming, heating at 230℃ is recommended[1].

Weldability of alloy GH5605: The alloy can be fusion welded, resistance welded, and brazed. For fusion welding, manual or automatic inert gas shielded arc welding can be used with tungsten electrode or HGH5605 wire as filler, using low heat input. Submerged arc welding is not recommended to avoid coarse grains and embrittlement. Welding should be performed in the solution-treated condition, followed by rapid cooling and subsequent annealing treatment[1].

Surface treatment process of alloy GH5605: Oxide scale formed during solution treatment can be removed by sandblasting or alkali-acid pickling. Alkali-acid pickling process: Immerse in 370℃ hydroxide solution for 15 min; immerse in 60℃～70℃ 8%～12% sulfuric acid for 10 min～15 min; immerse in 50℃～70℃ mixture of 12%～15% nitric acid + 1%～3% hydrofluoric acid for 15 min. Rinse thoroughly with water after each step.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy GH5605: The alloy can be satisfactorily machined and ground.

Overview of alloy K418: K418 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-crystal cast superalloy, strengthened by γ' phase precipitation, for use at temperatures below 900℃. The alloy has high creep strength, resistance to thermal fatigue, and oxidation resistance. The alloy has a simple composition, contains no cobalt, and has low density, typically used in the as-cast condition. Suitable for manufacturing precision cast hot-section rotating and stationary components operating below 900℃, such as turbine blades, guide vanes, nozzle guide vanes, and integral cast turbines.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy K418: The alloy has been widely used to manufacture turbine blades, guide vanes, and integral cast turbines for aviation, land, and marine gas turbines; integral turbine rotors and nozzle guide vanes for aerospace missile engines; turbocharger turbines for diesel and gasoline engines; and hot extrusion dies. Long-term production and use by multiple domestic manufacturers have proven stable metallurgical quality and reliable performance. Hot isostatic pressing (HIP) treatment and subsequent heat treatment can improve the alloy's plasticity and fatigue resistance, and can also restore the microstructure and service performance of parts degraded after long-term use. A protective coating is required for long-term use under hot corrosion conditions. After long-term stress aging at 800℃, a small amount of σ phase precipitates, but it has no significant effect on properties.
Material designation of alloy K418: K418 (K18, 518)
Similar designations of alloy K418: Inconel 713C or 713C (USA)

Material technical standards for alloy K418: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 1: Master Alloys for Ordinary Precision Castings of Superalloys; HB 7728 Specification for Precision Cast Blades of Superalloys for Aviation; HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed-Crystal Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero Engines; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation
Melting and casting process of alloy K418: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, remelted in a vacuum induction furnace to cast precision castings and test bars.

Heat treatment system of alloy K418: Excerpted from HB/Z 140 and literature[10], the alloy is used in the as-cast condition or after heat treatment. a) Standard heat treatment system: 1180℃×2h/AC + 930℃×16h/AC; b) Hot isostatic pressing + standard heat treatment: HIP (1210℃, 126 MPa, 3h) + 1180℃×2h/AC + 930℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy K418: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1, HB 7728 and HB 7763. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy and precision castings are supplied in the as-cast condition.
Melting temperature range of alloy K418: 1295℃~1345℃[3]
Density of alloy K418: ρ=8.0 g/cm³[3]
Magnetic properties of alloy K418: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of alloy K418: As-cast alloy, room temperature hardness is HRC 33～37[3].
Forming process and properties of alloy K418: Good castability, blades with thickness less than 1mm and integral turbines can be cast using investment casting, casting shrinkage is about 2%.
Parts heat treatment process for alloy K418: Turbine blades typically undergo stress relief treatment, specification: 930℃×16h/AC.
Surface treatment process of alloy K418: The alloy can be aluminized (Al), chromaluminized (CrAl), or coated with CoNiCrSi for protection.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy K418: Carbide tools are typically used for machining.

Overview of alloy GH2761: GH2761 is an Fe-Ni-Cr base precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy for use at temperatures below 750℃. The alloy is strengthened by adding high aluminum, high titanium, and controlling a suitable Al/Ti ratio to form γ' precipitation strengthening phase, with significant additions of tungsten and molybdenum for solid solution strengthening, and trace boron and cerium for grain boundary strengthening. This alloy has high yield strength, good resistance to thermal and low-cycle fatigue, and good hot workability and machinability. Main products include discs, ring blanks, disk forgings, rings, forged stock, and bars [1,2].

Application overview and characteristics of alloy GH2761: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero engine turbine disks, compressor disks, drums, turbine casings, labyrinth rings, stator blades, precision bolts, and other components, with good results in batch production. The alloy has good resistance to gas corrosion below 900℃ and stress corrosion in gas atmospheres [3]. After aging at (650～700)℃×2000h, no new phases precipitate; after aging at 750℃×2000h, a small amount of Laves phase precipitates [4].
Material designation of alloy GH2761: GH2761 (GH761, 761).

Material technical standards for alloy GH2761: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation; BTXH 524 GH761 Alloy Bar Technical Agreement; QJ/DT 0160027 GH761 Alloy Bar Technical Specification
Melting process of alloy GH2761: Vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting process is used.

Heat treatment system of alloy GH2761: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, BTXH 524, QJ/DT 0160027 and literature [5～7], there are two standard heat treatment systems: System I: 1090℃±10℃×2h/WQ + 850℃±10℃×4h/AC + 750℃±10℃×24h/AC, applicable to hot-rolled bars, forgings for non-rotating parts, forged bars, and ring-rolled parts. System II: 1120℃±10℃×2h/WQ + 850℃±10℃×4h/AC + 750℃±10℃×24h/AC, applicable to forgings and forged bars for rotating parts.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy GH2761: Bars from d20mm to 300mm; disk forgings and ring forgings of various specifications. Bars are generally supplied without heat treatment, polished or turned; discs, ring blanks, forgings, and rings are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy GH2761: 1325℃～1345℃[8].
Density of alloy GH2761: ρ=8.10 g/cm³[9].
Fracture toughness of alloy GH2761: Samples taken chordwise from die-forged discs, after System II treatment, room temperature fracture toughness KIC = 108.5 MN/m3/2[9].
Weldability of alloy GH2761: The alloy can be argon arc welded and electron beam welded. Argon arc welding is performed in the solution-treated condition, followed by aging treatment.

Parts heat treatment process for alloy GH2761: Depending on part requirements, the heat treatment system can be chosen as 1120℃±10℃×2h/WQ + 850℃±10℃×4h/AC + 750℃±10℃×24h/AC or 1090℃±10℃×2h/WQ + 850℃±10℃×4h/AC + 750℃±10℃×24h/AC[10].
Machining and grinding performance of alloy GH2761: The alloy has relatively good machinability and can be machined in the standard heat-treated condition.

Phase transformation temperature of alloy GH2761: Temperature range for maximum γ' phase precipitation is 750℃～850℃, complete dissolution temperature is 1000℃[10]. η-Ni₃Ti phase precipitation is maximum around 980℃, complete dissolution temperature is about 1050℃[12].

Overview of alloy DD404: DD404 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened single-crystal superalloy, belonging to the first generation of single-crystal superalloys, for use at temperatures below 1050℃. The alloy has good intermediate and high-temperature properties, and good oxidation and corrosion resistance. The alloy has low density, low cost, and good processability. Main products are turbine rotors and guide vanes.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy DD404: The alloy has been used to manufacture high-temperature components for advanced aero engines. Suitable for manufacturing turbine rotors operating around 1000℃ and guide vanes operating around 1050℃, and other high-temperature parts. The alloy can be surface treated using solid-phase or gas-phase aluminizing, or physical vapor deposition. After exposure at 850℃ and 950℃ for 1000h, the microstructure remains unchanged except for slight coarsening of the γ´ phase; no TCP phases were found.
Material designation of alloy DD404: DD404 (DD4).
Similar designations of alloy DD404: René N4 (USA).
Material technical standards for alloy DD404: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; Q/6SZ 1311 DD4 Alloy Ingot

Melting and casting process of alloy DD404: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, single-crystal parts and specimens are manufactured using investment precision casting and directional solidification grain selection or seed crystal methods.
Heat treatment system of alloy DD404: Excerpted from Q/6SZ 1311 and literature[2], the standard heat treatment system for the alloy is: 1260℃±10℃×2h/air cool + 1080℃±10℃×4h/AC + 900℃±10℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy DD404: Excerpted from Q/6SZ 1311. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 70mm to 90mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; single-crystal blades are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy DD404: 1284℃～1339℃ (differential thermal analysis)[1].
Density of alloy DD404: ρ=8.49 g/cm³[1]
Magnetic properties of alloy DD404: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of alloy DD404: In standard heat-treated condition, average room temperature hardness is HRC41[2].
Forming process and properties of alloy DD404: The alloy has good castability and can be cast into complex-shaped thin-walled hollow single-crystal blades.
Weldability of alloy DD404: Vacuum brazing is possible.

Parts heat treatment process for alloy DD404: Net-shape parts should be heat treated under vacuum or protective atmosphere. Heat treatment specification: 1260℃±10℃×2h/WQ + 1080℃±10℃×4h/AC + 900℃±10℃×16h/AC.
Surface treatment process of alloy DD404: Solid-phase or gas-phase aluminizing or physical vapor deposition processes can be used.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy DD404: No special requirements.

Overview of alloy GH4169: GH4169 is a Ni-Cr-Fe base precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy for long-term use in the temperature range -253℃ to 650℃, with short-term use temperature up to 800℃. The alloy has high strength below 650℃, good fatigue, radiation, oxidation, and corrosion resistance, as well as good workability, weldability, and long-term microstructural stability. Suitable for manufacturing turbine disks, rings, blades, shafts, fasteners, elastic elements, plate structural parts, casings, etc., in aviation, aerospace, nuclear, and petrochemical industries. Main products include hot-rolled and forged bars, cold-drawn bars, plates, strips, wires, tubes, rings, and forgings. Compositionally, the alloy is divided into ordinary and premium grades. Premium GH4169 alloy has stricter control over carbon, niobium, sulfur, and gas contents, mainly used for rotating parts. Process-wise, it is divided into standard, high-strength, and direct aging types. High-strength and direct aging processes are used for premium GH4169 alloy forgings, with decreasing hot working temperatures, finer average grain sizes, and increasing strength, meeting the requirements of different rotating components in aero engines.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy GH4169: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero engine turbine disks, rings, casings, shafts, blades, fasteners, elastic elements, gas ducts, sealing elements, and welded structures; turbine rotors in liquid hydrogen/liquid oxygen rocket engines; various elastic elements and grids in nuclear energy applications; and many parts in petroleum and chemical industries. Batch production and usage are satisfactory. During vacuum consumable remelting, helium cooling can be used to effectively reduce niobium segregation; spray forming can produce rings, reducing cost and cycle time; superplastic forming can expand the product range.
Material designation of alloy GH4169: GH4169 (GH169).
Similar designations of alloy GH4169: Inconel 718 (USA), NC19FeNb (France).

Material technical standards for alloy GH4169: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; GJB 2611A Cold-Drawn Bars of Superalloys for Aviation; GJB 2612 Cold-Drawn Wires for Welding of Superalloys; GJB 3318A Cold-Rolled Strips of Superalloys for Aviation; GJB 3527 Cold-Drawn Wires for Springs of Superalloys; GJB 5280 Disk Forgings of Superalloys for Aero Engines; GJB 5301 Rings of Superalloys for Aero Engines; GJB 712A Forged Discs of GH4169 Superalloy for Aerospace; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation; Q/5B 4040 Premium GH4169 Alloy Forgings; Q/3B 4048 (Q/5B4029, Fugaoxin 13, Xieshangwu High 22, C3S280) Premium GH4169 Alloy Bars; Q/3B 4056 (Q/5B4009, Fugaoxin 11, Xieshangwu High 24) High-Strength GH4169 Alloy Compressor Disk Forgings; Q/3B 4054 (RJTO-10, Fugaoxin 10, Xieshangwu High 23) Direct-Aged GH4169 Alloy Compressor Disk, Turbine Disk Forgings; Q/3B 4050 (Q/5B4037, Fugaoxin 9, Xieshangwu High 32) GH4169 Alloy Thick Plates, Sheets and Strips; Q/3B 4052 GH4169 Alloy Capillary Tubes

Melting process of alloy GH4169: Vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting + electroslag remelting process is used.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy GH4169: Hot-rolled bars d15mm～100mm; forged bars d100mm～350mm; round cold-drawn bars d8mm～45mm (for ≤25mm round cold-drawn bars, 8%～12% or 12%～30% cold reduction), square cold-drawn bars side length 8mm～30mm; cold-drawn wires d0.2mm～8mm; spring wire class A, d≤6mm (50%～60% cold reduction), d>6mm (≥30% cold reduction). Class B d≤0.65mm (15% cold reduction), d>0.65mm (cold reduction unlimited); hot-rolled plates δ4.00mm～14.00mm; cold-rolled sheets δ0.50mm～4.00mm; cold-rolled strips δ0.10mm～0.80mm; tubes d≤5mm, wall thickness ≤0.5mm, length ≥0.5m; various specifications of forged discs, rings, die forgings (disks, integral forgings). Hot-rolled and forged bars are supplied without heat treatment, turned or ground; cold-drawn bars are supplied cold-drawn + ground, or cold-drawn + solution treated + ground; wires are supplied cold-drawn or solution treated, after descaling; spring wires are supplied bright annealed in straight lengths or coils; tubes Class A are supplied cold-drawn + solution treated + descaled, or cold-drawn + bright annealed; tubes Class B are supplied cold-drawn; strips are supplied in coils after bright annealing + edge trimming; cold-rolled sheets are supplied solution treated + alkali-acid pickled + leveled + straightened + edge trimmed; hot-rolled plates are supplied solution treated + alkali-acid pickled + leveled + edge trimmed; disk forgings are supplied as-forged + descaled, or after standard heat treatment + rough machining; rings are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy GH4169: 1260℃～1320℃[9].
Density of alloy GH4169: ρ=8.24 g/cm³[9].
Magnetic properties of alloy GH4169: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of alloy GH4169: For all product forms after standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness HBS 450～346[1].
Parts heat treatment process for alloy GH4169: Same as the standard heat treatment systems for each product form.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy GH4169: The alloy can be satisfactorily machined. During machining, ensure fillets meet design requirements and transitions are smooth.

Phase transformation temperature of alloy GH4169: γ'' phase maximum stable temperature 650℃, begins to dissolve at 840℃～870℃, completely dissolves at 950℃; γ' phase precipitates at 600℃, completely dissolves at 840℃; δ phase begins to precipitate at 700℃, precipitation peak at 940℃, begins to dissolve at 980℃, completely dissolves at 1020℃[2,3].

Overview of alloy K4130: K4130 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-crystal cast superalloy for use at temperatures below 850℃. The alloy is solid-solution strengthened by adding chromium and molybdenum, and precipitation strengthened by aluminum and titanium forming γ' phase. The alloy has high strength and fatigue resistance below 850℃, good oxidation and corrosion resistance. The microstructure is stable with high recrystallization temperature. Main products include turbine guide vanes and other aero engine components for use below 850℃.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy K4130: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero engine turbine guide vanes, sectors, engine inlet flange segments 1 and 3, high-pressure stage I, II guide vane sector locating pieces, inner covers, and other high-temperature components. Batch production and usage are satisfactory. Compared to nickel-based alloys for similar applications, this alloy has better high-temperature oxidation resistance and hot corrosion resistance, requiring no coating in service.
Material designation of alloy K4130: K4130 (K130).
Similar designations of alloy K4130: C130 (UK).

Material technical standards for alloy K4130: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 1: Master Alloys for Ordinary Precision Castings of Superalloys; HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed-Crystal Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero Engines; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation

Melting and casting process of alloy K4130: Master alloy is melted in vacuum induction furnace or non-vacuum induction furnace. Parts and test bars are cast using vacuum induction furnace remelting and investment precision casting.
Heat treatment system of alloy K4130: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, the alloy is used in the as-cast condition. Stress relief annealing system is: 1040℃±10℃×10h/AC.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy K4130: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1 and HB 7763. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy and precision castings are supplied in the as-cast condition.
Melting temperature range of alloy K4130: Melting point 1350℃±10℃[1].
Density of alloy K4130: ρ=8.33 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic properties of alloy K4130: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming process and properties of alloy K4130: The alloy has good fluidity and low hot tearing tendency. Complex-shaped turbine guide vanes and other parts can be cast using investment casting.
Parts heat treatment process for alloy K4130: Stress relief annealing system is: 1040℃±10℃×10h/AC.
Surface treatment process of alloy K4130: Used without coating.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy K4130: The alloy is easy to machine, and grinding cracks rarely occur.

Overview of alloy GH1333: GH1333 is an Fe-Ni-Cr base solid-solution strengthened wrought superalloy, strengthened by adding chromium, tungsten, molybdenum, etc., for use at temperatures below 900℃. The alloy has high high-temperature strength, oxidation resistance, and hot corrosion resistance, good workability and weldability. Main products include sheets, bars, wires, strips, and tubes.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy GH1333: The alloy can be used for long-term service in land-based and marine gas turbine combustion liners, transition sections, and other high-temperature load-bearing components. The alloy is also selected for high-temperature furnace equipment such as muffle furnace covers and radiant tubes requiring high-temperature, oxidation, and corrosion resistance. During thin sheet hot working, if the heating temperature or intermediate quenching temperature is too high, network carbides tend to form. Therefore, higher temperatures for hot rolling and lower temperatures for intermediate quenching should be used to prevent network carbide formation. Finished thin sheets should be solution treated at sufficiently high temperatures to completely eliminate network carbides.
Material designation of alloy GH1333: GH1333 (GH333)
Similar designations of alloy GH1333: RA333 (USA)
Material technical standards for alloy GH1333: AMS 5593A Corrosion-Resistant Alloy Sheet, Strip, and Plate; AMS 5717B Heat-Resistant Alloy Bar, Forgings, and Rings
Melting process of alloy GH1333: Non-vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting, or electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting process is used.

Heat treatment system of alloy GH1333: Excerpted from AMS 5593A, AMS 5717B, and literature[1], the standard heat treatment systems for various product forms are: a) Sheet, strip, and plate, 1170℃～1200℃, rapid air cool, holding time determined by thickness; b) Bars, forgings, and rings, 1093℃±28℃, water quench or rapid air cool, holding time 10 min per 25.4 mm thickness.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy GH1333: Plates δ0.5mm～4.0mm; strips δ0.05mm～0.8mm; bars d≤300mm; forgings of various sizes. Sheet, strip, and plate are supplied solution treated + pickled; strip can also be supplied cold-rolled; bars, forgings, and rings are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy GH1333: 1310℃～1365℃[1].
Density of alloy GH1333: ρ=8.23 g/cm³[1].
Parts heat treatment process for alloy GH1333: Same as the standard heat treatment systems for each product form.

Machining and grinding performance of alloy GH1333: Use heavy-duty tools and low speed with deep cuts to minimize work hardening. Recommended cutting speed is about 40% of that for screw cutting. Sulfur-based cutting fluids are recommended. For most machining operations, carbide and stainless steel tools are recommended[7].

Overview of alloy DZ468: DZ468 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened directionally solidified columnar grain superalloy, a high-strength hot corrosion resistant alloy containing rhenium, for use at temperatures below 1000℃. The alloy has good weldability and castability. Main products are turbine blades and other high-temperature parts for corrosive conditions.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy DZ468: The alloy is suitable for manufacturing aircraft engine turbine blades and marine gas turbine blades operating in maritime environments. The mechanical properties of the alloy are similar to DZ4125L alloy, and hot corrosion resistance is comparable to K438 alloy. After long-term aging at 900℃×1000h, no TCP phases were found in the microstructure.
Material designation of alloy DZ468: DZ468 (DZ68).
Material technical standards for alloy DZ468: Q/KJ.J02.37 DZ468 Alloy Ingot (Interim)

Melting process of alloy DZ468: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, directionally solidified test bars and parts are cast using a vacuum directional solidification furnace remelting.

Heat treatment system of alloy DZ468: Excerpted from literature[1] and Q/KJ.J02.37, the standard heat treatment system for the alloy is: 1240℃±5℃×0.5h→1260℃±5℃×0.5h→1280℃±5℃×2h/AC + 1120℃±5℃×4h/FC (cool slowly over 1h)→1080℃±5℃×4h/AC + 900℃±10℃×4h/AC.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy DZ468: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.37. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 85mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition.
Melting temperature range of alloy DZ468: 1314℃～1393℃ (differential thermal analysis)[1].
Density of alloy DZ468: ρ=8.455 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic properties of alloy DZ468: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of alloy DZ468: As-cast alloy, room temperature hardness HBW 355.6; after standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness HBW 358.9.

Parts heat treatment process for alloy DZ468: Net-shape blades should be heat treated under vacuum or protective atmosphere. Vacuum heat treatment specification: 1240℃±5℃×1h→1260℃±5℃×1h→1280℃±5℃×2h/argon cool + 1120℃±10℃×4h/FC (cool slowly over 1h)→1080℃±5℃×4h/argon cool + 900℃±10℃×4h/argon cool.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy DZ468: No special requirements.

Overview of alloy GH1131: GH1131 is an Fe-Ni-Cr base solid-solution strengthened wrought superalloy, strengthened by adding chromium, tungsten, molybdenum, etc., for use at temperatures 700℃～1000℃. The alloy has good hot workability, weldability, and cold formability. Main products include cold-rolled sheets, hot-rolled plates, bars, flats, and wires.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy GH1131: The alloy has been used to manufacture rocket engine components for short-term service at 700℃～1000℃, such as combustion chamber baffles, turbine gas inlet ducts, gas generator convergent sections, conical guide vanes, turbine elbows, and flanges; aero engine components for long-term service at 700℃～750℃, such as afterburner liners, afterburner combustion chambers, heat shields, stabilizers, and trim tabs; land-based gas turbine components for long-term service below 700℃, such as flame tubes. Compared to nickel-based alloys for similar applications, this alloy has poorer high-temperature oxidation resistance and microstructural stability. After long-term use at 700℃～900℃, the room temperature plasticity of the alloy decreases.
Material designation of alloy GH1131: GH1131 (GH131)
Similar designations of alloy GH1131: Х21Н28В5М3БАР, ЭП126, ВЖ100 (Russia).

Material technical standards for alloy GH1131: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; GB/T 14995 Hot-Rolled Plates of Superalloys; GB/T 14996 Cold-Rolled Sheets of Superalloys; GJB 1952A Cold-Rolled Sheets of Superalloys for Aviation; GJB 1954 Cold-Rolled Sheets of GH1131 Superalloy for Aerospace; GJB 1955 Hot-Forged/Rolled Bars of GH1131 Superalloy for Aerospace; GJB 2612 Cold-Drawn Wires for Welding of Superalloys; YB/T 5245 Hot-Rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for General Load-Bearing Components; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation
Melting process of alloy GH1131: Non-vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting, or electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting process is used.

Heat treatment system of alloy GH1131: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, GB/T 14995, GJB 1955, and YB/T 5245, the standard heat treatment systems for various product forms are: a) Cold-rolled sheets, hot-rolled plates, solution treatment: 1130℃～1170℃/AC, where holding time: for thickness δ≤3mm, 8min～12min; for thickness δ 3mm～5mm, 12min～16min; b) Hot-rolled and forged bars, 1160℃±10℃/AC, holding time depending on bar diameter.
Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy GH1131: Excerpted from GB/T 14995, GB/T 14996, GJB 1952A, GJB 1954, GJB 1955, and YB/T 5245.
Density of alloy GH1131: ρ=8.33 g/cm³[4]

Parts heat treatment process for alloy GH1131: Final heat treatment temperature for sheet parts is 1150℃±10℃ or 1080℃～1120℃; intermediate heat treatment temperature is 1000℃±10℃. After heat treatment, cool in inert gas or air.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy GH1131: No special requirements.

Overview of alloy K4537: K4537 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-crystal cast superalloy, a hot corrosion resistant alloy for use at temperatures below 850℃. The mechanical properties and corrosion resistance are equivalent to US IN738 alloy. This alloy contains no expensive tantalum, contains more tungsten, and has a suitable Ti/Al ratio. The alloy has high rupture strength, good castability, and can be used in the as-cast condition. Suitable for manufacturing land-based and marine gas turbine turbine rotor blades for long-term service below 850℃.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy K4537: The alloy has been used to manufacture turbine rotor blades for various gas turbine generator sets. Shot peening of parts can significantly improve notch fatigue strength and also improve smooth fatigue strength to some extent[4]; Hot isostatic pressing (HIP) treatment can close porosity, significantly improving casting density. 600℃ rotating bending fatigue tests show smooth fatigue increased by 59 MPa, notch fatigue increased by 98 MPa[6]; Rupture life at 800℃, 225 MPa can reach 14000 h; After stress-free aging at 600℃～850℃ for 7000 h～10000 h, no harmful TCP phases were found[2].
Material designation of alloy K4537: K4537 (K537, 537).
