Material technical standards for alloy K4537: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 1: Master Alloys for Ordinary Precision Castings of Superalloys; Q/GYB 05049 K4537 Alloy Turbine Blade Precision Castings
Melting and casting process of alloy K4537: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, remelted in a vacuum induction furnace to cast investment precision castings and test bars.

Heat treatment system of alloy K4537: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1 and Q/GYB 05049, the alloy is used in the as-cast condition. After HIP or surface coating, heat treatment should be performed: 1150℃×2h/AC + 1050℃×4h/AC + 850℃×16h/AC[6]. Net-shape blade castings should be heat treated under vacuum or protective atmosphere.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy K4537: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1 and Q/GYB 05049. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied in as-cast or after HIP (or surface coating) + heat treatment condition.
Melting temperature range of alloy K4537: 1255℃～1335℃[3].
Density of alloy K4537: ρ=8.30 g/cm³[3].
Magnetic properties of alloy K4537: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of alloy K4537: As-cast alloy, average room temperature hardness is HRC 42[3,10].

Forming process and properties of alloy K4537: The alloy has good castability. Complex-shaped turbine rotor blades can be cast using vacuum investment precision casting. Pouring temperature 1400℃±10℃, mold temperature 930℃±10℃[7].
Weldability of alloy K4537: Finished blades are not allowed to be welded or repaired.
Parts heat treatment process for alloy K4537: Parts are used in the as-cast condition, or can be used after HIP or surface coating followed by 1150℃×2h/AC + 1050℃×4h/AC + 850℃×16h/AC treatment.

Surface treatment process of alloy K4537: Blade airfoils can use sintered aluminide coating. Coating slurry composition: 69%Ni-15%Cr-15%Co-1%Si; diffusion sintering system: 1050℃×4h/AC, hydrogen protection; pack ratio: Cr:Al = 10:1. Blade fir-tree root areas are shot peened.

Machining and grinding performance of alloy K4537: No special requirements for cutting tools. However, during grinding, to avoid grinding cracks, it is recommended to use GD/GG46-60CR2P400×40×127 grinding wheel.

Overview of alloy GH4698: GH4698 is a Ni-Cr base precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy for use at 750℃~800℃. This alloy was developed based on GH4033 alloy, with increased aluminum and titanium, added niobium and molybdenum, and reduced chromium compared to GH4033. The alloy has high creep and tensile strength, good plasticity, and comprehensive properties in the range of 550℃~800℃, and stable long-term microstructure. Suitable for manufacturing long-life heavily loaded components such as aero engine turbine disks and compressor disks. Main products include hot-rolled and forged stock, disks, and rings.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy GH4698: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero engine turbine disks, compressor disks, guide vanes, load-bearing rings, fasteners, etc., and large-scale turbine disks for marine gas turbines, with good batch production and usage. After long-term aging at 650℃~750℃ for 100h~1000h, the microstructure and properties are stable, with no harmful phases precipitated.
Material designation of alloy GH4698: GH4698 (GH698).
Similar designations of alloy GH4698: ЭΠ698 (XH73MБTЮ) (Russia).

Material technical standards for alloy GH4698: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; GJB 3782 Specification for Forged Discs of Superalloys for Aviation; GJB 5280 Disk Forgings of Superalloys for Aero Engines; HB 5285 Disk Forgings of GH698 Alloy for Aviation; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation; Q/GYB 05011 GH4698 Alloy Hot-Rolled and Forged Bars; Q/GYB 05025 GH4698 Alloy Die Forgings; Q/GYB 05033 Bars of GH4698 Alloy for Fasteners; Q/GYB 665 Forged Bar Stock of GH4698 Alloy for Gas Turbines; Q/GYB 666 Forged Discs of GH4698 Alloy for Gas Turbines; Q/GYB 667 Die Forgings of GH4698 Alloy for Gas Turbines
Melting process of alloy GH4698: Vacuum induction melting + vacuum arc remelting, or vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting process is used.

Heat treatment system of alloy GH4698: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, GJB 3782, GJB 5280, HB 5285, Q/GYB 05011, Q/GYB 05025, Q/GYB 05033, Q/GYB 665, Q/GYB 666, and Q/GYB 667, the standard heat treatment systems for various product forms are: a) Aviation forgings, (1110~1130)℃×(8~8.5)h/AC + (990~1010)℃×(4~4.5)h/AC + (765~785)℃×(16~16.5)h/AC; b) Aviation disk forgings, 1120℃±10℃×8h/AC + 1000℃±10℃×4h/AC + 775℃+10℃×16h/AC; c) Aviation fastener bars, (1100~1120)℃×8h/AC + 1000℃±10℃×4h/AC + 775℃+10℃×16h/AC; d) Gas turbine hot-rolled and forged bars, forged discs, die forgings, System I: (1100~1120)℃×8h/AC + 1000℃±10℃×4h/AC + 775℃±10℃×16h/AC + 700℃±10℃×16h/AC; System II: (1100~1120)℃×8h/AC + 1000℃±10℃×4h/AC + 775℃±10℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy GH4698: Excerpted from GJB 3782, GJB 5280, HB 5285, Q/GYB 05011, Q/GYB 05025, Q/GYB 05033, Q/GYB 665, Q/GYB 666, and Q/GYB 667. Hot-rolled bars d10mm~30mm; forged bars d50mm~160mm and d>300mm; forged discs d200mm~800mm; die forgings d300mm~1200mm. Bars are supplied without heat treatment, polished or turned; forgings, die forgings are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy GH4698: 1340℃~1365℃[1].
Density of alloy GH4698: ρ=8.32 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic properties of alloy GH4698: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Parts heat treatment process for alloy GH4698: Same as the standard heat treatment system for the product form.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy GH4698: No special requirements.
Phase transformation temperature of alloy GH4698: Large γ' phase precipitation temperature is 980℃~1010℃, dissolution temperature is 1020℃.

Overview of alloy GH3625: GH3625 is a Ni-Cr base solid-solution strengthened wrought superalloy, with chromium, molybdenum, and niobium as main solid-solution strengtheners, maximum use temperature 950℃. The alloy has good tensile strength and fatigue properties from low temperature to 980℃, and good workability and weldability. The alloy has excellent corrosion and oxidation resistance, and resistance to stress corrosion in salt spray atmospheres. Widely used for aero engine components, aerospace structures, and chemical equipment. Main products include sheets, bars, tubes, wires, strips, and forgings.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy GH3625: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero and aerospace engine casings, guide vanes, flanges, cylinders, fuel manifolds, etc. The corrosion rate is very low in both marine and non-marine atmospheres, with good seawater corrosion resistance. The alloy resists corrosion in various salt solutions, has good resistance to nitric and phosphoric acid corrosion. Also has considerable resistance to hydrochloric acid below boiling point and low concentration sulfuric acid. After long-term use at 550℃～700℃, some age hardening occurs, leading to some decrease in plasticity.
Material designation of alloy GH3625: GH3625 (GH625).
Similar designations of alloy GH3625: Inconel 625, UNS N06625 (USA), NC22DNb (France).

Material technical standards for alloy GH3625: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; GJB 3165A Specification for Hot-Rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for Aviation Load-Bearing Components; GJB 3317A Specification for Hot-Rolled Sheets of Superalloys for Aviation; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation; Liaoxin 7-0023 GH625 Alloy Bar Technical Specification; Liaoxin 7-0038 GH625 Alloy Cold-Rolled Sheet Technical Specification; Q/3B 4080 GH625 Alloy Tubes
Melting process of alloy GH3625: Vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting process is used.

Heat treatment system of alloy GH3625: Excerpted from HB/Z 140 and literature[10], the standard heat treatment systems for various product forms are: a) Bars, forgings, (1090～1200)℃×(1.5～2)h/WQ; b) Wires, (1090～1200)℃/≥air cooling rate, where for d≤3mm, hold (9～15)min; for d 3mm～5mm, hold (15～20)min; c) Hot-rolled sheets, (950～1030)℃/AC, HB≤305 HV; d) Welded parts, stress relief: (1080～1100)℃/≥air cooling rate, where for d(δ)≤3mm, hold (9～15)min; for d(δ) 3mm～5mm, hold (15～20)min; e) Tubes, (960～1030)℃/AC or WQ.
Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy GH3625: Excerpted from GJB 3165A, GJB 3317A, Liaoxin 7-0023, Liaoxin 7-0038, and Q/3B 4080.
Melting temperature range of alloy GH3625: 1290℃～1350℃[1].
Density of alloy GH3625: ρ=8.44 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic properties of alloy GH3625: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and properties of alloy GH3625: Ingot forging heating temperature 1120℃, start forging temperature ≥1030℃, finish forging temperature ≥930℃; die forging final reduction 15%～20%; rolling heating temperature 1120℃～1160℃, start rolling temperature ≥1050℃, finish rolling temperature ≥930℃; cold rolling reduction for sheet semi-finished product 25%～30%, finished product reduction ≥30%; intermediate and finished annealing temperature 1100℃±10℃[1,8].
Weldability of alloy GH3625: The alloy can be argon arc welded using tungsten electrode or the alloy itself as filler under protective atmosphere, and can also be brazed and resistance seam welded.

Parts heat treatment process for alloy GH3625: Stress relief: 900℃; Annealing: 930℃～1040℃×1h; Solution treatment: 1090℃～1200℃/AC or WQ. When the alloy operates at or below 650℃, material in hot-rolled, cold-rolled, or annealed condition can be used as required. When operating above 650℃, annealed material can be used for parts requiring good fatigue and tensile strength; solution treated material can be used for parts requiring better creep and rupture properties.
Surface treatment process of alloy GH3625: For removing surface oxide scale, alkali clean followed by acid pickling in nitric acid-hydrofluoric acid-water solution.

Machining and grinding performance of alloy GH3625: Use carbide or high-speed steel tools. Tools should have positive rake angle. Water-based coolant is recommended for high-speed machining; more viscous coolant is recommended for low-speed machining.

Overview of alloy K419: K419 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-crystal cast superalloy for use at temperatures below 1000℃. This alloy contains no expensive metallic elements; its composition is characterized by high tungsten and low chromium. The alloy contains w(W+Mo) 12%, w(Al+Ti+Nb) 9%, and relatively high boron. The alloy has high high-temperature strength, good comprehensive properties, stable microstructure, and good castability. Suitable for manufacturing gas turbine hollow blades and integral cast turbines and other high-temperature components.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy K419: The alloy has been used to manufacture integral cast turbine rotors for turbojet engine starters, with component operating temperature about 900℃ and maximum speed up to 80000 r/min. Within the typical stress range for turbine rotor blades (147 MPa～245 MPa), compared to foreign alloys of similar type, the operating temperature of this alloy is about 10℃ higher than MAR-M246 and 25℃~30℃ higher than IN100 alloy[1]. The alloy shows no notch sensitivity in rupture properties in the as-cast or after aluminizing condition from 700℃ to 1150℃, and the base material is fully oxidation resistant below 1050℃.
Material designation of alloy K419: K419 (K19)
Material technical standards for alloy K419: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; HB 5163 K419 Alloy Ingot
Melting and casting process of alloy K419: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, remelted in a vacuum induction furnace to cast investment precision cast parts and test bars.
Heat treatment system of alloy K419: The alloy is used in the as-cast condition.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy K419: Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 70mm to 90mm. Master alloy and precision castings are supplied in the as-cast condition.
Melting temperature range of alloy K419: 1260℃~1340℃[2]
Density of alloy K419: ρ=8.5 g/cm³[2]
Magnetic properties of alloy K419: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of alloy K419: As-cast alloy, room temperature hardness is HBS 388～359[2].

Forming process and properties of alloy K419: The alloy has good castability. Complex thin-walled hollow blades can be cast using investment casting, and integral turbines can also be cast. Casting shrinkage is approximately 2%.
Parts heat treatment process for alloy K419: Parts are used in the as-cast condition.
Surface treatment process of alloy K419: Solid-state aluminizing can be used. Process: Aluminize at 950℃ for 1h, then diffuse at 1050℃×2h.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy K419: No special requirements.

Functional test of alloy K419: Integral turbine rotors cast from this alloy underwent the following tests: a) Performance test: Gas inlet temperature 815℃～975℃; inlet pressure 294 MPa; oil pressure 196 MPa; b) Overspeed test: 23 overspeed starts, maximum speed 77000 r/min; c) Life test: Three consecutive starts within 5 min, cool to room temperature, then another three consecutive starts, repeat for 400 cycles. Each start reached 45000 r/min; d) Vibration strength test: According to actual conditions, tests were conducted at three frequencies (25, 50, 100 Hz); e) Vertical impact overload test: Loading frequency 56 times/min, total cycles 104; f) Low-temperature test: Direct start at ambient temperature -40℃～-50℃. During all tests, the turbine rotor operated normally with good performance.

Overview of alloy DZ408: DZ408 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened directionally solidified columnar grain superalloy for use at temperatures below 1050℃. The alloy has good creep and fatigue resistance, high strength and load-bearing temperature capability, good castability, oxidation and corrosion resistance. Main products are turbine blades and other high-temperature components.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy DZ408: The alloy is suitable for manufacturing turbine rotor blades operating below 1000℃ and guide vanes operating below 1050℃ in aero engines. After aging at 980℃×1000h, no TCP phases were found in the microstructure.
Material designation of alloy DZ408: DZ408.
Similar designations of alloy DZ408: CM247LC, René108 (USA).
Material technical standards for alloy DZ408: Q/6S 2477 DZ408 Alloy Ingot Specification; Q/KJ.J02.39 DZ408 Alloy Ingot (Interim)

Melting process of alloy DZ408: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, directionally solidified test bars and parts are cast using a vacuum directional solidification furnace remelting.

Heat treatment system of alloy DZ408: Excerpted from Q/6S 2477, Q/KJ.J02.39, and literature[1]. a) Standard heat treatment system: 1230℃±10℃×2h + 1260℃±10℃×2h/AC + 1080℃±10℃×4h/AC + 870℃±10℃×20h/AC, where cooling rate can be equivalent to air cooling; b) Modified heat treatment system: 1205℃±10℃×2h + (1235～1245)℃±10℃×2h/AC + 1080℃±10℃×4h/AC + 815℃±10℃×16h/AC, where cooling rate can be equivalent to air cooling.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy DZ408: Excerpted from Q/6S2477 and Q/KJ.J02.39. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 85mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy DZ408: 1312℃～1379℃ (differential thermal analysis)[2].
Density of alloy DZ408: ρ=8.577 g/cm³[2].
Magnetic properties of alloy DZ408: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming process and properties of alloy DZ408: Good castability, can cast directionally solidified blades and other parts with wall thickness as thin as 0.5 mm and complex internal passages.

Parts heat treatment process for alloy DZ408: Same as the standard or modified heat treatment system of the alloy. Net-shape blades should be treated under vacuum or protective atmosphere, cooled with argon.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy DZ408: No special requirements.

Overview of alloy JG4006(IC6A): JG4006A is a directionally solidified columnar grain high-temperature material based on the intermetallic compound Ni3Al, developed from JG4006. While keeping the basic composition unchanged, adding rare earth element (Y) significantly improves high-temperature oxidation resistance and further enhances high-temperature rupture properties and thermal fatigue resistance, while maintaining other mechanical properties at a high level. Suitable for manufacturing gas turbine guide vanes and other high-temperature structural components operating below 1150℃.
Material designation of alloy JG4006(IC6A): JG4006A (IC6A).

Melting and casting process of alloy JG4006(IC6A): Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, directionally solidified castings and test bars are cast using a vacuum directional solidification furnace remelting.
Heat treatment system of alloy JG4006(IC6A): Excerpted from Q/6S 1595, the standard heat treatment system for the alloy is: 1260℃±10℃×10h/AC or OQ.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy JG4006(IC6A): Excerpted from Q/6S 1595. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters typically 70mm～90mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; castings are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of alloy JG4006(IC6A): 1340℃～1410℃[3].
Density of alloy JG4006(IC6A): ρ=7.90 g/cm³[1].
Magnetic properties of alloy JG4006(IC6A): The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of alloy JG4006(IC6A): After standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness is HRC 30～35[1].
Forming process and properties of alloy JG4006(IC6A): The alloy has good castability and can cast complex-shaped hollow blades and other parts.
Parts heat treatment process for alloy JG4006(IC6A): Heat treatment process for parts without surface machining: Under vacuum, 1260℃±10℃×10h/ argon cool.
Surface treatment process of alloy JG4006(IC6A): NiCoCrAlYHf physical vapor deposition overlay coating is applied.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy JG4006(IC6A): No special requirements.

Typical microstructure of alloy JG4006(IC6A): With appropriate Y addition, the microstructure of JG4006A is basically the same as JG4006. That is, besides γ' phase, the as-cast structure contains γ phase and a small amount of boride (Mo₂Ni)B₂. The γ' phase accounts for about 75%～85% (volume fraction) of the alloy, γ phase about 20% (volume fraction), and γ is the main strengthening phase. After treatment at 1260℃±10℃×10h/OQ, the microstructure mainly consists of two regions: interdendritic region and dendrite core region. In the interdendritic region, there are cubic black γ'-Ni₃(Al,Mo) phases of about 0.3μm～0.5μm size and a network of gray γ-Ni solid solution phase; in the dendrite core region, there are γ' phases of about 1μm～5μm size and (γ+γ') two-phase regions similar to the interdendritic region. After the γ strengthening phase transforms into rafted structure perpendicular to the stress axis during primary creep, under temperature and stress, it can maintain good morphology and sufficient quantity stably for a long time without change, ensuring high and stable rupture strength of the material.

Overview of alloy K418B: K418B is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-crystal cast superalloy, a modified version of K418, for use at temperatures below 900℃. Compared to K418, carbon content is reduced, and chromium and molybdenum contents are adjusted. The alloy has good creep, rupture, and thermal fatigue resistance, and significantly improved room temperature plasticity and impact properties, with improved microstructural stability. This good comprehensive performance is advantageous in integral cast turbine applications. Additionally, the alloy is insensitive to casting section size and cooling rate, making it suitable for casting large castings with sections over 130 mm. Main products are precision castings such as turbine blades and integral cast turbines.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy K418B: The alloy has been used to manufacture free turbine rotor blades, turbine nozzle guide vanes, and integral rotors for aero engines. The similar alloy 713LC is widely used abroad, including for aero engine turbine nozzle guide vanes, integral rotors, rotating blades, turbine disks for UAV engines, and integral cast turbines for power generation gas turbines for long-term use. Surface aluminizing (Al) or chromaluminizing (CrAl) of parts can further improve oxidation and corrosion resistance. After long-term stress aging at 700℃~900℃, no σ phase precipitation was found.
Material designation of alloy K418B: K418B (K18B)
Similar designations of alloy K418B: Inconel 713LC or 713LC (USA)

Material technical standards for alloy K418B: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 1: Master Alloys for Ordinary Precision Castings of Superalloys; HB 7728 Specification for Precision Cast Blades of Superalloys for Aviation; HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed-Crystal Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero Engines; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation
Melting and casting process of alloy K418B: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, remelted in a vacuum induction furnace to cast parts and test bars.
Heat treatment system of alloy K418B: Excerpted from HB/Z 140 and literature[1], the alloy is used in the as-cast or heat-treated condition. Standard heat treatment system: 1180℃×2h/AC.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy K418B: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1, HB 7728 and HB 7763. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy and precision castings are supplied in the as-cast condition.
Melting temperature range of alloy K418B: 1288℃~1321℃[2]
Density of alloy K418B: ρ=8.01 g/cm³[2]
Magnetic properties of alloy K418B: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of alloy K418B: As-cast alloy, room temperature hardness is HRC 32～37[2,6].

Forming process and properties of alloy K418B: The alloy has good castability and low hot tearing tendency. Using investment casting, blades with wall thickness less than 1 mm and integral turbine rotors over 130 mm in diameter can be cast.
Weldability of alloy K418B: Electron beam welding, argon arc welding, isothermal solidification diffusion bonding, and friction welding are possible.
Parts heat treatment process for alloy K418B: Castings typically undergo stress relief annealing, specification: (930～950)℃×2h/AC.
Surface treatment process of alloy K418B: The alloy can be aluminized (Al) or chromaluminized (CrAl).
Machining and grinding performance of alloy K418B: The alloy is relatively easy to machine. Carbide tools should be used.

Overview of alloy GH4037: GH4037 is a Ni-Cr base precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy for use at temperatures below 850℃. The alloy contains about ω(Al+Ti) 4% to form γ' precipitation strengthening phase, with significant additions of tungsten and molybdenum for solid solution strengthening, and trace boron and cerium for grain boundary strengthening. This alloy has high thermal strength, good comprehensive properties, and microstructural stability. Suitable for manufacturing gas turbine blades operating at 800℃～850℃. Main products include hot-rolled and forged bars, die-forged turbine blades.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy GH4037: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero engine turbine blades, with good long-term service performance. When ω(C) is between 0.02% and 0.06%, high-carbon alloy performs better than low-carbon; when ω(C) is 0.05%～0.06%, the alloy has the best comprehensive properties; when ω(C) is above 0.06%, the alloy tends to form banded structure with coarse and fine grains. Chip-off during machining of blade blanks is related to silicon content. The alloy shows some notch sensitivity around 700℃.
Material designation of alloy GH4037: GH4037 (GH37).
Similar designations of alloy GH4037: ЭИ617, XH70BMTЮ (Russia).

Material technical standards for alloy GH4037: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; GB/T 14993 Hot-Rolled Bars of Superalloys for Rotating Components; GJB 1953A Specification for Hot-Rolled Bars of Superalloys for Aero Engine Rotating Parts; HB 5189 Bars of Wrought Superalloys for Aviation Blades; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation

Melting process of alloy GH4037: Non-vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable melting process is used.

Heat treatment system of alloy GH4037: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, the standard heat treatment system for hot-rolled bars for rotating parts is: (1170～1180)℃×2h/AC + 1050℃±10℃×4h/slow cool + 800℃±10℃×16h/AC, HBS 341～269.

Product specifications and supply conditions of alloy GH4037: Excerpted from GB/T 14993 and GJB 1953A. Hot-rolled bars and flats d20mm～55mm. Hot-rolled bars and flats are supplied without heat treatment, surface turned or ground.
Melting temperature range of alloy GH4037: 1278℃～1346℃.[2]
Density of alloy GH4037: ρ=8.40 g/cm³[6]
Magnetic properties of alloy GH4037: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Parts heat treatment process for alloy GH4037: Finished blades should undergo stress relief tempering, specification: In argon, 950℃×2h/FC→700℃/AC + 800℃×8h/AC.
Machining and grinding performance of alloy GH4037: No special requirements.

Overview of alloy K417: K417 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed-crystal cast superalloy for use at temperatures below 950℃. The alloy has high aluminum and titanium content, forming an age-hardening phase w(γ') accounting for about 65%～70% of the alloy. The alloy has high strength, good plasticity, low density (7.8 g/cm³), and high specific strength. Main products are precision castings such as turbine blades, guide vanes, and turbocharger rotor wheels.

Application overview and characteristics of alloy K417: The alloy has been used to manufacture gas turbine rotating parts and other high-temperature components. The alloy has low chromium content and relatively poor hot corrosion resistance. When titanium and chromium are near the upper limit and casting parameters are not well controlled, a small amount of acicular σ phase may appear in thick sections after long-term aging at 850℃~900℃.
Material designation of alloy K417: K417 (K17, M17)
Similar designations of alloy K417: IN 100 (USA)

Material technical standards for alloy K417: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials; GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 1: Master Alloys for Ordinary Precision Castings of Superalloys; HB 7728 Specification for Precision Cast Blades of Superalloys for Aviation; HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed-Crystal Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero Engines; HB/Z140 Heat Treatment Processes for Superalloys for Aviation

Melting and casting process of alloy K417: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace, remelted in a vacuum induction furnace, and cast into parts and test bars using investment precision casting with sand filling. Pouring temperature 1400℃~1420℃, mold temperature 780℃~950℃.
Heat treatment system of alloy K417: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, the alloy is used in the as-cast condition.

Alloy K417 product specifications and supply conditions: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1, HB 7728, and HB 7763. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm to 90mm and a length not less than 250mm. The master alloy and precision castings are supplied in the as-cast condition.
Alloy K417 melting temperature range: 1260°C~1340°C[2,4]
Alloy K417 density: ρ=7.8g/cm³[1]
Alloy K417 magnetic properties: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Alloy K417 hardness: The alloy in as-cast condition, room temperature hardness is HRC 30~44[1].

Alloy K417 forming process and properties: Using the investment casting method, thin-walled parts with wall thickness as small as 1mm can be cast, and integral turbines can also be cast. The casting shrinkage rate is approximately 2%.
Alloy K417 weldability: Argon arc surfacing welding can be performed.
Alloy K417 part heat treatment process: Parts are used in the as-cast condition. They can also undergo Al diffusion coating and stress-relief annealing treatment at temperatures below 1120°C.

Alloy K417 surface treatment process: Using solid Al diffusion coating, the alloy can be coated with Al, AlCr, and AlSi; in terms of process, powder packing, low-pressure gas diffusion, and slurry methods can be employed.
Alloy K417 machinability and grinding performance: Requires turning, drilling, milling, planing, and grinding at lower rotational speeds.

Alloy GH4500 overview: GH4500 is a Ni-Cr-Co-based precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy, used at temperatures below 870°C, and can reach 980°C for short-term use. The alloy contains aluminum and titanium to form the γ' precipitation hardening phase, and cobalt, chromium, and molybdenum for solid solution strengthening. The alloy has good high-temperature strength, oxidation resistance, and hot corrosion resistance, suitable for manufacturing rotating turbine components and load-bearing parts of gas turbine engines, as well as forged or cast gas turbine blades. Main products include bars, forgings, plates, wires, and precision castings.

Alloy GH4500 application overview and characteristics: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine stage I and II turbine disks, free turbine disks, labyrinth shafts, and labyrinth seal rings, with good batch production and service performance. Similar grades are widely used abroad, mainly for manufacturing aero-engine turbine blades operating below 750°C, and gas turbine blades. The alloy can be welded using argon arc welding with alloy filler wire; after heat treatment, the weld strength is lower than the base material. To improve the corrosion resistance of turbine blades, parts can be surface-coated with Al diffusion. After high-temperature long-term aging or high-temperature stress aging, a small amount of σ phase precipitates.
Alloy GH4500 material designation: GH4500 (GH500).
Alloy GH4500 similar grades: Udimet500 (USA), Nimonic PK25 (UK), NCK19DAT (France).

Alloy GH4500 material technical standards: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Aeronautical Superalloys Liaoxin 7-0022 Technical Conditions for GH500 Alloy Hot-Rolled and Forged Bars for Load-Bearing Rotating Components
Alloy GH4500 melting process: Vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting process.

Alloy GH4500 heat treatment system: Excerpted from HB/Z140 and literature [8], the standard heat treatment system for bars and disks is: 1120°C±10°C×2h/AC+1080°C±10°C×4h/AC+845°C±5°C×24h/AC+760°C±5°C×16h/AC.

Alloy GH4500 product specifications and supply conditions: Excerpted from Liaoxin 7-0022 and literature [2]. d20mm~180mm hot-rolled and forged bars; d≤355mm octagonal forging blanks; δ3.2mm~38mm medium-thick plates; δ0.5mm~3.2mm thin sheets and strips. Bars are supplied without heat treatment, turned or ground.
Alloy GH4500 melting temperature range: 1288°C~1343°C[1].
Alloy GH4500 density: ρ=8.05g/cm³[1].
Alloy GH4500 magnetic properties: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Alloy GH4500 weldability: The alloy can be welded using argon arc welding with similar filler wire; after heat treatment, the weld strength is lower than the base material.

Alloy GH4500 part heat treatment process: Before heat treatment, surface contaminants such as oil must be removed. The heat treatment process is: load at <850°C, heat to 1120°C±10°C, hold for 2h, air cool; load at 850°C, heat to 1080°C±10°C, hold for 4h, air cool. For small parts, they must be packed in boxes for treatment, e.g., small turbine disks: 845°C±5°C, 24h, cool with the protective box to 400°C, then remove and air cool; 760°C±5°C, 16h, cool with the protective box to 400°C, then remove and air cool.

Alloy GH4500 machinability and grinding performance: The alloy is generally machined after standard heat treatment using low speed and small feed; drilling and turning should be done in the solution-treated state, and grinding after aging.
Alloy GH4500 phase transformation temperature: The dissolution temperature of the γ' phase is 1080°C~1100°C; the dissolution temperature of grain boundary M23C6 carbides is about 1130°C[9].

Alloy DD402 overview: DD402 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened single-crystal superalloy, belonging to the first generation of single-crystal alloys. Compared with the foreign first-generation single-crystal alloy PWA1480, this alloy contains less tantalum, making it more affordable. The alloy has a simple composition, low density, high creep strength and fatigue resistance, good single-crystal castability, a wide solution treatment temperature range, good microstructural stability, environmental performance, and coating performance. Suitable for manufacturing turbine blades operating below 1050°C and other high-temperature components.

Alloy DD402 application overview and characteristics: The alloy has been used to manufacture turbine blades for advanced aero-engines. The similar alloy CMSX-2 has been successfully used abroad in engines such as Arriel. The alloy has a high ω(γ') phase content of up to 68%, with an average γ' phase size of 0.45μm. Due to the small γ/γ' misfit, the alloy exhibits high creep strength[1]. The absence of vanadium, low molybdenum, appropriate tungsten and tantalum, and optimal Ti/Al ratio ensure good environmental and coating performance[1]. After aging at 850°C×3000h, no TCP phase precipitates.
Alloy DD402 material designation: DD402.
Alloy DD402 similar grades: CMSX-2 (USA), NWC8TA6AK (France).

Alloy DD402 material technical standards: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GJB5512.3 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 3: Cast Superalloy Master Alloys for Single-Crystal Castings HB7762 Specification for Directionally Solidified Columnar Crystal and Single-Crystal Superalloy Ingots for Aero-Engines HB 7728 Specification for Precision Cast Turbine Blades of Aeronautical Superalloys HB/Z140 Heat Treatment Processes for Aeronautical Superalloys Q/GYB 05047 DD402 Alloy Single-Crystal Turbine Blade Castings

Alloy DD402 melting and casting process: Vacuum induction melting of master alloy, remelting in a vacuum directional solidification furnace, and single-crystal parts or test bars are manufactured using spiral grain selection or seed crystal methods.
Alloy DD402 heat treatment system: Excerpted from HB/Z140, the standard heat treatment system for the alloy is: 1315°C×3h/AC+1080°C×6h/AC+870°C×20h/AC.

Alloy DD402 product specifications and supply conditions: Excerpted from GJB5512.3, HB7762, and Q/GYB05047. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm to 90mm and a length not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; single-crystal blades are supplied after solution treatment or standard heat treatment.
Alloy DD402 melting temperature range: 1330°C~1382°C[2].
Alloy DD402 density: ρ=8.67g/cm³[2.3].
Alloy DD402 magnetic properties: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Alloy DD402 forming process and properties: The alloy has good castability and can be used to manufacture various complex-shaped single-crystal turbine blades.

Alloy DD402 part heat treatment process: Heat treatment of net-shape blades should be performed in a vacuum gas quenching furnace. The vacuum heat treatment system is: 1315°C×3h/argon cooling+1080°C×6h/argon cooling+870°C±10°C×20h/argon cooling, with an argon cooling rate greater than 300°C/min.
Alloy DD402 surface treatment process: Parts are coated with multi-element coatings via low-pressure plasma spraying according to specialized processes.
Alloy DD402 machinability and grinding performance: No special requirements.

Alloy GH5188 overview: GH5188 is a Co-Ni-Cr-based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy, used at temperatures below 1100°C. The alloy contains ω(W)14% tungsten for solid solution strengthening, providing good comprehensive properties; high chromium content and trace lanthanum provide good high-temperature oxidation resistance. The alloy has good hot and cold workability and weldability, suitable for manufacturing aero-engine parts requiring high strength below 980°C and oxidation resistance below 1100°C. Main products include cold-rolled sheets, hot-rolled plates, bars, forgings, and wires.

Alloy GH5188 application overview and characteristics: The alloy has been used to manufacture high-temperature components such as combustion chamber liners and guide vanes in aero-engines, with good batch production and service performance. Similar alloys abroad are also widely used in high-temperature components of gas turbines and missiles, such as combustion chambers, tailpipes, and heat exchangers in the nuclear industry. During the manufacturing process of sheet parts, any process (e.g., heat treatment, welding) must prevent carburization and copper contamination to avoid damaging mechanical properties and corrosion resistance. The corrosion resistance of the alloy sheets is better than that of GH3536 alloy.
Alloy GH5188 material designation: GH5188(GH188).
Alloy GH5188 similar grades: Haynes Alloy No.188(HA188), UNSR 30188(USA), KCN22W(France).

Alloy GH5188 material technical standards: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GJB 3317A Specification for Hot-Rolled Plates of Aeronautical Superalloys HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Aeronautical Superalloys Q/5B 4002 GH188 Alloy Cold-Rolled Strips Q/5B 4003 GH188 Alloy Cold-Rolled Sheets Q/5B 4014 GH188 Alloy Forgings Q/5B 4022 GH188 Alloy Bars Q/5B 4057 GH188 Superalloy Cold-Drawn Welding Wires
Alloy GH5188 melting process: Vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction melting + vacuum arc remelting processes.

Alloy GH5188 heat treatment system: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, the standard heat treatment systems for various products are: a) Forged bars and rings: 1180°C±10°C, cool at a rate ensuring qualified mechanical properties, HB≤293; b) Cold-rolled sheets and strips: 1165°C~1230°C, rapid air cooling, holding time determined by product cross-sectional thickness; c) Hot-rolled plates: 1170°C~1190°C, air cooling, HV≤282.
Alloy GH5188 product specifications and supply conditions: Excerpted from GJB 3317A, Q/5B 4002, Q/5B 4003, Q/5B 4014, Q/5B 4022, and Q/5B 4057.
Alloy GH5188 melting temperature range: 1300°C~1360°C[1].
Alloy GH5188 density: ρ=9.09g/cm³[2].
Alloy GH5188 magnetic properties: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Alloy GH5188 part heat treatment process: Same as the standard heat treatment for each product type.

Alloy GH5188 surface treatment process: The oxide film formed on the alloy surface after heating adheres strongly to the substrate. Methods for cleaning stainless steel scale are not easily applicable; instead, a composite alkali-acid pickling process should be used to remove the oxide scale.
Alloy GH5188 machinability and grinding performance: No special requirements.

Alloy GH2747 overview: GH2747 is an Fe-Ni-Cr-based precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy, used in the solution-treated state, with long-term service temperatures of 1100°C~1250°C and short-term use up to 1300°C. The alloy has high strength, good microstructural stability, excellent oxidation and corrosion resistance. The alloy has good weldability and can be welded using various processes. Main products include bars, plates, tubes, wires, and forgings.

Alloy GH2747 application overview and characteristics: The alloy has been used to manufacture high-temperature oxidation-resistant components such as combustion chambers and afterburners in aero and aerospace engines, as well as heat-resistant parts like furnace rollers, transmission devices, and thermocouple sleeves in industrial applications. It is particularly suitable for high-temperature oxidation-resistant equipment parts in petrochemical, nuclear, and metallurgical fields. The material cost of the alloy is lower than that of similar superalloys. By increasing chromium content, raising aluminum content, and adding trace rare earth elements, the oxidation resistance of the alloy is greatly improved at 1000°C~1300°C.
Alloy GH2747 material designation: GH2747(GH747).
Alloy GH2747 similar grades: ЭП747(Russia).

Alloy GH2747 material technical standards: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Q/GYB 511 GH2747 Alloy Hot-Rolled and Forged Bars Q/GYB 512 GH2747 Alloy Cold-Drawn (Rolled) Seamless Tubes
Alloy GH2747 melting process: Vacuum induction melting, or vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting processes.

Alloy GH2747 heat treatment system: Excerpted from Q/GYB 511 and Q/GYB 512, the alloy is used in the solution-treated state. The standard heat treatment system for hot-rolled, forged bars and cold-drawn (rolled) seamless tubes is: 1000°C~1200°C/AC, holding time depending on material thickness.
Alloy GH2747 product specifications and supply conditions: Excerpted from Q/GYB 511 and Q/GYB 512.
Alloy GH2747 density: ρ=7.78g/cm³[1].

Alloy GH2747 forming process and properties: The ingot loading temperature should not exceed 1000°C, forging heating temperature 1000°C~1200°C, hot rolling starting temperature not lower than 1000°C, finishing rolling temperature not lower than 900°C; cold rolling of seamless tubes, single-pass maximum deformation ≤80%, intermediate annealing treatment temperature ≥1000°C, air cooling.

Alloy GH2747 weldability: The alloy can be welded well using all welding methods. Argon arc welding, spot welding, seam welding, and electron beam welding yield excellent results. When welding with dissimilar materials, base metal wire or similar alloy filler can be used.
Alloy GH2747 part heat treatment process: Same as the standard heat treatment system for each product type.
Alloy GH2747 machinability and grinding performance: No special requirements.

Alloy GH4220 overview: GH4220 is a Ni-Co-Cr-based precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy, used at temperatures of 900°C~950°C. The alloy contains relatively high aluminum and titanium to form the γ' precipitation hardening phase, ω(γ') can reach over 40%. Cobalt, chromium, tungsten, and molybdenum are added for solid solution strengthening, and trace cerium, boron, and magnesium for grain boundary strengthening. The alloy has high high-temperature strength and ductility, with good comprehensive properties. Suitable for manufacturing gas turbine blades operating at 900°C~950°C. Main products include hot-rolled materials for rotating components and forged turbine blades.

Alloy GH4220 application overview and characteristics: The alloy has been used to manufacture stage I turbine blades for aero-engines, with good batch production and service performance. Through special curved grain boundary heat treatment, the precipitation type and morphology of second phases at grain boundaries are controlled to form curved grain boundaries. This improves the match between grain boundary and grain interior strength, reduces grain boundary brittleness, and significantly enhances high-temperature ductility and creep strength[9].
Alloy GH4220 material designation: GH4220(GH220).
Alloy GH4220 similar grades: эΠ220BД, XH51BMTЮКФP-BД(Russia).

Alloy GH4220 material technical standards: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GJB 1953A Specification for Hot-Rolled Bars of Superalloys for Aero-Engine Rotating Components HB 5284 Technical Conditions for GH220 Alloy Hot Bars for Aviation HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Aeronautical Superalloys
Alloy GH4220 melting process: Vacuum induction melting + vacuum arc remelting, or vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting processes.

Alloy GH4220 heat treatment system: Excerpted from HB/Z 140 and literature [9], for hot-rolled bars for rotating components: a) Standard heat treatment system: 1220°C±10°C×4h/AC+1050°C±10°C×4h/AC+950°C±10°C×2h/AC, HB 341~285; b) Curved grain boundary heat treatment system: 1220°C×4h/(3~7)min→1100°C/AC+1050°C×4h/AC+950°C×2h/AC.

Alloy GH4220 product specifications and supply conditions: Excerpted from GJB 1953A and HB 5284. d20mm~55mm hot-rolled bars. Bars are supplied without heat treatment, fully ground or turned; forged blades are supplied after part heat treatment.
Alloy GH4220 melting temperature range: 1330°C~1360°C[8].
Alloy GH4220 density: ρ=8.36g/cm³.
Alloy GH4220 magnetic properties: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Alloy GH4220 hardness: Hot-rolled bars after standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness HBS 341~285.

Alloy GH4220 part heat treatment process: The alloy parts adopt curved grain boundary heat treatment systems. There are three main curved grain boundary heat treatment processes[8]: a) Isothermal curved grain boundary heat treatment: 1220°C×4h/AC to 1070°C±30°C, transfer to 1070°C furnace ×2.5h/AC+950°C×2h/AC. b) Slow cooling curved grain boundary heat treatment: 1220°C×4h/ cool at (3~7)°C/min to 1100°C/AC+1050°C×4h/AC+950°C×2h/AC. c) Re-solution curved grain boundary heat treatment: 1220°C×4h/FC to 1050°C×4h/AC+1125°C×4h/AC+950°C×2h/AC.
Alloy GH4220 machinability and grinding performance: The alloy is easy to machine.

Alloy GH4220 phase transformation temperature: γ' phase precipitation temperature about 1110°C, dissolution temperature 1140°C~1170°C; M6C carbide precipitation temperature about 1160°C, dissolution temperature 1160°C~1170°C; M3B2 boride dissolution temperature about above 1220°C[8].

Alloy K487 overview: K487 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed crystal casting superalloy. The alloy contains ω(Al+Ti) 3%~4%, forming ω(γ') 10%~20% strengthening phase, giving the alloy good high-temperature strength and weldability; the alloy contains ω(W+Mo) 13%~15%, further improving thermal strength; the alloy has ω(Cr) content of 17%~20%, providing good hot corrosion resistance. The alloy has high tensile and creep strength, good casting processability, and weldability. Suitable for manufacturing components for aero and aerospace engines for short-term use at 700°C~850°C and long-term use at 700°C~750°C.

Alloy K487 application overview and characteristics: The alloy has been used to manufacture precision castings for the inner tailpipe of small turbofan engines, with good batch production and service performance. The alloy has good weldability and can be welded using argon arc welding, electron beam welding, etc.
Alloy K487 material designation: K487.
Alloy K487 similar grades: Эπ487 (Russia).
Alloy K487 material technical standards: Q/GYB 05039 K487 Master Alloy for Casting Q/GYB 05114 K487 Alloy Castings
Alloy K487 melting and casting process: Vacuum induction melting of master alloy, vacuum induction remelting, investment casting to pour castings and test bars.
Alloy K487 heat treatment system: Excerpted from Q/GYB 05039 and Q/GYB 05114, the standard heat treatment system for the alloy is: 1120°C±15°C×4h/AC+900°C±20°C×(5~10)h/AC.

Alloy K487 product specifications and supply conditions: Excerpted from Q/GYB 05039 and Q/GYB 05114. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm to 90mm and a length not less than 250mm. The master alloy and precision castings are supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings can also be supplied after heat treatment.
Alloy K487 melting temperature range: 1304°C~1337°C[1].
Alloy K487 density: ρ=8.486g/cm³[1].
Alloy K487 magnetic properties: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Alloy K487 forming process and properties: The alloy has good castability and can be used to manufacture precision castings with wall thickness of 2.5mm via investment casting.
Alloy K487 weldability: Can be welded using argon arc welding, electron beam welding, etc.
Alloy K487 part heat treatment process: Same as the standard heat treatment system for the alloy.
Alloy K487 machinability and grinding performance: No special requirements.

Alloy MGH2757 overview: MGH2757 is an iron-based oxide dispersion strengthened (ODS) superalloy produced by mechanical alloying (MA) technology, used at temperatures below 700°C. The alloy contains nano-sized (≤50nm) Y₂O₃ powder with high thermal and chemical stability. The alloy has excellent high-temperature creep strength and resistance to neutron irradiation swelling, as well as good compatibility with liquid sodium and nuclear fuel. Suitable for manufacturing high-temperature, radiation-resistant components. Main products include hot-rolled and forged bars, cold-rolled thin-walled tubes, etc.
Alloy MGH2757 material designation: MGH2757(MGH957).
Alloy MGH2757 similar grades: MA957 (USA), DT2203Y05 (Europe), PNC-ODS (Japan).

Alloy MGH2757 melting and main manufacturing process: Raw materials include elemental powders, alloy powders, and Y₂O₃ rare earth oxide powder. Mechanical alloying is performed under inert gas protection to produce alloy powder, which is then solidified via hot extrusion or hot isostatic pressing, and formed by hot or cold working.

Alloy MGH2757 product specifications and supply conditions: d10mm~50mm hot-rolled and forged bars; outer diameter (6~5.2)mm, wall thickness 0.4mm, length 2000mm cold-rolled thin-walled tubes. Bars are supplied in hot-rolled or forged condition; cold-rolled thin-walled tubes are supplied after high-temperature recrystallization annealing treatment.
Alloy MGH2757 magnetic properties: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Alloy MGH2757 weldability: Electron beam welding can be used with welding current 5mA, sample rotation speed 21s/n, achieving weld strength up to 70% of the thin-walled tube base material strength[1].
Alloy MGH2757 part heat treatment process: Same as the standard heat treatment system for each product type.
Alloy MGH2757 machinability and grinding performance: No special requirements.

Alloy JG1204(TAC-3D) overview: JG1204 is a Ti₂AlNb-based intermetallic compound high-temperature structural material strengthened by tantalum solid solution. Components can work long-term below 750°C and short-term at 1000°C. Compared to general Ti2AlNb-based alloys, this alloy has about (100~200)MPa higher strength at room and high temperatures, and the service temperature is about 200°C higher than traditional high-temperature titanium alloys, with weight reduction of about 30% compared to superalloys at similar service temperatures. The alloy has excellent high-temperature mechanical properties and oxidation resistance, as well as good hot workability. Main products include bars, plates, and rings.
Alloy JG1204(TAC-3D) material designation: JG1204(TAC-3D).
Alloy JG1204(TAC-3D) melting process: Vacuum consumable arc furnace multiple melting process.

Alloy JG1204(TAC-3D) product specifications and supply conditions: Excerpted from Q/GYB 542 and literature [1]. d10mm~300mm bars; 02mm~20mm hot-rolled plates; rings of various specifications. Bars and rings are supplied after solution + aging treatment, directly or after surface machining; hot-rolled plates are supplied after solution + aging + sandblasting + straightening + edge trimming; cold-rolled plates and foils are supplied after cold rolling or annealing + edge trimming.
Alloy JG1204(TAC-3D) density: ρ=5.95g/cm³[1].
Alloy JG1204(TAC-3D) magnetic properties: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Alloy JG1204(TAC-3D) oxidation resistance: In air medium, the oxidation rate after 100h at 750°C is 0.58g/(m²·h)[1].
Alloy JG1204(TAC-3D) machinability and grinding performance: No special requirements.

Alloy GH2130 overview: GH2130 is an Fe-Ni-Cr-based precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy, with aluminum and titanium added to form aging precipitation hardening phases, and boron and cerium added to purify and strengthen grain boundaries. The alloy can be used long-term below 800°C and short-term at 900°C. It has high thermal strength and good hot workability. Main products include bars, flats, and rounds.

Alloy GH2130 application overview and characteristics: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine blades, marine power turbine supercharger turbines, aerospace fasteners, etc. After fine-grain process + direct aging treatment, the alloy can be used to manufacture disks with high yield and fatigue properties at 500°C~700°C and long-life rotor blades. When used above 700°C, the creep and rupture limits begin to decrease; after long-term aging, Laves phase precipitates, reducing room temperature impact toughness. When used at 800°C, the surface can be aluminized to improve oxidation and corrosion resistance.
Alloy GH2130 material designation: GH2130(GH130).

Alloy GH2130 material technical standards: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GB/T 14993 Hot-Rolled Bars of Superalloys for Rotating Components

Alloy GH2130 melting process: Vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting processes.

Alloy GH2130 heat treatment system: Excerpted from GB/T 14993, literature [8], and literature [9]: a) Standard heat treatment for hot-rolled bars, flats, rounds: 1180°C±10°C×(1.5~2)h/AC+1050°C±10°C×4h/AC+800°C±10°C×16h/AC; b) Fine-grain process + direct aging: After controlled hot deformation to obtain fine grain structure + 830°C×8h/AC+700°C×16h/AC.

Alloy GH2130 product specifications and supply conditions: d20mm~55mm hot-rolled bars; round forgings of various sizes. Hot-rolled bars are supplied in hot-rolled condition, may be turned or ground; round forgings are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Alloy GH2130 melting temperature range: 1348°C~1380°C[6].
Alloy GH2130 density: ρ=8.20g/cm³[1].
Alloy GH2130 part heat treatment process: 1180°C±10°C×1.5h/AC+1050°C±10°C×4h/AC+800°C±10°C×16h/AC.

Alloy GH2130 surface treatment process: To eliminate residual stress on finished parts, argon protection can be used, with supplementary aging: 1000°C±10°C×(10~15)min/AC+800°C±10°C×4h/AC. When used at 800°C, the surface can be aluminized to improve oxidation and corrosion resistance.

Alloy GH2130 simulation test performance: To compare the thermal fatigue performance of GH2130 alloy and GH4037 alloy, turbine blades were made from these materials for single-tube thermal shock tests. GH2130 blades were surface-aluminized with a coating about 7μm, while GH4037 blades were unprotected. Test conditions: fuel quantity 29 divisions (fuel flow meter scale), atmospheric pressure 2.48Pa, fuel flow rate 0.5kg/s, fuel temperature 900°C, blade measured temperature 790°C~810°C, distance from blade to single tube 300mm. Cycle: burn for 70s, stop for 15s, water spray cooling for 1s, stop for 15s, total cycle 111s. Results: After 502 cycles, GH2130 blades showed a 4mm long crack at the trailing edge 96mm from the root. After 629 cycles, GH4037 blades showed a 5mm long crack at 117mm from the root.

Alloy GH2130 phase transformation temperature: During heating, γ' begins to precipitate at 600°C~700°C, with peak precipitation at 850°C, dissolving into the matrix after 1100°C. γ' begins to dissolve at 830°C~940°C. During cooling, precipitation starts at 900°C~875°C; Laves phase precipitation peak at 950°C; secondary M3B2 precipitation peak at 900°C.

Alloy DZ4951 overview: DZ4951 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened directionally solidified columnar crystal superalloy with high melting point and low density, used at temperatures below 1100°C. The alloy has stable microstructure, good medium to high temperature mechanical properties, and thermal fatigue resistance. It has high oxidation resistance, hot corrosion resistance, cost-effectiveness, and excellent castability. Suitable for manufacturing gas turbine engine guide vanes operating around 1100°C and turbine blades operating at 1050°C.

Alloy DZ4951 application overview and characteristics: The alloy has been used to manufacture gas turbine engine guide vanes and turbine blades. The alloy has good microstructural stability; no TCP phase precipitates after long-term aging at 900°C~1100°C. Using powder packing or low-pressure aluminizing processes, aluminide or AlSi coatings can be formed on the blade surface to improve oxidation and hot corrosion resistance.
Alloy DZ4951 material designation: DZ4951 (DZ951).
Alloy DZ4951 material technical standards: Q/KJ.J02.30 DZ951 Cast Superalloy Ingot Specification

Alloy DZ4951 melting and casting process: Vacuum induction melting of master alloy, remelting and pouring in a vacuum directional solidification furnace to produce directionally solidified castings and test bars.
Alloy DZ4951 heat treatment system: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.30, the standard heat treatment system for the alloy is: 1220°C×4h/AC+1050°C×4h/AC+870°C×24h/AC.

Alloy DZ4951 product specifications and supply conditions: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.30. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm to 90mm and a length not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; directionally solidified castings are supplied after solution treatment or standard heat treatment.
Alloy DZ4951 melting temperature range: 1352°C~1388°C.
Alloy DZ4951 density: ρ=8.17g/cm³[1]
Alloy DZ4951 magnetic properties: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Alloy DZ4951 hardness: After standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness is HRC 36.0~38.0[1].
Alloy DZ4951 impact properties: After standard heat treatment, longitudinal room temperature impact toughness of directionally solidified test bars ɑku=680kJ/m²[1].
Alloy DZ4951 forming process and properties: Using directional solidification, thin-walled parts with wall thickness less than 1mm can be cast. Casting shrinkage rate is approximately 2%.
Alloy DZ4951 weldability: Brazing welding can be performed; B-Ni55NbCoWCrAlSiMoTi(C）-S brazing filler metal is recommended.

Alloy DZ4951 part heat treatment process: Same as the standard heat treatment system for the alloy; parts can also be used in the as-cast condition. Aluminizing and stress-relief annealing treatment system: 1100°C×4h/AC.
Alloy DZ4951 surface treatment process: Using powder packing or low-pressure aluminizing processes, aluminide or AlSi coatings are formed on the alloy surface.
Alloy DZ4951 machinability and grinding performance: Can be turned, drilled, milled, planed, and ground at lower rotational speeds.

Alloy JG4010(IC10) overview: JG4010 is a directionally solidified columnar crystal high-temperature material based on the intermetallic compound Ni₃Al, independently developed in China, used at temperatures below 1100°C. The alloy has high high-temperature creep resistance, good thermal fatigue resistance, and stable microstructure at high temperatures. It has good oxidation and corrosion resistance. The alloy contains no titanium but ω(Hf) 1.5%, with good castability, allowing directional solidification of large-flange, thin-walled (0.6mm), complex internal cavity guide blades. Main products include gas turbine guide vanes and other high-temperature components.

Alloy JG4010(IC10) application overview and characteristics: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine gas turbine guide vanes. The alloy has stable microstructure at high temperatures; no TCP phase precipitates after long-term aging at 950°C×3000h. Parts coated with MCrAlY physical vapor deposition overlay coatings and TBC coatings exhibit good service performance.
Alloy JG4010(IC10) material designation: JG4010(IC10).
Alloy JG4010(IC10) material technical standards: Q/6S 1613 IC10 Alloy Ingot Specification

Alloy JG4010(IC10) melting and casting process: Vacuum induction melting of master alloy, remelting and pouring in a vacuum directional solidification furnace to produce investment cast directionally solidified castings and test bars. Pouring temperature 1480°C~1550°C, mold temperature 1480°C~1530°C, withdrawal rate (2.5~7)mm/min.
Alloy JG4010(IC10) heat treatment system: Excerpted from Q/6S 1613, the standard heat treatment system for the alloy is: 1180°C×2h→1265°C×2h/AC+1050°C×4h/AC.

Alloy JG4010(IC10) product specifications and supply conditions: Excerpted from Q/6S 1613. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 70mm to 90mm and a length not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; castings are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Alloy JG4010(IC10) melting temperature range: 1337°C~1370°C (differential thermal analysis)[1].
Alloy JG4010(IC10) density: ρ=8.29g/cm³[1].
Alloy JG4010(IC10) magnetic properties: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Alloy JG4010(IC10) forming process and properties: The alloy has good castability and can be integrally cast into directionally solidified blades and other parts with wall thickness as small as 0.6mm, large flanges, and complex internal cavities, as well as complex equiaxed crystal guide parts.
Alloy JG4010(IC10) part heat treatment process: For parts without surface machining (e.g., blades), heat treatment should be performed under argon protection or vacuum.
Alloy JG4010(IC10) surface treatment process: MCrAlY physical vapor deposition overlay coatings and TBC coatings can be applied.
Alloy JG4010(IC10) machinability and grinding performance: No special requirements.

Alloy DZ438G overview: DZ438G is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened directionally solidified columnar crystal superalloy, developed based on K438G alloy. Compared to K438G, this alloy has about 45°C higher service temperature, higher thermal strength, and more stable microstructure. The alloy also has excellent hot corrosion resistance, suitable for manufacturing turbine blades and other high-temperature components operating below 900°C in hot corrosive environments, especially for gas turbine blades operating long-term in marine atmospheres.

Alloy DZ438G application overview and characteristics: The alloy blades have passed long-term bench tests for shipboard gas generators and various operational condition tests, with broad application prospects. Parts require AlSi coating to further improve hot corrosion resistance. After long-term aging at 850°C×500h, no σ brittle phase precipitates.
Alloy DZ438G material designation: DZ438G (DZ38G).
Alloy DZ438G similar grades: IN738 (USA).

Alloy DZ438G material technical standards: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GJB5512.2 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 2: Cast Superalloy Master Alloys for Directionally Solidified Columnar Crystal Castings SGDZ 38G DZ38G Alloy Ingot (Provisional)
Alloy DZ438G melting process: Vacuum induction melting of master alloy, remelting and pouring in a vacuum directional solidification furnace to produce directionally solidified test plates and parts.
Alloy DZ438G heat treatment system: Excerpted from GJB5512.2 and SGDZ38G, the standard heat treatment system for the alloy is: 1190°C±10°C×2h/AC+1090°C±10°C×2h/AC+850°C×2h/AC.

Alloy DZ438G product specifications and supply conditions: Excerpted from GJB5512.2 and SGDZ38G. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 70mm to 90mm and a length not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Alloy DZ438G melting temperature range: 1242°C~1332°C (metallographic-probe method)[4].
Alloy DZ438G density: ρ=8.10g/cm³[1]
Alloy DZ438G magnetic properties: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Alloy DZ438G forming process and properties: The alloy has good directional solidification properties and can be cast into directionally solidified turbine blades with good columnar grain growth, approximately 150mm long, 50mm wide, edge thickness 1mm, with low cracking tendency.

Alloy DZ438G part heat treatment process: Same as the standard heat treatment system for the alloy. During heating and cooling, parts should be placed flat and single-layered to avoid deformation. Heating process: load at 700°C, heat at 200°C/h to specified temperature.
Alloy DZ438G surface treatment process: Parts finally undergo slurry method heat treatment for AlSi coating at 850°C for 24h, which can be combined with aging heat treatment.
Alloy DZ438G machinability and grinding performance: No special requirements.

Alloy DZ438G typical microstructure: In standard heat-treated condition, the alloy microstructure consists of γ solid solution, γ' phase, TiC, and small amounts of M₂₃C₆ carbide. The γ' phase exists in two forms: fine γ' precipitates and dispersed small blocks of (γ+γ') eutectic. TiC is distributed as fine polygonal particles; small amounts of M₂₃C₆ mainly precipitate during aging at 850°C, dispersed at grain boundaries and around TiC and (γ+γ') eutectic. After long-term aging at 850°C×100h, no σ brittle phase precipitates.

Alloy K213 overview: K213 is an iron-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed crystal casting superalloy, mainly strengthened by aluminum and titanium forming γ' precipitation phase, with certain chromium and tungsten for solid solution strengthening, and appropriate boron for grain boundary strengthening. The alloy has good castability, good comprehensive properties, and microstructural stability below 750°C. Mainly used for manufacturing supercharger turbines, gas turbine, and flue gas turbine moving and stationary blades operating below 750°C.

Alloy K213 application overview and characteristics: The alloy has been used to manufacture radial flow supercharger turbines, axial flow supercharger blades, gas turbine, and flue gas turbine moving and stationary blades for long-term operation below 750°C, with good batch production and service performance. The alloy has good castability and microstructural stability; only trace Laves phase precipitates after long-term aging at 700°C×2000h.
Alloy K213 material designation: K213(K13).
Alloy K213 similar grades: 9M787JI, BK36-JI3 (Russia).

Alloy K213 material technical standards: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 1: Cast Superalloy Master Alloys for Ordinary Precision Castings Q/GYB 502 K213 Alloy Ingot Q/GYB05090 K213 Alloy Flue Gas Turbine Blade Castings Q/GYB 05091 K213 and K418 Alloy Radial Flow Supercharger Turbine Castings
Alloy K213 melting and casting process: Vacuum induction melting of master alloy, vacuum or non-vacuum induction remelting, investment casting to produce parts and test bars.
Alloy K213 heat treatment system: Excerpted from GJB5512.1, Q/GYB502, Q/GYB05090, and Q/GYB 05091. The standard heat treatment system for the alloy is: 1100°C±10°C×4h/AC.

Alloy K213 product specifications and supply conditions: Excerpted from GJB5512.1, Q/GYB 502, Q/GYB 05090, and Q/GYB 05091. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm to 90mm and a length not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Alloy K213 melting temperature range: 1324°C~1361°C[1]
Alloy K213 density: ρ=8.14g/cm³[1]
Alloy K213 hardness: After standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness is HBS 325[1].
Alloy K213 forming process and properties: The alloy has good castability, average linear shrinkage 1.44%, average volumetric shrinkage 3.81%.

Alloy K213 weldability: The alloy has good weldability and can be friction welded with materials like 40Cr and 18CrNiWA. Surface defects on static precision castings can be repaired using HGH2130 alloy filler wire.
Alloy K213 part heat treatment process: Same as the standard heat treatment system for the alloy; net-shape precision castings should be treated in inert atmosphere.
Alloy K213 machinability and grinding performance: The alloy has good machinability.

Alloy K640 overview: K640 is a cobalt-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed crystal casting superalloy, can be used long-term at 950°C and short-term at 1050°C. The alloy has good thermal fatigue resistance, moderate stress levels; good oxidation and wear resistance, strong sulfide resistance. The alloy has excellent casting fluidity and can be cast into complex hollow blades. Main product is gas turbine guide vanes.

Alloy K640 application overview and characteristics: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine guide vanes, with good batch production and service performance. The alloy has poor weldability and is difficult to machine. After long-term use at 700°C~900°C, the tensile ductility at 20°C is very low[2]. The alloy has stable microstructure during long-term aging at 800°C, with fine dispersed carbides, no plate-like or TCP phase precipitation[1].
Alloy K640 material designation: K640 (K40).
Alloy K640 similar grades: Stellite 31 (USA), KC25NW (France).

Alloy K640 material technical standards: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Intermetallic Materials Part 1: Cast Superalloy Master Alloys for Ordinary Precision Castings HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed Crystal Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero-Engines HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Processes for Aeronautical Superalloys

Alloy K640 melting and casting process: Vacuum induction melting or arc furnace melting for master alloy; for vacuum induction melting, refining temperature 1580°C, pouring temperature 1460°C, with argon pressure 5000Pa~7000Pa during pouring. Vacuum induction melting or non-vacuum arc tilting furnace remelting, investment casting to pour parts and test bars, pouring temperature 1450°C~1490°C, mold temperature below 850°C; for vacuum pouring, argon pressure 5000Pa~7000Pa is required[1].

Alloy K640 heat treatment system: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, the alloy is used in the as-cast condition. Excerpted from literature [1], the alloy is used in the as-cast condition. It can also undergo stabilization treatment after casting: 1175°C×30min/AC (neutral atmosphere protection).

Alloy K640 product specifications and supply conditions: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1 and HB 7763. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm to 90mm and a length not less than 250mm. The master alloy and precision castings are supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings can also be supplied after stabilization treatment.
Alloy K640 melting temperature range: Melting point 1260°C, solidification temperature range 1415°C~1260°C[2].
Alloy K640 electrical resistivity: 20°C, ρ=97μΩ·cm[2].
Alloy K640 specific heat capacity: 20°C, c=98J/(kg·℃)[2].
Alloy K640 density: ρ=8.6g/cm³[2].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K640: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Fatigue properties of Alloy K640: For samples taken from cast blanks, after 10^8 cycles (f=120 cycles/s), the fatigue limit σ=±386MPa at 649℃; the fatigue limit σ=±321MPa at 816℃.

Forming process and performance of Alloy K640: The casting performance of the alloy is superior to austenitic stainless steel. Investment casting can be used to cast thin-walled hollow blades and other parts.

Welding performance of Alloy K640: Welding the alloy is relatively difficult. It should be treated at 1175℃×30min/AC before welding. Welding is performed without filler metal in a vacuum or neutral atmosphere.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy K640: Parts are used in the as-cast condition. After casting, stabilization treatment can be performed: 1175℃, holding for 30min, neutral atmosphere protection, air cooling.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K640: The alloy is relatively difficult to machine.

Typical microstructure of Alloy K640: The as-cast microstructure consists of γ austenite, skeletal M7C3 type carbides, and a very small amount of M6C and M23C6 type carbides. Among them, ω(M7C3) accounts for about 5.3% of the alloy. After long-term aging at 750℃～800℃, M7C3 decomposes, precipitating fine dispersed M23C6, with no precipitation of harmful phases.

Overview of Alloy DZ422: DZ422 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening directionally solidified columnar crystal superalloy. Its operating temperature is below 1050℃, and it is one of the alloys with the highest performance level in its class in China. The alloy has good comprehensive properties at medium and high temperatures as well as excellent thermal fatigue resistance. The alloy contains 1.5% ω(Hf), which improves the transverse strength of the alloy and gives it good casting performance. It can be cast into no-allowance directionally solidified hollow blades with complex internal cavities and wall thicknesses as small as 0.5mm. It is suitable for manufacturing gas turbine rotor blades, guide vanes, and other high-temperature parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy DZ422: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine gas turbine rotor blades working below 1000℃ and guide vanes working below 1050℃, with good performance. Similar foreign alloys are widely used to manufacture various advanced aviation engine rotor blades and guide vanes. The surface of the parts can be treated with aluminizing or AlSi coating for protection, which can significantly improve the alloy's oxidation resistance and hot corrosion resistance. After long-term aging at 850℃～950℃, a small amount of μ phase precipitates.
Material grade of Alloy DZ422: DZ422 (DZ22).
Similar grade to Alloy DZ422: PWA1422 (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy DZ422: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; HB 7762 Specification for directionally solidified columnar and single crystal superalloy ingots for aviation engines; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.

Melting process of Alloy DZ422: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum directional solidification furnace is used for remelting and pouring directionally solidified test bars and parts.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy DZ422: Excerpted from HB/Z140, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1205℃±10℃×2h/AC + 870℃±10℃×32h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy DZ422: Excerpted from HB 7762. The master alloy is cast into d75mm～90mm round long bars, with a length of about 1m. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and castings are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy DZ422: Incipient melting temperature 1230℃～1240℃ (metallographic method); Melting temperature 1307℃～1366℃ (differential thermal analysis method) [2].
Density of Alloy DZ422: ρ=8.56g/cm³ [2].
Magnetic properties of Alloy DZ422: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy DZ422: After standard heat treatment, the room temperature hardness of the alloy is HBS 395～385 [1].

Forming process and performance of Alloy DZ422: Good casting performance. Investment precision casting can be used to cast thin-walled hollow directionally solidified turbine blades and other parts with complex internal cavities and wall thicknesses as small as 0.5mm.
Welding performance of Alloy DZ422: Vacuum brazing can be performed, and the performance of the brazed joint reaches 60%～80% of the base metal performance.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy DZ422: No-allowance blades should be heat treated in vacuum or protective atmosphere, with the specification: 1210℃×2h/Argon rapid cooling + 870℃×32h/Argon cooling.
Surface treatment process of Alloy DZ422: Parts can be subjected to surface protection treatments such as aluminizing or AlSi coating.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy DZ422: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH4105: GH4105 is a Ni-Co-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with an operating temperature of 750℃～950℃. The alloy has high room temperature and high temperature strength and good oxidation resistance, but its hot working performance and welding performance are average. It is suitable for manufacturing high-temperature components such as aviation engine turbine blades and high-temperature bolts. Main products include hot rolled bars and flats, cold drawn bars, profiles, and ring parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH4105: The alloy has been used to manufacture components such as aviation engine turbine blades, sector seals, and high-temperature bolts, with good batch production and usage conditions. The alloy has high deformation resistance, so cracking should be prevented during forging and rolling. The suitable drawing temperature for cold drawn bars is about 600℃.
Material grade of Alloy GH4105: GH4105 (GH105).
Similar grade to Alloy GH4105: Nimonic 105 (UK).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH4105: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; WS9 7017 Hot rolled bars and flats for GH105 alloy turbine blades; WS9 7063 Cold drawn bars for GH105 alloy bolts; WS9 7134 GH105 alloy flash butt welded profiles; WS9 7134 Disk Cake material for GH105 alloy rolled rings.

Melting process of Alloy GH4105: Non-vacuum melting + vacuum remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum arc remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH4105: Excerpted from HB/Z 140 and WS9 7063, the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Hot rolled bars and flats for blades, forgings: 1150℃±10℃×4h/AC + 1030℃±10℃×16h/AC + 700℃±5℃×16h/AC; b) Cold drawn bars: 1125℃±10℃/AC + 850℃±10℃×16h/AC, where solution holding time is: 1h for d≤3mm; 2h for d>3mm～6mm; 4h for d>6mm～40mm.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH4105: Excerpted from WS9 7017, WS9 7063, WS9 7134, and WS9 7134 Disk. d20mm～200mm hot rolled and forged bars; hot rolled flats with cross-sections of 68.5mm×38mm×160mm, 77.5mmx44.5mm×200mm; d≤40mm cold drawn bars; various sizes of forgings and profiles. Hot rolled bars are supplied without heat treatment, with fully polished or turned surfaces; hot rolled flats are supplied as hot rolled + descaled.
