Cold drawn bars are supplied as cold drawn (deformation 8%～12% after final annealing) + solution treated + centerless ground or chemically descaled; profiles are supplied as extruded or rolled + softened (1150℃±10℃, 30min, rapid cooling) + descaled; cakes are supplied without heat treatment, with both ends turned or fully turned.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH4105: 1340℃～1380℃ [1].
Density of Alloy GH4105: ρ=7.97g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH4105: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Oxidation resistance of Alloy GH4105: In air medium, the oxidation weight loss after continuous heating for 100h at 800℃ and 950℃ is 0.11mg/cm³ and 0.99mg/cm³ respectively; the oxidation weight loss after heating at 950℃ for 100h with cooling every 24h is 1.89mg/cm³.

Corrosion resistance of Alloy GH4105: The corrosion weight loss of the alloy after testing for 1000h at 800℃ in air containing 3% SO2 is 0.6mg/cm³, and the corrosion weight loss after testing for 1000h in argon containing 3% SO2 is 15.0mg/cm³ [2].

Welding performance of Alloy GH4105: The alloy is prone to microcracks in the welding heat-affected zone, so Tungsten Inert Gas (TIG) welding and Metal Inert Gas (MIG) welding are not suitable. Flash butt welding and brazing in vacuum, liquid hydrogen medium, or inert gas (not exceeding 1150℃) yield relatively good results.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH4105: The heat treatment of parts is the same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product. For blades with local work hardening generated during manufacturing after solution treatment (before aging), surface annealing protected by argon or hydrogen should be performed according to specified requirements, with an annealing temperature of 1020℃～1040℃.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH4105: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH2150A: GH2150A is an Fe-Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with a long-term operating temperature less than 700℃ and a short-term operating temperature of 800℃. Elements such as chromium, tungsten, and molybdenum are added for solid solution strengthening, and elements such as aluminum, titanium, and niobium are added to form aging precipitation phases. The alloy has high strength, good plasticity, and good cold forming, welding, and machining performance. Main products include hot rolled and forged bars, square steel, plates, etc.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH2150A: The alloy has been used to manufacture high-pressure compressor stator and rotor blades for high-thrust aviation engines. It is recommended for load-bearing and high-load structural parts under all-weather conditions, such as aircraft parts, welded annular parts, turbine casing load-bearing parts, and compressor parts. The alloy exhibits satisfactory corrosion resistance under all-weather conditions.
Material grade of Alloy GH2150A: GH2150A.
Similar grades to Alloy GH2150A: ЭP718, KhN45MVTYuBR, VZh105 (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH2150A: GJB 1953A Specification for hot rolled superalloy bars for aviation engine rotating parts; Q/3B 990 Standard for GH150 alloy hot rolled and forged bars.
Melting process of Alloy GH2150A: Vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting process is adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH2150A: Excerpted from GJB 1953A and Q/3B 990, the standard heat treatment regime for hot rolled bars, forged bars, square steel, flat steel, and forgings is: (1000～1130)℃×(2～3)h/OQ + (780～830)℃×5h/AC + (650～730)℃×16h/AC.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH2150A: Excerpted from GJB 1953A and Q/3B 990.
Density of Alloy GH2150A: ρ=8.31g/cm³ [1].
Oxidation resistance of Alloy GH2150A: After standard heat treatment, the oxidation rate of the alloy tested in air medium at 800℃ for 100h is 0.025g/(m²·h) [1].
Fatigue properties of Alloy GH2150A: None None

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH2150A: The final heat treatment temperature for parts is: Vacuum furnace, 1020℃×1.5h/Argon cooling + 780℃×5h/Argon protection box cooling + 650℃×16h/Argon protection box cooling [2].
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH2150A: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy DZ4125L: DZ4125L is a nickel-based precipitation hardening directionally solidified columnar crystal superalloy with an operating temperature below 1050℃. This alloy is one of the alloys with the highest performance level in China currently. It is a hafnium-free directionally solidified alloy developed based on the composition of the American René125 alloy by reducing the content of impurities such as phosphorus through low segregation technology to reduce dendritic segregation. Compared with similar alloys, this alloy increases the temperature capability by 20℃ and can double the medium-temperature transverse properties. It is suitable for manufacturing aviation gas turbine rotor blades working below 1000℃ and guide vanes and other high-temperature parts working below 1050℃.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy DZ4125L: The alloy is a high-performance, low-cost new generation aviation turbine blade material, which has been used to manufacture new aviation engine hollow no-allowance high-pressure turbine rotor blades. When used, the surface of the alloy can be coated by low-pressure vapor phase aluminizing or vapor deposition processes to improve the corrosion resistance of the components.
Material grade of Alloy DZ4125L: DZ4125L (DZ125L).
Similar grade to Alloy DZ4125L: DS René 125 (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy DZ4125L: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 5512.2 Specification for cast superalloy master alloys for cast superalloys and cast intermetallic high temperature materials - Part 2: Master alloys for directionally solidified columnar crystal castings; HB 7762 Specification for directionally solidified columnar and single crystal superalloy ingots for aviation engines; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; Q/5B4041 DZ125L alloy directionally solidified investment cast turbine working blades.

Melting and casting process of Alloy DZ4125L: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum directional solidification furnace is used for remelting and pouring directionally solidified test bars and parts.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy DZ4125L: Excerpted from HBZ140 and Q/5B4041, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1220℃±10℃×2h/AC + 1080℃±10℃×4h/AC + 900℃±10℃×16h/AC, cooling with argon gas.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy DZ4125L: Excerpted from GJB5512.2, HB7762, and Q/5B4041. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. Master alloys and blade castings are supplied in as-cast condition; finished blades are supplied after vacuum heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy DZ4125L: 1250℃～1360℃ (metallographic method), 1243℃～1360℃ (differential thermal analysis method) [1].
Density of Alloy DZ4125L: ρ=8.35g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy DZ4125L: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming process and performance of Alloy DZ4125L: The casting performance of the alloy is good, and investment casting method can be used to cast directionally solidified turbine blades.
Welding performance of Alloy DZ4125L: Vacuum brazing can be performed.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy DZ4125L: No-allowance blades should be heat treated in vacuum or protective atmosphere, and the heat treatment specification is: 1220℃±10℃×2h/AC + 1080℃±10℃×4h/AC + 900℃±10℃×16h/AC.
Surface treatment process of Alloy DZ4125L: Low-pressure vapor phase aluminizing or vapor deposition processes can be used.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy DZ4125L: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy MGH4754: MGH4754 is a nickel-based oxide dispersion strengthened (ODS) superalloy produced by mechanical alloying (MA) technology, with a long-term operating temperature range of 900℃～1100℃ and a maximum operating temperature of up to 1200℃. The nanoscale (≤50nm) Y₂O₃ powder in the alloy has high thermal and chemical stability, and its strengthening effect can be maintained up to temperatures close to the melting point of the alloy. The alloy has very high strength and oxidation resistance at high temperatures (>1000℃), making it suitable for manufacturing guide vanes, rear labyrinth rings of guides, and other parts for advanced aviation engines. Main products include hot extruded bars and hot rolled sheets.
Material grade of Alloy MGH4754: MGH4754 (MGH754).
Similar grades to Alloy MGH4754: MA754 (USA), PM1000 (Europe).

Melting and main manufacturing process of Alloy MGH4754: Elemental powders, alloy powders, and Y₂O₃ rare earth oxide powders are processed into alloy powder by mechanical alloying under inert gas protection, and then formed by hot extrusion process.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy MGH4754: Excerpted from literature [3] and Q/5B 4035, the standard heat treatment (recrystallization annealing) regime for various products is: 1315℃×1h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy MGH4754: Excerpted from literature [3] and Q/5B 4035. Various sizes of hot extruded bars and sheets. Hot extruded bars and sheets are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy MGH4754: 1400℃～1450℃ [1].
Density of Alloy MGH4754: ρ=8.3g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy MGH4754: At 21℃, under a 200 Oersted magnetic field, the magnetic permeability is: μ=1.001×10^-6 H/m [1].

Corrosion resistance of Alloy MGH4754: After 240h corrosion test in 1150℃ molten glass liquid, the corrosion rate of the alloy is 1.17g/(m²·h). The chemical composition (mass fraction) of the molten glass liquid is 73.0% SiO₂ + 16.3% Na₂O + 1.7% CaO + 3.1% MgO + 1.7% Al₂O₃ + 0.15% Li₂O + 0.4% H₂O [1].
Forming process and performance of Alloy MGH4754: Hot extrusion temperature range is 1000℃～1200℃, extrusion ratio range is 10:1～20:1, and extrusion speed varies with the former two parameters [3,4].
Part heat treatment process of Alloy MGH4754: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product [1,3].
Surface treatment process of Alloy MGH4754: Shot peening can be performed as needed.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy MGH4754: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy DD403: DD403 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening single crystal superalloy, belonging to the first generation of single crystal superalloys. The alloy has good medium and high temperature performance and structural stability, as well as excellent thermal fatigue resistance; the alloy composition is relatively simple, does not contain scarce precious elements, and the cost is low. Compared with the foreign first-generation single crystal alloy PWA1480, the mechanical properties are comparable, but the high-temperature oxidation and hot corrosion resistance are poorer. Compared with the compositionally similar DZ403 alloy, the rupture strength at 760℃/100h is about 39MPa higher; the rupture strength at 1040℃/100h is about 39MPa higher.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy DD403: The alloy is suitable for manufacturing gas turbine rotor blades working below 1040℃ and guide vanes working below 1100℃. An engine blade made of this alloy has passed 3 bench tests and flight test assessments, and passed the material type appraisal. Parts made of the alloy need to be coated with PtAl or MCrAlY coating to further improve the alloy's oxidation and corrosion resistance. No σ phase precipitation was found in the alloy after long-term aging at 850℃.
Material grade of Alloy DD403: DD403 (DD3).
Material technical standards for Alloy DD403: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; Q/6S366 DD403 alloy ingot.

Melting and casting process of Alloy DD403: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, vacuum induction furnace for remelting, and investment precision casting method and directional solidification spiral selector method or seed crystal method are used to manufacture single crystal parts or specimens.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy DD403: Excerpted from literature [1], literature [2], and Q/6S366. a) Standard heat treatment regime: 1250℃×4h/AC + 870℃×32h/AC; b) Alternative heat treatment regime: 1265℃×4h/AC + 1060℃×4h/AC + 870℃×32h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy DD403: None. Master alloy is cast into cylindrical long bars with a diameter range of 70mm～90mm. Master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, single crystal parts are supplied after standard heat treatment or alternative heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy DD403: 1328℃～1376℃ [1].
Density of Alloy DD403: ρ=8.20g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy DD403: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming process and performance of Alloy DD403: The alloy has good casting performance and can be cast into various hollow single crystal blades with complex shapes.
Welding performance of Alloy DD403: Vacuum brazing or diffusion bonding can be used.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy DD403: No-allowance blades should be solution treated at 1250℃ for 4h under argon protection or vacuum, followed by forced argon cooling with a cooling rate greater than 300℃/min, and then aged at 870℃ for 32h, followed by argon cooling.
Surface treatment process of Alloy DD403: Parts surface is coated with PtAl coating or MCrAlY coating.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy DD403: No special requirements.
Phase transformation temperature of Alloy DD403: The complete solution temperature of γ´ phase is 1250℃.

Overview of Alloy DZ4125: DZ4125 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening directionally solidified columnar crystal superalloy. Its operating temperature is below 1050℃, and it is one of the alloys with the highest performance level in its class in China. The alloy has good comprehensive properties at medium and high temperatures as well as excellent fatigue resistance. This alloy is developed based on the composition of American René125 alloy by adjusting the titanium content and adding hafnium element, giving the alloy good casting performance. It can be cast into no-allowance directionally solidified blades with complex internal cavities and wall thicknesses as small as 0.5mm. It is suitable for manufacturing high-temperature components such as gas turbine rotor blades and guide vanes [1].

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy DZ4125: The alloy has been widely used to manufacture directionally solidified hollow no-allowance turbine rotor blades for a certain advanced aviation engine. The blades have been mass-produced and are in good condition. The alloy can be treated with various surface protective coatings. The advanced vacuum arc plating NiCrAlYSi overlay coating used significantly improves the oxidation and hot corrosion resistance of the alloy. After long-term aging at 900℃ for 4000h, no TCP phase precipitated in the alloy.
Material grade of Alloy DZ4125: DZ4125 (DZ125).
Similar grade to Alloy DZ4125: DS René 125 (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy DZ4125: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; HB 7762 Specification for directionally solidified columnar and single crystal superalloy ingots for aviation engines; HB/Z140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; Q/6S 1321 DZ125 and DZ125L alloy high-pressure turbine working blade castings.

Melting process of Alloy DZ4125: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum directional solidification furnace is used for remelting and pouring directionally solidified test bars and parts.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy DZ4125: Excerpted from HB/Z140, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1180℃±10℃×2h → 1230℃±10℃×3h/AC + 1100℃±10℃×4h/AC + 870℃±10℃×20h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy DZ4125: Excerpted from HB7762 and Q/6S1321. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of about 1m. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and castings are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy DZ4125: 1230℃～1240℃ (metallographic method); 1295℃～1377℃ (differential thermal analysis method) [2].
Density of Alloy DZ4125: ρ=8.595g/cm³ [2].
Magnetic properties of Alloy DZ4125: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy DZ4125: After standard heat treatment, the room temperature hardness of the alloy is HRC 42.0～43.6 [2].
Impact properties of Alloy DZ4125: For directionally solidified specimens after standard heat treatment, the room temperature impact toughness αKU=(77～95)kJ/m² [2].

Forming process and performance of Alloy DZ4125: The casting performance of the alloy is good. Investment precision casting can be used to cast thin-walled hollow directionally solidified turbine blades with complex internal cavities and wall thicknesses as small as 0.5mm.
Welding performance of Alloy DZ4125: Vacuum brazing can be performed, and the strength of the brazed joint reaches 60%～80% of the base metal strength.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy DZ4125: Heat treatment of no-allowance blade castings should be carried out in vacuum or protective atmosphere. The heat treatment specification is: 1180℃×2h + 1230℃×3h/Argon rapid cooling + 1180℃×4h/Argon cooling + 870℃×20h/Argon cooling. General castings are heat treated according to standard processes in the atmosphere.

Surface treatment process of Alloy DZ4125: The alloy can be subjected to various surface protection treatments. The alloy hollow blade castings use vacuum arc plated NiCrAlYSi surface overlay coating, showing good coating condition and good process performance.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy DZ4125: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH2706: GH2706 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with an operating temperature of 550℃～650℃. This alloy is a modified version of GH4169 alloy, with characteristics similar to GH4169 alloy. Due to the reduced niobium content in the alloy, it has better production process performance, hot and cold processing performance, and welding performance. The alloy has high strength and good oxidation and corrosion resistance below 700℃. This alloy can produce large and ultra-large superalloy products, suitable for manufacturing aviation engine casings, diffuser casings, turbine disks, fasteners, and other components. It can also be used to manufacture large-diameter disks for gas turbines used in combined cycle power generation [12]. Main products include bars, forgings, ring parts, thin sheets, and strips.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH2706: The alloy has been used to manufacture components such as engine high-pressure compressor extension casings. The alloy has a wide hot working temperature range and high plasticity between 850℃ and 1150℃. At a deformation rate of 0.01, it quickly enters the steady-state rheological stage at 900℃～1100℃, after which the true stress changes very little, which is extremely beneficial for cogging and hot working of large shapes. The 843℃×3h step in the alloy's three-stage heat treatment is a stabilization treatment. After this treatment, a large amount of η phase exists on the grain boundaries, which is conducive to improving the alloy's rupture performance [14].
Material grade of Alloy GH2706: GH2706 (GH706).
Similar grades to Alloy GH2706: Inconel 706, UNS N09706 (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH2706: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; QJ/DT 0160028 GH706 alloy bar technical conditions; Q/3B 4070 GH706 alloy annular forgings.

Melting process of Alloy GH2706: Vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum arc furnace remelting, or double vacuum arc furnace remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH2706: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, the standard heat treatment regime for forged bars and annular parts is: (925～980)℃×(≥30min)/(Cooling as required) + 845℃±10℃×3h/AC + 720℃±10℃×8h/FC (55℃/h) → 620℃±10℃×8h/AC; HBS≥285.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH2706: Excerpted from QJ/DT 0160028 and Q/3B 4070.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH2706: 1335℃～1371℃ [2].
Electrical resistivity of Alloy GH2706: At 20℃, ρ=1.25×10^-6 Ω·m [2].
Density of Alloy GH2706: ρ=8.06g/cm³ [2].
Hardness of Alloy GH2706: After standard heat treatment, the room temperature hardness of 90mm square billet is HBS 370 [11].
Welding performance of Alloy GH2706: The alloy has satisfactory welding process performance. Solution treatment is required before welding, and aging is performed after welding.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH2706: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH2706: No special requirements.

Phase transformation temperature of Alloy GH2706: Precipitation temperature range of γ' phase is 540℃～870℃, precipitation peak is 704℃; Precipitation temperature range of γ” phase is 760℃～816℃; Precipitation temperature range of η phase is 760℃～950℃, precipitation peak temperature is 871℃, dissolution temperature is 950℃; Precipitation temperature range of MC type carbides is 580℃～1000℃.

Overview of Alloy JG1101 (TAC-2): JG1101 (TAC-2) is a TiAl intermetallic based cast high-temperature material with an operating temperature below 750℃. The alloy has high elastic modulus, high temperature strength, and creep resistance; it has high oxidation resistance and stable microstructure at high temperatures. The performance level of this alloy is close to that of nickel-based superalloys, but its density is only half that of superalloys. The alloy is low cost and suitable for manufacturing high-temperature structural parts such as turbocharger turbines for automotive and aviation engines, turbine disks, blades, and valves.
Material grade of Alloy JG1101 (TAC-2): JG1101 (TAC-2).
Melting process of Alloy JG1101 (TAC-2): Multiple remelting process in vacuum consumable arc furnace is adopted, followed by pouring investment precision cast parts and test bars.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy JG1101 (TAC-2): Excerpted from literature [2] and Q/GYB 539. d100mm～200mm master alloy ingots. Master alloy ingots are supplied after annealing treatment; investment precision castings are supplied after annealing treatment + grinding + sandblasting.
Density of Alloy JG1101 (TAC-2): p=3.87g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy JG1101 (TAC-2): The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming process and performance of Alloy JG1101 (TAC-2): The alloy has good casting performance and can be cast into turbocharger turbines with complex shapes and other parts.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy JG1101 (TAC-2): For parts without surface machining, annealing treatment should be carried out in vacuum, with the specification: 900℃×12h/FC → 300℃/AC (or argon cooling).
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy JG1101 (TAC-2): No special requirements.

Overview of Alloys MGH4755 and GH3007: MGH4755 is a nickel-based oxide dispersion strengthened (ODS) superalloy produced by mechanical alloying (MA) technology, with an operating temperature range of 1100℃～1300℃. GH3007 is a Ni-Cr based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy with an operating temperature range of 900℃～1100℃. MGH4755 alloy is developed based on GH3007 by further adjusting the carbon content, increasing ω(Cr) to about 30%, and introducing nanoscale rare earth oxide particles as strengthening phases. Both alloys have high strength at high temperatures, excellent oxidation and corrosion resistance, and good hot working plasticity and cold forming process performance. They are suitable for manufacturing parts working under high creep stress and various strong corrosion conditions at high temperatures. Main products include bars, wires, strips, cold rolled thin sheets, and hot rolled medium plates.
Material grades of Alloys MGH4755 and GH3007: MGH4755 (MGH5K), GH3007 (GH5K).

Product specifications and supply status of Alloys MGH4755 and GH3007: Excerpted from Q/GYB 537 and literature [1]. δ0.5mm～4.0mm cold rolled thin sheets; δ4mm～14mm hot rolled plates; d10mm～300mm hot rolled and forged bars; various sizes and specifications of forgings, tubes, and wires. Hot rolled plates and cold rolled thin sheets are supplied after annealing + pickling + straightening + trimming; bars and forgings are generally supplied without heat treatment; tubes and wires are supplied after annealing + pickling.
Melting temperature range of Alloys MGH4755 and GH3007: 1365℃～1405℃ [1].
Density of Alloys MGH4755 and GH3007: ρ=8.10g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloys MGH4755 and GH3007: The alloys are non-magnetic.
Elastic properties of Alloys MGH4755 and GH3007: At 20℃, E=218GPa [1].

Part heat treatment process of Alloys MGH4755 and GH3007: The final heat treatment temperature for sheet parts is 1250℃±10℃ or 1140℃～1180℃, and the intermediate heat treatment temperature is 1100℃±10℃. Cooling is performed in inert gas or air after heat treatment.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloys MGH4755 and GH3007: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH2132 and Premium GH2132: GH2132 and Premium GH2132 are Fe-Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloys with an operating temperature range of -253℃～650℃. Premium GH2132 alloy further improves purity, reduces the content of sulfur, gases, and trace elements, and adjusts the heat treatment regime to increase strength. Both alloys have good high and low temperature strength and long-term stability, good corrosion resistance and hot deformation performance, as well as good processing plasticity and satisfactory welding performance. They are suitable for manufacturing high-temperature load-bearing components such as turbine disks, compressor disks, and fasteners for aviation, aerospace, ground gas turbines, and flue gas turbines. Main products include disk parts, forgings, plates, bars, wires, and ring parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH2132 and Premium GH2132: GH2132 and Premium GH2132 alloys have been used to manufacture fasteners for aviation and aerospace engines, as well as large-diameter turbine disks and various fasteners for ground gas turbines and flue gas turbines, with good batch production and usage conditions. Similar alloys are widely used abroad to manufacture high-temperature load-bearing components such as compressor disks, turbine disks, load-bearing rings, casings, shafts, and plate welded load-bearing parts for aviation engines. After long-term aging at 650℃, G phase and cellular η phase precipitate on the grain boundaries of the alloy, and after aging for 6000h, the η phase presents a Widmanstätten structure. When the alloy composition exceeds the technical standard specifications or certain processes are improper, excessive η, G, and σ phases will appear, and an abnormal cellular γ' phase will appear.
Material grades of Alloy GH2132 and Premium GH2132: GH2132 (GH132); Premium GH2132.
Similar grades to Alloy GH2132 and Premium GH2132: A286, UNS S66286, P.Q.A286 (USA), ZbNCT25 (France).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH2132 and Premium GH2132: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GB/T 14994 Cold drawn superalloy bars; GB/T 14995 Hot rolled superalloy plates; GB/T 14996 Cold rolled superalloy thin sheets; GJB 2611A Specification for cold drawn superalloy bars for aviation; GJB 2612A Specification for cold drawn superalloy wires for welding; GJB 3020A Specification for superalloy ring blanks for aviation; GJB 3165A Specification for hot rolled and forged superalloy bars for aviation load-bearing parts; GJB 3167A Cold drawn superalloy wires for cold heading; GJB 3317A Specification for hot rolled superalloy plates for aviation; GJB 3782A Specification for superalloy forged round cakes for aviation; GJB 5280 Specification for superalloy disk forgings for aviation engines; GJB 5301 Specification for superalloy annular parts for aviation; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; YB/T 5245 Hot rolled and forged superalloy bars for general load-bearing parts; YB/T 5249 Cold drawn superalloy wires for cold heading; Q/5B 4026 GH2132 alloy bars; Q/3B 4071 Premium GH2132 alloy hot rolled bars.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH2132 and Premium GH2132: Excerpted from GB/T 14994, GB/T 14995, GB/T 14996, GJB 2611A, GJB 2612, GJB 3020A, GJB 3165A, GJB 3167A, GJB 3317A, GJB 3782A, GJB 5280A, GJB 5301A, YB/T 5245, YB/T 5249, Q/5B 4026, and Q/3B 4071.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH2132 and Premium GH2132: 1364℃～1424℃ [9].
Density of Alloy GH2132 and Premium GH2132: GH2132: ρ=7.93g/cm³ [1]; Premium GH2132: ρ=7.99g/cm³ [2].
Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH2132 and Premium GH2132: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product.
Surface treatment process of Alloy GH2132 and Premium GH2132: Parts working at high temperatures can be effectively protected using W-2 enamel coating.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH2132 and Premium GH2132: No special requirements.

Phase transformation temperature of Alloy GH2132 and Premium GH2132: Dissolution temperature of γ' phase is 830℃～850℃, precipitation starts at 650℃, precipitation peak is 700℃～730℃; Precipitation temperature of η phase is 700℃～900℃; M₃B₂ phase dissolves largely during solution at 1040℃～1080℃, and can dissolve completely at 1180℃～1210℃, precipitates during aging at 650℃; L phase dissolves during solution between 950℃～990℃, precipitation peak is about 850℃; G phase starts to dissolve above 982℃, can dissolve completely at 1120℃, precipitation peak is about 850℃; Precipitation temperature of σ phase is 650℃～900℃, precipitation peak is 750℃～850℃.

Overview of Alloy K465: K465 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with a relatively high tungsten addition and an operating temperature below 1050℃. The alloy has high high-temperature strength and good hot corrosion resistance, a wide range of applications, and superior comprehensive performance. The alloy has good casting performance and can be cast into precision castings with complex shapes. It is suitable for manufacturing gas turbine guide vanes working below 1050℃, gas turbine working blades working below 1000℃, and integral turbine nozzle guides.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K465: The alloy has been used to manufacture gas turbine working blades, turbine guide vanes, and integral turbine nozzle guides for various types of aviation engines. A certain model of engine turbine working blade and turbine guide vane developed using K465 alloy has passed long-term bench test assessments. Part surface treatment processes can use low-pressure vapor phase aluminizing or vapor deposition processes. AlSi coating, NiCr-CrAl coating, or NiCrAlYSi coating can further improve the parts' resistance to hot corrosion. No harmful phases precipitate in the alloy after long-term aging.
Material grade of Alloy K465: K465.
Similar grade to Alloy K465: KC6Y (Russia).
Material technical standards for Alloy K465: Q/6S 1966 K465 alloy ingot specification; Q/KJ.J02.33 K465 cast superalloy master alloy ingot.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K465: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting and pouring investment precision cast parts and test bars.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy K465: Excerpted from Q/6S 1966 and Q/KJ.J02.33, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1210℃±10℃×4h, air cooling or vacuum controlled cooling; the alloy can also be used in the as-cast condition.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K465: Excerpted from Q/6S 1966 and Q/KJ.J02.33. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and precision castings are supplied in as-cast or heat-treated condition.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K465: Incipient melting point: 1250℃ (metallographic method) [1], 1300℃~1338℃ (differential thermal analysis method) [2].
Density of Alloy K465: ρ=8.341g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K465: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and performance of Alloy K465: The casting performance of the alloy is good. Investment casting method can be used to cast gas turbine working blades, turbine guide vanes, and integral turbine nozzle guides with complex structures.
Welding performance of Alloy K465: Vacuum brazing can be performed.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy K465: Heat treatment of no-allowance precision cast parts should be carried out in vacuum or protective atmosphere, with the specification: 1210℃±10℃×4h, air cooling or vacuum controlled cooling.
Surface treatment process of Alloy K465: Low-pressure vapor phase aluminizing or vapor deposition processes, AlSi coating, NiCr-CrAl coating, or NiCrAlYSi coating can be used.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K465: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH3128: GH3128 is a Ni-Cr based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy with an operating temperature below 950℃. The alloy is solid solution strengthened by adding 16% w(W+Mo), and grain boundaries are purified and strengthened by adding boron, cerium, and zirconium elements. The alloy has high plasticity, relatively high rupture and creep strength, as well as good oxidation resistance, stamping, and welding process performance. Its comprehensive performance is superior to similar nickel-based solid solution strengthened alloys like GH3044 and GH3536. It is suitable for manufacturing aviation engine combustion chamber flame tubes, afterburner casings, and other components working long-term at 950℃. Main products include cold rolled thin sheets, hot rolled plates, bars, forgings, wires, and tubes.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH3128: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine flame tubes, diffusers, afterburners, exhaust nozzles, adjusting flaps, stabilizers, diffusers, and gas ducts, with good batch production and usage conditions. The alloy is also promoted for use in structural parts under high-temperature oxidizing atmospheres such as thermocouple protection tubes for temperature measurement, flux gates, magnetometer probes, skeleton materials, boats for W and Mo reduction sintering, and railway locomotive pre-chamber nozzles. Parts working at high temperatures can be effectively protected using W-2 enamel coating. μ phase precipitates in the alloy after long-term aging.
Material grade of Alloy GH3128: GH3128 (GH128, Red Star No. 11).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH3128: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GB/T 14995 Hot rolled superalloy plates; GB/T 14996 Cold rolled superalloy thin sheets; GJB 1952A Specification for cold rolled superalloy thin sheets for aviation; GJB 2612 Specification for cold rolled superalloy wires for welding; GJB 3317A Specification for hot rolled superalloy plates for aviation; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; Liaoxin 7-0062 Technical conditions for GH128 alloy hot rolled and forged bars for aviation non-rotating parts; Liaoxin 7-0087 Technical conditions for GH128 alloy hot rolled bars for aviation non-rotating parts (Provisional); Liaoxin 7-0090 Technical conditions for GH128 alloy hot rolled and forged bars for aviation; QJ/DT01.73092 Technical conditions for GH3128 medium plates; QJ/DT01.73131 Technical conditions for GH3128 alloy cakes and bars for aerospace.
Melting process of Alloy GH3128: Non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH3128: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, GB/T 14995, GB/14996, GJB 1952A, GJB 3317A, Liaoxin 7-0062, Liaoxin 7-0087, Liaoxin 7-0090, QJ/DT01.73092 and QJ/DT 01.73131, the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Cold rolled sheets and hot rolled plates: (1140～1180)℃/AC, holding time depends on the thickness of the plate; b) Medium plates (δ15mm～65mm): (1180～1200)℃/AC, holding time depends on the thickness of the plate; c) Hot rolled and forged bars: (1140～1200)℃/AC×(1.5～2)h/AC; d) Cakes: (1180～1200)℃/AC, holding time should ensure the part is heated through, fully dissolved, or meets annealing requirements.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH3128: d90mm～200mm forged bars; δ0.5mm～4.0mm cold rolled sheets; δ4.00mm～14.00mm hot rolled plates; δ15mm～65mm medium plates; d0.3mm～10mm cold drawn welding wires. Bars are supplied in forged or rolled state, and can be turned or ground; hot rolled plates and cold rolled thin sheets are supplied after solution treatment + pickling + straightening + trimming; medium plates are supplied after solution treatment + acid/alkali washing; welding wires are supplied in cold drawn, semi-hard, or solution treated + pickled state. Cakes are supplied in forged + turned or ground state.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH3128: 1340℃～1390℃ [6].
Density of Alloy GH3128: ρ=8.81g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH3128: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and performance of Alloy GH3128: The charging temperature for ingot forging is not higher than 700℃, the heating temperature is 1160℃±10℃, and the final forging temperature is greater than 900℃; the heating temperature for slab rolling is 1170℃±10℃, and the final rolling temperature is greater than 900℃; the heating temperature for thin sheet hot rolling is 1140℃～1180℃, and the final rolling temperature is greater than 800℃. The total reduction rate of cold rolling is 30%～35%.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH3128: a) Solution treatment regime for afterburner components: 1200℃±10℃/AC; b) Solution treatment regime for combustion chamber flame tube components: 1160℃±10℃/AC; c) Intermediate treatment temperature for parts undergoing multiple stamping forming: 1100℃±20℃, holding time determined by part thickness and furnace loading conditions.
Surface treatment process of Alloy GH3128: Parts working at high temperatures can be effectively protected using W-2 enamel coating.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH3128: No special requirements.
Phase transformation temperature of Alloy GH3128: Precipitation temperature range of μ phase is 700℃～1050℃.

Overview of Alloy GH2907: GH2907 is an Fe-Ni-Co based precipitation hardening low-expansion wrought superalloy, comprehensively strengthened by adding niobium, titanium, silicon, and trace boron elements. The alloy has very high strength below 650℃, a low coefficient of expansion, good thermal fatigue resistance, and an almost constant elastic modulus. The Curie point is 400℃~450℃. The expansion coefficient of the alloy remains basically unchanged below the Curie point. It is suitable for manufacturing various aviation and aerospace engine ring parts and casings used below 650℃. Main products include bars, forgings, plates, and strips.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH2907: The alloy has been used to manufacture parts such as aviation engine high-pressure compressor rear casings, load-bearing rings, heat insulation rings, combustion chamber seal rings, honeycomb seats, and turbine outer rings, with good usage conditions. Since the alloy does not contain chromium or aluminum elements, its high-temperature oxidation resistance is poor. When used for a long time above 700℃, appropriate protective coatings should be applied. The alloy surface can be shot peened, which can increase the rotating bending fatigue by about 18%.
Material grade of Alloy GH2907: GH2907 (GH907).
Similar grades to Alloy GH2907: Incoloy 907, UNS N09907 (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH2907: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 5261 Specification for low-expansion superalloy bars; HB 7682 Low-expansion GH907 superalloy ring blanks and ring parts; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; Q/GYB 05067 GH2907 alloy cold rolled thin sheets and strips; Q/GYB 05069 Cold drawn HGH2907 alloy wires for welding.
Melting process of Alloy GH2907: Vacuum induction furnace + vacuum arc remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH2907: Excerpted from HB/Z140, GJB5261, HB7682, Q/GYB05067, and Q/GYB05069, there are two standard heat treatment regimes: Regime A: 1040℃±15℃×1h/AC or rapid cooling + 800℃±15℃×16h/FC (55℃/h) → 620℃±15℃×8h/AC, suitable for brazed parts; Regime B: 980℃±15℃×1h/AC or rapid cooling + 775℃±15℃×12h/FC (55℃/h) → 620℃±15℃×8h/AC, HB375～302, suitable for bars and ring parts.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH2907: Excerpted from GJB5261, HB7682, Q/GYB05067, and Q/GYB05069. Various specifications of round cakes, ring blanks, and ring parts; d20mm～300mm bars; δ0.5mm～6.5mm cold rolled thin sheets and strips; d0.3mm～10.0mm welding wires. Round cakes and ring blanks are supplied without heat treatment. Round cakes and ring blanks are supplied with turned end faces, and turned and chamfered (r5mm～10mm) inner and outer diameters; ring parts are supplied after complete heat treatment. Ring parts for brazing can be supplied without heat treatment; bars are supplied without heat treatment, with turned or ground surfaces; plates and strips are supplied after cold rolling + annealing + descaling + leveling + straightening + trimming; welding wires are supplied in cold drawn state, semi-hard state, or heat treated + pickled state.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH2907: 1332℃~1400℃ [10].
Phase transformation points of Alloy GH2907: Curie point 410℃~450℃ [1,10].
Specific heat capacity of Alloy GH2907: At 20℃, c=431J/(kg·℃) [1].
Density of Alloy GH2907: ρ=8.28g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH2907: Ferromagnetic below 450℃, paramagnetic above 450℃.
Hardness of Alloy GH2907: Excerpted from HB7682, 90mm square billet, ring blanks, and ring parts treated by Regime B have a room temperature hardness of HBS 375～302.

Fracture toughness of Alloy GH2907: Room temperature fracture toughness of ring parts KC=87.18MPa√m (chordwise sampling, treated by Regime B; loading speed: (0.5～1.0)mm/min; full scale load 20kN; specimen notch tip is a chevron notch; angle: 37.4°).

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH2907: Heat treatment Regime A applies only to brazed parts. Heat treatment Regime B can be used for various parts, including parts that need brazing. Using Regime B allows parts to obtain better comprehensive properties. When parts are heat treated finally without further machining, it is recommended to perform it in a vacuum furnace or under a protective atmosphere.

Surface treatment process of Alloy GH2907: The alloy surface can be shot peened. Shot peening process: Shot BZ10～BZ50, nominal diameter 0.09mm～0.425mm, shot intensity 0.08mm～0.25mm. After shot peening, the rotating bending fatigue limit can be increased by about 18%.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH2907: The alloy has good machining performance and can be satisfactorily machined and ground.

Coating of Alloy GH2907: Solid state aluminizing process can be used to form an oxide film mainly composed of Al2O3 and Fe(CoAl)2O4 on the surface for protection. Plasma sprayed NiCoCrAlY as the bond coat and NiCrAlSi as the top coat can also be used as a seal coating. Both coatings can significantly improve the alloy's oxidation resistance.

Phase transformation temperature of Alloy GH2907: Precipitation temperature range of γ' phase is 540℃~760℃, precipitation peak is about 630℃. Precipitation temperature range of ε and ε” phases is 700℃~920℃, precipitation peak temperature is about 800℃.

Overview of Alloy K4708: K4708 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with an operating temperature less than 850℃. The alloy is mainly strengthened by adding aluminum and titanium elements to form γ' precipitation strengthening phase, adding chromium, tungsten, and molybdenum elements for solid solution strengthening, and adding appropriate amounts of boron and cerium elements to strengthen and purify grain boundaries. The alloy has relatively high strength and medium-temperature rupture performance, stable microstructure, high oxidation resistance, and good casting and welding performance. It is mainly used for manufacturing load-bearing components of aviation engines working at 400℃～850℃.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K4708: The alloy has been used to manufacture parts such as aviation engine support seats and bases, with good usage conditions. When welding the alloy, Tungsten Inert Gas (TIG) welding method with good protective effect should be used. After long-term aging at 800℃～900℃, no harmful phases precipitate in the microstructure.
Material grade of Alloy K4708: K4708 (K708).
Similar grade to Alloy K4708: XH62BMTЮЛ (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy K4708: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; Q/GYB 530 K4708 master alloy for casting; Q/GYB 532 K4708 alloy castings.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K4708: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting and pouring investment precision castings and test bars. Pouring temperature is 1460℃～1520℃, shell firing temperature is 1000℃.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K4708: Excerpted from Q/GYB 530 and Q/GYB 532. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K4708: Melting point 1337.9℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K4708: ρ=8.55g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K4708: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Oxidation resistance of Alloy K4708: The alloy has good oxidation resistance in air medium below 850℃.
Forming process and performance of Alloy K4708: The alloy has good casting performance, with an average linear shrinkage of 1.44% and an average volumetric shrinkage of 3.81%.

Welding performance of Alloy K4708: The welding performance of the alloy is good. Tungsten Inert Gas (TIG) welding method with good protective effect should be used during welding. HGH3113 and HGH3533 alloys can be selected as filler wire materials.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy K4708: Same as the alloy's standard heat treatment regime. It is recommended to perform heat treatment for no-allowance precision castings in an inert atmosphere.
Surface treatment process of Alloy K4708: Aluminizing treatment can be performed according to part usage requirements.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K4708: The machining performance of the alloy is good.

Overview of Alloy K2136: K2136 is an iron-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with an operating temperature below 700℃. The alloy is mainly strengthened by aluminum and titanium elements forming γ' precipitation phase. The alloy has relatively good oxidation resistance, a small coefficient of linear expansion, good comprehensive performance, and stable microstructure during long-term use. The alloy can be used to manufacture various castings with simple structures, especially suitable for manufacturing large turbine disks used in the range of 550℃~650℃ [1,6,8]. The alloy uses electroslag casting process to produce turbine disks [1,6,8], which can replace forging with casting and simplify the process flow. After high-temperature slag washing purification and water-cooled sequential solidification, the material of the cast disk blank has high purity, relatively uniform composition, dense structure, and fine and dispersed inclusions.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K2136: The electroslag cast turbine disk of the alloy has passed two 800h bench tests and flight tests accumulating 800h on a certain aviation engine, with good results. The alloy has two heat treatment regimes, both of which have passed long-term engine test assessments and can be selected based on component requirements. After solution treatment at 980℃, the alloy's room temperature strength, plasticity, impact toughness, fracture toughness, high temperature rupture life, and low cycle fatigue properties are lower than those treated at 1050℃, but the rupture elongation and thermal fatigue resistance are higher [8].
Material grade of Alloy K2136: K2136 (K136).
Similar grade to Alloy K2136: V-57 (USA).
Material technical standards for Alloy K2136: Provisional technical conditions for electroslag cast stage I and II turbine disks for a certain engine [10].

Melting and casting art of Alloy K2136: Vacuum or non-vacuum induction furnace is used to pour electrode plate blanks, and then castings of the required shape are made according to the special electroslag casting process.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy K2136: Excerpted from literature [10], there are two standard heat treatments for cast turbine disks: Regime I: 980℃×1h/OQ + 700℃×16h/AC + 650℃×16h/AC; Regime II: 1050℃×4h/OQ + 710℃×32h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K2136: None. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 70mm～90mm. Electroslag cast formed disk blanks are supplied in as-cast condition.
Density of Alloy K2136: ρ=8.04g/cm³ [12].

Forming process and performance of Alloy K2136: Vacuum or non-vacuum induction furnace pours plate electrodes, which are then electroslag cast into disk blanks. Main parameters of the casting process are: melting current 10000A; furnace mouth voltage control 35V～40V; slag system: CaF₂:Al₂O₃:CaO=65:25:10; slag cap thickness control at 60mm～70mm. The alloy has good forming performance during electroslag casting. However, if process parameters are selected or controlled improperly, slag grooves may appear at the bottom of the disk body or steel beads may appear on the disk surface.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy K2136: Both Regime I and Regime II heat treatments are suitable for part production. To prevent the alloy strength from being too low, it is recommended to use the upper limit temperature for treatment.

Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K2136: Carbide tools are used for cutting. Broaching uses M42 tools. Good results can be obtained by improving cooling methods and coolant, and adjusting machining process parameters.

Phase transformation temperature of Alloy K2136: γ' phase precipitation temperature range is 650℃~900℃, precipitation peak is 750℃. Secondary η phase appears in the range of 750℃~1020℃, cellular η phase precipitates in large quantities at 800℃~850℃, and acicular η phase precipitation peak is 900℃.

Overview of Alloy JG4246B (MX246B): JG4246B is a Ni₃Al intermetallic based equiaxed cast high-temperature material independently developed in China. The alloy features low density (7.54g/cm³) and excellent high-temperature oxidation resistance, as well as good high-temperature strength. It is suitable for manufacturing high-temperature ablation-resistant components applied in the temperature range of 1100℃～1200℃.
Material grade of Alloy JG4246B (MX246B): JG4246B (MX246B).
Material technical standards for Alloy JG4246B (MX246B): Q/GYB 05089 JG4246B master alloy for casting.

Melting and casting process of Alloy JG4246B (MX246B): Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting and pouring investment precision cast parts and test bars.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy JG4246B (MX246B): The alloy is used in the as-cast condition.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy JG4246B (MX246B): Excerpted from Q/GYB 05088. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 70mm～90mm. Master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition.
Melting temperature range of Alloy JG4246B (MX246B): 1314℃～1347℃ [1].
Density of Alloy JG4246B (MX246B): ρ=7.54g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy JG4246B (MX246B): The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy JG4246B (MX246B): In as-cast condition, room temperature hardness is HRC 21 [1].
Forming process and performance of Alloy JG4246B (MX246B): Investment casting method can be used to cast complex thin-walled sealing strip parts and other high-temperature ablation-resistant parts.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy JG4246B (MX246B): Parts are used in the as-cast condition.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy JG4246B (MX246B): Carbide tools are used.

Overview of Alloy K4169: K4169 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy. The alloy uses γ” phase as the main strengthening phase and γ' phase as the auxiliary strengthening phase. The alloy has high strength and plasticity, excellent corrosion and irradiation resistance, as well as good welding and forming performance within a wide medium and low temperature range (-253℃~700℃), and has good resistance to strain age cracking. It is widely used in aviation, aerospace engines, nuclear reactors, and petrochemical fields, and is suitable for manufacturing engine blades, casings, and other structural parts working below 650℃.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K4169: The alloy has been used to manufacture more than a dozen types of precision castings such as aviation engine combustion chamber pre-diffusers and load-bearing rings, as well as aerospace high-thrust engine pump casings, etc., which have been mass-produced and used in good condition. To reduce the segregation of niobium element between dendrites, the casting process and thermal processes such as heat treatment should be strictly controlled. When used for large structural parts, hot isostatic pressing (HIP) treatment is required to obtain a dense and uniform microstructure. HIP treatment can eliminate porosity, reduce segregation, and improve weldability. Suitable heat treatment after HIP can improve the service performance of castings.
Material grade of Alloy K4169: K4169.
Similar grade to Alloy K4169: Inconel 718C (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy K4169: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 5512.1 Specification for cast superalloy master alloys for cast superalloys and cast intermetallic high temperature materials - Part 1: Master alloys for ordinary precision castings; HB 7763 Specification for equiaxed cast superalloy ingots for aviation engines; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; Q/GYB 05048 K4169 alloy castings.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K4169: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting before pouring parts and test bars. Refining temperature 1500℃~1530℃, pouring temperature 1440℃~1460℃ [2].

Hot isostatic pressing and heat treatment regime of Alloy K4169: Excerpted from Q/GYB 05048, 1165℃—1175℃, 130MPa～140MPa, 4h. Excerpted from HB/Z140, 1095℃±10℃×（1～2）h/AC or faster cooling + 955℃±10℃×1h/AC or faster cooling + 720℃±10℃×8h/FC (56℃/h) → 620℃±10℃×8h/AC. Excerpted from Q/GYB 05048, 1095℃±10℃×（1～2）h/AC + 955℃±10℃×1h/AC (350℃/h) → 605℃/AC + 720℃±10℃×8h/FC (50℃/h) → 620℃±10℃×8h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K4169: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1, HB 7763, and Q/GYB 05048. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition; investment castings are supplied after heat treatment, or after HIP + modified heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K4169: 1243℃~1359℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K4169: ρ=8.22g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K4169: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K4169: After standard heat treatment, the room temperature hardness of the alloy is HRC 34～42 [1].
Forming process and performance of Alloy K4169: The casting performance of the alloy is good. Large thin-walled castings can be cast.
Welding performance of Alloy K4169: The welding performance of the alloy is good, and castings can be repair welded.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy K4169: Same as the standard heat treatment regime of the alloy. Homogenization and solution treatment of castings are carried out in a vacuum furnace or argon protection furnace, and aging treatment is carried out after machining and final assembly welding of the parts.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K4169: Machining performance is better than general cast superalloys.

Phase transformation temperature of Alloy K4169: Dissolution temperature of γ" phase is 770℃~950℃; Dissolution temperature of γ' phase is 680℃~780℃; Dissolution temperature of Laves phase is 1160℃~1259℃.

Overview of Alloy K4202: K4202 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy, a multi-purpose superalloy with an operating temperature less than 700℃. The alloy is mainly strengthened by adding aluminum and titanium elements to form γ' precipitation strengthening phase, and adding tungsten and molybdenum elements for solid solution strengthening. The alloy has good comprehensive properties and structural stability, as well as good casting and welding performance. It is suitable for manufacturing turbine pump turbine guides and guide vanes working below 700℃.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K4202: The alloy has been used to manufacture various components such as turbine pumps, turbine stators, conical flow tubes, and guide vanes for aerospace engines working long-term below 700℃, with good batch production and usage conditions. The alloy has good welding performance and can be used for components that require welding.
Material grade of Alloy K4202: K4202 (K202).
Similar grade to Alloy K4202: ЧP202 (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy K4202: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; Q/GYB 529 K4202 master alloy for casting; Q/GYB 531 K4202 alloy castings.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K4202: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting, using investment precision casting method to cast parts and test bars. Pouring temperature under vacuum is 1450℃～1470℃.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K4202: Excerpted from Q/GYB 529 and Q/GYB 531, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1170℃±10℃×4h/AC + 840℃±10℃×10h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K4202: Excerpted from Q/GYB 529 and Q/GYB 531. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K4202: 1336℃～1372℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K4202: ρ=8.47g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K4202: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K4202: After standard heat treatment, the room temperature hardness of the alloy is HRC 34.3.
Forming process and performance of Alloy K4202: The alloy has good casting performance, and investment casting method can be used to cast castings.
Welding performance of Alloy K4202: The welding performance of the alloy is good, and argon arc welding can be used for welding.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy K4202: Same as the standard heat treatment regime of the alloy.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K4202: The machining performance of the alloy is good.

Overview of Alloy K423A: K423A is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy developed based on K423 alloy, with an operating temperature below 1000℃. By reducing the content of chromium, cobalt, and molybdenum elements and increasing the boron element content, the supersaturation of the alloy solid solution is reduced, improving the structural stability and comprehensive mechanical properties of the alloy [1,2]. The alloy has good oxidation resistance and thermal fatigue resistance, as well as good casting and welding performance, and stable microstructure. Main products include gas turbine hollow and solid guide vanes and integral guides.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K423A: The alloy has been used to manufacture Stage II integral guides for turboshaft engines. Compared with similar types of alloys, this alloy has higher strength, better plasticity, and better thermal fatigue and oxidation resistance. When parts are used above 900℃, CrAl co-diffusion method can be used to improve the material's corrosion resistance. After long-term aging, the precipitation amount of TCP phase in the alloy is significantly reduced.
Material grade of Alloy K423A: K423A (K23A).
Similar grade to Alloy K423A: C1023 (UK).

Material technical standards for Alloy K423A: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 5512.1 Specification for cast superalloy master alloys for cast superalloys and cast intermetallic high temperature materials - Part 1: Master alloys for ordinary precision castings; HB 7241 K423A alloy ingot; HB 7763 Specification for equiaxed cast superalloy ingots for aviation engines; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K423A: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting, using investment precision casting with sand filling method to cast parts and test bars. Refining temperature 1550℃~1600℃, pouring temperature 1390℃~1420℃.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K423A: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1, HB7241, HB7763, and literature [5]. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied in as-cast condition or after surface coating.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K423A: 1260℃~1335℃ [2].
Density of Alloy K423A: ρ=7.98g/cm³ [4].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K423A: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K423A: In as-cast condition, room temperature hardness is HRC 35～41 [1].

Forming process and performance of Alloy K423A: The casting performance of the alloy is good. Hollow blades with complex internal cavities and wall thickness less than 1mm can be cast. The linear shrinkage of the alloy is about 2.2%.
Welding performance of Alloy K423A: Argon arc welding and electron beam welding can be performed. Overlay welding tests revealed no cracks in the heat-affected zone of the weld.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy K423A: Before surface protection treatment, parts should undergo stabilization heat treatment with the regime: heat to 1190℃×15min/FC (≤45min) → 1000℃/AC under argon protection.

Surface treatment process of Alloy K423A: Parts generally do not undergo surface protection treatment. However, when used above 900℃, CrAl co-diffusion method can be used to improve the material's corrosion resistance [5].
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K423A: Carbide tools are used.

Overview of Alloy K4163: K4163 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with an operating temperature below 850℃. Elements such as chromium, cobalt, and molybdenum are added to the alloy for solid solution strengthening, and elements such as aluminum and titanium are added to form γ' aging strengthening phase. The alloy has relatively good comprehensive mechanical properties and fatigue resistance, good oxidation and corrosion resistance, and good casting and welding performance. Main products are investment precision cast structural parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K4163: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine components requiring high oxidation resistance, such as strut inner and outer mounting edges, fuel inlet edges, air pipe joints, igniters, diffusers, etc. The similar C263 alloy has been maturely applied in foreign aviation engines. After long-term aging at 850℃×432h, acicular η phase precipitates in the alloy.
Material grade of Alloy K4163: K4163 (K163).
Similar grade to Alloy K4163: C263 (UK).

Material technical standards for Alloy K4163: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; HB 7763 Specification for equiaxed cast superalloy ingots for aviation engines; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K4163: Vacuum induction furnace or non-vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting and pouring investment precision castings and test bars.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K4163: Excerpted from HB 7763, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1150℃±10℃×2h/AC + 800℃±10℃×8h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K4163: Excerpted from HB 7763. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter generally of 75mm～90mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition. Castings requiring welding are supplied after solution treatment; non-welded castings are supplied after solution + aging treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K4163: Melting point 1370℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K4163: ρ=8.33g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K4163: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming process and performance of Alloy K4163: The casting performance of the alloy is good. Thin-walled castings can be cast.
Welding performance of Alloy K4163: The welding performance of the alloy is good, and castings can be repair welded.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy K4163: Heat treatment is required to be performed under protective atmosphere such as vacuum or argon: Castings requiring welding are supplied after solution treatment; non-welded castings are supplied after solution + aging treatment. Cooling method for both is: cooling at a rate equivalent to air cooling or faster.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K4163: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH2135: GH2135 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with an operating temperature below 700℃. Elements such as aluminum and titanium are added to form aging precipitation strengthening phases, and elements such as chromium, tungsten, and molybdenum are added for solid solution strengthening. The alloy features good low cycle fatigue resistance, yield strength increasing with temperature below 700℃, and good hot working plasticity. Main products include bars, cakes, plates, forgings, and precision castings.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH2135: The alloy has been used to manufacture Stage I and II turbine disks for two types of aviation engines, equipped on two types of fighter jets for field use. China's first 3000-horsepower gas turbine flame tube made of the alloy sheet was installed on the "Long March 1" locomotive and passed a test run of more than 300 hours. This alloy can also be used to manufacture hot forming dies working at 750℃～850℃, such as forging hammer anvils and hydraulic press anvils or insert anvils, hot stamping dies, etc. Compared with nickel-based alloys for similar purposes, GH2135 alloy can save 41% ω(Ni) and 5.5% ω(Cr). After long-term aging above 800℃, acicular σ phase precipitates within the grains and Laves phase precipitates at the grain boundaries.
Material grade of Alloy GH2135: GH2135 (GH135, 808).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH2135: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GB/T 14997 Superalloy forged round cakes; GJB 3020A Specification for superalloy ring blanks for aviation; GJB 5301 Specification for superalloy annular parts for aviation; YB/T 5245 Hot rolled and forged superalloy bars for general load-bearing parts; YB/T 5247 Cold drawn superalloy wires for welding.

Melting process of Alloy GH2135: Electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting, or electric arc furnace + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH2135: Excerpted from GB/T 14997, GJB 3020A, GJB 5301, YB/T 5245, and literature [9], the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Disk forgings, ring blanks, annular parts: 1140℃±10℃×4h/AC + 830℃±10℃×8h/AC + 650℃±10℃×16h/AC, HBS 321～255; b) Hot rolled and forged bars: 1080℃±10℃×8h/AC + 830℃±10℃×8h/AC + 700℃±10℃×16h/AC, HBS 352～277; c) Plates: 1030℃±10℃×0.12h/AC + 750℃±10℃×16h/AC.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH2135: Excerpted from GB/T 14997, GJB 3020A, GJB 5301, YB/T 5245, and YB/T 5247.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH2135: Incipient melting temperature about 1260℃ [9].
Specific heat capacity of Alloy GH2135: Average specific heat capacity from 13℃ to 793℃: c=537J/(kg·℃) [9].
Density of Alloy GH2135: ρ=7.92g/cm³ [1].
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH2135: No special requirements.

Simulated overspeed test performance of Alloy GH2135: According to the design requirements of a certain turboprop engine: maximum speed 15600r/min, maximum hub stress 519MPa, hub temperature 395℃±10℃, rim temperature 446℃±16℃. First, a turbine disk of this alloy equipped with 72 shrouded, deep-root blades was subjected to an overspeed test at room temperature; it worked for 5 minutes at 15% overspeed. Then, after working for 1 minute at the maximum working temperature and maximum design speed, it worked for 5 minutes at 15% overspeed. The turbine disk successfully passed the test. Subsequently, an overtemperature test was conducted, passing the test of 45℃ overtemperature for 5 minutes at the maximum design speed, with good inspection results [1]. The turbine disk of this alloy was subjected to an overspeed test on another turboshaft engine model, with a hub temperature of 320℃ and rim temperature of 420℃. It worked for 3 minutes each at 10%, 20%, and 30% overspeed, and the conditions were normal. After inspection, the overspeed test continued, and the dovetail only broke at 43% overspeed, with a total safety factor of about 1.43 [5].

Overview of Alloy DZ406: DZ406 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening directionally solidified columnar crystal superalloy containing 3% ω(Re) and no titanium element. The operating temperature of the alloy is 30℃ higher than that of the first-generation directionally solidified alloys (DZ404, DZ422, DZ4125, etc.) and is the same as that of the first-generation single crystal alloys; the rupture strength is comparable to that of the second-generation directionally solidified alloys and first-generation single crystal alloys. The alloy has good oxidation resistance and casting performance, and can be directionally solidified into hollow turbine blades of different sizes and structures. It is suitable for manufacturing gas turbine rotor blades working at 1050℃.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy DZ406: The alloy has been used to manufacture Stage II high-pressure turbine working blades for a certain advanced aviation engine and has undergone test run assessments. No TCP harmful phase precipitation was found in the alloy after no-load long-term aging at 900℃×3000h and 980℃×1000h.
Material grade of Alloy DZ406: DZ406.
Similar grade to Alloy DZ406: René 142 (USA).
Material technical standards for Alloy DZ406: Q/6S 2364 DZ406 alloy ingot specification.

Melting process of Alloy DZ406: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum directional solidification furnace is used for remelting and pouring directionally solidified test bars and parts.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy DZ406: Excerpted from Q/6S 2364, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1260℃×1h → 1275℃×1h/AC + 1120℃×4h/AC + 1080℃×4h/AC + 870℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy DZ406: Excerpted from Q/6S 2364. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 70mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition.
Melting temperature range of Alloy DZ406: 1334℃～1360℃ (differential thermal analysis method) [1].
Density of Alloy DZ406: ρ=8.698g/cm³ [3].
Magnetic properties of Alloy DZ406: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and performance of Alloy DZ406: The casting performance of the alloy is good, and it can be cast into directionally solidified columnar crystal blades and other parts with complex internal cavities and wall thicknesses as small as 0.5mm.
Welding performance of Alloy DZ406: Vacuum brazing can be performed.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy DZ406: Parts with unmachined surfaces (e.g., blades) should be heat treated under argon protection or vacuum, with the heat treatment specification same as the standard heat treatment regime.
Surface treatment process of Alloy DZ406: Parts can be treated with aluminizing, or MCrAlY coating and TBC coating.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy DZ406: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy JG4006E (IC6E): JG4006E is a Ni₃Al intermetallic based equiaxed cast high-temperature material, an alloy developed based on JG4006 (IC6) by adjusting the content of trace elements boron and carbon, with an operating temperature below 1150℃. The alloy features low cost, low density, high high-temperature rupture performance, and stable microstructure at high temperatures. It is suitable for manufacturing high-temperature structural parts working below 1100℃.
Material grade of Alloy JG4006E (IC6E): JG4006E (IC6E).
Material technical standards for Alloy JG4006E (IC6E): Q/6S 1978 IC6E alloy ingot specification.

Melting and casting process of Alloy JG4006E (IC6E): Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting and pouring investment precision cast parts and test bars.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy JG4006E (IC6E): Excerpted from Q/6S 1978, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1260℃±10℃ (vacuum) ×10h/Argon cooling.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy JG4006E (IC6E): Excerpted from Q/6S 1978. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter generally of 70mm～90mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied in as-cast condition or after standard heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy JG4006E (IC6E): Incipient melting point: 1315℃ (metallographic method), Melting: 1340℃～1410℃ (differential thermal analysis method).
Density of Alloy JG4006E (IC6E): ρ=7.90g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy JG4006E (IC6E): The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming process and performance of Alloy JG4006E (IC6E): The alloy has good casting performance and can be cast into hollow blades and other parts with complex shapes.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy JG4006E (IC6E): For parts without surface machining, heat treatment should be carried out in vacuum, with the specification: 1260℃±10℃ (vacuum) ×10h/Argon cooling.
Surface treatment process of Alloy JG4006E (IC6E): Use NiCrAlYSi physical deposition overlay coating, or aluminizing and AlSi coating.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy JG4006E (IC6E): No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH2018: GH2018 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with an operating temperature less than 800℃. Elements such as chromium, tungsten, and molybdenum are added for solid solution strengthening, and elements such as aluminum and titanium are added to form aging strengthening phases. The alloy has a relatively high high-temperature strength level, good oxidation resistance, hot working plasticity, welding and cold forming process performance. Main products include cold rolled thin sheets, hot rolled medium plates, etc.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH2018: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine combustion chamber components. The alloy precipitates σ phase during long-term aging at 800℃.
Material grade of Alloy GH2018: GH2018 (GH18).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH2018: GB/T 14995 Hot rolled superalloy plates; GB/T 14996 Cold rolled superalloy thin sheets; GJB 1952A Specification for cold rolled superalloy plates for aviation; HB 5199 Cold rolled superalloy thin sheets for aviation.
Melting process of Alloy GH2018: Vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting process is adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH2018: Excerpted from GB/T 14995, GB/T 14996, GJB 1952A, and HB 5199, the standard heat treatment regime for hot rolled plates and cold rolled thin sheets is: 1100℃～1150℃/AC + 800℃±10℃×16h/AC, where solution holding time depends on plate thickness.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH2018: Excerpted from GB/T 14995, GB/T 14996, GJB 1952A, and HB 5199.
Density of Alloy GH2018: ρ=8.16g/cm³ [1,3].

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH2018: Intermediate annealing for cold stamped parts and final solution treatment specification for stamped parts are consistent, which is (1120～1150)℃×(8～12)min/AC. The specification for welded aging parts is 800℃×16h/AC [3].
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH2018: No special requirements.

Typical microstructure of Alloy GH2018: The alloy is austenitic in the solution treated state, containing primary MC type carbides and trace amounts of M₃B₂ type borides. After aging treatment, γ' and M₂₃C₆ type carbides precipitate from the alloy matrix, with ω(γ') accounting for about 7% of the alloy. The alloy precipitates σ phase and M₂₃C₆ type carbides after long-term aging. After long-term aging at 800℃ for 500h, ω(M₂₃C₆+σ) increases from 0.11% to 1.58%.

Overview of Alloy GH1139: GH1139 is an Fe-Cr-Ni based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy with an operating temperature less than 950℃. The alloy is solid solution strengthened and austenite structure stabilized by adding chromium, manganese, and nitrogen elements, and grain boundaries are strengthened and purified by adding boron element. The alloy has good rupture performance and oxidation resistance, and is easy to form by welding. It is suitable for manufacturing high-temperature load-bearing parts such as combustion chambers that undergo stamping and welding. Main products include plates and bars.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH1139: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine mounts, pipe joints, crosses, casings, and rotating sleeves, with good batch production and usage conditions. No harmful phases precipitate in the alloy after long-term aging at 600℃ for 1000h.
Material grade of Alloy GH1139: GH1139 (GH139).
Similar grades to Alloy GH1139: ЭN835, 12Kh25N16G7AR (Russia).
