Material technical standards for Alloy GH1139: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; Q/3B 1063 GH1139 alloy hot rolled bars; QJ/DT 0160006 Technical conditions for GH139 alloy bar new products; Q/GYB 05003 GH1139 alloy hot rolled and forged bars.
Melting process of Alloy GH1139: Non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting process is adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH1139: Excerpted from Q/3B 1063, QJ/DT 0160006, and literature [2], the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Hot rolled and forged bars: (1050～1150)℃×(30～60)min/WQ or AC; b) Cold rolled thin sheets: 1080℃±10℃/AC, holding time determined by actual thickness of the sheet.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH1139: Excerpted from Q/3B 1063, QJ/DT 0160006, and Q/GYB 05003.
Density of Alloy GH1139: ρ=7.83g/cm³ [1].
Welding performance of Alloy GH1139: The alloy has good welding performance and can be welded by argon arc welding, etc.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH1139: Forging heat treatment regime: Quenching 1050℃～1150℃, air cooling or water cooling.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH1139: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy K452: K452 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with an operating temperature below 950℃. The alloy contains w(Co+W+Mo) 15%, w(Al+Ti+Nb) 6%, w(Cr) 22% and various trace elements for full solid solution strengthening, precipitation strengthening, and grain boundary strengthening. The alloy has good high-temperature strength and hot corrosion resistance. Main products are precision cast blades.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K452: The alloy is suitable for manufacturing turbine guide vanes and other high-temperature parts for advanced power generation and marine gas turbines working below 950℃. The alloy does not contain precious metal elements and has a lower cost. The hot corrosion resistance of the alloy is significantly better than that of the widely used K438 and IN738 alloys at home and abroad. No σ or other TCP phases precipitate in the alloy after long-term aging at 800℃~950℃ for 10000h [7].
Material grade of Alloy K452: K452 (K52, K4104).
Similar grade to Alloy K452: ChS104Y-VM (Russia).
Material technical standards for Alloy K452: Q/KJ.J02.24 K452 cast nickel-based superalloy ingot.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K452: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting, using investment precision casting with sand filling method to cast parts and specimens. Pouring temperature 1410℃~1430℃, shell temperature 850℃~950℃.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K452: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.24, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1170℃±10℃×4h/FC ((15～30)℃/min) → 900℃/AC + 1050℃±10℃×4h/AC + 850℃±10℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K452: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.24. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K452: 1304℃~1349℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K452: ρ=8.326g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K452: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K452: After standard heat treatment, the room temperature hardness of the alloy is HBS 351~334 [2].

Forming process and performance of Alloy K452: The alloy has good casting performance. Investment precision casting method can be used to manufacture single or multi-joint, hollow and solid turbine guide vanes, and sector segments. Solidification start temperature 1341℃ [1], linear shrinkage 1.95%, volumetric shrinkage 6.7%.
Welding performance of Alloy K452: Casting defects can be repaired by argon arc welding.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy K452: Same as the standard heat treatment regime of the alloy.
Surface treatment process of Alloy K452: Surface thermal barrier coating or AlSi coating; inner bore AlCo coating.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K452: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH4099: GH4099 is a Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy. It can be used for long terms below 900℃, and its short-term maximum operating temperature can reach 1000℃. The alloy is solid solution strengthened by adding chromium, cobalt, tungsten, and molybdenum elements, age-hardened by adding aluminum and titanium elements to form strengthening phases, and grain boundaries are purified and strengthened by adding boron, cerium, and magnesium elements. The alloy has high thermal strength, stable microstructure, and satisfactory cold and hot forming and welding process performance. It is suitable for manufacturing high-temperature welded structural parts such as aviation engine combustion chambers and afterburners. Main products include hot rolled bars, plates, wires, and forgings.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH4099: Alloy plates have been used to manufacture components such as adjustable nozzle casings and afterburner cylinders for aviation engines; rolled bars have been used to manufacture compressor blades for aviation engines; forged bars have been used to manufacture structural components for large marine gas turbines and cruise missiles. Large plate structural parts made of this alloy can be used directly after solution treatment without aging treatment. No harmful phases were found in the alloy after long-term aging at 700℃-950℃.
Material grade of Alloy GH4099: GH4099 (GH99).
Similar grades to Alloy GH4099: EI693, KhN68MVKYuR (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH4099: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 1952A Specification for cold rolled superalloy thin sheets for aviation; HB 5332 GH99 alloy cold rolled thin sheets; HB 5333 Technical conditions for HGH99 alloy welding wire for aviation; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; QJ/DT 0160018 Technical conditions for GH99 alloy hot rolled bars for aviation engines; QJ/DT 0160020 Technical conditions for GH99 alloy hot rolled bars for aviation engines; QJ/DT 0160021 Technical conditions for large specification GH99 alloy bars for ground gas turbines.
Melting process of Alloy GH4099: Vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum arc remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH4099: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, QJ/DT 0160018, QJ/DT 0160020, and QJ/DT 0160021, the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Cold rolled sheet: (1080～1140)℃ (max not exceeding 1160℃), air cooling or rapid cooling; holding time (8～10) min for δ≤3mm; holding time (10～15) min for δ3mm～5mm; HB≤300HV; b) Hot rolled bar: Regime I: (1080～1120)℃×1h/AC; Regime II: 1090℃±10℃×2h/AC + 900℃±10℃×5h/AC; Regime III: 1000℃±15℃×4h/AC + 700℃±10℃×16h/AC; c) Large specification forged bar: 1130℃±10℃×(30～40)min/AC + 900℃±10℃×4h/AC; d) Welding wire, solution treatment: (1100～1140)℃/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH4099: δ0.8mm～4.0mm cold rolled thin sheets; d8mm～350mm bars; d0.2mm～10.0mm cold drawn wires. Cold rolled sheets are supplied after solution + alkali/acid pickling + leveling + straightening + trimming; hot rolled and forged bars are generally supplied without heat treatment, after polishing or turning; welding wires are supplied after heat treatment + alkali/acid pickling.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH4099: 1345℃～1390℃ [3].
Density of Alloy GH4099: ρ=8.47g/cm³ [3].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH4099: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Surface treatment process of Alloy GH4099: Before heat treatment of parts, surface oil and other contaminants should be cleaned to avoid local surface corrosion during heat treatment. Iron oxide scale after heat treatment can be cleaned by sandblasting or using pickling solution containing hydrofluoric acid [18].
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH4099: No special requirements.
Phase transformation temperature of Alloy GH4099: The peak precipitation temperature of γ' phase is 800℃.

Overview of Alloy K412: K412 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with an operating temperature below 900℃. The alloy is solid solution strengthened by adding chromium, tungsten, and molybdenum elements, and precipitation strengthened by γ' phase formed by adding aluminum and titanium elements. The alloy has good oxidation resistance below 1000℃, high high-temperature strength, and good casting process performance. Main products are precision cast blades.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K412: The alloy has been used to manufacture turbine starters, gas turbine guide vanes, and other high-temperature static parts with working temperatures below 900℃. The alloy has certain strength below 900℃, but its plasticity is relatively low.
Material grade of Alloy K412: K412 (K12).
Similar grade to Alloy K412: ZhS3 (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy K412: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; YB/T 5248 Master alloys for cast superalloys; HB 7763 Specification for equiaxed cast superalloy ingots for aviation engines; HB 5159 K412 alloy ingot; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K412: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace, electroslag rollover furnace, or high-frequency rollover furnace is used for remelting and pouring parts and test bars.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K412: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1150℃±10℃×7h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K412: Excerpted from YB/T 5248, HB 7763, and HB 5159. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Density of Alloy K412: ρ=8.2g/cm³ [2].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K412: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Oxidation resistance of Alloy K412: The alloy belongs to the completely oxidation-resistant class below 1000℃ [1].

Forming process and performance of Alloy K412: Master alloy is melted in a vacuum induction furnace and remelted in a vacuum induction furnace. The pouring temperature under vacuum is controlled at 1550℃～1650℃ depending on the weight and structure of the part. When poured into ordinary molds, the linear shrinkage rate of the alloy is about 2.1%～2.3%; in hot ceramic molds, it is 0.4%～0.8%. When parts are investment precision cast, 50% of the alloy return scrap can be utilized.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy K412: Same as the standard heat treatment regime of the alloy.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K412: No special requirements.

Typical microstructure of Alloy K412: The alloy uses γ solid solution as the matrix, containing γ' phase, Ti(CN), M23C6 type carbides, and M3B2 type borides. γ' phase is the main strengthening phase of the alloy.

Overview of Alloy GH4145: GH4145 is a Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy, mainly strengthened by γ' phase aging precipitation, with an operating temperature below 800℃. The alloy has good corrosion and oxidation resistance below 980℃, high strength below 800℃, good creep resistance below 700℃, and relatively good relaxation resistance below 540℃. The alloy also has very good forming and welding performance. Main products include plates, strips, bars, profiles, forgings, ring parts, wires, and tubes.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH4145: The alloy has been used to manufacture ring parts, structural parts, and bolts for aviation engines working below 800℃ requiring high strength and corrosion resistance; flat wave springs, circumferential helical springs, helical compression springs, spring clips, and seal rings working below 540℃ with medium or lower stress requiring relaxation resistance; as well as gas turbine blades. The alloy can undergo various welding processes after annealing or solution treatment. Heat treatment of parts should be carried out in a sulfur-free neutral or reducing atmosphere to avoid sulfidation. Parts should avoid "heat ⇌ cold" treatment between 870℃ and 650℃. For large cross-section parts, to prevent cracks, they should be air-cooled after solution treatment.
Material grade of Alloy GH4145: GH4145 (GH145).
Similar grades to Alloy GH4145: Inconel X-750 (USA), NiCr15Fe7TiAl (Germany), NC15FeTNbA (France), ATGF (French company), NCF750 (Japan).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH4145: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 2612A Specification for cold drawn superalloy wires for welding; GJB 3527A Specification for cold drawn superalloy wires for springs; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; Q/3B 4088 GH145 alloy capillary tubes; Q/3B 4198 GH145 alloy cold rolled plates and strips.

Melting process of Alloy GH4145: Vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH4145: a) Supply condition: 980℃±10℃/AC or rapid cooling; b) Aging treatment: 730℃±10℃×8h/FC (55℃/h) → 620℃±10℃×8h/AC, or unlimited FC rate but total holding time shall not be less than 18h. Regime I: 650℃±10℃×4h/AC; Regime II: 1150℃±10℃×2h/AC + 840℃±10℃×24h/AC + 750℃±10℃×20h/AC; Regime III: 730℃±10℃×16h/AC. 885℃±14℃×24h±0.5h/AC + 705℃±14℃×20h±1h/AC. a) For use below 595℃: (870～900)℃×24h/AC + (690～720)℃×30h/AC; b) (970～995)℃×1h/AC + (725～740)℃×8h/FC (55℃/h) → (615～630)℃/AC, total holding time 18h.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH4145: Excerpted from GJB 2612A, GJB 3527A, Q/3B 4088, Q/3B 4198, and literature [1]. Various specifications of bars, forgings, ring parts, hot rolled plates, cold rolled plates, strips, and tubes; cold drawn wires for springs with diameter, side length, or thickness of 0.3mm～15.90mm; d0.2～10.0mm welding wires; capillary tubes with outer diameter < 5.0mm and wall thickness < 0.5mm. Plates are supplied after solution + descaling + leveling + trimming; strips are supplied in coils after solution + descaling + trimming; bars, forgings, and ring parts are supplied in forged or hot rolled condition, or machined + solution treated; bars can also be supplied after solution + polished or turned, bars smaller than 12.5mm in diameter can be supplied in cold drawn condition; tubes are supplied after cold drawing + solution + descaling, or cold drawing + bright solution treatment; welding wires are supplied in hard, semi-hard (reduction area not greater than 20%), solution + pickled, or bright solution treated (applicable for diameter not greater than 1.5mm) condition in coils or straight strips. Cold drawn wires are supplied in coils or straight strips, divided into two categories based on cold drawing deformation: Class A: Wires with nominal diameter, side length, or thickness not greater than 6.35mm, cold drawing deformation is 50%～65%; wires with nominal diameter, side length, or thickness greater than 6.35mm, cold drawing deformation is more than 30%; Class B: Wires with nominal diameter, side length, or thickness greater than 0.65mm, cold drawing deformation is about 15%.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH4145: 1395℃～1425℃ [1].
Electrical resistivity of Alloy GH4145: At 50℃, ρ=1.22×10^-6 Ωm [4].
Density of Alloy GH4145: ρ=8.25g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH4145: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Elastic properties of Alloy GH4145: At 20℃, Poisson's ratio μ=0.29 [1].
Forming process and performance of Alloy GH4145: Ingot forging cogging heating temperature 1200℃, forging temperature 1220℃～950℃, final forging temperature ≥950℃.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH4145: The alloy can be machined in various conditions, and the machining performance is good in the annealed or solution treated state.
Phase transformation temperature of Alloy GH4145: γ' precipitation temperature 595℃～815℃, precipitation peak about 800℃, begins to dissolve at 900℃, and almost completely dissolves at 970℃.

Overview of Alloy GH2984: GH2984 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with an operating temperature of 700℃~750℃. The alloy uses chromium, molybdenum, and niobium elements for solid solution strengthening, and a small amount of aluminum and titanium to form precipitation strengthening phases. The alloy has good long-term rupture strength, oxidation and corrosion resistance, good hot and cold processing performance, relatively good structural stability, and weldability. Main products include bars, tubes, plates, strips, and ring parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH2984: The alloy has been used to manufacture superheater tubes for marine and ultra-supercritical boilers working at 700℃~750℃. It has been tested in marine boilers for ten years with good results and has been put into mass use. Compared with nickel-based alloys for similar purposes, this alloy can save 32%～34% ω(Ni) and does not contain cobalt, so the cost is low. After long-term aging, a small amount of granular σ phase precipitates, which has no effect on performance. The alloy shows no intergranular corrosion in various heat treatment states and during welding.
Material grade of Alloy GH2984: GH2984 (GH984, 984).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH2984: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 5263 Specification for GH2984 alloy seamless tubes for marine high-pressure boilers; 4001.101.JD3 Technical conditions for GH2984 alloy tubes.
Melting process of Alloy GH2984: Vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting process is adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH2984: Excerpted from GJB5263, 4001.101.JD3, and literature [5], there are 3 standard heat treatment regimes: Regime I: 1100℃±10℃×1h/AC; Regime II: 1100℃±10℃×1h/AC + 750℃±10℃×8h/AC; Regime III: 1100℃±10℃×1h/AC + 750℃±10℃×8h/(50℃/h) → 650℃±10℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH2984: None. d25mm, δ2.0mm～2.5mm tubes; d20mm～450mm hot rolled and forged bars; δ4mm～14mm hot rolled plates; δ<4mm cold rolled thin sheets and strips; different specifications of ring parts. Tubes are supplied after solution treatment + pickling; hot rolled and forged bars, plates (strips), and ring parts are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH2984: Incipient melting temperature 1400℃ [5].
Density of Alloy GH2984: ρ=8.09g/cm³ [1].
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH2984: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH4079: GH4079 is a Ni-Co-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with a long-term operating temperature range of 750℃～800℃ and a short-term operating temperature of 850℃. The alloy contains a relatively large amount of aluminum, titanium, and silver elements to form γ' phase precipitation strengthening phase, with ω(γ') accounting for about 45% of the alloy. The alloy has excellent mechanical properties at high temperatures. Compared with the similar grade GH4742 alloy, the yield strength is increased by 15%, the tensile strength is increased by 5%, and the rupture limit is increased by 5%. The alloy has relatively good structural stability and corrosion resistance, suitable for manufacturing turbine disks for aviation and aerospace engines. Main products include bars, disk forgings, and die forgings.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH4079: The alloy has been used to manufacture turbine disks for aerospace engines and has passed relevant ground tests; development applications for aviation engine turbine disks and compressor disks have also been completed. Similar foreign grades have been used for aerospace engine turbine disks and are widely used for gas turbine engine turbine disks and compressor disks below 800℃. Since the alloy belongs to the critical deformation alloy, its hot working plasticity is very poor, the applicable temperature range for hot working is very narrow, and hot working parameters directly affect the microstructure and properties of the alloy.
Material grade of Alloy GH4079: GH7079 (GH742Y).
Similar grades to Alloy GH4079: EK79, EP742U (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH4079: Liaoxin 6-0025 Technical conditions for GH742Y alloy turbine disk forgings for aerospace; Q/GYB 05127 GH4079 alloy forged round cakes; Q/GYB 05126 GH4079 alloy hot rolled and forged bars.
Melting process of Alloy GH4079: Vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting process is adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH4079: Excerpted from Liaoxin 6-0025, Q/GYB 05126, and Q/GYB 05127, the standard heat treatment regime for bars and disk forgings is: 1040℃±10℃×4h/AC + (1130～1150)℃±10℃×8h/AC + 850℃±15℃×6h/AC + 780℃±10℃×(10～16)h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH4079: Excerpted from Liaoxin 6-0025, Q/GYB 05126, and Q/GYB 05127. d50mm～150mm hot rolled and forged bars; d150mm～350mm forged round cakes; d100mm～250mm die forgings. Bars are generally supplied without heat treatment after machining; disk forgings are supplied after heat treatment + machining + ultrasonic testing.
Electrical resistivity of Alloy GH4079: At 20℃, ρ=1.388×10^-6 Ω·m [1].
Density of Alloy GH4079: ρ=8.32g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH4079: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Oxidation resistance of Alloy GH4079: The alloy has good oxidation resistance below 700℃.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH4079: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for the product variety.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH4079: No special requirements.
Phase transformation temperature of Alloy GH4079: γ' phase begins to dissolve at 1060℃ and completely dissolves at 1167℃.

Overview of Alloy GH4137 (SG37A): GH4137 (SG37A) is a Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with an operating temperature below 700℃. The alloy has high strength and high-temperature oxidation resistance. It also possesses good elastic modulus, wear resistance, hot working plasticity, welding, and cold forming process performance. Main products are cold drawn wires.
Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH4137 (SG37A): The alloy has been used to manufacture new advanced brush seals for aviation engines.
Material grade of Alloy GH4137 (SG37A): GH4137 (SG37A).
Material technical standards for Alloy GH4137 (SG37A): Q/HDBYG 015 Wires for brush seals.
Melting process of Alloy GH4137 (SG37A): Vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH4137 (SG37A): Excerpted from literature [1] and Q/HDBYG 015, the alloy is used in the solution treated state. The standard heat treatment regime is (1100～1150)℃/AC, holding time determined by wire diameter.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH4137 (SG37A): Excerpted from Q/HDBYG 015. d0.04mm～0.2mm cold drawn wires. Wires are supplied in cold drawn state or after heat treatment.
Density of Alloy GH4137 (SG37A): ρ=7.9g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH4137 (SG37A): The alloy is non-magnetic.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH4137 (SG37A): Same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH4137 (SG37A): No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH4648: GH4648 is a Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with a long-term operating temperature less than 900℃ and a short-term operating temperature up to 1100℃. The alloy is characterized by the addition of about ω(Cr) 32%, giving it excellent hot corrosion resistance. At the same time, the alloy has medium strength, good fatigue and creep resistance, and good cold working and welding performance. It is suitable for manufacturing high-temperature structural parts requiring excellent corrosion resistance at temperatures of 900℃~1100℃. Main supplied varieties include plates, strips, tubes, bars, forgings, and ring parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH4648: The alloy has been used to manufacture advanced aviation engine combustion chamber components and other hot-end parts, with good batch production and usage conditions. Similar grades have been extremely widely used in foreign aviation engines. The comprehensive performance of the alloy is superior to domestic GH3044 and GH3128 levels. Since GH4648 has a lower specific gravity than GH3044, the alloy has higher specific strength.
Material grade of Alloy GH4648: GH4648 (GH648).
Similar grade to Alloy GH4648: EP648 (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH4648: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; Liaoxin 7-0085 Technical conditions for GH648 alloy cake new products; Liaoxin 7-0068 Technical conditions for GH648 alloy forged and rolled bar new products; Liaoxin 7-0079 Technical conditions for GH648 alloy cold rolled sheet new products; Liaoxin 7-0084 Technical conditions for GH648 alloy sheet and strip new products; Q/GYB 05061 GH4648 ring blank technical agreement; Q/3B 1143 GH4648 alloy welding wire.
Melting process of Alloy GH4648: Vacuum induction furnace, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH4648: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, Liaoxin 7-0085, Liaoxin 7-0068, Liaoxin 7-0079, Liaoxin 7-0084, Q/GYB05061, the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Forged and rolled bars: 1150℃±20℃×1h/AC + 900℃±20℃×16h/AC; b) Plates and strips: 1140℃±10℃/AC + 900℃±20℃×6h/AC, with solution holding time (1~2) min/mm; c) Cakes, ring parts: 1140℃±20℃×1h/AC + 900℃±20℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH4648: Excerpted from Liaoxin 7-0085, Liaoxin 7-0068, Liaoxin 7-0079, Liaoxin 7-0084, Q/GYB 05061, and Q/3B 1143. d≤300mm bars; δ0.5mm~5.0mm plates; δ0.25mm~0.8mm strips; d0.2mm~10.0mm welding wires; various specifications of round cakes and ring parts. Plates and strips are supplied after solution + pickling + leveling + straightening + trimming; welding wires are supplied in hard, semi-hard, solution + pickled, or bright solution treated condition in coils, or can be supplied in straight strips; bars, cakes, and ring parts are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH4648: 1336℃~1353℃ [1].
Density of Alloy GH4648: ρ=8.28g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH4648: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH4648: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for the product variety.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH4648: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloys GH3536 and K4536: GH3536 is a Ni-Cr-Fe based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy, and its corresponding grade for casting use is K4536. The alloy has medium rupture and creep strength below 900℃; good oxidation and corrosion resistance, and good cold and hot forming and welding performance. It is suitable for manufacturing aviation engine combustion chambers and other components used for a long time below 900℃, as well as high-temperature components for short-term use up to 1080℃. Main products include plates, strips, tubes, bars, forgings, ring parts, and precision castings.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloys GH3536 and K4536: The alloy has been extremely widely used in foreign aviation engines and civil industries. In China, it has been used to manufacture aviation engine combustion chamber components, honeycomb structures, diffusers, exhaust nozzles, and other hot-end parts. The cooling rate in the finished alloy quenching process is one of the important factors affecting alloy technology; rapid cooling can control the precipitation of brittle carbide films at grain boundaries [8]. When the solution temperature of the alloy is higher than 1175℃, carbide films are also prone to form at grain boundaries [3]. After long-term aging, the alloy exhibits a certain degree of age hardening, resulting in decreased plasticity and reduced high-temperature strength [8].
Material grades of Alloys GH3536 and K4536: GH3536 (GH536, SG-5), K4536 (K536).
Similar grades to Alloys GH3536 and K4536: UNS N06002, Hastelloy X (USA), NC22FeD (France), NiCr22FeMo (Germany), Nimonic PE13 (UK).

Material technical standards for Alloys GH3536 and K4536: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 1952A Specification for cold rolled superalloy plates for aviation; GJB 2297A Specification for cold drawn (rolled) superalloy seamless tubes for aviation; GJB 2612A Specification for cold drawn superalloy wires for welding; GJB 3020A Specification for superalloy ring blanks for aviation; GJB 3165A Specification for hot rolled and forged superalloy bars for aviation load-bearing parts; HB 5496 GH536 alloy round cakes, ring blanks, and ring parts; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; Q/GYB 05099 Master alloy for K4536 casting; Q/GYB 05104 K4536 alloy investment castings; Q/GYB 05115 GH3536 alloy cold rolled strips.

Heat treatment regime of Alloys GH3536 and K4536: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, Q/GYB 05104, and Q/GYB 05115, the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Hot rolled and forged bars, round cakes, ring parts: 1175℃±10℃, air cooling or faster cooling, holding time ≥20min, HB≤241; b) Tubes: 1130℃～1170℃, rapid cooling, holding time ≤30min; c) Cold rolled plates: 1130℃～1170℃, rapid air cooling or water cooling; d) Cold rolled strips: (1065～1105)℃±10℃, holding time ≤30min, rapid air cooling; e) Forgings: 1175℃±10℃, air cooling or faster cooling, holding time ≥50min (calculated by section thickness); f) Castings and test blocks: 800℃±10℃×50h/AC.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloys GH3536 and K4536: Excerpted from GJB 1952A, GJB 2297A, GJB 3020A, GJB 3165A, HB 5496, Q/GYB 05099, Q/GYB 05104, and Q/GYB 05115.
Melting temperature range of Alloys GH3536 and K4536: 1295℃～1381℃ [1].
Density of Alloys GH3536 and K4536: ρ=8.28g/cm³ [9].
Magnetic properties of Alloys GH3536 and K4536: The alloys are non-magnetic.

Welding performance of Alloys GH3536 and K4536: The alloy has good welding process performance and can be welded by argon arc welding, seam welding, and spot welding. HGH3536 or HGH113 welding wire is recommended for argon arc welding.

Part heat treatment process of Alloys GH3536 and K4536 : a) Solution treatment for sheet metal parts: 1150℃±10℃×(2～15)min/AC (rapid); b) Treatment for welded parts or flame tubes: 980℃±10℃×60min/AC; c) Stress relief treatment after machining: 870℃±10℃×(30～60)min/AC; d) Part dimensional stabilization treatment: 760℃±10℃×2h/AC.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloys GH3536 and K4536: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy JG1302 (TAC-1B): JG1302 is a Ti₃Al based intermetallic high-temperature structural material with added niobium to improve plasticity and toughness. Components can work for a long time below 650℃ and for a short time at 900℃. The alloy has excellent high-temperature mechanical properties, oxidation resistance, and weldability, as well as good hot working plasticity, and can be cold formed. Main products include bars, plates, foils, cakes, and ring parts.
Material grade of Alloy JG1302 (TAC-1B): JG1302 (TAC-1B).
Melting process of Alloy JG1302 (TAC-1B): Multiple remelting process in vacuum consumable arc furnace is adopted.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy JG1302 (TAC-1B): None. d10mm～300mm bars; δ2mm～20mm hot rolled plates; δ0.3mm～4.0mm cold rolled thin sheets; δ0.05mm～0.2mm foil plates; various specifications of cakes and ring parts. Bars, cakes, and ring parts are supplied after standard heat treatment or annealing, either directly or after surface machining; hot rolled plates are supplied after standard heat treatment or annealing + sandblasting + straightening + trimming; cold rolled sheets and foils are supplied after cold rolling or annealing + trimming.
Density of Alloy JG1302 (TAC-1B): p=4.94g/cm³ [1,2].
Magnetic properties of Alloy JG1302 (TAC-1B): The alloy is non-magnetic.
Surface treatment process of Alloy JG1302 (TAC-1B): No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy K477: K477 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with an operating temperature below 900℃. Elements such as chromium, cobalt, molybdenum, aluminum, titanium, and niobium are added to the alloy for solid solution and precipitation strengthening, giving the alloy relatively good comprehensive properties. The alloy has high strength and plasticity, stable microstructure, and good casting performance. It is suitable for manufacturing high-temperature parts such as aviation engine gas turbine blades and turbine outer rings.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K477: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine turbine outer rings, with good batch production and usage conditions. No TCP phase precipitation was found in the alloy after long-term aging at 850℃~950℃.
Material grade of Alloy K477: K477 (K77).
Similar grade to Alloy K477: René 77 (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy K477: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; HB 7763 Specification for equiaxed cast superalloy ingots for aviation engines; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K477: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting, using investment precision casting method to cast parts and specimens.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K477: Excerpted from HB/Z140, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1160℃±10℃×2h/FC → 1080℃/AC + 760℃±10℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K477: Excerpted from HB 7763. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K477: 1210℃~1290℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K477: ρ=7.95g/cm³ [2].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K477: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and performance of Alloy K477: The forming and casting performance of the alloy is good. Turbine blades and integral turbine outer rings can be cast, with a casting shrinkage rate of about 2%.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy K477: Same as the standard heat treatment regime of the alloy.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K477: No special requirements.

Typical microstructure of Alloy K477: The as-cast microstructure of the alloy consists of γ solid solution, γ phase, (γ+γ') eutectic, MC type carbides, and M3B2 type borides. The γ' phase is the main strengthening phase of the alloy, with w(γ') accounting for about 60% of the alloy; (γ+γ') eutectic exists at grain boundaries and between dendrites, accounting for about 6%～7% of the alloy volume; MC exists in blocky and strip shapes, with w(MC) accounting for about 0.8% of the alloy; w(M3B2) accounts for about 1% of the alloy. After long-term aging at 850℃~950℃, γ' phase aggregates and grows, and M6C type carbides precipitate, but no TCP phase precipitation is found.

Overview of Alloy DZ422B: DZ422B is a nickel-based precipitation hardening directionally solidified columnar crystal superalloy with an operating temperature below 1050℃. The alloy has good comprehensive properties at medium and high temperatures as well as excellent thermal fatigue resistance, and its microstructure is stable during long-term high-temperature use. The alloy still has good casting performance under thin-walled conditions, ensuring the safety and reliability of thin-walled blades [1]. It is suitable for manufacturing gas turbine rotor blades and other high-temperature parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy DZ422B: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine gas turbine rotor blades working below 980℃, which have been put into airline application with good batch production and usage conditions. Similar foreign alloys have been widely used in aviation engine turbine blades. Parts surface treated with aluminizing, AlSi coating, or vapor deposition coating further improves the alloy's oxidation and hot corrosion resistance. No TCP phase precipitated in the alloy after long-term aging at 800℃ for 5000h.
Material grade of Alloy DZ422B: DZ422B (DZ22B).
Similar grade to Alloy DZ422B: NW12KCATHf (France).

Material technical standards for Alloy DZ422B: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; HB 7762 Specification for directionally solidified columnar and single crystal superalloy ingots for aviation engines; HB/Z140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.

Melting process of Alloy DZ422B: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum directional solidification furnace is used for remelting and pouring directionally solidified test bars and parts.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy DZ422B: Excerpted from HB/Z140 and HB7762, there are two standard heat treatment regimes for the alloy: Regime I: 1175℃±10℃×0.33h → 1220 (+5, -10)℃×0.5h/AC → 1230 (+5, -10)℃×0.5h/AC + 1080℃±10℃×(4～6)h/AC + 870℃±10℃×32h/AC. Regime II: 1175℃×0.5h + 1205℃×2h/AC + 1080℃×4h/AC + 870℃×32h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy DZ422B: Excerpted from HB7762. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of d75mm～90mm and a length of about 1m. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy DZ422B: 1315℃～1374℃ (differential thermal analysis method) [1].
Density of Alloy DZ422B: ρ=8.56g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy DZ422B: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and performance of Alloy DZ422B: The casting performance of the alloy is good, and it can be cast into thin-walled directionally solidified hollow turbine blades and parts with complex internal cavities.
Welding performance of Alloy DZ422B: Vacuum brazing can be performed.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy DZ422B: Parts without surface machining (such as blades) should be heat treated under vacuum or protective atmosphere according to the prescribed regime, and cooled with argon gas.
Surface treatment process of Alloy DZ422B: Parts can be treated with aluminizing, AlSi coating, or vapor deposition coating.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy DZ422B: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH4080A: GH4080A is a Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with an operating temperature below 800℃. The alloy is mainly strengthened by adding aluminum and titanium elements to form γ' phase precipitation. The alloy has good creep resistance and oxidation resistance in the range of 650℃～850℃. The cold and hot working performance of the alloy is good. Main products include hot rolled and forged bars, cold drawn bars, hot rolled plates, cold rolled sheets, strips, and ring parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH4080A: The alloy has been used to manufacture rotor blades, guide vane supports, sector mounting rings, bolts, blade lock plates, etc. for aviation engines. In addition, it is also used to manufacture automotive engine fasteners and blades, as well as train valves and shaft parts. In recent years, with the development of domestic and foreign shipbuilding industries, this alloy is widely used to manufacture marine engine valves. No TCP phase precipitates in the alloy after long-term aging at 700℃～850℃ for 1000h.
Material grade of Alloy GH4080A: GH4080A (GH80A).
Similar grade to Alloy GH4080A: Nimonic 80A (UK).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH4080A: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; WS9-7009 Hot rolled bars for GH80A alloy turbine blades; WS9-7011 GH80A alloy hot rolled, forged and cold drawn bars; WS9-7011 Ring parts GH80A alloy rolled ring parts; WS9-7095 GH80A alloy hot rolled plates, cold rolled thin sheets and strips; WS9-7156 GH80A alloy cold drawn (rolled) seamless tubes; Liaoxin 6-0051 Technical conditions for GH80A alloy hot rolled and forged bars for vehicles and ships; QJ/DT 01.63018 Technical conditions for GH80A alloy hot rolled and forged bars for steam turbine blades; QJ/DT 01.63019 Technical conditions for GH80A alloy forgings for valves; QJ/DT 01.63022 Technical conditions for GH80A alloy round bars for diesel engine valves.
Melting process of Alloy GH4080A: Vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting processes are adopted.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH4080A: Excerpted from WSP-7009, WSP-7011, WSP-7001 Ring parts, WSP-7095, WSP-7156, Liaoxin 6-0051, QJ/DT 01.63018, QJ/DT 01.63019, and QJ/DT 01.63022. d20mm～55mm hot rolled bars for blades; d≤300mm hot rolled and forged bars; d8mm～45mm round cold drawn bars and inscribed circle d8mm～36mm hexagonal cold drawn bars; rolled ring parts with OD 1000mm, ID 900mm, thickness 130mm; δ≤9.5mm hot rolled plates; δ≤4.0mm cold rolled thin sheets; δ≤0.8mm cold rolled strips; various specifications of shafts and forgings are supplied. Hot rolled bars for blades are supplied without heat treatment, with fully polished or turned surfaces; hot rolled bars for machining are supplied after solution treatment + descaling; cold drawn bars for heading are supplied after polishing; cold drawn bars for machining are supplied after solution treatment + descaling; bars for hot working are supplied after descaling (bars for forging plants should be supplied turned, with surface roughness not less than 3.2μm); rolled ring parts are supplied after solution treatment + rough machining; hot rolled plates, cold rolled sheets, and strips are supplied after softening treatment + alkali/acid pickling + trimming + leveling or straightening; shafts and forgings are supplied after rough turning.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH4080A: Melting point 1405℃ [1].
Electrical resistivity of Alloy GH4080A: At 20℃, ρ=1.34×10^-6 Ω·m [2].
Density of Alloy GH4080A: ρ=8.15g/cm³ [2].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH4080A: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH4080A: Stamped parts of plates and strips need intermediate vacuum annealing after each forming, with a regime of 1060℃±10℃, 10 min, argon fan cooling. The final heat treatment of parts is aging treatment in a vacuum furnace, with a regime of 750℃±10℃, 4h, argon fan cooling. For local work hardening generated during the manufacturing process of blades after solution (before aging), surface annealing protected by argon or hydrogen should be performed according to specified requirements, with annealing temperature at 1070℃～1090℃.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH4080A: The alloy has good machining performance in fully heat-treated condition.

Overview of Alloy K4242: K4242 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with an operating temperature below 800℃. Elements such as cobalt, molybdenum, and chromium are added to the alloy for solid solution strengthening, and carbides are used as precipitation strengthening phases. The alloy has a lower strength level but better oxidation and corrosion resistance, and has better casting and welding performance. Main products are investment precision cast structural parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K4242: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine components requiring high oxidation resistance, such as intake sections 1 and 3 mounting edges, high-pressure stage I and II guide vane sector positioning parts, and inner covers. The similar C242 alloy has been maturely applied in foreign aviation engines.
Material grade of Alloy K4242: K4242 (K242).
Similar grade to Alloy K4242: C242 (UK), NIMOCAST 242 (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy K4242: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 5512.1 Specification for cast superalloy master alloys for cast superalloys and cast intermetallic high temperature materials - Part 1: Master alloys for ordinary precision castings; HB 7763 Specification for equiaxed cast superalloy ingots for aviation engines; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K4242: Vacuum induction furnace or non-vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting and pouring investment precision castings and test bars.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K4242: Excerpted from HB/Z 140 and HB 7763, the alloy is used in as-cast condition or after stress relief annealing. Stress relief annealing regime: 750℃±10℃×2h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K4242: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1 and HB 7763. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and castings are supplied in as-cast condition or after annealing treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K4242: 1340℃～1370℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K4242: ρ=8.41g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K4242: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming process and performance of Alloy K4242: The casting performance of the alloy is good. Thin-walled castings can be cast.
Welding performance of Alloy K4242: The welding performance of the alloy is good, and castings can be repair welded.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy K4242: Stress relief annealing regime: 750℃±10℃×2h/AC.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K4242: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy K825: K825 is a chromium-based equiaxed cast superalloy with high plasticity. The alloy can work for a long time below 700℃ and for a short time at 1000℃. The composition of the alloy is simple, containing no rare precious elements, and the cost is low. Compared with nickel-based alloys, it has the advantages of higher melting point, higher thermal conductivity, lower coefficient of linear expansion, higher elastic modulus, smaller density, and good high-temperature oxidation resistance and thermal fatigue resistance. Main products are investment cast structural parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K825: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine afterburner igniter flow dividers, performing well after 200h test run without coating, and is now in mass production. The alloy is also suitable for manufacturing cast structural castings working long-term in alternating temperature fields below 700℃ and short-term at 1000℃. After aging at 800℃×300h, the microstructure and properties of the alloy are stable, and the room temperature plasticity does not change.
Material grade of Alloy K825: K825 (K25).
Similar grade to Alloy K825: VX-4 (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy K825: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; HB 7763 Specification for equiaxed cast superalloy ingots for aviation engines; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K825: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting, using investment precision casting pouring method to cast parts and test bars. Pouring temperature is 1450℃～1550℃, mold shell temperature is 850℃～950℃.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K825: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, the alloy is used in the as-cast condition.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K825: Excerpted from HB 7763. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm. Master alloy and precision castings are supplied in as-cast condition.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K825: 1312℃～1435℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K825: ρ=7.98g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K825: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K825: In as-cast condition, room temperature hardness is HBS 320～290 [1].
Impact properties of Alloy K825: In as-cast condition, room temperature impact toughness αKU=30kJ/m²～41kJ/m² [1].
Forming process and performance of Alloy K825: Parts of various shapes can be cast using investment casting method.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy K825: Parts are used in the as-cast condition.
Surface treatment process of Alloy K825: The alloy can be used without coating.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K825: The alloy is easy to machine and does not exhibit grinding cracks.

Overview of Alloy GH1040: GH1040 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy, solid solution strengthened and austenite matrix stabilized by elements such as chromium and molybdenum. The alloy has high thermal strength indicators below 700℃ and high instantaneous strength at 900℃～1000℃. The alloy has good heat resistance and corrosion resistance, good structural stability, good hot working plasticity, welding, and cold forming process performance. The alloy's strength can be significantly improved after warm working strengthening. Main products include cakes, forgings, bars, seamless tubes, plates, strips, and wires.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH1040: The alloy has been used to manufacture gas turbine welded rotors, shafts, and fasteners, as well as short-term working rocket engine components. After warm working strengthening process, the alloy can be used to manufacture turbine disks working below 650℃. This alloy is a carbide-strengthened material. Carbides between cast dendrites are not easily broken during forging, and obvious dendritic structures will remain on disk forgings.
Material grade of Alloy GH1040: GH1040 (GH40).
Similar grades to Alloy GH1040: ЭN395 (Russia), 16-25-6 (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH1040: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GB/T 14994 Cold drawn superalloy bars; GJB 2611A Specification for cold drawn superalloy bars for aviation; GJB 2612A Specification for cold drawn superalloy wires for welding; YB/T 5247 Cold drawn superalloy wires for welding.

Melting process of Alloy GH1040: Electric arc furnace, or non-vacuum induction furnace, or electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH1040: Excerpted from GB/T 14994, GJB2611A, literature [5], and literature [6], the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Cold drawn bars: 1200℃×1h/AC + 700℃×16h/AC; b) Bars: 1160℃～1180℃×2h/WQ + 700℃×5h/AC; c) Disk material: 1200℃×8h/WQ + Warm working strengthening (deformation 10%～12%) + 700℃×25h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH1040: d8mm～45mm round bars; a8mm～30mm square bars; d (inscribed circle) 8mm～36mm hexagonal bars; d0.3mm～10mm welding wires; various sizes and specifications of disk forgings. Cold drawn bars are supplied in cold drawn, solution (annealed), solution (annealed) + pickled, or solution (annealed) + polished state; welding wires are supplied in cold drawn, semi-hardened, or solution state; disk forgings are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Density of Alloy GH1040: ρ=8.08g/cm³ [5].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH1040: The alloy is slightly magnetic after warm working strengthening process.
Welding performance of Alloy GH1040: The welding performance of the alloy is good.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH1040: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH1040: No special requirements, same as general heat-resistant steels.

Typical microstructure of Alloy GH1040: The alloy is austenite in the solution state. After long-term aging at 700℃～900℃, dispersed composite carbonitrides (Fe, Ni)₂(Mo, Cr)₄(C, N) precipitate, which have high thermal stability and strengthen the alloy.

Overview of Alloy GH4738: GH4738 is a Ni-Cr-Co based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with an operating temperature below 815℃. The alloy is solid solution strengthened by adding cobalt, chromium, and molybdenum elements, precipitation strengthened by adding aluminum and titanium elements to form γ', and grain boundaries are purified and strengthened by adding boron and zirconium elements. The alloy has high yield strength and fatigue resistance at 760℃~870℃; good oxidation and corrosion resistance in gas turbine atmospheres below 870℃; good processing plasticity and stable structural properties. It is suitable for manufacturing turbine disks, working blades, high-temperature fasteners, flame tubes, shafts, and turbine casings. Main products include cold rolled and hot rolled plates, tubes, strips, wires, forgings, castings, and bolt fasteners.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH4738: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine turbine disks, blades, seal rings, etc., as well as flue gas turbine blades, turbine disks, and large bolts. The alloy is widely used abroad in rotating parts of aviation, aerospace, petroleum, chemical, and power generation equipment, such as compressor blades, turbine disks, valve bodies, ring parts, and shafts. The alloy has good oxidation and corrosion resistance in various atmospheres encountered in jet engines or similar working conditions. The continuous working oxidation resistance temperature can reach 1040℃, and intermittent working up to 870℃. The alloy also has good resistance to salt spray corrosion, especially in the solution-treated state. Notched rupture tests of bars at 650℃, 750℃, and 815℃ after 1080℃×4h/AC + stabilization + aging treatment show that the alloy has no notch sensitivity. No new phase precipitates in the alloy after long-term aging at 650℃ and 730℃ for up to 3000h, and the microstructure is stable.
Material grade of Alloy GH4738: GH4738 (GH864, GH738).
Similar grades to Alloy GH4738: Waspaloy (USA), NC20K14 (France).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH4738: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; Q/5B 4017 GH738 alloy bars; Liaoxin 6-0044 Technical agreement for trial production of GH4738 alloy smoke turbine disks; Liaoxin 6-0045 Technical conditions for GH4738 alloy cold drawn bars for high temperature fasteners; QJ/DT 01.63026 Technical conditions for new GH4738 alloy cold rolled strip products; QJ/DT 01.63056 Technical conditions for GH4738 alloy forged bars; QJ/DT 01.63080 Technical conditions for new GH4738 alloy hot rolled bar products; QJ/DT 01.63099 Technical conditions for new GH4738 alloy hot rolled bar products for flue gas turbine blades.

Melting process of Alloy GH4738: Vacuum induction furnace + vacuum arc (or consumable furnace) remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting + vacuum arc remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH4738: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, Q/5B 4017, Liaoxin 6-0044, Liaoxin 6-0045, QJ/DT01.63026, QJ/DT 01.63056, QJ/DT 01.63080, QJ/DT 01.63099, literature [21], and literature [22]. Complete heat treatment for various products includes: solution treatment + stabilization treatment + aging treatment. Among them: Solution treatment for various products: a) Cold drawn bars: (1040~1080)℃±10℃×(1~4)h/AC or faster cooling; b) Hot rolled bars: 1080℃±10℃×4h/AC or faster cooling; c) Cold drawn wires: (996~1038)℃±10℃/AC or air cooling; d) Cold rolled plates, strips, annealing treatment (supply state): 1080℃±10℃/AC or faster cooling, holding time ≤30min; strip solution treatment in protective atmosphere: 995℃±15℃×2h±0.25h/AC or faster cooling; e) Forged bars, disk forgings, ring parts: (1000~1040)℃±10℃×4h/AC or faster cooling. Stabilization treatment: 845℃+8℃×4.0h±0.25h/AC. Aging treatment: 760℃±8℃×16h±1h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH4738: Excerpted from Q/5B 4017, Liaoxin 6-0044, Liaoxin 6-0045, QJ/DT 01.63026, QJ/DT 01.63056, QJ/DT 01.63080, and QJ/DT 01.63099. d≤25mm cold drawn bars; d≤80mm hot rolled bars; d100mm~300mm forged bars; δ0.05mm~0.80mm cold rolled strips; d0.2mm~10.0mm wires; various specifications of ring and disk forgings. Hot rolled bars are supplied in hot rolled state, or solution + descaled; forged bars are supplied in forged + turned state; cold drawn bars, Grade A via 8%~12% cold deformation, polished if necessary. Grade B via 8%~12% cold deformation + solution + polished. Grade C via 8%~12% cold deformation + solution + stabilization + aging + polished; soft strips are supplied after solution + alkali/acid pickling + trimming in coils, bright solution treated ones do not require pickling; hard strips are supplied after cold rolling + annealing + polishing + trimming, bright annealed ones do not require polishing; cold drawn wires are supplied in bright solution state, or solution + pickled, in coils or straight strips; welding wires are supplied in hard, or semi-hard (reduction area not greater than 20%), or solution + pickled, or bright solution treated state, in coils (spools) or straight strips; ring and disk forgings are generally supplied without heat treatment, after rough machining or descaling.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH4738: 1330℃~1360℃ [20].
Density of Alloy GH4738: ρ=8.22g/cm³ [20].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH4738: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Corrosion resistance of Alloy GH4738: The alloy has good resistance to salt spray corrosion.

Welding performance of Alloy GH4738: Welding this alloy is relatively difficult; argon arc welding can be used. Generally, welding is performed in the solution state using the same alloy as filler wire. The high-temperature strength of the weld after heat treatment is slightly lower than that of the base metal after heat treatment, so the weld should not be located in high-stress areas. In addition, due to the alloy's hot shortness and strain cracking sensitivity, welding should be performed in the solution state, and the welded components must undergo solution treatment again.

Surface treatment process of Alloy GH4738: When the alloy is used as rotating parts such as turbine blades and turbine disks, argon-protected stress relief annealing and shot peening are performed to improve fatigue performance. Diffusion aluminizing treatment can be performed to improve the gas corrosion resistance and thermal fatigue resistance of turbine blades.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH4738: No special requirements.

Phase transformation temperature of Alloy GH4738: Dissolution temperature of γ' phase is 980℃~1050℃, starts to precipitate from the matrix at 630℃, precipitation peak is 800℃; M23C6 type carbide starts to precipitate at 700℃, complete dissolution temperature is 1020℃.

Overview of Alloy GH4141: GH4141 is a Ni-Cr-Co based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy, mainly strengthened by aging precipitation γ' phase and M6C type carbides, with an operating temperature below 980℃. The alloy has high tensile, rupture, and creep strength, yield and fatigue resistance, and good oxidation resistance in the range of 650℃～900℃. It is one of the sheet alloys with the highest mechanical properties for use below 980℃. The alloy has good machining performance and is suitable for manufacturing turbine disks, guide vanes, combustion chamber sheet load-bearing parts, turbine rotors, guides, fasteners, and high-temperature springs for aviation and aerospace engines requiring high strength below 870℃ and oxidation resistance below 980℃. Main products include hot rolled and forged bars, plates and strips, wires, disk forgings, and ring parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH4141: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine combustion chamber inner liner front and rear sections, high-pressure turbine outer ring support rings and fixing rings, high-pressure guide outer rings and inner support rings, seal support ring rear mounting edges, combustion chamber rear casings, seals and fasteners, as well as rocket engine turbine rotors, with good batch production and usage conditions. Due to the high content of aluminum, titanium, and molybdenum in the alloy, ingot forging cogging is relatively difficult, but plasticity is better after cogging. The alloy can be cold stamped and spun after annealing. The alloy can be welded by fusion welding and contact welding. Before welding, overaging treatment or controlled cooling rate after solution treatment should be performed to avoid strain age cracking during fusion welding.
Material grade of Alloy GH4141: GH4141 (GH141).
Similar grades to Alloy GH4141: UNS N07041, René 41, R41, Carpenter 41 (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH4141: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 2456 Specification for GH4141 superalloy hot rolled bars for aerospace; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; Q/3B 4060 GH141 alloy bars; Q/3B 4063 GH141 alloy cold rolled strips; Q/5B 4027 GH141 alloy round cakes, ring blanks, ring parts; Liaoxin 7-0073 GH141 alloy forged bars; Liaoxin 7-0099 GH141 alloy cold drawn bars for high temperature fasteners; Liaoxin 7-0077 Technical conditions for GH141 alloy hot rolled bars for aerospace; Q/GYB 05023 GH4141 alloy die forgings for aerospace; Q/GYB 05028 HGH4141 alloy cold drawn wires for welding.
Melting process of Alloy GH4141: Vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting processes are adopted.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH4141: Excerpted from GJB 2456, Q/3B 4060, Q3B 4063, Q5B 4027, Liaoxin 7-0073, Liaoxin 7-0099, Liaoxin 7-0077, Q/GYB 05023, and Q/GYB 05028. d100mm～300mm forged bars; d8mm～100mm hot rolled bars; δ0.5mm～4.0mm cold rolled plates; δ0.10mm～0.8mm cold rolled strips; d10mm～25mm cold drawn bars; d1.0mm～5.0mm welding wires; various specifications of round cakes, ring blanks, and ring parts. Forged bars are supplied forged + turned; hot rolled bars are supplied hot rolled + turned or polished, or annealed + turned or polished; cold rolled plates are supplied after solution + alkali/acid pickling + leveling + straightening + trimming; cold rolled strips are supplied after solution + alkali/acid pickling + trimming in coils, bright solution treated ones do not require pickling; disk forgings and ring forgings are supplied forged, or annealed + solution, or annealed + solution + aged. Die forgings are supplied after heat treatment + rough machining + turning. Welding wires are supplied in hard, semi-hard (reduction area not greater than 20%), solution + pickled, or bright solution state, in coils (spools) or straight strips. Supplied cold drawn bars are divided into three grades: Grade A with 8%～12% cold drawing deformation, polished if necessary; Grade B with 8%～12% cold drawing deformation + annealed + polished; Grade C with 8%～12% cold drawing deformation + annealed + solution + aged + polished.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH4141: Melting point 1316℃～1371℃ [2].
Density of Alloy GH4141: ρ=8.27g/cm³ [2].

Welding performance of Alloy GH4141: The alloy can be welded by fusion welding, diffusion welding, brazing, and friction welding. Fusion welding can use electron beam welding or argon arc welding. To eliminate the tendency of weld strain age cracking before welding, solution treatment or overaging treatment is required. Solution treatment regime is 1080℃, cool to 650℃ at 22℃/min; overaging treatment regime is 1080℃×30min, cool to 980℃ at (1.7～4.4)℃/min, hold for 4h, cool to 760℃ at (1.7～4.4)℃/min, hold for 4h, air cool [9, 16~19]. After welding, when eliminating welding stress and restoring properties, rapid heating through the age hardening temperature range should be used to eliminate strain age cracking tendency. Fine grain and low impurity content base metal can eliminate machining hardening of the alloy; low welding heat input can also reduce strain age cracking tendency.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH4141: When parts work at lower temperatures and require high tensile strength and fatigue resistance, 1080℃/AC + 760℃×16h/AC can be used; when working at higher temperatures requiring high thermal strength, 1180℃/AC + 900℃×4h/AC can be used; components such as ring parts requiring welding can use 1120℃×30min/AC + 900℃×4h/AC. Depending on the needs of the finished product, the aging holding time can be reduced to 1h.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH4141: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH2787: GH2787 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with a long-term operating temperature range of 500℃～750℃ and a maximum operating temperature of 800℃. The alloy is solid solution strengthened by adding chromium and tungsten elements, precipitation strengthened by adding aluminum and titanium elements, and grain boundaries are purified and strengthened by adding boron and cerium elements. The alloy has high strength level and good hot working plasticity. Main products include disk parts, forgings, forged bars, and hot rolled bars.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH2787: The alloy can be used to manufacture gas turbine or other engine working blades operating at 700℃～800℃, compressor blades operating at 500℃～700℃, and turbine disks, deflectors, and ring parts operating up to 750℃ [2].
Material grade of Alloy GH2787: GH2787 (GH78, GH787).
Similar grades to Alloy GH2787: EI787-VD, KhN35VTYu-VD (Russia).
Material technical standards for Alloy GH2787: QJ/DT 01.63015 Technical conditions for GH2787 alloy hot rolled bars.
Melting process of Alloy GH2787: Vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting process is adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH2787: Excerpted from QJ/DT 01.63015 and literature [1], there are 3 heat treatment regimes for bars and disk blanks: Regime I: 1140 (+20, -10)℃×(4～8)h/AC + 1050℃±10℃×4h/AC + (750～790)℃×(16～25)h/AC; Regime II: (1080～1120)℃×10h/AC + (750～800)℃×(16～25)h/AC; Regime III: (1180～1200)℃×(2.5～8)h/AC + 1050℃×4h/AC + (750～800)℃×16h/AC.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH2787: Excerpted from QJ/DT 01.63015 and literature [1].
Density of Alloy GH2787: ρ=8.04g/cm³ [1].
Oxidation resistance of Alloy GH2787: In air medium, the oxidation rate of the alloy tested at 800℃ for 100h is 0.059g/(m²·h).
Corrosion resistance of Alloy GH2787: The alloy has good resistance to gas corrosion below 850℃.

Forming process and performance of Alloy GH2787: Forging cogging charging temperature not higher than 700℃, heating temperature 1080℃～1110℃, finish forging temperature greater than 930℃. Rolling heating temperature is the same as forging temperature.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH2787: Excerpted from literature [2], the suggested final heat treatment regimes are: Working blades: (1180～1200)℃×(2.5～8)h/AC + 1050℃×4h/AC + (750～800)℃×16h/AC; Turbine disks: (1150～1160)℃×(6～10)h/AC + 1050℃×4h/AC + 830℃×16h/AC; Compressor blades: heat blank at 1120℃, hold for 60min, die forge/air cool, then 750℃×16h/AC.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH2787: The alloy is easy to machine.

Typical microstructure of Alloy GH2787: The microstructure of the alloy after solution treatment consists of γ solid solution and a small amount of TiC, Ti(CN). After primary aging, Cr₂₃C₆ type carbides precipitate along grain boundaries in a discontinuous chain distribution; after secondary aging, the main strengthening phase of the alloy is γ' phase, with composition approximate to Ni₃(Al₀.₃₅Ti₀.₆₅), which also dissolves about 2%～6% ω(Fe+Cr) and about 1% ω(W).

Overview of Alloy GH4413: GH4413 is a Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with an operating temperature below 850℃. The alloy contains ω(Al+Ti) 4.5% to form γ' precipitation strengthening phase, ω(W+Mo) 9% for solid solution strengthening, and trace amounts of boron and cerium elements for grain boundary strengthening. The alloy has high high-temperature strength and plasticity, good comprehensive performance, and stable microstructure. It is suitable for manufacturing gas turbine working blades operating below 850℃. Main products include hot rolled and forged bars, and die forged blades.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH4413: The alloy has been used to manufacture high-temperature structural parts such as turbine working blades in gas turbines. The alloy has stable microstructure and no TCP phase precipitation after long-term aging at 800℃, 850℃, and 900℃ for 10000h.
Material grade of Alloy GH4413: GH4413 (GH413).
Similar grade to Alloy GH4413: EI826-VD (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH4413: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; Q/KJ.J02.15 Technical conditions for GH413 nickel-based superalloy hot rolled bars.
Melting process of Alloy GH4413: Vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable duplex melting process is adopted.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH4413: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.15, the standard heat treatment regime for bars and die forged blades is: 1210℃±10℃×2h/AC + 1050℃±10℃×4h/AC + 850℃±10℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH4413: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.15. d20mm~450mm hot rolled and forged bars. Bars are supplied without heat treatment, fully polished or turned; die forged blades are supplied after part heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH4413: 1350℃~1386℃ [1].
Density of Alloy GH4413: ρ=8.498g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH4413: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Impact properties of Alloy GH4413: For hot rolled bars after standard heat treatment, room temperature impact toughness αKU=135kJ/m² [1].
Welding performance of Alloy GH4413: Electron beam welding can be used.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH4413: The heat treatment regime for parts is the same as the standard heat treatment regime for the variety. To eliminate residual stress on the surface of finished parts, annealing treatment can be performed by heating in air at 950℃ for 2h, cooling to 600℃ in argon, then continuing to cool in air, then heating in air at 800℃ for 8h, and air cooling.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH4413: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy DZ411: DZ411 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening directionally solidified columnar crystal superalloy with an operating temperature below 1000℃. The alloy contains a moderate amount of chromium and high content of aluminum and titanium, making the ω(γ') strengthening phase account for about 50% of the alloy. The alloy has high strength and excellent oxidation and hot corrosion resistance. It is suitable for manufacturing turbine blades and other high-temperature parts working in hot corrosion environments.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy DZ411: The alloy is used for turbine blades working in hot corrosion environments below 1000℃, as well as marine and ground gas turbine blades operating for long periods in corrosive environments. When using the alloy, applying solid aluminizing or arc ion plating NiCoCrAlYSiB coating on the part surface can provide better high-temperature oxidation and hot corrosion resistance. The alloy has good structural stability and no TCP phase precipitation after long-term aging.
Material grade of Alloy DZ411: DZ411 (DSM11).
Similar grade to Alloy DZ411: DS GTD111 (USA).
Material technical standards for Alloy DZ411: Q/KJ.J02.31 DSM11 alloy ingot.

Melting process of Alloy DZ411: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction directional furnace is used to prepare cast parts and test bars. Shell holding temperature 1520℃±20℃, withdrawal speed (5±1) mm/min.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy DZ411: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.31, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1225℃±10℃×2h/AC + 1120℃±10℃×2h/AC + 850℃±10℃×24h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy DZ411: None. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameter range 75mm～90mm and length not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition; castings are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy DZ411: 1245℃～1350℃ (metallographic method) [1]; 1297℃～1345℃ (DSC method, 20K/min) [1].
Density of Alloy DZ411: ρ=8344kg/m³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy DZ411: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming process and performance of Alloy DZ411: Investment casting method can be used to cast thin-walled parts with wall thickness as small as 0.8mm. Casting shrinkage is about 2%.
Welding performance of Alloy DZ411: Brazing can be performed.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy DZ411: Parts are heat treated in a vacuum heat treatment furnace.
Surface treatment process of Alloy DZ411: Parts surface can be treated with solid aluminizing or arc ion plating such as NiCoCrAlYSiB coating.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy DZ411: Turning, drilling, milling, planing, and grinding are required to be performed at lower speeds.
Phase transformation temperature of Alloy DZ411: DSC determination shows γ′ phase starts to dissolve at 1174℃, and eutectic reaction temperature is 1247℃ [1].

Overview of Alloy MGH2756: MGH2756 is an iron-based oxide dispersion strengthened (ODS) superalloy produced by mechanical alloying (MA) technology, with a long-term operating temperature range of 900℃～1100℃ and a maximum operating temperature of up to 1250℃. The nanoscale (≤50nm) Y₂O₃ powder in the alloy has high thermal and chemical stability, and its strengthening effect can be maintained up to temperatures close to the melting point of the alloy. The alloy has very high strength, oxidation resistance, and hot corrosion resistance at high temperatures (>1000℃), making it suitable for manufacturing components such as combustion chamber flame tube liners and afterburner heat shields for advanced aviation engines. Main products include hot rolled and forged bars, and cold rolled thin sheets.
Material grade of Alloy MGH2756: MGH2756 (MGH956).
Similar grades to Alloy MGH2756: MA956 (USA), PM2000 (Europe).

Melting and casting process of Alloy MGH2756: Raw materials utilize elemental powders, alloy powders, and Y₂O₃ rare earth oxide powders, processed into alloy powder by mechanical alloying under inert gas protection, consolidated by hot extrusion or hot isostatic pressing, and formed by hot or cold working.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy MGH2756: Excerpted from Q/GYB 533 and Q/GYB 534. d10mm～50mm hot rolled and forged bars; δ0.8mm～4.0mm cold rolled thin sheets. Bars are supplied in hot rolled or forged condition; cold rolled thin sheets are supplied after high-temperature recrystallization annealing + leveling + trimming + sandblasting.
Melting temperature range of Alloy MGH2756: Melting point 1480℃ [1].
Density of Alloy MGH2756: ρ=7.2g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy MGH2756: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy MGH2756: 1100℃×60min/AC.
Surface treatment process of Alloy MGH2756: According to needs, finished sheets can be sandblasted with less than 150μm (-100 mesh) grit.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy MGH2756: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH2909: GH2909 is an Fe-Ni-Co based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with an operating temperature below 650℃. The alloy has high strength, high thermal fatigue resistance, low expansion coefficient, constant elastic modulus, and good hot working plasticity, cold forming, and welding performance. Main products include bars, wires, plates, strips, and ring parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH2909: The alloy has been used to manufacture annular components for turbine engines, such as high-pressure compressor rear casings, load-bearing rings, heat insulation rings, combustion chamber seal rings, honeycomb seats, and turbine outer rings; rocket engine thrust chambers, exhaust ducts, and manifolds. It has also been extended to the manufacture of gun barrels, cannon components, glass-to-metal sealing materials, gauge molds, and components for high-pressure hydrogen environments. Due to the low thermal expansion coefficient and small rate of change with temperature, such alloys can effectively control the clearance between engine rotating and static parts over a wide temperature cycle, reducing gas loss, saving energy, improving engine efficiency, and extending component life. In application, the alloy surface can be shot peened to improve the rotating bending high cycle fatigue strength.
Material grade of Alloy GH2909: GH2909 (GH909).
Similar grades to Alloy GH2909: Incoloy 909, Pyromet CTX-909 (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH2909: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; Q/6S1406 Low expansion GH909 alloy bars, round cakes, and ring parts; Q/GYB 05036 GH2909 alloy bars and ring parts; Q/GYB 05069 HGH2909 alloy cold drawn wires for welding; Q/GYB 05072 GH2909 low expansion alloy cold rolled thin sheets and strips.
Melting process of Alloy GH2909: Vacuum induction furnace + vacuum arc remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH2909: Excerpted from Q/6S1406, Q/GYB05036, and Q/GYB05072, there are three standard heat treatment regimes: Regime I: For bars, forgings, and ring parts, (965～1010)℃±15℃/AC + 980℃±15℃×(1±0.25)h/AC + 720℃±10℃×(8±0.5)h/FC (55℃/h) → 620℃±10℃×(8±0.5)h/AC; Regime II: For short-term aging bars, forgings, and ring parts, (965～1010)℃±15℃/AC + 980℃±15℃×(1±0.25)h/AC + 745℃±10℃×(4±0.5)h/FC (55℃/h) → 620℃±10℃×(4±0.5)h/AC; Regime III: For cold rolled sheets and strips, 980℃±15℃/AC + 980℃±15℃/AC + 745℃±10℃×(4±0.5)h/FC (55℃/h) → 620℃±10℃×(4±0.5)h/AC. Solution treatment holding time depends on product section thickness.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH2909: Excerpted from Q/6S1406, Q/GYB05036, Q/GYB05069, and Q/GYB05072. Various specifications of round cakes, ring blanks, ring parts, and forgings; d20mm～300mm bars; δ0.46mm～6.35mm cold rolled thin sheets and strips; d0.3mm～10.0mm welding wires. Round cakes, ring blanks, and forgings are supplied without heat treatment, with turned end faces, turned inner/outer diameters, and chamfered r5mm～10mm; ring parts are supplied in machined state after rough machining, or solution, or solution + aging; bars are supplied without heat treatment, turned or polished; plates and strips are supplied after cold rolling + annealing + descaling + leveling + straightening + trimming; welding wires are supplied in cold drawn, solution + pickled, or semi-hard state.
