Melting temperature range of Alloy GH2909: 1336℃~1384℃ (differential thermal analysis method) [1].
Phase transformation points of Alloy GH2909: Curie point (inflection point): 400℃~455℃ [2].
Density of Alloy GH2909: ρ=8.26g/cm³ [2].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH2909: The alloy is ferromagnetic below 450℃ and paramagnetic above 450℃.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH2909: Using Regime I can achieve good comprehensive properties, suitable for bars, forgings, and ring parts. Using Regime II is suitable for short-term aging bars, forgings, and ring parts. Using Regime III is suitable for cold rolled plates and strips. When parts undergo final heat treatment without further machining, it is recommended to perform it in a vacuum furnace or under a protective atmosphere.
Surface treatment process of Alloy GH2909: The alloy surface can be shot peened, which can improve the rotating bending fatigue limit. Solid state aluminizing process can be used.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH2909: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH3600: GH3600 is a Ni-Cr-Fe based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy with relatively good thermal strength and high plasticity below 800℃, and good high-temperature corrosion and oxidation resistance below 1000℃. The alloy has stable microstructure during long-term use, excellent cold and hot working and welding performance, and low-temperature mechanical properties. The alloy can be strengthened by cold working and can be connected by resistance welding, fusion welding, or brazing. It is suitable for manufacturing engine combustion chambers working below 700℃～800℃, and oxidation-resistant parts bearing low loads below 1100℃. Main products include cold rolled thin sheets, hot rolled thick plates, strips, wires, bars, tubes, round cakes, ring blanks, and ring forgings.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH3600: The alloy has been used to make rocket engine nozzle extensions, aviation engine high-pressure compressors, static inner and outer rings, high-pressure guide vane orifice plates, seals, etc. It is also used in petrochemical, nuclear reactor, chemical food equipment, and electronic component fields to make exhaust pipes, rollers for carburizing container heat treatment equipment, springs, heat exchanger pipes, reactor control rods, and pipes. The alloy has good corrosion resistance in fresh water and flowing sea water, but corrosion spots may occur in static sea water. It has high resistance to various waste gases, alkaline solutions, and most organic acids and compounds. It is not prone to chloride ion stress corrosion cracking, but stress corrosion cracking is likely to occur under high concentration caustic alkali or high-temperature mercury conditions.
Material grade of Alloy GH3600: GH3600 (GH600).
Similar grade to Alloy GH3600: Inconel 600 (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH3600: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 5060 Specification for GH3600 superalloy precision thin-walled seamless tubes for aerospace; Liaoxin 7-0003 Technical conditions for GH600 alloy cold rolled thin sheets; Liaoxin 7-0004 Technical conditions for GH600 alloy hot rolled thick plates; Liaoxin 7-0065 Technical conditions for GH600 alloy bars, round cakes, ring blanks and ring forgings; Liaoxin 7-0100 Technical standard for GH600 alloy cold rolled (drawn) seamless tubes; Liaoxin 7-0110 Technical conditions for GH600 alloy bars; Q/5B 4030 GH600 alloy cold drawn wires for cold heading; Q/3B 4082 GH600 alloy cold rolled strips; Q/3B 4083 GH600 alloy wires for rivets.

Melting process of Alloy GH3600: Non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH3600: Excerpted from GJB 5060, Liaoxin 7-0003, Liaoxin 7-0004, Liaoxin 7-0065, Liaoxin 7-0100, Liaoxin 7-0110, Q/5B 4030, Q/3B 4082, and Q/3B 4083, the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Bars, round cakes, ring parts: (1010～1030)℃/AC, holding time: (1～1.5) min/mm; b) Plates: (1010～1050)℃/AC, holding time: (3～5) min/mm; c) Strips: 1010℃±10℃/AC; d) Wires: 1065℃±10℃/AC; e) Thin-walled tubes: (960～980)℃ vacuum heat treatment.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH3600: Excerpted from GJB 5060, Liaoxin 7-0003, Liaoxin 7-0004, Liaoxin 7-0065, Liaoxin 7-0100, Liaoxin 7-0110, Q/5B 4030, Q/3B 4082, and Q/3B 4083.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH3600: 1370℃～1430℃ [1].
Density of Alloy GH3600: ρ=8.43g/cm³ [6].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH3600: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and performance of Alloy GH3600: The alloy has good hot working performance. Ingot forging heating temperature is 1110℃～1140℃; final forging temperature is not less than 950℃. Slab rolling heating temperature is 1130℃～1170℃, finish rolling heating temperature is 1090℃～1130℃. Finished product solution treatment temperature is 1010℃～1050℃.

Process performance of Alloy GH3600: For δ1.5mm plate in supply state, when bending coefficient is 2, no cracks were found after bending 180° and observing at 5x magnification. The number of repeated bends to fracture is (19～23) times.

Welding performance of Alloy GH3600: The alloy has good welding performance and can be connected by arc welding, argon arc welding, resistance welding, and brazing. Large or complex welded structural parts should be annealed at 870℃ for 1h after fusion welding to eliminate welding stress.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH3600: Parts are treated according to the standard heat treatment regime of the corresponding variety. Annealing of thin sheets and strips should be carried out in a protective atmosphere. Heating and annealing should be performed in a weakly reducing sulfur-free atmosphere. The content of hydrogen and carbon monoxide in the reducing atmosphere should be at least 2%, and bright annealing should be performed in dry argon [1].
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH3600: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH3044: GH3044 is a Ni-Cr based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy with an operating temperature below 900℃. The alloy contains a large amount of tungsten and a small amount of aluminum and titanium elements. The alloy has high plasticity and moderate thermal strength, as well as excellent oxidation resistance and good stamping and welding process performance. It is suitable for manufacturing aviation engine main combustion chamber and afterburner components, as well as heat shields and guide vanes, working long-term below 900℃. Main products include plates, strips, wires, bars, and ring parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH3044: The alloy has been used to manufacture sheet metal stamped and welded structural parts for aviation engine combustion chambers and afterburners, as well as mounting edges, ducts, and guide vanes, with reliable performance and good usage. The alloy can be welded by argon arc welding, spot welding, seam welding, and brazing, and can be combined with 1Cr18Ni9Ti, GH1140, GH3030, and GH3039 alloys. Parts working at high temperatures can be effectively protected using W-2 enamel coating.
Material grade of Alloy GH3044: GH3044 (GH44).
Similar grades to Alloy GH3044: EI868, KhN60VT, VZh98 (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH3044: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 1952A Specification for cold rolled superalloy thin sheets for aviation; GJB 3020A Specification for superalloy ring blanks for aviation; GJB 3165A Specification for hot rolled and forged superalloy bars for aviation load-bearing parts; GJB 3317A Specification for hot rolled superalloy plates for aviation; GJB 3318A Specification for cold rolled superalloy strips for aviation; GJB 5301A Specification for superalloy ring parts for aviation; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.

Melting process of Alloy GH3044: Electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting, or electric arc furnace + vacuum consumable remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + vacuum arc remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH3044: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, GJB 1952A, GJB 3020A, GJB 3165A, GJB 3317A, GJB 3318A, and GJB 5301A, the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Hot rolled plates, cold rolled thin sheets, cold rolled strips: (1120～1160)℃/AC. Where: holding time (8～12) min for δ≤3mm; holding time (12～16) min for δ3mm～5mm; b) Hot rolled and forged bars, ring blanks: (1120～1160)℃/AC; c) Ring parts: (1120～1160)℃/AC or OQ.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH3044: Excerpted from GJB 1952A, GJB 3020A, GJB 3165A, GJB 3317A, GJB 3318A, and GJB 5301A.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH3044: 1352℃～1375℃ [1].
Density of Alloy GH3044: ρ=8.89g/cm³ [2].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH3044: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy GH3044: Technical standards for bars, ring blanks, and ring parts stipulate that after solution treatment at 1120℃～1160℃, room temperature hardness is HBS≤285.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH3044: Intermediate heat treatment temperature for parts is 1140℃±10℃×(3～5)min/AC. Final heat treatment temperature is determined by part working conditions. For parts requiring good thermal fatigue resistance: 1150℃×(3～5)min/AC; for parts requiring high thermal strength: 1200℃×(3～5)min/AC.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH3044: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH1180: GH1180 (NS111) is an Fe-Ni-Cr based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy used below 1000℃. The alloy has excellent high-temperature corrosion resistance, good process performance, and good structural stability. The alloy has good processing and welding performance, and is easy to form by cold and hot working. This alloy is suitable for manufacturing components requiring long-term operation at high temperatures under harsh corrosive media conditions. Main products include hot rolled and forged bars, hot rolled plates, cold rolled thin sheets, cold rolled (drawn) seamless tubes, cold rolled strips, and forgings.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH1180: The alloy has been used to manufacture catalyst tubes, convection tubes, and cracking tubes in the petrochemical field; radiant tubes, retorts, and different types of furnace fixtures in various heat treatment furnaces or heating furnaces; heat exchangers for power plants; liquid evaporators for the paper industry, etc. The alloy has good resistance to stress corrosion cracking; high resistance to stress corrosion cracking in aqueous chlorides, resistance to steam, as well as steam, air, and carbon dioxide mixtures; good resistance to nitric acid solution corrosion; good resistance to organic acids such as formic acid, acetic acid, and propionic acid.
Material grade of Alloy GH1180: Superalloy grade GH1180 (GH180); Corrosion-resistant alloy grade NS111.
Similar grade to Alloy GH1180: Incoloy 800 (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH1180: GB/T 15007 Corrosion-resistant alloy grades; GB/T 15008 Corrosion-resistant alloy bars; GB/T 15009 Corrosion-resistant alloy hot rolled plates; GB/T 15010 Corrosion-resistant alloy cold rolled thin sheets; GB/T 15011 Corrosion-resistant alloy cold rolled (drawn) seamless tubes; GB/T 15012 Corrosion-resistant alloy cold rolled strips; YB/T 5264 Corrosion-resistant alloy forgings.
Melting process of Alloy GH1180: Vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting process is adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH1180: Excerpted from GB/T 15008, GB/T 15009, GB/T 15010, GB/T 15011, GB/T 15012, and YB/T 5264, the standard heat treatment regime for bars, hot rolled plates, cold rolled thin sheets, cold rolled (drawn) seamless tubes, cold rolled strips, and forgings is: 1000℃～1060℃/AC, holding time depends on material diameter or thickness.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH1180: Excerpted from GB/T 15008, GB/T 15009, GB/T 15010, GB/T 15011, GB/T 15012, and YB/T 5264.
Density of Alloy GH1180: ρ=7.97g/cm³ [1].

Corrosion resistance of Alloy GH1180: The alloy has good resistance to stress corrosion cracking. It has high resistance to stress corrosion cracking in aqueous chlorides, resistance to steam, as well as steam, air, and carbon dioxide mixtures; good resistance to nitric acid solution corrosion, but sensitization occurs after heating, causing intergranular corrosion; it can only resist corrosion by lower concentrations of sulfuric acid and hydrochloric acid; good resistance to organic acids such as formic acid, acetic acid, and propionic acid. The alloy has good resistance to flowing seawater, but deposits will form on the alloy surface in stagnant seawater, causing severe corrosion cracking.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH1180: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH1180: No special requirements.
Phase transformation temperature of Alloy GH1180: Precipitation temperature range of M₂₃C₆ type carbide is 540℃～1095℃.

Overview of Alloy JG4006 (IC6): JG4006 is a Ni3Al intermetallic based directionally solidified columnar crystal high-temperature material, and is China's first intermetallic high-temperature material used for aviation engines. The alloy features simple composition, low cost, low density, and superior high-temperature performance. Its rupture strength at 760℃/100h reaches 824MPa; rupture strength at 1100℃/100h reaches 100MPa. The alloy has good thermal fatigue resistance and stable microstructure at high temperatures, but its high-temperature oxidation and hot corrosion resistance are relatively poor. It is suitable for manufacturing turbine guide vanes working below 1150℃.
Material grade of Alloy JG4006 (IC6): JG4006 (IC6).

Melting and casting process of Alloy JG4006 (IC6): Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and directional solidification furnace is used for remelting and pouring directionally solidified castings and test bars.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy JG4006 (IC6): Excerpted from HB 7762 and HB/Z 140, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1260℃±10℃ (vacuum) ×10h/Argon cooling.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy JG4006 (IC6): Excerpted from HB 7762 and Q/6S 1053. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter generally of 75mm～90mm and a length of about 1m. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition; castings are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy JG4006 (IC6): Incipient melting point: 1315℃ (metallographic method); Melting: 1340℃～1410℃ (differential thermal analysis method) [4].
Density of Alloy JG4006 (IC6): ρ=7.90g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy JG4006 (IC6): The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy JG4006 (IC6): After standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness of the alloy is HRC 33～39 [1].
Forming process and performance of Alloy JG4006 (IC6): The alloy has good casting performance and can be cast into hollow blades and other parts with complex shapes.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy JG4006 (IC6): Parts are heat treated under vacuum with the specification: 1260℃×10h/Argon cooling.
Surface treatment process of Alloy JG4006 (IC6): MCrAlYX (M is Ni, Co; X is Si or Hf, etc.) physical deposition overlay coating is adopted.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy JG4006 (IC6): No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH3230: GH3230 is a Ni-Cr based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy with an operating temperature range of 700℃～1050℃. The alloy has high strength and thermal fatigue resistance, and stable microstructure. It has excellent hot corrosion and oxidation resistance, suitable for manufacturing aviation engine flame tubes and other parts. Main products include cold rolled plates and strips.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH3230: The alloy has been used to manufacture combustion chamber components for advanced aviation engines, with good usage status. No TCP harmful phase precipitates in the alloy after long-term aging at 800℃ for 1000h.
Material grade of Alloy GH3230: GH3230 (GH230).
Material technical standards for Alloy GH3230: QJ/DT 0160044 Technical conditions for GH230 plates and strips for aviation engines; Q/GYB 05062 GH3230 alloy cold rolled thin sheets and strips.
Melting process of Alloy GH3230: Vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting process is adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH3230: Excerpted from QJ/DT 0160044 and Q/GYB 05062, the standard heat treatment regime for cold rolled thin sheets, hot rolled thick plates, and strips is: (1180～1240)℃/AC, or OQ, or WQ, holding time determined by material thickness.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH3230: Excerpted from QJ/DT 0160044 and Q/GYB 05062.
Density of Alloy GH3230: ρ=8.934g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH3230: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH3230: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH3230: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy DD426: DD426 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening single crystal superalloy, belonging to the first generation of single crystal superalloys, with an operating temperature below 1100℃. The alloy has high creep strength and fatigue resistance, and good structural stability. The alloy has good single crystal casting performance, welding performance, and coating performance. It is suitable for manufacturing aviation engine gas turbine rotor blades, guide vanes, and other high-temperature parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy DD426: The alloy has been used to manufacture advanced aviation engine gas turbine rotor blades and guide vanes. The similar ZhS26y alloy has been successfully applied to advanced aviation engines abroad. Parts need to be coated with MCrAlY coating when in use to further improve the alloy's hot corrosion resistance. No harmful TCP phase precipitates in the alloy during long-term aging at 900℃ for 2000h.
Material grade of Alloy DD426: DD426 (DD26).
Similar grade to Alloy DD426: ZhS26y (Russia).
Material technical standards for Alloy DD426: Q/KJ.J02.45 DD26 cast superalloy ingot.

Melting and casting process of Alloy DD426: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, vacuum directional solidification furnace is used for remelting, and spiral selector method or seed crystal method is used to manufacture single crystal test bars or parts.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy DD426: Excerpted from literature [1], the alloy is used in as-cast condition or after heat treatment. The standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1230℃±10℃×1.5h/AC + 1080℃±10℃×2h/AC + 870℃±10℃×24h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy DD426: Excerpted from literature [1] and Q/KJ.J02.45. The master alloy is cast into cylindrical long bars with a diameter range of 70mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. Master alloy and single crystal blades are supplied in as-cast condition.
Melting temperature range of Alloy DD426: 1317℃～1397℃ [1].
Density of Alloy DD426: ρ=8.477g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy DD426: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and performance of Alloy DD426: The alloy has good casting performance. Investment precision casting method can be used to cast thin-walled hollow blades and other castings with complex structures.
Welding performance of Alloy DD426: Brazing can be performed.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy DD426: Parts are used in as-cast condition or after heat treatment; heat treatment is the same as the standard heat treatment regime of the alloy.
Surface treatment process of Alloy DD426: MCrAlY coating can be applied.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy DD426: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy K6509: K6509 is a cobalt-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy using carbides as the main strengthening phase, with an operating temperature below 1050℃. The ω(Cr) content in the alloy is about 23%, and carbide-forming elements such as tantalum, tungsten, and titanium are added, so the alloy has excellent hot corrosion resistance and relatively high high-temperature strength. The alloy's rupture strength at 900℃/100h is 40MPa～50MPa higher than similar alloy X40, and the temperature capability is increased by 40℃～50℃. Main products are engine turbine guide vanes.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K6509: The alloy is suitable for manufacturing hot-end parts such as gas turbine engine turbine guide vanes working below 1050℃. It is recommended for engines with harsh service conditions such as aviation engines and advanced marine engines. The alloy surface can be treated with aluminizing, AlCr, and AlSi coating by powder packing, slurry, and low-pressure gas permeation methods to further improve the alloy's oxidation and hot corrosion resistance.
Material grade of Alloy K6509: K6509 (M509).
Similar grade to Alloy K6509: Mar-M509 (USA).
Material technical standards for Alloy K6509: Q/6S 2459 K6509 alloy ingot specification; Q/KJ.J02.41 K6509 cobalt-based cast superalloy ingot.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K6509: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting, using investment precision casting with sand filling method to cast parts and test bars.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K6509: Excerpted from Q/6S 2459 and Q/KJ.J02.41, the alloy is used in the as-cast condition.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K6509: None. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. Master alloy and precision castings are supplied in as-cast condition.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K6509: 1346℃～1395℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K6509: ρ=8.865g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K6509: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and performance of Alloy K6509: The casting performance of the alloy is good. Investment casting method can be used to cast thin-walled parts with wall thickness as small as 1mm, with a casting shrinkage of about 2%.
Welding performance of Alloy K6509: Argon arc hardfacing can be performed.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy K6509: Parts are used in the as-cast condition. Aluminizing and stress relief annealing can also be performed, with treatment temperatures below 1120℃.
Surface treatment process of Alloy K6509: The alloy surface can be treated with aluminizing, AlCr, and AlSi coating by powder packing, slurry, and low-pressure gas permeation methods.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K6509: Turning, drilling, milling, planing, and grinding are required to be performed at lower speeds.

Overview of Alloy DD406: DD406 is a second-generation nickel-based single crystal superalloy developed in China. It possesses high high-temperature strength, good comprehensive properties, stable microstructure, and good casting process performance. Compared with foreign second-generation single crystal superalloys PWA1484, René N5, and CMSX-4, its tensile properties, rupture properties, creep properties, oxidation resistance, and hot corrosion resistance all reach or partially exceed their levels. It also has the advantage of low cost due to its low Re content. The alloy's rupture strength σ (1100℃, 140MPa) ≥ 100h and σ (1150℃, 100MPa) ≥ 100h. It is suitable for manufacturing gas turbine working blades with complex internal cavities operating below 1100℃ and guide vanes operating below 1150℃.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy DD406: The alloy has been selected for manufacturing turbine working blades and guide vanes for various advanced aviation engines and is undergoing test run assessments on multiple engines. The alloy has passed test run assessments, flight assessments, and design finalization technical appraisal for a certain engine.
Material grade of Alloy DD406: DD406 (DD6).
Material technical standards for Alloy DD406: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; Q/6S 1678 DD6 alloy ingot.

Melting and casting process of Alloy DD406: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum directional solidification furnace is used for remelting and pouring single crystal castings or test bars.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy DD406: Excerpted from literature [1] and Q/6S1678, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1290℃×1h + 1300℃×2h + 1315℃×4h/AC + 1120℃×4h/AC + 870℃×32h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy DD406: None. The master alloy is cast into cylindrical long bars with a diameter range of 70mm～90mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and single crystal parts are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy DD406: 1342℃～1399℃ [1].
Density of Alloy DD406: ρ=8.78g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy DD406: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and performance of Alloy DD406: The alloy has good casting performance. Investment precision casting method can be used to cast thin-walled hollow blades and other castings with complex shapes.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy DD406: Blades with unmachined surfaces should be heat treated under argon protection or vacuum, followed by forced argon cooling, with a cooling rate greater than 260℃/min.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy DD406: No special requirements.
Phase transformation temperature of Alloy DD406: The complete solution temperature of γ´ phase is 1305℃.

Overview of Alloy K414: K414 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with a long-term operating temperature of 600℃ and a short-term operating temperature of 800℃~900℃. Elements such as chromium and molybdenum are added to the alloy for solid solution strengthening, aluminum and titanium are added to form γ' aging strengthening phase, and boron and cerium elements are added to purify and strengthen grain boundaries. Under medium and low temperature conditions, the alloy has high strength and plasticity, and high medium-temperature rupture life. The alloy has good resistance to gas erosion, as well as good casting and machining performance. Main products are aerospace engine integral cast guides and aviation engine tail nozzle investment castings.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K414: The alloy has been used to manufacture aerospace engine guide investment castings and aviation engine tail nozzle castings, with good batch production and usage conditions. The alloy has good casting performance and can be cast into thin-walled castings with complex structures and complex internal cavities.
Material grade of Alloy K414: K414 (K41).
Similar grade to Alloy K414: VZhL-14 (Russia).
Material technical standards for Alloy K414: Q/GYB 05037 K414 master alloy for casting; Q/GYB 05057 K414 (K41) alloy guide II investment castings.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K414: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting and pouring investment precision castings and test bars.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K414: Excerpted from Q/GYB 05037 and Q/GYB 05057, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1130℃±10℃×4h/Furnace Cool + 700℃±10℃×16h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K414: Excerpted from Q/GYB 05037 and Q/GYB 05057. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K414: 1309℃~1354℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K414: ρ=8.106g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K414: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and performance of Alloy K414: The casting performance of the alloy is good. It can be cast into thin-walled castings with complex structures and hollow blades with complex internal cavities and wall thickness less than 1mm. The linear shrinkage of the alloy is 2.2%.
Welding performance of Alloy K414: Argon arc welding and electron beam welding can be performed. No cracks were found in the heat-affected zone of the weld in overlay welding tests.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy K414: Test bars and simple structural parts only undergo standard heat treatment; complex structural castings require an additional pre-heat treatment with the regime: 930℃±10℃×(2～4)h, furnace cooling.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K414: Carbide tools are used.

Overview of Alloy K4222: K4222 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with an operating temperature below 1000℃. The alloy has medium high-temperature strength, good creep and fatigue resistance. It has excellent oxidation and corrosion resistance at high temperatures, and good casting and welding process performance.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K4222: The alloy is suitable for manufacturing gas turbine guide vanes working below 1000℃ and other long-term service static parts. Compared with nickel-based alloys for similar purposes, this alloy has good long-term aging structural stability.
Material grade of Alloy K4222: K4222 (M22).
Similar grade to Alloy K4222: GTD222 (USA).

Melting and casting process of Alloy K4222: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting, using investment precision casting with sand filling method to cast parts and specimens.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K4222: Excerpted from literature [1], the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1150℃±10℃×4h/AC + 800℃±10℃×8h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K4222: None. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Density of Alloy K4222: ρ=8.26g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K4222: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Oxidation resistance of Alloy K4222: In air medium, the oxidation rate of the alloy tested at 1000℃ for 100h is 0.0435g/(m²·h).
Forming process and performance of Alloy K4222: The alloy has good casting performance, with a casting shrinkage of about 2%.
Welding performance of Alloy K4222: The welding performance of the alloy is good, and repair welding is allowed for precision castings used statically.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy K4222: Same as the standard heat treatment regime of the alloy. It is recommended to perform heat treatment for no-allowance precision castings in an inert atmosphere.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K4222: The machining performance of the alloy is good.

Overview of Alloy K640S: K640S is a cobalt-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with an operating temperature below 1000℃. This alloy is a new type of cast superalloy developed based on X40 alloy by adjusting the content of silicon and manganese elements and adding boron. Compared with X40 alloy, the performance stability and plasticity indicators of K640S alloy are significantly improved. The alloy has a high incipient melting temperature, stable microstructure, excellent thermal fatigue resistance, oxidation resistance, and hot corrosion resistance. At the same time, the alloy has good comprehensive properties such as creep, rupture, and fatigue. Main products are precision castings such as turbine guide vanes.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K640S: The alloy has been used to manufacture turbine guide vanes with complex cooling cavities for aviation, marine, and land gas turbines. The alloy is used in the as-cast condition. After high-temperature long-term aging, the precipitated M23C6 and M6C type carbides improve the mechanical properties of the alloy but reduce the room temperature plasticity.
Material grade of Alloy K640S: K640S (K40S).
Similar grade to Alloy K640S: X40 (USA).

Material technical standards for Alloy K640S: GJB 5512.1 Specification for cast superalloy master alloys for cast superalloys and cast intermetallic high temperature materials - Part 1: Master alloys for ordinary precision castings; HB 7763 Specification for equiaxed cast superalloy ingots for aviation engines; Q/KJ.J02.36 K640S cast cobalt-based superalloy ingot.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K640S: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting, using investment precision casting with sand filling method to cast parts and test bars.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K640S: The alloy is used in the as-cast condition.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K640S: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1, HB 7728, and Q/KJ.J02.36. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. Both master alloy and precision castings are supplied in as-cast condition.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K640S: 1342℃～1395℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K640S: ρ=8.68g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K640S: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K640S: In as-cast condition, room temperature hardness is HB 272～255, HRC 20.0～25.5 [1].
Forming process and performance of Alloy K640S: The casting performance of the alloy is good. Investment casting method can be used to cast complex thin-walled hollow blades.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy K640S: Parts are used in the as-cast condition.

Surface treatment process of Alloy K640S: Low-pressure aluminizing process is used to form a coating on the alloy surface. Aluminizing process: Penetrant: 80% FeAl powder + 20% TiAl powder; Activator: NH4Cl; 1080℃, 4h; Reaction pressure: 0.01MPa～0.05MPa.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K640S: It is required to use appropriate processes for creep feed grinding of blade dovetails to prevent grinding cracks.

Overview of Alloy GH4199: GH4199 is a Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with an operating temperature below 950℃. The alloy is solid solution strengthened by adding tungsten, molybdenum, and chromium elements, precipitation strengthened by adding aluminum and titanium elements to form strengthening phases, and grain boundaries are purified and strengthened by adding boron and magnesium elements. The alloy has high strength, good oxidation resistance, and welding performance. The alloy has good comprehensive properties, with a tensile strength greater than 490MPa at 900℃, making it one of the precipitation hardening sheet alloys with the highest operating temperature. Main products include hot rolled and forged bars, hot rolled plates, cold rolled thin sheets, strips, and tubes.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH4199: The alloy has been used to manufacture welded load-bearing components such as afterburner heat shields, adjustable nozzle heat shields, and sleeves used below 950℃ in aviation engines, as well as aircraft airborne equipment parts, with good batch production and usage conditions. Due to the high degree of alloying and high deformation resistance, the alloy has poor hot and cold working plasticity. During processing, the cold deformation amount of forging (rolling) passes should be strictly controlled to avoid processing cracks. The alloy precipitates σ phase after long-term aging at 700℃～800℃.
Material grade of Alloy GH4199: GH4199 (GH199).
Similar grades to Alloy GH4199: EP199 (VZh101, KhN56VMYu) (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH4199: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; Q/6S 1818 Specification for GH4199 alloy hot rolled and forged bars; Q/6S 1819 Specification for GH4199 alloy cold rolled thin sheets; Liaoxin 7-0074 Technical conditions for GH199 alloy cold rolled thin sheets; Liaoxin 7-0075 Technical conditions for GH199 alloy bars; Q/GYB 517 GH4199 alloy cold rolled strips.
Melting process of Alloy GH4199: Vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum arc remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH4199: Excerpted from Liaoxin 7-0074, Liaoxin 7-0075, Q/6S 1818, Q/6S 1819, and Q/GYB 517, the alloy is used in the solution treated state. The standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Hot rolled and forged bars: (1150～1200)℃/AC, holding time depends on material diameter; b) Plates and strips: (1100～1150)℃/WQ or AC, holding time depends on material thickness.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH4199: Excerpted from Liaoxin 7-0074, Liaoxin 7-0075, Q/6S 1818, Q/6S 1819, and GYB 517. d10mm～80mm hot rolled bars; d90～300mm forged bars; δ0.5mm～4.0mm cold rolled plates; δ0.1mm～1.2mm cold rolled strips. Bars are generally supplied without heat treatment; cold rolled plates are supplied after solution + pickling + straightening + trimming; cold rolled strips are supplied after solution + alkali/acid pickling + trimming in coils, alkali/acid pickling can be omitted after bright heat treatment.
Density of Alloy GH4199: ρ=8.64g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH4199: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH4199: Satisfactory cutting can be performed using Y130 carbide tools and cutting fluids with strong cooling performance and high wettability.
Phase transformation temperature of Alloy GH4199: The peak precipitation range of γ' phase is 700℃～800℃.

Overview of Alloy JG1102 (TAC-2M): JG1102 (TAC-2M) is a TiAl intermetallic based wrought high-temperature material with an operating temperature below 750℃. The alloy possesses high elastic modulus, high temperature strength, and creep resistance; it has high oxidation resistance and stable microstructure at high temperatures. The performance level of this alloy is close to that of nickel-based superalloys, but its density is only half that of superalloys. With a density of 3.9g/cm³ and low cost, it is suitable for manufacturing lightweight components such as turbine rotors and compressor blades. Main products include bars and cakes.
Material grade of Alloy JG1102 (TAC-2M): JG1102 (TAC-2M).
Material technical standards for Alloy JG1102 (TAC-2M): GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials.
Melting process of Alloy JG1102 (TAC-2M): Multiple remelting process in vacuum consumable arc furnace is adopted.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy JG1102 (TAC-2M): Excerpted from literature [2], annealing treatment regime for extruded bars: 900℃×12h/AC; standard heat treatment regime: 1340℃×5min/AC.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy JG1102 (TAC-2M): None. d20mm～50mm extruded bars. Bars are supplied in extruded + turned condition.
Density of Alloy JG1102 (TAC-2M): ρ=3.87g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy JG1102 (TAC-2M): The alloy is non-magnetic.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy JG1102 (TAC-2M): Same as the standard heat treatment regime for the product.

Overview of Alloy GH3039: GH3039 is a Ni-Cr based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy, mainly solid solution strengthened by elements such as chromium and molybdenum. The alloy has medium thermal strength and good thermal fatigue resistance below 800℃, good oxidation resistance below 1000℃, and stable microstructure during long-term use. It has good cold forming and welding performance. Main products include plates, bars, wires, tubes, and forgings.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH3039: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine combustion chamber and afterburner components, tested by long-term production and use, showing good performance. Thin sheets in the supply state have good hot working process plasticity and good stamping performance. It can be welded by argon arc welding, spot welding, or seam welding, with excellent welding performance and small crack tendency in argon arc welding. Welding characteristics are close to GH3030 alloy [10]. It can be combined with GH1140, GH3030, and GH3044 alloys for welding. No harmful phases precipitate in the alloy, and the structural stability is good [11]. When working long-term at high temperatures, there is a tendency for intergranular oxidation [12].
Material grade of Alloy GH3039: GH3039 (GH39).
Similar grades to Alloy GH3039: EI602, KhN75MBTYu (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH3039: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GB/T 15062 Superalloy tubes for general purposes; GJB 1952A Specification for cold rolled superalloy thin sheets for aviation; GJB 2297A Specification for cold drawn (rolled) superalloy seamless tubes for aviation; GJB 2612 Specification for cold drawn superalloy wires for welding; GJB 3165A Specification for hot rolled and forged superalloy bars for aviation load-bearing parts; GJB 3317A Specification for hot rolled superalloy plates for aviation; GJB 3318A Specification for cold rolled superalloy strips for aviation; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.

Melting process of Alloy GH3039: Electric arc furnace, or electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting, or electric arc furnace + vacuum consumable remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable melting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH3039: Excerpted from GB/T 15062, GJB 1952A, GJB 2297A, GJB 3165A, GJB 3317A, GJB3 318A, and HB/Z 40, the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Hot rolled plates, cold rolled thin sheets, cold rolled strips: (1050～1090)℃/AC. Where: holding time (8～12) min for δ≤3mm; holding time (12～16) min for δ3mm～5mm; b) Hot rolled and forged bars: (1050～1080)℃/AC or OQ; c) Cold drawn tubes: (1050～1080)℃/AC or WQ.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH3039: d20mm～300mm bars; δ4mm～14mm hot rolled plates; δ0.5mm～4mm cold rolled plates; δ0.1mm～0.8mm cold rolled strips; cold drawn (rolled) seamless tubes with outer diameter ≤50mm; d0.2mm～10.0mm cold drawn wires for welding. Bars are supplied without heat treatment, polished or turned; hot rolled plates are supplied after solution + alkali/acid pickling + leveling + trimming; cold rolled plates are supplied after solution + alkali/acid pickling + leveling + straightening + trimming; cold rolled strips are supplied after solution + alkali/acid pickling + leveling + trimming in coils; cold drawn tubes are supplied after solution + pickling, or in cold drawn/rolled condition; welding wires are supplied in hard, or semi-hard (reduction area not greater than 20%), or solution treatment + pickled, or bright solution treated (applicable for diameter not greater than 1.5mm) condition in coils, or straight strips.
Electrical resistivity of Alloy GH3039: At 20℃, ρ=1.18×10^(-6)Ω·m [12].
Density of Alloy GH3039: ρ=8.3g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH3039: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH3039: Intermediate solution treatment for parts is 1050℃/AC; final heat treatment for combustion chamber parts is 1080℃/AC. For parts requiring higher rupture performance, the solution temperature can be increased to 1170℃.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH3039: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy JG1201 (TAC-3A): JG1201 is a Ti₂AlNb based intermetallic high-temperature structural material. Components can work for a long time below 750℃ and for a short time at 1000℃. The alloy has excellent high-temperature mechanical properties, oxidation resistance, and weldability, as well as good hot working plasticity, and can be cold formed. Main products include bars, plates, foils, tubes, and ring parts.
Material grade of Alloy JG1201 (TAC-3A): JG1201 (TAC-3A).
Melting process of Alloy JG1201 (TAC-3A): Multiple remelting process in vacuum consumable arc furnace is adopted.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy JG1201 (TAC-3A): None. d10mm～300mm bars; δ2mm～20mm hot rolled plates; δ0.3mm～4.0mm cold rolled thin sheets; δ0.05mm～0.2mm foil plates; d10mm～300mm tubes; various specifications of ring parts. Bars, tubes, and ring parts are supplied after solution + aging treatment, either directly or after surface machining; hot rolled plates are supplied after solution + aging + sandblasting + straightening + trimming; cold rolled sheets and foils are supplied after cold rolling or annealing + trimming.
Density of Alloy JG1201 (TAC-3A): p=5.3g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy JG1201 (TAC-3A): The alloy is non-magnetic.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy JG1201 (TAC-3A): No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy K417L: K417L is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy characterized by low segregation and low density, with an operating temperature below 900℃. The alloy has high strength, excellent fatigue resistance, and stable structural properties. Its comprehensive performance is better than K403 alloy, and it has good welding process performance. It is suitable for manufacturing gas turbine engine guide vanes, afterburner adjustable flaps, and other high-temperature components working below 900℃.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K417L: The mechanical performance level of the alloy is close to that of cobalt-based X40 alloy. It has been used to manufacture aviation engine tail nozzle adjustable flap base plates and has passed test run assessments. Since w(Cr) < 15% in the alloy and Mo content is relatively high, it is prone to acidic fluxing corrosion under hot corrosion conditions. No harmful phase precipitates in the alloy after long-term aging at 850℃×1000h.
Material grade of Alloy K417L: K417L (K17L, M17L).
Material technical standards for Alloy K417L: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; Q/JK.J02.04 K417L alloy ingot.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K417L: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting and pouring investment precision cast parts and test bars.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K417L: Excerpted from Q/JK.J02.04, the alloy is used in the as-cast condition.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K417L: The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. Master alloy and precision castings are supplied in as-cast condition.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K417L: 1270℃~1340℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K417L: ρ=7.8g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K417L: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming process and performance of Alloy K417L: Can be cast into thin-walled parts and hollow turbine blades with wall thickness as small as 1mm, with a casting shrinkage of about 2%.
Welding performance of Alloy K417L: The alloy can be welded by argon arc welding.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy K417L: Parts are used in the as-cast condition.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K417L: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy K644: K644 is a cobalt-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with an operating temperature below 900℃. The alloy contains high chromium and tungsten elements, thus possessing good oxidation resistance and gas corrosion resistance. The alloy has high thermal fatigue resistance, excellent welding performance, and good process performance. Main products include precision cast blades, etc.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K644: The alloy is used to manufacture guide vanes for high-power gas turbines with operating temperatures below 900℃. The alloy has stable microstructure and no TCP phase precipitation when working for a long time at high temperatures.
Material grade of Alloy K644: K644 (K44).
Similar grade to Alloy K644: FSX-414 (USA).

Melting and casting process of Alloy K644: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting, using investment precision casting with sand filling method to cast parts and test bars.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K644: Excerpted from literature [1], the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1150℃±10℃×4h/FC → 930℃±10℃×10h/FC → 540℃/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K644: The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and precision castings are generally supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K644: Melting point 1404℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K644: ρ=8.64g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K644: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming process and performance of Alloy K644: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum remelting process is used to cast precision castings and inspection test bars. The mold shell temperature is 800℃～900℃, held for more than 2h. The pouring temperature is controlled at 1500℃±10℃ [1].
Part heat treatment process of Alloy K644: Same as the standard heat treatment of the alloy.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K644: No special requirements.

Typical microstructure of Alloy K644: The as-cast microstructure of the alloy consists of γ solid solution, rib-like M23C6 type carbides, and (γ+M23C6) eutectic phase. The eutectic type carbides are not affected by heat treatment. During long-term aging at 815℃～900℃, in addition to further precipitation of M23C6 type carbides, extremely trace amounts of M6C type carbides also precipitate.

Overview of Alloy GH1035: GH1035 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy, solid solution strengthened by elements such as chromium and tungsten, with an operating temperature below 900℃. The alloy has high thermal strength, close to the level of GH3039 nickel-based alloy. It has good hot working plasticity and welding, cold forming process performance. Main products include plates, bars, wires, and ring parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH1035: The alloy has been used to manufacture turbine engine combustion chambers, turbine outer rings, and exhaust devices working long-term below 900℃. It is suitable for manufacturing flame tubes, combustion chamber casings, afterburners, guide rings, and other annular parts for various aviation and aerospace engines. The fatigue performance of the alloy in the medium temperature range is lower than that of nickel-based alloys for similar purposes, and its resistance to hydrochloric acid corrosion is slightly worse than that of nickel-based alloys for similar purposes [4].
Material grade of Alloy GH1035: GH1035 (GH35).
Similar grades to Alloy GH1035: EIM703, KhN38VT, KhN38VB (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH1035: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 1952A Specification for cold rolled superalloy plates for aviation; GJB 2612 Specification for cold drawn superalloy wires for welding; GJB 3317A Specification for hot rolled superalloy plates for aviation; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.
Melting process of Alloy GH1035: Electric arc furnace, or electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH1035: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Plates: (1100～1140)℃/AC; b) Bars and forgings: (1130～1150)℃×(1～1.5)h/WQ + (710～730)℃×(8～10)h/AC; c) Ring parts: 1120℃×(1.5～2.5)h/WQ + 720℃×(8～16)h/AC.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH1035: Excerpted from GJB 1952A, GJB 3317A, and GJB 2612.
Density of Alloy GH1035: ρ=8.17g/cm³ [1].
Oxidation resistance of Alloy GH1035: In air medium, the oxidation rate is (0.0820～0.1170)g/(m²·h) when tested at 900℃ for 100h; and 0.4238g/(m²·h) when tested at 1100℃ for 100h [1].
Fatigue properties of Alloy GH1035: For cold rolled plate after 1120℃ solution treatment, the rotating bending fatigue limit σ(-1) (10^7 cycles) at 900℃ is 127MPa [1].
Process performance of Alloy GH1035: The alloy has good stamping performance, with a limiting deep drawing coefficient of 2.03～2.15.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH1035: The recommended part heat treatment specification is: intermediate solution treatment 1060℃～1080℃, air cool or water cool; final solution treatment 1080℃～1120℃, air cool or water cool. Holding time is calculated as (1.2～2.0) min/mm.
Surface treatment process of Alloy GH1035: Flame tube surface treatment process is electropolishing according to general specifications.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH1035: The alloy can be machined satisfactorily.

Overview of Alloy K446: K446 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with an operating temperature below 700℃. The alloy contains w(Fe) 15% and no cobalt. The alloy has good high-temperature strength, hot corrosion resistance, good casting performance, and weldability. Main products are investment cast structural parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K446: The alloy is suitable for manufacturing turbine rectifier struts and other parts in industrial and marine gas turbines with operating temperatures not exceeding 700℃. The alloy is low cost. In media containing sulfur and sea salt, the alloy can work reliably for a long time under static load conditions. After long-term aging at 650℃×8000h, only a small amount of μ phase precipitates, which has no significant effect on performance, and tensile and rupture properties are still far greater than the technical specifications.
Material grade of Alloy K446: K446 (K46, K4191).
Similar grade to Alloy K446: ChS91-VI (Russia).
Material technical standards for Alloy K446: Q/KJ.J02.25 K446 cast nickel-based superalloy ingot.

Melting and casting process of Alloy K446: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum induction furnace is used for remelting, using investment precision casting with sand filling method to cast parts and specimens. Pouring temperature 1400℃~1440℃, mold shell temperature 850℃~950℃.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K446: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.25, the standard heat treatment regime is: 1110℃±10℃×4.5h/AC + 750℃±10℃×10.5h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K446: Excerpted from Q/KJ.J02.25. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and precision castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K446: 1330℃~1355℃ [1,2].
Density of Alloy K446: ρ=8.143g/cm³ [1,3].
Magnetic properties of Alloy K446: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K446: After standard heat treatment, the room temperature hardness of the alloy is HBS 358～346.
Impact properties of Alloy K446: For precision cast test bars after standard heat treatment, the average room temperature impact toughness αku=186kJ/m² [1,2].

Forming process and performance of Alloy K446: The alloy has good casting performance and can be used to manufacture parts by investment precision casting. Solidification start temperature 1347℃ [1,2], linear shrinkage 2.1%.
Welding performance of Alloy K446: Electron beam welding can be used for welding.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy K446: Same as the standard heat treatment regime of the alloy.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy K446: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH5605S: GH5605S is a Co-Cr-Ni based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy, solid solution strengthened by adding nickel, chromium, and tungsten elements, with an operating temperature below 650℃. The alloy has high strength and high-temperature oxidation resistance. It also has good elastic modulus, wear resistance, and welding performance. Trace rare earth element yttrium added to the alloy improves hot working plasticity and cold forming process performance. Main products are cold drawn wires.
Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH5605S: The alloy has been used to manufacture new advanced brush seals for aviation engines.
Material technical standards for Alloy GH5605S: Q/HDBYG 015 Wires for brush seals.
Melting process of Alloy GH5605S: Vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable melting processes are adopted.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH5605S: Excerpted from literature [1] and Q/HDBYG 015, the standard heat treatment regime for wires: (1170～1230)℃/AC, holding time determined by wire d.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH5605S: Excerpted from Q/HDBYG 015.
Density of Alloy GH5605S: ρ=9.1g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH5605S: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH5605S: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for the product.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH5605S: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH4708: GH4708 is a Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with an operating temperature below 850℃. The alloy is comprehensively strengthened by elements such as tungsten, molybdenum, aluminum, and titanium, giving it high thermal strength. The alloy has good oxidation resistance, stable microstructure, and satisfactory cold and hot forming and welding process performance. It is suitable for manufacturing load-bearing parts working below 850℃ in different parts of aviation engines such as compressors, combustion chambers, turbines, and adjustable nozzles [1]. Main products include hot rolled and forged bars, plates, wires, ring parts, and forgings.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH4708: The alloy has been used to manufacture high-temperature load-bearing parts such as aviation engine mounting edges, eccentric rings, half rings, seal ring seats, low-pressure turbine outer rings, nozzle casings, tie rods, pins, and base plates. It has passed long-term engine test assessments. The alloy surface can be treated with aluminizing, passivation, silver plating, and chrome plating. No harmful phase precipitates in the alloy after aging at 800℃ for 1000h.
Material grade of Alloy GH4708: GH4708 (GH708).
Similar grades to Alloy GH4708: EP708, KhN62BMTYu (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH4708: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; Q/3B 988 GH708 alloy cold rolled thin sheets; Q/3B 989 GH708 alloy hot rolled and forged bars; Q/3B 998 GH708 alloy hot rolled thick plates; Liaoxin 7-0055 GH708 alloy hot rolled thick plates; Liaoxin 7-0057 GH708 alloy cold rolled thin sheets; Liaoxin 7-0060 GH708 alloy hot rolled and forged bars.
Melting process of Alloy GH4708: Vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting process is adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH4708: Excerpted from Q/3B 988, Q/3B 989, Q/3B 998, Liaoxin 7-0055, Liaoxin 7-0057, and Liaoxin 7-0060, the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Hot rolled and forged bars: 1140℃±10℃×1h/AC + 800℃±10℃×15h/AC; b) Cold rolled thin sheets, supply state (annealed): (1080~1120)℃/AC or WQ, standard heat treatment: 1140℃±10℃/AC + 800℃±10℃×15h/AC, where annealing and solution treatment holding times depend on the actual thickness of the sheet; c) Hot rolled medium thick plates: (1080~1120)℃/WQ + 800℃±10℃×(5~15)h/AC, where solution holding time depends on the actual thickness of the plate.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH4708: Excerpted from Liaoxin 7-0060, Liaoxin 7-0055, and Liaoxin 7-0057. d10mm~220mm hot rolled and forged bars; δ3.5mm~11.0mm hot rolled medium thick plates; δ0.8mm~3.0mm cold rolled thin sheets. Bars are supplied in rolled or forged condition; plates are supplied after solution + alkali/acid pickling + leveling + straightening + trimming.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH4708: 1352℃~1364℃ [3].
Density of Alloy GH4708: ρ=8.55g/cm³ [5].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH4708: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Oxidation resistance of Alloy GH4708: In air medium, the oxidation rate of the alloy tested at 1000℃ for 100h is 0.155g/(m²·h) [5].

Surface treatment process of Alloy GH4708: The alloy surface can be treated with aluminizing, passivation, silver plating, and chrome plating. For aluminizing heat treatment, depending on the required depth of the aluminized layer, generally for a layer of 0.03mm~0.08mm, 920℃ for 8h~12h, or 950℃ for 3h~5h aluminizing process can be used.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH4708: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH1016: GH1016 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy, solid solution strengthened by elements such as chromium, tungsten, and molybdenum. The alloy can be used for a long time below 950℃ and for a short time below 1000℃. The alloy has good oxidation resistance, high plasticity, relatively high thermal strength, and good thermal fatigue resistance. The alloy has good cold stamping forming and welding process performance. Main products include cold rolled thin sheets, medium thick plates, bars, wires, forgings, and ring forgings.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH1016: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine combustion chamber sheet metal stamped parts and welded structural parts and other high-temperature components, such as load-bearing elements, structural casings, mounting edges, mounting seats, and pipe joints, as well as parts working short-term below 1000℃. When working long-term at 700℃～900℃, the alloy shows a certain age-hardening phenomenon, leading to decreased room temperature plasticity and slightly reduced high-temperature rupture strength. Intergranular oxidation occurs when working long-term above 700℃. Its high-temperature oxidation resistance above 1000℃ is slightly worse than nickel-based alloys for similar purposes. The alloy can be used as a substitute material for nickel-based alloys GH3030, GH3039, and GH3044.
Material grade of Alloy GH1016: GH1016 (GH16, GH15A, GR-5A).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH1016: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 1952A Specification for cold rolled superalloy plates for aviation; GJB 5301 Specification for superalloy ring parts for aviation; HB 5417 GH1016 alloy bars; HB 5418 GH1016 alloy cold rolled thin sheets; HB 5419 GH1016 alloy round cakes, ring blanks, and ring parts; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.
Melting process of Alloy GH1016: Electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting melting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH1016: Excerpted from HB/Z 140, GJB 1952A, GJB 5301, HB 5417, HB 5418, and HB 5419, the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Cold rolled plates: (1140～1180)℃/AC. Where holding time: 8min～12min for δ≤3mm; 12min～16min for δ3mm～5mm. b) Tubes, wires, sheet metal parts, and welded assemblies: (1150～1170)℃/AC. Where holding time: 8min～12min for d(δ)≤3mm; 12min～16min for d(δ)3mm～5mm. c) Bars, forgings, round cakes, and ring parts: 1160℃±10℃/AC.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH1016: Excerpted from GJB 1952A, GJB 5301, HB 5417, HB 5418, and HB 5419.
Density of Alloy GH1016: ρ=8.31g/cm³ [19].
Impact properties of Alloy GH1016: After standard heat treatment of ring parts, room temperature impact toughness: Longitudinal, αK=(1290～1750)kJ/m²; Transverse, αK=334kJ/m² [19].

Surface treatment process of Alloy GH1016: Oxide scale on the surface of parts after heat treatment can be removed by sandblasting or pickling. When using pickling to remove oxide scale, a single pickling process with hydrofluoric acid-sulfuric acid-nitric acid aqueous solution can be used, or a composite alkaline-acid pickling process with sodium hydroxide-sodium nitrate and sulfuric acid-sodium chloride aqueous solution can be used.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH1016: The alloy can be machined satisfactorily.
Phase transformation temperature of Alloy GH1016: Laves phase precipitation peak is at 700℃, and begins to dissolve at 800℃.

Overview of Alloy GH2696: GH2696 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with a long-term operating temperature below 650℃ and a short-term operating temperature up to 750℃. The alloy has high yield strength, rupture and creep strength, as well as good high-temperature elastic properties, gas corrosion resistance, and processing plasticity. It is suitable for manufacturing turbine and compressor fasteners, disks, and working blades, turbine casings, annular parts (including connecting rings) working long-term below 650℃, and cylindrical helical springs working at 400℃～650℃. Main products include bars, forgings, ring parts, plates, strips, and wires.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH2696: Similar alloys are widely used abroad to manufacture various high-temperature components of aviation engines, with long-term reliable use experience. In China, this alloy is used to manufacture various parts such as quick-release rings, fasteners, springs, pipe joints, mounts, and brackets on various engines, and has passed usage assessments and been put into mass production and use. The alloy has satisfactory oxidation and gas corrosion resistance below 700℃. The alloy has satisfactory corrosion resistance under all-weather conditions. When the alloy is aged for a long time at 800℃, a large amount of rod-like Laves phase precipitates at grain boundaries and within grains, significantly reducing strength and toughness [11].
Material grade of Alloy GH2696: GH2696 (GH696, GH696M).
Similar grades to Alloy GH2696: EP33, EI696M, 10Kh11N23T3MR (Russia).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH2696: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GB/T 14994 Specification for cold drawn superalloy bars; GJB 2611A Specification for cold drawn superalloy bars for aviation; GJB 3318A Specification for cold rolled superalloy strips for aviation; HB 5463 GH696 alloy hot rolled and forged bars; HB 5464 GH696 alloy cold drawn bars; HB 5465 GH696 alloy cold rolled thin sheets; HB 5466 GH696 alloy cold rolled spring steel strips; HB 5467 GH696 alloy cold drawn wires; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.

Melting process of Alloy GH2696: The alloy adopts electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting, or electric arc furnace + vacuum arc remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + vacuum arc remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum arc remelting melting processes.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH2696: Excerpted from GB/T 14994, GJB 2611A, GJB 3318A, HB 5463, HB 5464, HB 5465, HB 5466, and HB 5467.
Density of Alloy GH2696: ρ=7.93g/cm³ [2].
Oxidation resistance of Alloy GH2696: The alloy has satisfactory oxidation resistance and gas corrosion resistance below 700℃ [1].
Corrosion resistance of Alloy GH2696: The alloy has satisfactory hot corrosion resistance under all-weather conditions [1].
Welding performance of Alloy GH2696: The alloy has satisfactory welding performance and can be welded by argon arc welding, spot welding, and seam welding.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH2696: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for the product variety. The choice of heat treatment depends on the working conditions of the part; the higher the working temperature of the part, the higher the quenching temperature. For parts working at 750℃～800℃, (1160～1180)℃/OQ or AC + 780℃×(16～25)h/AC is used; for parts working below 750℃, (1100～1130)℃×(3～5)h/OQ + (720～780)℃×16h/FC to (600～650)℃×(10～20)h/AC is used; for parts working at (400～700)℃ under stress concentration conditions, (1100～1130)℃×(3～5)h/OQ + (840～850)℃×(3～5)h/AC + (700～730)℃×(16～25)h/AC is used. For parts working at (-196～600)℃, (900～1000)℃×(2～5)h/OQ + (720～780)℃×16h + (600～650)℃×16h/AC is used. It is best to use furnace cooling from the first aging temperature to the second aging temperature [12].
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH2696: The alloy has good machining performance.
Phase transformation temperature of Alloy GH2696: The precipitation peak of γ' phase is around 750℃, secondary γ' phase precipitates at 600℃～650℃, and completely dissolves at 950℃.

Typical microstructure of Alloy GH2696: The microstructure of the alloy in the standard heat treatment state consists of γ solid solution, γ' (Ni₃AlTi) phase, Ti(CN), M₃B₂ type borides, η phase, Laves phase, and trace phases such as Y phase. γ' phase is the main strengthening phase of the alloy, with ω(γ) accounting for 10.1% of the alloy. γ' phase is a metastable phase, which transforms into η phase during long-term aging above 700℃, but since the amount of η phase is small, the impact on mechanical properties is not obvious. During long-term aging at 800℃, the amount of γ' phase decreases significantly, and a large amount of rod-like Laves phase precipitates at grain boundaries and within grains, significantly reducing strength and toughness. When the alloy is solution treated above 1020℃, MC type carbides, M₃B₂, and η phase decrease significantly at grain boundaries, causing grain growth. M₃B₂ dissolves in large quantities when solution treated above 1100℃. Y phase is a Ti(CS) compound, which does not participate in the heat treatment and strengthening of the alloy [4,5,9].

Overview of Alloys GH2302 and K232: GH2302 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy, and the alloy grade for casting use is K232. The alloy can be used for a long time below 700℃ and for a short time at 850℃. The structure and properties of the alloy are stable, with good cold and hot forming performance. Main products include bars, plates, and precision castings.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloys GH2302 and K232: The alloy has been used to manufacture turbojet engine afterburners, etc., as well as industrial gas turbine blades. The alloy has no notch sensitivity at 700℃ and 850℃; bamboo-leaf-like Laves phase precipitates generally within grains after aging at 750℃×10000h [4]; the alloy has small crack tendency in argon arc welding in solution state, and re-aging after welding. Contact welding of plates is relatively difficult, and appropriate welding processes should be adopted.
Material grades of Alloys GH2302 and K232: GH2302 (GH302, JF-43), K232 (K32).

Material technical standards for Alloys GH2302 and K232: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GB/T 14993 Hot rolled superalloy bars for rotating parts; GB/T 14995 Hot rolled superalloy plates; GJB 1952A Specification for cold rolled superalloy thin sheets for aviation; YB/T 5248 Master alloys for cast superalloys; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys.

Heat treatment regime of Alloys GH2302 and K232: Excerpted from GB/T 14993, GB/T 14995, GJB 1952A, HB/Z 140, and YB/T 5248, the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Hot rolled bars: 1180℃±10℃×2h/AC + 1050℃±10℃×4h/AC + 800℃±10℃×16h/AC; b) Hot rolled plates and cold rolled thin sheets: (1100～1130)℃/AC + 800℃±10℃×16h/AC; c) Castings: 1100℃×(3～5)h/AC + 800℃×16h/AC.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloys GH2302 and K232: Excerpted from GB/T 14993, GB/T 14995, GJB 1952A, and YB/T 5248.
Melting temperature range of Alloys GH2302 and K232: Melting point is 1375℃ [5].
Density of Alloys GH2302 and K232: ρ=8.09g/cm³ [5].
Part heat treatment process of Alloys GH2302 and K232: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product.

Phase transformation temperature of Alloys GH2302 and K232: The peak precipitation temperature of γ' phase is 800℃, and it completely dissolves at about 1000℃; Laves phase precipitation temperature is approximately 700℃～850℃.

Overview of Alloy GH4090: GH4090 is a Ni-Cr-Co based precipitation hardening wrought superalloy with an operating temperature up to 920℃. The alloy has high tensile strength and creep resistance, good oxidation and corrosion resistance at 815℃～870℃, and high fatigue strength under repeated alternating cold and hot action. The alloy has good processing and welding performance. It is suitable for manufacturing aviation turbine engine turbine disks, blades, high-temperature fasteners, clamps, seal rings, and elastic elements. Main products include hot rolled bars, cold drawn bars, cold rolled plates, strips, and cold drawn wires.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH4090: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine high-temperature spring elements, high-temperature fasteners, combustion chamber clamps, and stop pins. Similar grades are also used abroad to manufacture aviation engine turbine working blades, turbine disks, and other components. The alloy has good oxidation and corrosion resistance below 1040℃, but intergranular oxidation is likely to occur above 1040℃.
Material grade of Alloy GH4090: GH4090 (GH90).
Similar grade to Alloy GH4090: Nimonic 90 (UK).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH4090: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GB/T 14994 Cold drawn superalloy bars; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; WS9-7014 GH90 (N90) alloy cold drawn wires for springs; WS9-7015.1 GH90 (N90) alloy cold drawn and solution treated spring wires; WS9-7015.2 GH90 (N90) alloy cold drawn and solution treated flat spring wires; WS9-7016 GH90 (N90) alloy cold drawn bars; WS9-7086 GH90 (N90) alloy cold rolled thin sheets and strips (hard state); WS9-7087 GH90 (N90) alloy cold rolled thin sheets and strips (soft state).

Melting process of Alloy GH4090: Non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + vacuum arc remelting melting processes are adopted.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH4090: Excerpted from GB/T 14994, WS9-7014, WS9-7015.1, WS9-7015.2, WS9-7016, WS9-7086, and WS9-7087. Cold drawn bars include d8mm～45mm round bars, nominal side length a8mm～30mm square bars; inscribed circle nominal d8mm～36mm hexagonal bars; d0.05mm～8mm cold drawn wires; 1.65mm×0.5mm flat wires; δ≤4mm cold rolled thin sheets; δ≤0.8mm strips. The cold drawing deformation of bars after the final intermediate annealing should be 8%～12%. Cold drawn bars for upsetting are supplied after cold drawing + polishing, or after solution treatment. Cold drawn bars for machining are supplied after solution treatment + descaling; cold drawn wires for springs are supplied in cold drawn state. Cold drawn and solution treated spring wires are supplied after cold drawing, or cold drawing + 1080℃ solution treatment; soft state cold rolled thin sheets and strips are supplied after softening treatment + alkali/acid pickling or bright heat treatment + trimming + leveling or straightening, requiring hardness ≤250HV. Hard state cold rolled thin sheets and strips should have a cold rolling deformation of 20%～35% after the last intermediate annealing, supplied after cold rolling + trimming + leveling or straightening.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH4090: 1310℃～1370℃ [5].
Density of Alloy GH4090: ρ=8.18g/cm³ [2].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH4090: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Welding performance of Alloy GH4090: The alloy can undergo inert gas shielded tungsten arc welding and flash butt welding in the solution state.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH4090: Same as the standard heat treatment regime for each product.

Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH4090: The alloy has good machining performance in the solution treated state. After aging treatment, it should be machined slowly using hard tools according to the prescribed feed rate.
Phase transformation temperature of Alloy GH4090: Dissolution temperature range of γ' phase is 980℃～1000℃; Dissolution temperature range of M23C6 type carbide is 1100℃～1150℃.

Overview of Alloy FGH4097: FGH4097 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening powder metallurgy superalloy with a γ phase volume fraction of about 60%, used for long terms below 750℃. Compared with similar cast or wrought superalloys, this alloy has uniform microstructure, fine grains, good tensile and rupture strength, fatigue and creep resistance, as well as excellent corrosion and high-temperature oxidation resistance. It is a key material for high-performance engine turbine disks, ring parts, and other hot-end components.
Material grade of Alloy FGH4097: FGH4097 (FGH97).
Similar grade to Alloy FGH4097: EP741NP (Russia).

Melting and main manufacturing process of Alloy FGH4097: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt master alloy bars. Pre-alloyed powder is prepared by Plasma Rotating Electrode Process (PREP). The powder is vacuum degassed, canned, sealed, and then directly formed by hot isostatic pressing (AS-HIP). Powder particle size is 50μm～150μm. 1180℃～1210℃, holding time not less than 2h, pressure not less than 120MPa.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy FGH4097: Various specifications of hot isostatically pressed parts, such as turbine disks, shaft parts, and ring parts, can be provided according to design requirements. Hot isostatically pressed parts are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy FGH4097: 1290℃～1350℃ [1].
Density of Alloy FGH4097: ρ≥8.26g/cm³ [1,2].
Magnetic properties of Alloy FGH4097: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy FGH4097: AS-HIP parts treated by Regime I, room temperature HBW 388～302 [1]; treated by Regime II, room temperature HBW 415～302 [2].
Part heat treatment process of Alloy FGH4097: Same as the standard heat treatment Regime I or II of the alloy.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy FGH4097: No special requirements.
Phase transformation temperature of Alloy FGH4097: Complete solid solution temperature of γ' phase is 1180℃～1190℃.

Overview of Alloy GH1015: GH1015 is an Fe-Ni-Cr based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy with an operating temperature below 950℃. The alloy is solid solution strengthened by adding chromium, tungsten, and molybdenum elements. The alloy has high plasticity, medium rupture and creep strength, good thermal fatigue resistance, as well as good processing and welding performance, and structural stability. Main products include cold rolled thin sheets, bars, wires, and forgings.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH1015: The alloy has been used to manufacture sheet metal structural parts such as aviation engine combustion chambers and afterburner cylinders, and other high-temperature components.
