When working long-term at 700℃～900℃, there is a certain age-hardening phenomenon, causing room temperature plasticity to decrease; when working long-term above 700℃, there is a tendency for intergranular oxidation, which can be effectively protected by enamel coating. High-temperature oxidation resistance above 1000℃ is slightly worse than nickel-based alloys for similar purposes. This alloy can be used as a substitute material for GH2039, GH3030, GH3044, and GH3536 alloys.
Material grade of Alloy GH1015: GH1015 (GH15, GR5).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH1015: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 3165A Specification for hot rolled and forged superalloy bars for aviation load-bearing parts; HB/Z 140 Heat treatment process for aviation superalloys; HB 5199 Cold rolled superalloy thin sheets for aviation; YB/T 5245 Hot rolled and forged superalloy bars for general load-bearing parts.

Melting process of Alloy GH1015: Electric arc furnace + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting processes are adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH1015: Excerpted from GJB 3165A, YB/T 5245, HB 5199, and HB/Z 140, the standard heat treatment regimes for various products are: a) Cold rolled thin sheets, wires, sheet metal parts, and welded assemblies: 1130℃～1170℃/AC. Where: holding time 8min～12min for δ(d)≤3mm; holding time 12min～16min for δ(d)3mm～5mm; b) Hot rolled and forged bars, forgings, die forgings: 1140℃～1170℃/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH1015: d20mm～300mm bars; δ0.5mm～6.5mm strips and cold rolled thin sheets; various sizes and specifications of forgings and die forgings. Bars are supplied without heat treatment, polished or turned; sheets are supplied after heat treatment + finishing + descaling + leveling + trimming; forgings and die forgings are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Density of Alloy GH1015: ρ=8.32g/cm³ [14].
Short-term mechanical properties of Alloy GH1015: For bars after standard heat treatment, room temperature impact toughness ak=2880kJ/m² [9].
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH1015: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy DD407: DD407 is a nickel-based precipitation hardening single crystal superalloy, belonging to the first generation of single crystal superalloys. The alloy possesses excellent rupture and creep strength, medium-temperature performance, and fatigue resistance; it has good single crystal casting performance, good long-term structural stability, and environmental performance. The alloy has low density and high specific strength, suitable for solid or hollow gas turbine working blades operating below 1050℃, as well as guide vanes and other high-temperature parts operating below 1100℃.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy DD407: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine gas turbine working blades, with good batch production and usage conditions. To ensure alloy performance, the cooling rate during solution treatment should be ≥130℃/min. Blade surfaces are usually treated with multi-element low-pressure plasma spraying to further improve oxidation and corrosion resistance.
Material grade of Alloy DD407: DD407.
Similar grade to Alloy DD407: AM3 (France).

Material technical standards for Alloy DD407: Q/GYB 776 Technical conditions for DD407 superalloy master alloy for single crystal castings; Q/GYB 777 Technical conditions for DD407 alloy single crystal turbine blade castings.

Melting and casting process of Alloy DD407: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, and vacuum directional solidification furnace is used for remelting and pouring single crystal castings or test bars.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy DD407: Excerpted from Q/GYB 776 and Q/GYB 777, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1300 (+10, -5)℃×3h/AC + 1080℃±10℃×6h/AC + 870℃±10℃×20h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy DD407: Excerpted from Q/GYB 776 and Q/GYB 777. The master alloy is cast into cylindrical long bars with a diameter range of 75mm～90mm or 40mm～60mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition, and single crystal parts are supplied in as-cast condition or after solution treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy DD407: 1327℃～1363℃ (DTA) [1].
Density of Alloy DD407: ρ=8.24g/cm³ [1].
Magnetic properties of Alloy DD407: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming process and performance of Alloy DD407: The alloy has good casting performance. Investment casting method can be used to cast single crystal turbine blades.

Part heat treatment process of Alloy DD407: Same as the standard heat treatment regime of the alloy. No-allowance blades should be solution treated and aged under vacuum or low-pressure argon protection.
Surface treatment process of Alloy DD407: Low-pressure plasma spraying of NiCoCrAlYTa is adopted.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy DD407: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH3170: GH3170 is a Ni-Cr-Co based solid solution strengthened wrought superalloy, characterized by containing w(Co) 20%, w(Cr) 20%, and w(W) 20%, with trace lanthanum added to strengthen grain boundaries. The alloy has high strength and creep resistance at 1000℃, and still has relatively good instantaneous strength at 1100℃～1200℃. The alloy has excellent oxidation resistance and good cold forming and welding process performance. The main product is plate.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy GH3170: The alloy has been used to manufacture aviation engine afterburner adjustable flap parts. The performance level of this alloy is close to American solid solution strengthened cobalt-based alloys HA188 and MAR-M918. It is recommended for aviation engine combustion chambers and other high-temperature parts, as well as nozzle materials for rocket engines. The alloy precipitates μ phase after long-term aging at 900℃ and 1000℃, causing a decrease in room temperature plasticity, but it can still meet usage requirements [3].
Material grade of Alloy GH3170: GH3170 (GH170).

Material technical standards for Alloy GH3170: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; Fugao Xin 1985-10 Provisional technical conditions for GH170 alloy cold rolled thin sheets for aviation.
Melting process of Alloy GH3170: Vacuum induction furnace + electroslag remelting process is adopted.

Heat treatment regime of Alloy GH3170: Excerpted from Fugao Xin 1985-10 and literature [4], the standard heat treatment regime for plates is: (1190～1240)℃/AC, holding time determined by plate thickness.
Product specifications and supply status of Alloy GH3170: Excerpted from Fugao Xin 1985-10.
Melting temperature range of Alloy GH3170: 1395℃～1425℃ [1].
Density of Alloy GH3170: ρ=9.34g/cm³ [4].
Magnetic properties of Alloy GH3170: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy GH3170: After solution treatment, room temperature hardness of the alloy is HV 201 [4].

Welding performance of Alloy GH3170: The alloy can be welded by spot welding, seam welding, and argon arc welding. The joint strength coefficient for seam welding and argon arc welding is greater than 85%.
Part heat treatment process of Alloy GH3170: Final heat treatment temperature for sheet metal parts is 1230℃; intermediate heat treatment temperature is 1170℃.
Machining and grinding performance of Alloy GH3170: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy K214: K214 is an iron-based precipitation hardening equiaxed cast superalloy with an operating temperature below 900℃. The alloy composition is characterized by containing relatively high tungsten content and a suitable Al/Ti ratio [1]. The alloy has high strength, but relatively low plasticity. The alloy has good casting performance. It is suitable for manufacturing gas turbine guide vanes and other high-temperature parts.

Application overview and characteristics of Alloy K214: The alloy has been used to manufacture turbine guide vanes for various turbojet and turboprop engines, with good performance. Under 900℃, 100h rupture conditions, the rupture strength is 200MPa, reaching the level of nickel-based cast superalloy K401, but the plasticity is lower. After stress aging at 700℃, 343MPa for 18633h, no TCP phase precipitates; after stress aging at 850℃, 245MPa for 280h, Laves phase precipitates, with w(Laves) accounting for about 0.5% of the alloy [7].
Material grade of Alloy K214: K214 (K14).

Material technical standards for Alloy K214: GB/T 14992 Classification and designation of superalloys and high temperature intermetallic materials; GJB 5512.1 Specification for cast superalloy master alloys for cast superalloys and cast intermetallic high temperature materials - Part 1: Master alloys for ordinary precision castings; HB 5160 K214 alloy ingot.

Melting process of Alloy K214: Vacuum induction furnace is used to melt the master alloy, low vacuum induction furnace or non-vacuum high-frequency induction rollover furnace is used for rapid remelting, pouring investment precision castings and test bars. Pouring temperature: 1450℃~1500℃ for low vacuum; 1550℃~1620℃ for non-vacuum.
Heat treatment regime of Alloy K214: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1, the standard heat treatment regime for the alloy is: 1100℃±10℃×5h/AC.

Product specifications and supply status of Alloy K214: Excerpted from GJB 5512.1. The master alloy is cast into round long bars with a diameter range of 70mm～90mm and a length of not less than 250mm. The master alloy is supplied in as-cast condition; finished blades are supplied in as-cast condition or after heat treatment.
Melting temperature range of Alloy K214: 1330℃~1376℃ [1].
Density of Alloy K214: ρ=8.03g/cm³ [3].
Hardness of Alloy K214: For precision-cast test bars in the standard heat-treated condition, the room temperature hardness is HRC 44~47[1].
Creep Properties of Alloy K214: For the alloy at 850°C for 100h, the creep limit with εp=0.2% is σ=177MPa.
Forming Process and Performance of Alloy K214: The alloy has good castability, with a linear shrinkage rate similar to alloys K401 and K412, approximately 2%.

Weldability of Alloy K214: When welding parts to 1Cr18Ni9Ti sheet or 40Cr steel, the weld zone exhibits: σb=765MPa~794MPa, σP0.2=579MPa~647MPa, δ₅=4%~7%, ψ=28%~35%[1]. Recommended electrodes: HGH1113, BXH-1, Eu-602, D-275. For TIG welding, the welding current should not be too high, generally controlled between 40A~50A (voltage 20V), to prevent local overheating.
Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K214: Same as the standard heat treatment schedule for the alloy.

Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K214: Solid pack aluminizing uses aluminum-iron powder at 800°C~830°C, holding for 6h~8h. Vacuum vapor deposition-diffusion aluminizing uses pure aluminum evaporated under vacuum via tungsten wire heating. After obtaining a pure aluminum film on the part surface, diffusion is carried out in argon at 950°C for 1h.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy K214: Recommended cutting tools: lathe tools: YW4, YG6, YG8; milling cutters: W9Cr4V5, W2Mo9Cr4Co8; drills: YG9.
Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy K214: The dissolution temperature of the γ' phase is approximately 1050°C, and the dissolution temperature of the M₃B₂ type boride is approximately 1170°C.

Overview of Alloy K419H: K419H is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed crystal cast superalloy. It is a modified version of the K419 alloy with the addition of w(Hf) 1.2%~1.6%, for use at temperatures below 1000°C. The alloy exhibits relatively high medium- and high-temperature strength, stable microstructure, and good castability. It is suitable for manufacturing gas turbine rotor blades, guide vanes, integral turbines, and other high-temperature components.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy K419H: The alloy has been used to produce high-pressure turbine blades for aero engines. Due to its high hafnium content, the alloy shows significantly improved medium-temperature (760°C) stress rupture life and ductility, as well as room temperature and medium-temperature instantaneous ductility. Its medium- and high-temperature strength is higher than that of K419, K403, and K409 alloys, comparable to the American MAR-M002 alloy[3]. The alloy does not precipitate plate-like σ or μ phases after long-term high-temperature aging[5].
Material Designation of Alloy K419H: K419H(K19H)
Material Technical Standards for Alloy K419H: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; Q/6S145 K419H Alloy Ingot
Melting and Casting Process for Alloy K419H: Master alloy is melted using a vacuum induction furnace, and parts and test bars are cast via investment precision casting by vacuum induction remelting.
Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy K419H: Extracted from Q/6S 145, the standard heat treatment schedule for the alloy is: 870°C×16h/AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy K419H: Extracted from Q/6S 145. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 70mm to 90mm and lengths not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied either as-cast or after heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy K419H: 1290°C~1345°C[1]
Density of Alloy K419H: ρ=8.50g/cm³[1]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K419H: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K419H: In the standard heat-treated condition, the room temperature hardness is HB 385~366[1].
Forming Process and Performance of Alloy K419H: The alloy has good castability and can be cast into complex hollow turbine blades, guide vanes, integral turbine disks, and other parts.

Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K419H: Parts not requiring aluminizing are heat-treated according to standard specifications. For parts requiring aluminizing, the aluminizing diffusion treatment can be combined with heat treatment.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K419H: Surface coating is applied using solid pack aluminizing process.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy K419H: No special requirements.
Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy K419H: The complete solid solution temperature of the γ' phase is 1240°C.

Overview of Alloy GH2026: GH2026 is an Fe-Ni-Co-Cr-based precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy, with long-term service temperature ranging from 540°C to 570°C, and a maximum service temperature up to 677°C. The alloy is solid-solution strengthened by chromium and molybdenum, and age-hardened by the γ' phase formed from high titanium and low aluminum content. Cobalt reduces the solubility of titanium and aluminum in the solid solution, promoting increased precipitation of the γ' phase, thereby improving its thermal stability and lowering its stacking fault energy. The alloy offers superior comprehensive properties, excellent stress relaxation and creep resistance, and no notch sensitivity. It is suitable for manufacturing bolts, steam seal spring sheets, sealing rings, pins, etc. Main products include hot-rolled and forged bars, hot-rolled plates, and cold-rolled sheets.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy GH2026: The alloy has been used to manufacture fasteners for ground-based steam turbines, operating satisfactorily for over 100,000 hours under steam conditions of 540°C~570°C. Similar alloys are used abroad for aero-engine turbine fasteners and blades. The alloy exhibits good plasticity at medium temperatures, reducing the risk of fracture due to stress rupture and creep during long-term service. No TCP harmful phases are found after long-term aging or long-term stress aging within the service temperature range.
Material Designation of Alloy GH2026: GH2026(GH26, R-26)
Similar Designations of Alloy GH2026: Refractaloy26 (USA).
Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH2026: Fu Gaoxin 87-1 GNS26 (R-26) Alloy Technical Specification; HJ 418 R-26 Superalloy Fastener Blank Technical Specification
Melting Process for Alloy GH2026: Non-vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting, or vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting processes are used.

Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy GH2026: Extracted from literature[1] Fu Gaoxin 87-1 and HJ 418, the standard heat treatment schedules for various product forms are: 1025℃±13℃×1h/OQ + 815℃±8℃×20h/FC → 730℃±8℃×20h/AC.
Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy GH2026: Extracted from Fu Gaoxin 87-1 and HJ 418.
Density of Alloy GH2026: ρ=8.20g/cm³[1].
Hardness of Alloy GH2026: After standard heat treatment, at 20°C, HRC is 26~35 for various product forms[1].

Forming Process and Performance of Alloy GH2026: Ingot heating temperature: 1140°C, hold 3h; start forging temperature: 1050°C; stop forging temperature: ≥950°C; for billet conversion forging: heating temperature 1130°C, hold 2h; start forging temperature 1050°C; final forging temperature ≥900°C.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy GH2026: During machining, use low speed, small feed, and slow feed rate.

Overview of Alloy JG4246A(MX246A): JG4246A is a domestically developed equiaxed crystal cast high-temperature material based on the Ni₃Al intermetallic compound. It exhibits good high-temperature mechanical properties and oxidation resistance within the temperature range up to 1200°C. It is suitable for manufacturing high-temperature ablation-resistant load-bearing components operating between 1000°C and 1200°C, and can also be applied to high-temperature load-bearing components such as high-temperature isothermal forging dies and hot pressing dies.
Material Designation of Alloy JG4246A(MX246A): JG4246A(MX246A).

Melting and Casting Process for Alloy JG4246A(MX246A): Master alloy is melted using a vacuum induction furnace, and parts and test bars are cast via investment precision casting by vacuum induction remelting.

Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy JG4246A(MX246A): Extracted from Q/GYB 05088, the standard heat treatment schedule for the alloy is: 1160°C×2.5h/AC; the alloy can also be used in the as-cast condition.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy JG4246A(MX246A): Extracted from Q/GYB 05088. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 70mm to 90mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied after heat treatment or in the as-cast condition.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy JG4246A(MX246A): 1302°C~1357°C[1].
Density of Alloy JG4246A(MX246A): ρ=7.83g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy JG4246A(MX246A): The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy JG4246A(MX246A): In the as-cast condition, the room temperature hardness is HRC29[1].

Forming Process and Performance of Alloy JG4246A(MX246A): Complex thin-walled regulating sheet components and dies for hot pressing and isothermal forging can be cast using the investment casting method.
Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy JG4246A(MX246A): 1160°C×2.5h/AC; parts can also be used in the as-cast condition.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy JG4246A(MX246A): Use carbide tools.

Overview of Alloy GH4720Li: GH4720Li is a Ni-Cr-Co-based precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy for use at temperatures below 750°C. The alloy has high high-temperature strength, fatigue resistance, and creep resistance; excellent resistance to sulfur corrosion and oxidation; and long-term microstructural stability. It is suitable for manufacturing aero-engine turbine disks and turbine blades. Main products include hot-rolled and forged bars, disk forgings, etc.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy GH4720Li: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine turbine disks and has passed engine test assessment. It can also be used for manufacturing integral turbine rotors for new-generation strategic missiles and high-thrust rocket engines, as well as land-based gas turbine disks for long-term service below 750°C. Severe elemental segregation exists in the alloy ingot, resulting in poor hot workability and a tendency to crack during deformation. The microstructure is very sensitive to hot working parameters, so isothermal forging is recommended. After long-term aging at 750°C for 2000h, no harmful phases are precipitated.
Material Designation of Alloy GH4720Li: GH4720Li (GH720Li).
Similar Designations of Alloy GH4720Li: Udimet720Li (USA), P.E.R72 (France).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH4720Li: QJ/DT01.63044 Provisional Technical Specification for GH4720Li Alloy Bars for Aero Engines; YJ.0314 GH4720Li Alloy Disk Forgings for Aero Engines; Q/GYB05100 GH4720Li Alloy Disk Forgings for Aero Engines
Melting Process for Alloy GH4720Li: Vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting + vacuum consumable remelting processes are used.

Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy GH4720Li: Extracted from literature[1], QJ/DT01.63044, YJ.0314, and Q/GYB 05100, the standard heat treatment schedule for bars and disk forgings is: (1080~1110)℃×(2~4)h/OQ + 650℃×24h/AC + 760℃×16h/AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy GH4720Li: Extracted from QJ/DT01.63044, YJ.0314, and Q/GYB 05100. d100mm~300mm bars; d100mm~800mm disk forgings. Bars are supplied without heat treatment, turned or ground; disk forgings are supplied after solution treatment.
Density of Alloy GH4720Li: ρ=8.14g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy GH4720Li: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming Process and Performance of Alloy GH4720Li: Severe elemental segregation exists in the alloy ingot, requiring homogenization treatment above 1160°C. A reasonable initial forging temperature is 1130°C~1160°C, with a final forging temperature not less than 1020°C. Uniform temperature and strain fields should be ensured during disk forging; isothermal forging is a suitable method.
Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy GH4720Li: Same as the standard heat treatment schedule for respective product forms.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy GH4720Li: No special requirements.
Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy GH4720Li: The complete dissolution temperature of the γ' phase is approximately 1150°C.

Overview of Alloy K406: K406 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed crystal cast superalloy for use at temperatures below 850°C. The alloy has a relatively simple composition, contains a high content of B, lacks rare and precious elements, and is low cost. It possesses moderate strength, good high-temperature oxidation and hot corrosion resistance, along with good casting and welding processability. Suitable for manufacturing gas turbine rotor blades, guide vanes, and other high-temperature components for long-term service below 850°C.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy K406: The alloy has been used to produce turbine rotor blades and guide vanes for various aero engines, with good service records. Similar alloys abroad, GMR-235D and BжJI-8, are used for blades and high-temperature structural parts in many aero engines, such as regulating sheet bases, frames, and low-pressure turbine outer rings. The microstructure and properties are stable during long-term aging at 850°C, with no σ phase precipitation. Grinding cracks may sometimes occur during grinding; using ultra-soft grinding wheels or creep-feed grinding can avoid this.
Material Designation of Alloy K406: K406(K6)
Similar Designations of Alloy K406: GMR-235D (USA), BжJI-8 (Russia)

Material Technical Standards for Alloy K406: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds Part 1: Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys for General Precision Castings; HB 7728 Specification for Precision Cast Turbine Working Blades of Superalloys for Aviation; HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed Crystal Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero Engines; HB/Z140 Heat Treatment Process for Superalloys for Aviation

Melting Process for Alloy K406: Master alloy is melted using a vacuum induction furnace; parts and test bars are cast via investment precision casting by vacuum induction remelting. Test specimens for mechanical properties are cast with an inoculant/refiner applied to the inner surface of the shell mold[5].
Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy K406: Extracted from HB/Z140, the standard heat treatment schedule for the alloy is: 980℃±10℃×5h/AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy K406: Extracted from GJB 5512.1 and HB 7763. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and lengths not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy K406: 1260~1345℃[1]
Density of Alloy K406: ρ=8.05g/cm³[1]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K406: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K406: After standard heat treatment, the room temperature hardness is HRC 35~38[1].
Impact Properties of Alloy K406: Precision-cast test bars after standard heat treatment, room temperature impact toughness: αku=120kJ/m² (average)[1].

Forming Process and Performance of Alloy K406: The alloy has good fluidity and low hot tearing tendency. Complex-shaped turbine blades, guide vanes, and other parts can be cast using the investment casting method.
Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K406: Same as the standard heat treatment schedule for the alloy.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K406: Slurry aluminizing method is used.

Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy K406: The alloy is easy to machine, but grinding cracks may occur during grinding. Using ultra-soft grinding wheels or creep-feed grinding can avoid grinding cracks.

Overview of Alloy JG1301(TAC-1): JG1301 is a Ti₃Al-based intermetallic compound high-temperature structural material alloyed with niobium, vanadium, and molybdenum to improve ductility and toughness. Components can work long-term below 650°C and short-term at 900°C. The service temperature is about 100°C higher than that of traditional high-temperature titanium alloys, and the weight is about 40% less than superalloys for the same service temperature. The alloy has excellent high-temperature mechanical properties, oxidation resistance, and weldability, along with good hot workability and the ability to be cold-formed. Main products include bars, plates, foils, disks, and rings.
Material Designation of Alloy JG1301(TAC-1): JG1301(TAC-1).
Melting Process for Alloy JG1301(TAC-1): Multiple vacuum consumable arc remelting process is used.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy JG1301(TAC-1): Extracted from Q/GYB544 and literature[1]. d10mm~300mm bars; δ2mm~20mm hot-rolled plates; δ0.3mm~4.0mm cold-rolled sheets; δ0.05mm~0.2mm foils; disks and rings of various specifications. Bars, disks, and rings are supplied after standard heat treatment or annealing, either directly or after surface machining; hot-rolled plates are supplied after standard heat treatment or annealing + sandblasting + straightening + edge trimming; cold-rolled sheets and foils are supplied in cold-rolled or annealed + edge-trimmed condition.
Density of Alloy JG1301(TAC-1): ρ=4.85g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy JG1301(TAC-1): The alloy is non-magnetic.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy JG1301(TAC-1): No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH2036: GH2036 is an Fe-Cr-Ni-based precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy, primarily strengthened by VC precipitation, for use at temperatures below 650°C. The alloy has a simple composition and stable microstructure. It possesses good physical and mechanical properties between 600°C and 650°C, along with good machinability. Main products include disks, rings, hot-rolled bars, and cold-drawn sections.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy GH2036: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine turbine disks, load-bearing rings, fasteners, and other components. It is also used for turbocharger turbine blades and other high-temperature parts in diesel and steam turbines. Among superalloys, this alloy has large production volume and wide application. The alloy has a high coefficient of thermal expansion and relatively poor oxidation and corrosion resistance, especially severe corrosion in salt spray atmospheres and under thermal cycling, which can reduce low-cycle fatigue performance[3]. The alloy exhibits notch stress rupture sensitivity at 600°C~650°C[3].
Material Designation of Alloy GH2036: GH2036(GH36).
Similar Designations of Alloy GH2036: ЭН481, 37Х12Н8Г8МФБ (Russia).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH2036: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; GB/T 14994 Cold-Drawn Bars of Superalloys; GB/T 14997 Forged Disks of Superalloys; GB/T 14998 Ring Blanks of Superalloys; GJB 2611A Specification for Cold-Drawn Bars of Superalloys for Aviation; GJB 2612A Specification for Cold-Drawn Wires of Superalloys for Welding; GJB 3020A Specification for Ring Blanks of Superalloys for Aviation; GJB 3165A Specification for Hot-Rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for Aviation Load-Bearing Parts; GJB 3167A Specification for Cold-Drawn Wires of Superalloys for Cold Heading; GJB 3782A Specification for Forged Disks of Superalloys for Aviation; GJB 5280 Specification for Disk Forgings of Superalloys for Aero Engines; GJB 5301 Specification for Rings of Superalloys for Aviation; YB/T 5245 Hot-Rolled and Forged Bars of Superalloys for General Load-Bearing Parts; YB/T 5249 Specification for Cold-Drawn Wires of Superalloys for Cold Heading; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Superalloys for Aviation
Melting Process for Alloy GH2036: Arc furnace, or arc furnace + electroslag remelting, or non-vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting processes are used.

Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy GH2036: Extracted from HB/Z 140, the standard heat treatment schedules for various product forms are: a) Hot-rolled and forged bars: 1140℃+5℃/flowing water cooling + ≤670℃×(12~14)h → (770~800)℃×(12~14)h/AC. Solution treatment holding time: for d<50mm, hold 80min; for d≥50mm, hold 105min. Hardness: HB 311~277. b) Cold-drawn bars: 1140℃+5℃×80min/flowing water cooling + 670℃×(12~14)h → (770~800)℃×(10~12)/AC. Hardness: HB 311~277. c) Forgings: 1140℃±10℃/WQ + (800~850)℃×(6~7)h/AC. Solution treatment holding time ≥50min (calculated based on cross-section thickness). Hardness: HB302~241. d) Disks: 1140℃ (or 1130℃)×80min/WQ + (650~670)℃×(14~16)h → (770~800)℃×(14~20)h/AC. Hardness: HB 311~277. e) Disk Forgings: (1130~1150)℃×(1.75~3)h/WQ + 660℃±10℃×(14~16)h → (770~800)℃×(14~20)h/AC. Hardness: HB 311~277.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy GH2036: Extracted from GB/T 14994, GB/T 14997, GB/T 14998, GJB 2611A, GJB 3020A, GJB 3167A, GJB 3782A, GJB 5280, GJB 5301, YB/T 5245, and YB/T 5249.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy GH2036: 1365℃[6].
Density of Alloy GH2036: ρ=7.85g/cm³[2].
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy GH2036: No special requirements.

Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy GH2036: The maximum precipitation of VC occurs between 700°C~800°C. The precipitation peak for Cr₂₃C₆ is at 950°C. VC and Cr₂₃C₆ are essentially dissolved at 1100°C~1120°C. NbC begins to dissolve above 1200°C.

Overview of Alloy GH6159: GH6159 is a Co-Ni-Cr-based precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy for use at temperatures below 600°C. The alloy is solid-solution strengthened by nickel, chromium, and molybdenum, and precipitation hardened by the γ' phase formed from aluminum and titanium. Additionally, cold deformation induces the formation of a network of ε phase, which impedes long-range dislocation motion and strengthens the alloy. The alloy has a high work hardening rate, ultra-high strength at room and high temperatures, good ductility and toughness, and high stress corrosion cracking resistance. It is currently the best superalloy for engine fasteners for long-term use below 600°C, offering the highest strength and shear resistance along with excellent overall performance. The main supplied product is deep cold-drawn bars.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy GH6159: The alloy has been used to manufacture high-load bolts for sealing disks and turbine disks, high-pressure compressor shafts, and high-pressure turbine shaft connections in advanced aero engines; high-load bolts for rocket engines; and ultra-high-strength bolts for service in stress corrosion environments (e.g., marine environments). Process parameters, including cold-drawing pass reduction, must be strictly controlled during metallurgical production. Insufficient reduction leads to inadequate strength; excessive reduction increases strength but reduces ductility. The alloy has excellent resistance to crevice corrosion, stress corrosion cracking, and hydrogen embrittlement.
Material Designation of Alloy GH6159: GH6159 (GH159).
Similar Designations of Alloy GH6159: MP159 (USA).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH6159: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; C3S 284 Cold-Drawn Bars of GH159 Alloy for High-Temperature Fasteners; Q/6S 992 Cold-Drawn Bars of GH159 Alloy for High-Temperature Fasteners; Xie Shang Wu Gao 28 Cold-Drawn Bars of GH159 Alloy for High-Temperature Fasteners; AMS 5841B Vacuum Induction + Vacuum Consumable Remelted, Solution Heat-Treated Bars of 19Cr-36Co-25Ni-7.0Mo-0.5Nb-2.9Ti-0.2Al-9.0Fe Corrosion and Heat Resistant Alloy; AMS 5842B Vacuum Induction + Vacuum Consumable Remelted, Solution Heat-Treated and Cold-Worked Bars of 19Cr-36Co-25Ni-7.0Mo-0.5Nb-2.9Ti-0.2Al-9.0Fe Corrosion and Heat Resistant Alloy
Melting Process for Alloy GH6159: Vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting + vacuum consumable remelting processes are used.

Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy GH6159: Extracted from C3S 284, Q/6S 992, AMS 5841B, and AMS 5842B, the standard heat treatment schedules for hot-rolled and cold-drawn bars are: a) Hot-rolled bar solution treatment: (1040~1050)℃±15℃×(4~8)h/WQ. b) Cold-drawn bar complete treatment includes: Solution + Cold Drawing + Aging: Solution heat treatment: (1040~1050)℃±15℃×(4~8)h/WQ; Cold drawing reduction: 48%±1%; Aging heat treatment: (650~675)℃±15℃×(4~4.5)h/AC.
Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy GH6159: Extracted from C3S 284, Q/6S 992, and AMS 5841B. d5mm~25mm cold-drawn bars. Cold-drawn bars are supplied in the cold-drawn condition.
Density of Alloy GH6159: ρ=8.33g/cm³.
Magnetic Properties of Alloy GH6159: At 25°C, the magnetic permeability is 1.00265.

Corrosion Resistance of Alloy GH6159: The alloy has excellent resistance to crevice corrosion and stress corrosion cracking. It shows no crevice or pitting corrosion in typical ferric chloride tests. No damage occurs in salt spray tests. Alternate immersion tests demonstrate good resistance to hydrogen embrittlement and stress corrosion cracking.

Forming Process and Performance of Alloy GH6159: Forging heating temperature: 1150℃±10℃, start forging temperature ≥1100℃, final forging temperature ≥900℃. Hot rolling heating temperature: 1150℃±10℃, hold 30min~60min, maximum pass reduction should not exceed 20%, final rolling temperature ≥900℃. After hot rolling, the alloy is annealed at 1050°C~1075°C for 1h to obtain uniform grain size for subsequent cold working. Hot-rolled bars, after solution treatment, are pickled to remove scale, coated, and then cold drawn. Cold drawing reduction is 48%±1%.
Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy GH6159: The aging heat treatment process for bolts after hot upsetting is: (650~675)℃×4h/AC.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy GH6159: Cold-drawn bars are locally induction heated, hot upset into nuts, and then threads are cold rolled.

Overview of Alloy DZ405: DZ405 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened directionally solidified columnar grain superalloy for use at temperatures below 1000°C. Except for carbon and zirconium, its composition is identical to the K405 alloy. Compared to K405, its medium-temperature stress rupture life is increased by 4~6 times, rupture ductility by 3~4 times, high-temperature stress rupture life by about 1 time, and thermal fatigue life by about 10 times. The alloy has stable microstructure and properties and good castability. Main products are turbine working blades and guide vanes.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy DZ405: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine turbine blades and guide vanes, and other high-temperature components. Blades can be coated via vapor phase, slurry aluminizing, or physical vapor deposition processes. The microstructure remains stable after long-term aging, with no TCP phase precipitation.
Material Designation of Alloy DZ405: DZ405 (DZ5).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy DZ405: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; HB 7728 Specification for Precision Cast Turbine Working Blades of Superalloys for Aviation; Q/6S 193 DZ5 Alloy Technical Specification

Melting Process for Alloy DZ405: Master alloy is melted using a vacuum induction furnace; directionally solidified test bars and parts are cast by remelting in a vacuum directional solidification furnace.
Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy DZ405: Extracted from Q/6S 193, the standard heat treatment schedule for the alloy is: 1220℃±10℃×4h/AC + 870℃±10℃×32h/AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy DZ405: Extracted from Q/6S 193. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 100mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; directionally solidified castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy DZ405: 1290℃~1345℃[1].
Density of Alloy DZ405: ρ=8.12g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy DZ405: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming Process and Performance of Alloy DZ405: The alloy has good castability and can be cast into complex columnar grain blades or other parts, with a casting shrinkage of about 2%.
Weldability of Alloy DZ405: Can be vacuum brazed and argon arc welded.
Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy DZ405: Near-net-shape blades should be heat-treated in vacuum or protective atmosphere. The heat treatment specification is: 1220℃±10℃×4h/AC + 870℃±10℃×32h/AC.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy DZ405: Vapor phase or slurry aluminizing, or physical vapor deposition processes, etc. can be used.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy DZ405: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH2903: GH2903 is an Fe-Ni-Co-based precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy characterized by a low thermal expansion coefficient and nearly constant elastic modulus over a wide temperature range, for use at temperatures below 650°C. The alloy has high strength, good thermal fatigue resistance, weldability, and resistance to high-pressure hydrogen embrittlement. It is an ideal material for implementing engine clearance control technology, improving engine efficiency, and reducing fuel consumption. Suitable for manufacturing turbine casings, seal rings, etc. for aerospace engines. Main products include hot-rolled and forged bars, ring blanks, and rings.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy GH2903: The alloy has been used for turbine casings, outer turbine rings, inner and outer guide rings, and seal rings in aerospace engines, and is widely used in precision electronic instruments and meters. Warm working strengthening is required to achieve high strength. Due to the lack of chromium, protective coatings are needed for use at elevated temperatures. The alloy has a tendency for stress-accelerated grain boundary oxidation embrittlement, leading to reduced high-temperature notch stress rupture performance.
Material Designation of Alloy GH2903: GH2903(GH903).
Similar Designations of Alloy GH2903: Incoloy 903 (USA).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH2903: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; GJB 5261 Specification for Low-Expansion Superalloy Bars; HB6574 GH903 Low-Expansion Alloy Ring Blanks and Rings; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Superalloys for Aviation
Melting Process for Alloy GH2903: Vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting processes are used.

Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy GH2903: Extracted from HB/Z140, the standard heat treatment schedule for hot-rolled and forged bars and rings is: 845℃±10℃×1h/AC + 720℃±10℃×8h/FC (55℃/h) → 620℃±10℃×8h/AC, HBS415~341.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy GH2903: Extracted from GJB5261 and HB6574. Various specifications of hot-rolled and forged bars, ring blanks, and rings. Hot-rolled and forged bars are generally supplied without heat treatment; bars, forging blanks, and ring blanks for hot working are supplied without heat treatment, turned or in black condition; rings are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy GH2903: 1318℃~1393℃[7].
Density of Alloy GH2903: ρ=8.23g/cm³[7]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy GH2903: The alloy is ferromagnetic below the Curie point temperature and non-magnetic above it.
Hardness of Alloy GH2903: Rings after standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness HBS388[2].

Weldability of Alloy GH2903: The alloy has satisfactory weldability and can be welded by various methods. Welding temperature should be below the alloy's recrystallization temperature to avoid complete recrystallization and softening in the heat-affected zone, which can cause notch sensitivity.
Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy GH2903: Same as the standard heat treatment schedule for respective product forms.

Surface Treatment Process for Alloy GH2903: Components operating at higher temperatures can be coated via slurry aluminizing or slurry AlSi coating, combined with the part's heat treatment for coating and diffusion treatment. Argon-protected diffusion treatment specification: 845℃×1h (coating application) + 720℃×8h/FC (within 2h) → 620℃×8h/AC, coating thickness about 15μm.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy GH2903: The alloy can be satisfactorily machined.
Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy GH2903: The precipitation peak range for η-Ni3Ti phase is 830°C~850°C.

Overview of Alloy GH4093: GH4093 is a Ni-Cr-Co-based precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy for long-term use below 750°C and short-term use up to 800°C. The alloy has high high-temperature strength, high stress rupture and creep strength, and good fatigue performance, with stable microstructure and good overall properties. It has good hot workability and weldability. Suitable for manufacturing aero-engine turbine blades, small engine turbine disks, and fasteners. Main products include plates, bars, and forgings.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy GH4093: The alloy has been used to manufacture grade I and II free turbine blades, grade I and II turbine disks, as well as tension bolts, lock plates, gaskets, etc., for helicopter engines, with good production and service records. The forged material shows no notch sensitivity in stress rupture tests at 650°C and 750°C. After long-term aging at 800°C, part of the M23C6-type carbides at grain boundaries form a continuous film.
Material Designation of Alloy GH4093: GH4093(GH93).
Similar Designations of Alloy GH4093: Nimonic93 (UK), NCK2OTA (France).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH4093: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Superalloys for Aviation; C3S 164 GH93 Alloy Cold-Rolled Sheet Technical Specification; C3S 593 GH93 Alloy Hot-Rolled and Forged Bars Technical Specification
Melting Process for Alloy GH4093: Vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting processes are used.

Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy GH4093: Extracted from HB/Z 140, the standard heat treatment schedules for various product forms are: a) Cold-rolled sheet: (1100~1150)℃/AC + 710℃±10℃×16h/AC. Solution treatment holding time depends on sheet thickness. Hardness: HB≥290HV. b) Bars: (1050~1080)℃×8h/AC + 710℃±10℃×15h/AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy GH4093: Extracted from C3S 164 and C3S 593. δ0.4mm~4.0mm cold-rolled sheets; d20mm~30.0mm hot-rolled bars; ≤d120mm forgings and forged parts. Cold-rolled sheets are supplied after solution treatment + pickling + straightening + edge trimming; bars and forgings are generally supplied without heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy GH4093: 1360℃~1390℃[5].
Density of Alloy GH4093: ρ=8.19g/cm³[4].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy GH4093: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Weldability of Alloy GH4093: Sheets can be joined by argon arc welding. If welded in the solution-treated condition, subsequent stress relief and aging treatment should be performed.

Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy GH4093: Turbine disk blanks can be solution treated first, machined, and then aged under argon protection; or the disk blank can be fully heat-treated before machining. During heat treatment, blanks should be placed flat in a single layer within the furnace's uniform temperature zone, heated slowly, and dispersed for cooling after removal.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy GH4093: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy K406C: K406C is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed crystal cast superalloy, a modified version of K406, for use at temperatures below 850°C. It has lower carbon content and higher chromium content than K406, resulting in higher high-temperature mechanical properties and high-temperature oxidation and hot gas corrosion resistance compared to K406, along with good castability, weldability, and machinability. Main products include turbine rotor blades, guide vanes, integral turbines, and other components operating in hot gas corrosive environments.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy K406C: The alloy has been used to manufacture integral guide vanes for gas turbines and free turbines operating below 850°C in aero turboshaft engines, and missile fins, with good service records. The alloy has a relatively simple composition, contains no scarce or precious elements, and is inexpensive. During long-term aging at 850°C, there is a tendency to precipitate σ phase (which can be avoided with good composition control), but this has little impact on properties. When surface protection is needed, slurry aluminizing can be used.
Material Designation of Alloy K406C: K406C(K6C)

Material Technical Standards for Alloy K406C: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; HB 7728 Specification for Precision Cast Turbine Working Blades of Superalloys for Aviation; HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed Crystal Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero Engines; HB/Z140 Heat Treatment Process for Superalloys for Aviation

Melting Process for Alloy K406C: Master alloy is melted using a vacuum induction furnace; parts and test bars are cast via investment precision casting by vacuum induction remelting. Test specimens for mechanical properties are cast with an inoculant/refiner applied to the inner surface of the shell mold[5].
Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy K406C: Extracted from HB/Z140, the standard heat treatment schedule for the alloy is: 980℃±10℃×5h/AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy K406C: Extracted from HB 7763. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and lengths not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied after standard heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy K406C: 1260℃~1340℃[1].
Density of Alloy K406C: ρ=8.05g/cm³[1]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K406C: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K406C: After standard heat treatment, the room temperature hardness is HRC 34~36[1].
Forming Process and Performance of Alloy K406C: The alloy has good castability and low hot tearing tendency, enabling the casting of various complex integral parts, turbine blades, and guide vanes.
Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K406C: Same as the standard heat treatment schedule for the alloy.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K406C: When surface protection is required in service, slurry aluminizing can be used.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy K406C: The alloy is easy to machine and does not develop grinding cracks during grinding.

Overview of Alloy GH3181: GH3181 is a Ni-Cr-based solid-solution strengthened wrought superalloy for use in the temperature range of 900°C~1200°C. The alloy has relatively high strength and ductility, creep resistance, thermal fatigue resistance, good oxidation and corrosion resistance, along with good workability and weldability. Suitable for manufacturing gas turbine combustion chambers, heat exchangers for nuclear industry, etc. Main products include plates, bars, wires, and forgings.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy GH3181: The alloy has been used to manufacture series nozzles for petrochemical industry and heat exchangers for nuclear industry. It is resistant to liquid sodium corrosion at high temperatures and can be used as a structural material for high-temperature sodium heat pipes. The production process is stable, and the microstructure remains stable during long-term high-temperature service.
Material Designation of Alloy GH3181: GH3181(GH181)
Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH3181: Q/GYB 05119 GH3181 Alloy Hot-Rolled and Forged Bars; Q/GYB 05121 GH3181 Alloy Cold-Rolled Sheet
Melting Process for Alloy GH3181: Vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting process is used.

Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy GH3181: Extracted from Q/GYB 05119 and Q/GYB 05121, the standard heat treatment schedules for various product forms are: a) Hot-rolled and forged bars: 1200℃±10℃×1h/AC. b) Cold-rolled sheet: 1180℃±10℃×10min/AC.
Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy GH3181: Extracted from Q/GYB 05119 and Q/GYB 05121.
Density of Alloy GH3181: ρ=9.29g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy GH3181: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Corrosion Resistance of Alloy GH3181: After exposure to 1070°C liquid sodium for 50h, the corrosion weight loss is 0.0051g. Macroscopically, the surface shows slight corrosion. Metallographic cross-section observation shows a smooth surface with a maximum surface roughness depth of 0.02mm.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy GH3181: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy K405: K405 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed crystal cast superalloy strengthened by multiple metallic elements, for use at temperatures below 950°C. The alloy has relatively high medium- and high-temperature stress rupture properties. It has good castability and can be cast into complex hollow blades. Suitable for manufacturing gas turbine rotor blades and other high-temperature components operating below 950°C.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy K405: The alloy has been used to manufacture grade I hollow turbine blades and grade II turbine working blades for aero engines and has entered airline service. No σ phase precipitation is observed after long-term aging at 800°C~900°C. When composition is near the upper limit or casting process is improper, a small amount of σ phase may appear in the blade shank area, with little effect on 900°C stress rupture and 750°C instantaneous mechanical properties. Controlling composition to the mid-range can avoid σ phase formation[9]. Blade surface protection uses AlSi coating.
Material Designation of Alloy K405: K405(K5).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy K405: GB/T14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; GJB5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds Part 1: Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys for General Precision Castings; HB 7728 Specification for Precision Cast Turbine Working Blades of Superalloys for Aviation; HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed Crystal Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero Engines; HB/Z140 Heat Treatment Process for Superalloys for Aviation

Melting and Casting Process for Alloy K405: Master alloy is melted using a vacuum induction furnace; parts and test bars are cast via investment precision casting by vacuum induction remelting. Pouring temperature: 1400°C~1420°C, mold temperature: 780°C~950°C.
Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy K405: Extracted from HB/Z140, the alloy is used in the as-cast condition.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy K405: Extracted from GJB 5512.1 and HB 7763. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 70mm to 90mm and lengths not less than 250mm. Master alloy and precision castings are supplied in the as-cast condition.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy K405: 1290℃~1345℃[1]
Density of Alloy K405: ρ=8.12g/cm³[1]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K405: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy K405: In the as-cast condition, the average room temperature hardness is HRC 38[8].
Impact Properties of Alloy K405: Precision-cast test bars in as-cast condition, average room temperature impact toughness: αKU=123kJ/m²[8].
Forming Process and Performance of Alloy K405: The alloy has good castability; complex thin-walled hollow blades can be cast using the investment casting method.
Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K405: Parts are used in the as-cast condition.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K405: Blade surface protection uses AlSi coating. Coating process: 900°C, 5h.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy K405: No special requirements.

Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy K405: The precipitation temperature ranges for M23C6 and M6C carbides are 750°C~1080°C and 800°C~1220°C, with precipitation peaks at 950°C~1000°C and 1000°C~1050°C, respectively.

Overview of Alloy K424: K424 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed crystal cast superalloy for use below 1000°C. Its characteristics are high aluminum and titanium content and low density. The alloy has relatively high high-temperature strength and ductility, and good castability. Main products include turbine rotor blades, integral cast turbine rotors and guide vanes, tail nozzle regulating sheet bases, and other precision cast structural components.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy K424: The alloy has been applied in the manufacture of aerospace engine turbine blades, tail nozzle regulating vanes, integral turbine rotors and guide vanes, etc., with good production and service records. The alloy exhibits cyclic hardening during low-cycle fatigue at room temperature and 750°C, with increasing stress resistance that stabilizes over time. There is a tendency to precipitate σ phase during long-term service around 850°C, sensitive to part size. This can be reduced or delayed by controlling alloy composition, melting parameters, and using thin-walled sections. Also, σ phase precipitation tendency can be predicted by the average electron vacancy number (Nv); when Nv ≥ 2.7, significant σ phase precipitates; when Nv ≤ 2.28, no σ phase precipitates[2].
Material Designation of Alloy K424: K424(424)
Similar Designations of Alloy K424: B>KJI-12Y, B>KJI-129 (Russia)

Material Technical Standards for Alloy K424: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds Part 1: Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys for General Precision Castings; HB 7763 Specification for Equiaxed Crystal Cast Superalloy Ingots for Aero Engines; HB/Z 140 Heat Treatment Process for Superalloys for Aviation; Q/GYB 05054 K424 Alloy Turbine Rotor Precision Castings; Q/GYB 05055 K424 Alloy Low-Pressure Turbine Working Wheel Precision Castings; Q/GYB 05056 K424 Alloy Guide Vane I Precision Castings

Melting and Casting Process for Alloy K424: Master alloy is melted using a vacuum induction furnace; precision castings and test bars are cast via investment precision casting by vacuum induction remelting.
Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy K424: Extracted from HB/Z 140, the alloy is used either as-cast or after heat treatment. The standard heat treatment schedule is: 1210℃±10℃×4h/AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy K424: Extracted from GJB 5512.1, HB 7763, Q/GYB 05054, Q/GYB 05055, and Q/GYB 05056. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 70mm to 90mm and lengths not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision castings are supplied either as-cast or after heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy K424: 1271℃~1310℃[1]
Density of Alloy K424: ρ=7.87g/cm³[1]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K424: The alloy is non-magnetic.

Forming Process and Performance of Alloy K424: The alloy has good castability. Complex thin-walled hollow blades, integral turbines, and regulating vanes with varying thicknesses can be cast using the investment casting method.
Weldability of Alloy K424: The alloy has poor weldability; finished blades are not allowed to be repair welded.

Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K424: Parts are used either as-cast or after heat treatment at 1210℃×4h/AC. Near-net-shape blade castings should be heat-treated in vacuum or protective atmosphere.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K424: The shank teeth area of turbine rotor blades can be shot peened[6].
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy K424: Use carbide tools.

Overview of Alloy DZ640M: DZ640M is a cobalt-based precipitation-hardened directionally solidified columnar grain superalloy for use below 1040°C. Developed from the American X-40 alloy by adding tantalum, molybdenum, zirconium, aluminum, and titanium, it increases the service temperature by 40°C compared to X-40[1]. The alloy has a high incipient melting temperature, stable microstructure, excellent thermal fatigue resistance, oxidation and hot corrosion resistance, along with good creep, stress rupture, and fatigue resistance. Main products are directionally solidified castings like turbine guide vanes.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy DZ640M: The alloy has been used to manufacture turbine guide vanes with complex internal cooling channels for aviation, marine, and land-based gas turbines. The alloy has a relatively flat stress-rupture life-temperature parameter characteristic and no notch sensitivity. The microstructure remains stable after long-term high-temperature aging, with no TCP phase precipitation.
Material Designation of Alloy DZ640M: DZ640M (DZ40M).
Similar Designations of Alloy DZ640M: DSX40 (USA).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy DZ640M: GB/T14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; GJB5512.2 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds Part 2: Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys for Directionally Solidified Columnar Grain Castings; HB 7762 Specification for Directionally Solidified Columnar Grain and Single Crystal Superalloy Ingots for Aero Engines; HB/Z140 Heat Treatment Process for Superalloys for Aviation; Q/5B4042 DZ40M Alloy Investment Precision Cast High-Pressure Turbine Guide Vanes (Airfoil)

Melting and Casting Process for Alloy DZ640M: Master alloy is melted using a vacuum induction furnace; directionally solidified test bars and castings are cast by remelting and pouring in a vacuum directional solidification furnace.
Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy DZ640M: Extracted from HB/Z 140, the alloy is used in the as-cast condition.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy DZ640M: Extracted from GJB5512.2, HB7762, and Q/5B4042. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and lengths not less than 250mm. Master alloy and castings are supplied in the as-cast condition.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy DZ640M: 1345℃~1395℃[2].
Density of Alloy DZ640M: ρ=8.68g/cm³[2]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy DZ640M: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy DZ640M: In the as-cast condition, the room temperature hardness is HBS253~237[2].
Impact Properties of Alloy DZ640M: Directionally solidified test bars in as-cast condition, longitudinally sampled, room temperature impact toughness αku=(170~280)kJ/m²[3].
Shear Properties of Alloy DZ640M: Directionally solidified test bars in as-cast condition, longitudinally sampled, average room temperature shear strength τb=70.4MPa[3].
Forming Process and Performance of Alloy DZ640M: The alloy has good castability and can be cast into thin-walled directional hollow blades with complex internal cavities.
Weldability of Alloy DZ640M: Good brazing weldability.
Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy DZ640M: Parts are used in the as-cast condition.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy DZ640M: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy GH2136: GH2136 is an Fe-Ni-Cr-based precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy for use at temperatures below 700°C. Developed from GH2132, it has reduced manganese and silicon content, and appropriately increased titanium, boron, and carbon content, reducing the tendency for brittle phases like G-phase and σ-phase to precipitate during long-term service, thereby improving microstructural and property stability. The alloy has good overall properties, stable microstructure during long-term use, relatively good oxidation resistance, low thermal expansion coefficient, and is easy to weld and form. Main products include bars and disks.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy GH2136: The alloy has been used to manufacture aero-engine turbine disks and other high-temperature components operating at 650°C~700°C. After long-term aging at 600°C~700°C for 1000h~3000h, the γ' phase gradually transforms to η phase, reducing plasticity and stress rupture limit[2]. Cellular η phase forms around 800°C, appearing as plates or Widmanstätten structure at higher temperatures, reducing impact toughness and ductility[2]. The alloy can be used to produce electroslag cast turbine disks; see K2136 alloy.
Material Designation of Alloy GH2136: GH2136(GH136).
Similar Designations of Alloy GH2136: V-57 (USA).
Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH2136: GB/T 14997 Forged Disks of Superalloys
Melting Process for Alloy GH2136: Non-vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting process is used.
Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy GH2136: Extracted from GB/T 14997 and literature[1]. The standard heat treatment schedule for forged disks and bars is: 980℃×1h/OQ + 720℃×16h/AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy GH2136: d20mm~300mm bars; disks ≤d600mm, h60mm~150mm. Bars are generally supplied without heat treatment; disks are supplied without heat treatment, possibly turned.
Density of Alloy GH2136: ρ=8.03g/cm³[1].
Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy GH2136: Same as the standard heat treatment schedule for respective product forms.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy GH2136: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy FGH4096: FGH4096 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened powder metallurgy superalloy, with γ' phase volume fraction about 36%, for long-term use below 700°C. It is a second-generation damage-tolerant PM superalloy. Compared to first-generation PM superalloys, it has reduced γ' content, adjusted grain size, slightly lower strength, improved crack growth resistance, resulting in excellent overall mechanical properties, corrosion resistance, and high-temperature oxidation resistance. It is one of the highest-strength turbine disk superalloys for 700°C service and a key material for high-performance engine turbine disks, rings, and other hot-end components.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy FGH4096: The alloy, processed via argon atomization (AA) + hot isostatic pressing (HIP) + isothermal forging (HIF), has been used for high/low-pressure turbine disks, seal disks, and baffles in aero engines. Components made via plasma rotating electrode process (PREP) powder + as-HIP have been used for baffles and elastic rings. These have passed engine tests and operational assessment. The foreign counterpart René88DT primarily uses argon atomization (AA) + hot extrusion (HIX) + isothermal forging (HIF) process. Its turbine and compressor disks are widely used in various military and civil aero engines.
Material Designation of Alloy FGH4096: FGH4096(FGH96).
Similar Designations of Alloy FGH4096: René88DT (USA).

Melting and Processing for Alloy FGH4096: Master alloy bars are melted using a vacuum induction furnace. Pre-alloyed powder is prepared via argon atomization (AA) or plasma rotating electrode process (PREP). Powder is sieved, vacuum degassed, canned, and sealed. Blanks are then produced via AA+HIP+HIF+heat treatment, or parts are produced via PREP powder + direct HIP (AS-HIP) + heat treatment. AA powder typically uses -250 mesh (≤73μm); PREP powder typically uses -100 mesh (≤150μm).

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy FGH4096: Extracted from Q/11S 287 and Q/GYB 660. d590mm~640mm disks; rings with OD 500mm~650mm, ID 450mm~500mm. Disk and ring blanks are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy FGH4096: 1280℃~1360℃[3].
Density of Alloy FGH4096: ρ=(8.30~8.34)g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy FGH4096: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy FGH4096: Same as standard heat treatment schedule I or II for the alloy.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy FGH4096: Parts can be surface shot peened to improve notch fatigue performance.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy FGH4096: No special requirements.
Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy FGH4096: γ' phase precipitation peak temperature is 1030℃~1090℃; complete dissolution temperature is 1130℃~1140℃.

Overview of Alloy GH4098: GH4098 is a Ni-Cr-based precipitation-hardened wrought superalloy for long-term service up to 1000°C. The alloy is solid-solution strengthened by tungsten, molybdenum, chromium, and cobalt; precipitation hardened by the γ' phase formed from aluminum, titanium, and niobium; and grain boundary strengthened by trace boron and cerium. The strengthening effect is comprehensive. The alloy has high room and high-temperature strength, excellent oxidation resistance, and good hot workability and weldability. Main products include hot-rolled bars, hot-rolled plates, cold-rolled sheets and strips.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy GH4098: The alloy is used for turbine blades, heat shields, tail pipes, and afterburner chambers in aviation and missile engines. Similar alloys abroad are used for engine baffles, stiffeners, aircraft attachments, guide vanes, and other components up to 1000°C made by cold stamping and welding.
Material Designation of Alloy GH4098: GH4098(GH98).
Similar Designations of Alloy GH4098: ЭΠ99 (Russia).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy GH4098: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; QJ/DT 0160011 GH98 Alloy Hot-Rolled and Forged Bars Technical Specification; Liao Xin 7-0069 GH98 Alloy Cold-Rolled Sheet Technical Specification; QJ/DT 0160010 GH98 Alloy Cold-Rolled Strip Technical Specification
Melting Process for Alloy GH4098: Vacuum induction melting + vacuum consumable remelting, or vacuum induction melting + electroslag remelting processes are used.

Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy GH4098: Extracted from QJ/DT 0160011, Liao Xin 7-0069, and QJ/DT 0160010, the standard heat treatment schedules for various product forms are: a) Forged and rolled bars: (1080~1100)℃×(2~4)h/AC + 760℃±20℃×(12~16)h/AC. b) Cold-rolled sheet: 1090℃±10℃×20min/WQ + 760℃±10℃×(8~12)h/AC. c) Cold-rolled strip: (1080~1120)℃/WQ or AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy GH4098: Extracted from QJ/DT 0160011, Liao Xin 7-0069, and QJ/DT 0160010. d15mm~200mm hot-rolled and forged bars; δ3mm~20mm hot-rolled plates; δ0.5mm~2.5mm cold-rolled sheets; δ0.3mm~0.6mm cold-rolled strips. Bars are supplied in hot-forged or hot-rolled condition; sheets are supplied after solution treatment + pickling/cleaning + leveling + straightening + edge trimming; cold-rolled strips are supplied in coils after bright solution treatment + edge trimming.
Density of Alloy GH4098: ρ=8.40g/cm³[1].
Magnetic Properties of Alloy GH4098: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Oxidation Resistance of Alloy GH4098: Oxidation rates after 100h at 1000°C and 1100°C in air are 0.368 g/(m²·h) and 0.715 g/(m²·h), respectively.

Forming Process and Performance of Alloy GH4098: The alloy can be hot and cold worked. Hot working range: 950°C~1180°C, air cool after deformation. Ingot charging temperature ≤1000°C, heating temperature 1140°C~1180°C; start rolling temperature ≥900°C, finish rolling temperature ≥1050°C. Maximum single-pass cold reduction ≤80%. Intermediate annealing for cold rolling ≥1000°C, air cool.

Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy GH4098: To remove cold work hardening, intermediate heat treatment is recommended: quenching at 1080°C~1120°C; cooling method depends on thickness: air cool for thickness below 2mm, water quench or spray cool for thickness above 2mm.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy GH4098: The alloy can be satisfactorily machined.

Overview of Alloy DZ417G: DZ417G is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened directionally solidified columnar grain superalloy, developed from K417G as a low-density alloy for use below 980°C. It has high medium-temperature strength, good creep resistance, high ductility, stable microstructure, and relatively low cost. Main products are long-life gas turbine rotor blades, guide vanes, and other high-temperature components.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy DZ417G: DZ417G is a patented alloy with independent intellectual property rights (Patent No. ZL95110023.8). It has been used to manufacture aero-engine gas turbine rotor blades and guide vanes, and directionally solidified low-pressure I, II stage turbine blades for advanced aero engines, with batch production.
Material Designation of Alloy DZ417G: DZ417G (DZ17G).

Material Technical Standards for Alloy DZ417G: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; GJB 5512.2 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds Part 2: Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys for Directionally Solidified Columnar Grain Castings; HB 7762 Specification for Directionally Solidified Columnar Grain and Single Crystal Superalloy Ingots for Aero Engines; HB/Z140 Heat Treatment Process for Superalloys for Aviation; Q/14B-SYF2004 DZ417G Alloy Directionally Solidified Low-Pressure I, II Stage Turbine Blades Technical Specification

Melting Process for Alloy DZ417G: Master alloy is melted using a vacuum induction furnace; directionally solidified castings and test bars are cast by remelting and pouring in a vacuum directional solidification furnace.
Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy DZ417G: Extracted from HB/Z140, the standard heat treatment schedule for the alloy is: 1220℃±10℃×4h/AC + 980℃±10℃×16h/AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy DZ417G: Extracted from GJB5512.2, HB7762, and Q/14B-SYF2004. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 70mm to 90mm and lengths not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; castings are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy DZ417G: 1286℃~1342℃.
Density of Alloy DZ417G: ρ=7.84g/cm³[1]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy DZ417G: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Hardness of Alloy DZ417G: After standard heat treatment, room temperature hardness is HBS327~306[1].
Impact Properties of Alloy DZ417G: Directionally solidified test bars after standard heat treatment, longitudinal room temperature impact toughness average αku=200kJ/m²[1].
Forming Process and Performance of Alloy DZ417G: The alloy has good castability and can be cast into directionally solidified turbine blades.
Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy DZ417G: Near-net-shape blades should be heat-treated in vacuum or protective atmosphere. Specification: 1220℃×2h/Argon cooling + 980℃×16h/Argon cooling.
Surface Treatment Process for Alloy DZ417G: Low-pressure vapor phase aluminizing or AlSi coating can be used.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy DZ417G: No special requirements.

Overview of Alloy K438: K438 is a nickel-based precipitation-hardened equiaxed crystal cast superalloy, one of the best alloys for hot corrosion resistance, for use below 900°C. Its composition and properties are equivalent to the widely used IN738 alloy abroad. It contains high chromium, along with tantalum and niobium. It offers excellent hot corrosion resistance, moderate high-temperature strength, and good microstructural stability. Main products are precision castings like turbine working blades and guide vanes.

Application Overview and Characteristics of Alloy K438: The alloy has been widely used for long-life turbine working blades and guide vanes in marine and industrial gas turbines, as well as turbine parts and corrosion-resistant components in aero engines. Thermal fatigue tests confirm that controlling the ratio of columnar grain zone length to blade width and individual grain size ensures excellent thermal fatigue resistance[6]. During long-term aging, M23C6+γ' networks form at grain boundaries, beneficial for high-temperature strength and toughness[5]. No σ phase precipitates after long-term aging, but improper casting process or thick sections may lead to minor σ phase in last-to-solidify regions.
Material Designation of Alloy K438: K438(K38, M38)
Similar Designations of Alloy K438: IN738 (USA)

Material Technical Standards for Alloy K438: GB/T 14992 Classification and Designation of Superalloys and High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds; GJB 5512.1 Specification for Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys and Cast High-Temperature Materials for Intermetallic Compounds Part 1: Master Alloys of Cast Superalloys for General Precision Castings

Melting and Casting Process for Alloy K438: Master alloy is melted using a vacuum induction furnace; parts and test bars are cast via investment precision casting by vacuum induction remelting. Pouring temperature: 1400°C~1480°C, mold temperature: 880°C~1050°C.
Heat Treatment Schedule for Alloy K438: Extracted from GJB 5512.1, the standard heat treatment schedule for the alloy is: 1120℃±10℃×2h/AC + 850℃±10℃×24h/AC.

Product Specifications and Supply Condition of Alloy K438: Extracted from GJB 5512.1. Master alloy is cast into round long bars with diameters ranging from 75mm to 90mm and lengths not less than 250mm. Master alloy is supplied in the as-cast condition; precision cast blades are supplied after heat treatment.
Melting Temperature Range of Alloy K438: 1260℃~1330℃[1]
Density of Alloy K438: ρ=8.16g/cm³[1]
Magnetic Properties of Alloy K438: The alloy is non-magnetic.
Forming Process and Performance of Alloy K438: The alloy has good castability and can be cast into near-net-shape precision turbine blades and other parts.

Part Heat Treatment Process for Alloy K438: 1120℃×2h/AC + 850℃×24h/AC. Thick blades must be air-blast cooled after solution treatment; slow cooling significantly reduces stress rupture properties[1]. Near-net-shape precision parts must have controlled atmosphere during heat treatment to prevent surface depletion, oxidation, or intergranular corrosion.

Surface Treatment Process for Alloy K438: Turbine blades can be slurry aluminized. Coating diffusion can be combined with heat treatment. PtCrAl coating, applying 7μm Pt electroplating followed by CrAl coating, can double hot corrosion resistance. Blade shank teeth can be shot peened to improve fatigue resistance.
Machining and Grinding Performance of Alloy K438: No special requirements.
Phase Transformation Temperature of Alloy K438: (γ+γ') eutectic and complete γ' dissolution temperature is 1150°C.
